It hurt so much.

"I confess, I am Mr. Yun. Don't let him in. I don't want to see him. I'll tell you everything."

Because after entering, without saying a word, Jiang Tian had brutally beaten him and mentioned the name of his bar: "Even if you don't talk, the evidence of your drug trafficking is still there. We found over 300 kilograms of drugs. You're guilty of four crimes."



Chapter 71 Drug Dealer Ledger, The Imminent Thunderstorm


Mr. Yun clenched his fists, his eyes bloodshot.

He was done for.

He was truly done for.

Mr. Yun never imagined he would be defeated so easily.

As for explanations, sorry, Jiang Tian's baton would teach him a lesson, and even if he denied it, it would be useless.

Because after confirming Mr. Yun's identity, even if he stubbornly denied it, his crime was already punishable by death.

Moreover, at Jiang Tian's request, Captain Guo went directly to his hometown to raid his bar, confiscating eighty kilograms of drugs.

But this was definitely not all; they were absolutely certain.

"Yes, I wanted to kill Jiang Tian, ​​and I sent the men out. I just didn't expect Lao Chen to betray me." Mr. Yun was furious, as if he wanted to kill.

As long as he left Yunhai and crossed the border, no matter what chaos erupted in Yunhai, it wouldn't affect Mr. Yun at all.

To be precise, Mr. Yun came to Yunhai because of the significant problems that had recently arisen there. In the past few days, the plan targeting Gao Xiaolong had failed, and even his subordinates had been discovered.

They had just finished investigating and were preparing to take action against Jiang Tian when, in the next moment, he was gone.

Even now, sitting in the interrogation room, Mr. Yun felt somewhat dazed, as if in a dream.

Indeed, in Mr. Yun's mind, only Lao Chen's betrayal could be discovered.

Hiss!!!

As Mr. Yun spoke, everyone present erupted in wild joy.

Even Lao Mu and Gao Xiaolong, who were observing from the conference room...

After Mr. Yun confessed completely, they visibly breathed a sigh of relief.

"They really caught him! This kid's done a great job again," Gao Xiaolong exclaimed, clicking his tongue in amazement.

He could already imagine that if this were reported upfront, the Ministry of Public Security would be closely monitoring Jiang Tian.

The Ministry of Public Security had given them half a month to solve the case, but Jiang Tian had apprehended the man in just a few hours.

This was incredible.

If they reported it, not only the Ministry of Public Security, but even they themselves would feel like they were witnessing a dream.

However, while they were still in shock, in the interrogation room, Jiang Tian himself sat with the interrogation experts, with Miss Qiu observing them with deadpan eyes.

"Actually, I'm from a neighboring country, not from this country. I'm from the Yun family in Myanmar," Mr. Yun said, dropping a bombshell.

"The Yun family?"

Miss Qiu was unfamiliar with the situation and was inevitably full of questions. After all, she was a criminal investigator and didn't know much about foreign forces.

But Jiang Tian had heard of them.

However, he didn't know them in this world; he knew them from his previous life.

The Yun family.

One of the major families in Myanmar. They might not be well-known now, but in later generations, after their full exposure, these families were completely exposed.

It was a notorious explosion.

Even to the point that it implicated the government, reaching a point of no return.

Jiang Tian knew about the major families in Myanmar from his previous life, but the expressions of these leaders, especially Elder Mu and Gao Xiaolong, all changed.

"What are they trying to do?" Gao Xiaolong roared in fury.

"This is going too far."

Elder Mu's expression also changed slightly.

Although not everyone knows this now due to future generations, the leadership is naturally very familiar with the neighboring powers.

"So, your current heroin is all from the Yun family? Did the Yun family send you?" Jiang Tian asked.

Mr. Yun didn't deny it: "The family doesn't care where we sell it. We just need to buy drugs from them and resell them ourselves. Wei Youdao is one of my recruits. However, I wasn't satisfied with the family's drug purchase price, so I instructed Wei Youdao to set up a drug factory."

"Where is the ledger hidden?"

Jiang Tian asked the most crucial question at this moment.

That's right.

The ledger.

Jiang Tian had no interest in Mr. Yun's so-called family power. He wouldn't bother with him anyway.

But if they did, Jiang Tian would make them understand what fear was.

It's possible that Jiang Tian, ​​in a fit of rage, might march straight to their hometown and wipe out the Yun family.

And the so-called ledger...

is the most valuable thing a drug dealer possesses.

Small-time drug dealers wouldn't have one.

But big drug lords definitely have ledgers, and through these ledgers, they could even dismantle an entire drug trafficking network.

And the achievement of this...

If they really manage to wipe out Mr. Yun's entire drug trafficking network within the country, Jiang Tian even suspects his superiors might recommend him for a special merit award.

Sure enough, everyone held their breath.



Chapter 72 Promotion and Salary Increase: Success in Both Career and Love


"The ledger is in the safe in the bar's basement. The password is..."

There was nothing to hide. Mr. Yun sat in the interrogation room, watching Jiang Tian sharpen his knives at him, making Mr. Yun's eyelids twitch uncontrollably.

"Well done."

Gao Xiaolong was beaming with delight, then glanced at Jiang Peng.

Jiang Peng instantly understood and took out his phone to call Song Liguo.

That's why, although Jiang Tian had returned, there were still many things to deal with at the scene, especially since Jiang Tian had revealed the situation at the bar and the suspicions surrounding it. Song Liguo had immediately sealed off the bar.

In this era, Jiang Tian didn't know if this situation was unique to Yunhai, or if it was common throughout the country—bars always seemed to be rife with the old three vices: gambling, prostitution, and drugs.

Jiang Tian, ​​along with Mr. Yun and the leaders, returned to the police station immediately. As for Lao Chen, he had already gone for emergency treatment. Compared to Lao Chen, Mr. Yun and his group of three had the least injuries.

Although they were nearly disfigured, their faces were already swollen high in a short time.

“Chief Song, this is Jiang Peng. I need you to take control of the entire YG bar immediately. Also, inside the bar, there's a basement beneath the storage room. Bring the safes inside back to the police station. They contain the drug lord's ledgers. If this case can be solved, it might become one of the top ten cases in the history of Yunhai, or even the whole country, perhaps even a textbook example.” Jiang Peng immediately gave the order in a serious tone.

Hiss!!!

Song Liguo on the other end of the phone was almost stunned by this surprise.

“Chief Song, what’s wrong?”

The YG bar was completely sealed off. There weren't many staff members inside, because it was lunchtime. The bar's normal opening hours were after 8 or 9 pm, and that's when the male and female models usually came.

The only people inside were a few waiters on duty, the manager, and some junior management.

Even looking at the trembling people, Captain Guo began to doubt whether Jiang Tian had made a mistake.

Was this bar really the legendary headquarters of Mr. Yun?

“The ledger, the drug lord’s ledger, has been revealed.” Song Liguo tried to keep his voice steady.

This was a major drug lord!

“What?” Captain Guo was utterly shocked.

No one understood better than them what the drug lord’s ledger meant.

It meant how many suppliers the drug lord had, and how many drug dealers would be brought to justice.

And it meant that the seemingly impenetrable Yunhai criminal organization had been completely breached.

Did Kyoto not know about the situation in Yunhai?

No, the higher-ups had known for a long time. They had sent many special envoys, but the final result was either disappearance or accidental death.

Kyoto was furious with Yunhai and sent Gao Xiaolong to explain the reason, and even prepared for a major reshuffle.

But the criminal syndicate's cover-up, its ruthlessness and unbridled audacity, had instilled fear even in narcotics officers.

No one dared to be the first to act.

Yunhai was under constant scrutiny from Kyoto, and even the Ministry of Public Security was paying close attention.

If they had the ledger...

Hiss!!!

Good heavens, this is incredible!

Captain Guo's heart pounded wildly.

This seemingly ordinary bar actually hid such a crucial ledger.

As they excitedly searched for it...

At this very moment, in the city police station's conference room, everyone was eerily silent, the atmosphere so oppressive it was hard to breathe.

Jiang Tian even sat next to Gao Xiaolong, in the very center, surrounded by narcotics officers like a classroom.

But without a doubt, at this moment, all eyes were on Jiang Tian.

Everyone was waiting.

Waiting for news from the bar.

Everyone could already imagine that if this ledger were discovered, the entire Yunhai would be completely transformed.

The drug cartel would no longer be unbreakable, and Jiang Tian would enjoy unparalleled success, his achievements truly earth-shattering.

Everyone held their breath, awaiting news of the ledger's discovery.

The heavy atmosphere was palpable even to Xiao Bo, who was sweeping near the door.

"Xiao Bo, it's lunch break. Don't sweep anymore, you can go rest," Yu Hui said kindly, holding a stack of documents. Seeing Xiao Bo standing not far from the conference room, head down, sweeping, she couldn't help but offer a reminder.

But it was just a reminder.

Heaven's sins may be forgiven, but man's sins are unforgivable. Xiao Bo had so cruelly harmed the rising Jiang Tian; he deserved this fate.

Mainly, what he did was too hateful. It was consensual, and although not illegal, it would definitely incite everyone's hostility.

Thinking of this, Yu Hui couldn't help but sigh. How successful Xiao Bo had been in the police force! He was well-liked by his colleagues, and Deputy Director Sun Haibo treated him like one of his own, grooming him relentlessly and even preparing for a promotion.

But now, seeing the envious look in Xiao Bo's eyes as he stared at the conference room, she couldn't help but think, he truly deserved it!

Jealousy kills.

Seeing Xiao Tian bullying him so much, he deserved it.

Yu Hui cursed inwardly, just as she was about to enter the room.

"Sister Yu Hui, let me help you."

Without waiting for Yu Hui's consent, Xiao Bo grabbed half of the documents and went into the conference room.

Xiao Bo didn't want to give up, and he was unwilling to give up, because he knew that if he did, his life would be truly over.

He wanted to prove himself. Actually, part of the reason Sun Haibo valued him was because Xiao Bo himself possessed some abilities—simply put, a bit of cleverness—able to analyze things that others overlooked.

In front of so many leaders, he wanted to seize a chance to turn things around and completely crush Jiang Tian.

Xiao Bo, his heart pounding with excitement, walked into the conference room with the documents.

The next moment, a deafening, thunderous cheer startled Xiao Bo.

And that wasn't all.

Before the cheers even subsided, Gao Xiaolong's voice rang out.

"Excellent people should be in more outstanding positions, utilizing their abilities to protect more people. I hereby announce that, following instructions from the Ministry of Public Security, Comrade Jiang Tian is being promoted a second time, exceptionally, to the rank of First-Class Police Superintendent, enjoying the待遇 of a deputy section chief."

Boom!!!

Xiao Bo, who had just walked in, was stunned.

What? You say Jiang Tian's promotion has nothing to do with him?

Indeed, it has nothing. If they were strangers, Xiao Bo would only be envious. But this was someone he had once treated like a fool, someone he despised and looked down upon from the bottom of his heart.

Now, however, he was being promoted exceptionally.

He had directly reached a position that a normal police officer might not achieve even after seven years of service.

And all of this, Jiang Tian had accomplished in just a few days.

As for him, he had been with the force for over two years since graduation, and was currently only a newly promoted Third-Class Police Superintendent. Normally, he would have two more years of grueling training to be promoted, and then another three years to advance a level.

In other words, Xiao Bo would have to endure five years of grueling work to reach the rank of First-Class Superintendent. The most terrifying part is, what does "Deputy Section Chief level treatment" even mean?

It means that although the rank hasn't been reached yet, it's already been reserved for him; they'll find an opportunity to promote him sometime.

Stunned.

Rip!!!

The next moment, Gao Xiaolong solemnly tore open Jiang Tian's police rank insignia. At that moment, Gao Xiaolong's secretary was already there with a small tray, on which was a rank of one bar and three stars.



Chapter 73 Showing off one's skills in front of Guan Yu is courting death.


"Great, great, great!" Jiang Tian exclaimed, "This is ridiculously grand!"

Looking at his colleagues in the conference room, there were quite a few with one bar and three rank badges.

But it was already a miracle if even a few people witnessed it.

Since when has awarding a one-bar-three rank badge resulted in such a massive spectacle?

But no one said anything, because it was all expected, no matter how great the achievement.

Some even felt that a promotion of one rank was too little for such a significant contribution.

"Xiao Jiang, I know this rank is far from enough compared to your merit this time, but don't worry, there will definitely be better things to come," Gao Xiaolong said, patting Jiang Tian's shoulder with a beaming smile.

His eyes were full of admiration.

"Serving the people," Jiang Tian saluted instinctively.

"Alright," Gao Xiaolong glanced at him.

Actually, the truth was, just now, Song Liguo had called—the ledger had been found.

Not only that, but the ledger contained detailed records of all of Mr. Yun's transactions since his arrival in Yunhai, including the counterparties, locations, amounts, and quantities involved.

It even included detailed information about the counterparties' families.

In short, this saved the police the trouble of investigation; they could simply arrest him based on the ledger.

Song Liguo, immediately, personally carried the ledger and, escorted by several police cars, sped towards the city police station.

The sheer quantity of drugs traded was simply incalculable, utterly incalculable. A quick glance at it was enough to shock him; each ledger was overflowing with transactions, each involving an enormous sum.

The total amount was terrifying.

Song Liguo even suspected that this could become the largest drug trafficking case cracked in recent years.

After Gao Xiaolong finished speaking, he glanced around for a moment before saying, "Alright, it's too early to open the champagne at halftime. Have you checked the information on those people whose names were randomly selected from the ledger?"

Although they were already certain the ledger was 100% correct, to be on the safe side, the police still randomly selected some people and compared their information with the ledger entries to check for any suspicious discrepancies.

"Chief Gao, over here."

Just then, the bewildered Xiao Bo finally snapped out of his daze, instantly concealing all his jealousy so deeply that no outsider could detect it.

It was precisely because of this disguise that Jiang Tian had been so thoroughly deceived in his previous life.

He jogged up behind Gao Xiaolong, looking at Jiang Tian sitting next to him. The fire of jealousy burned fiercely within him, almost driving him mad.

Why him?

Xiao Bo wouldn't feel this way if it were anyone else.

"Why is it you?"

Gao Xiaolong frowned slightly upon seeing Xiao Bo.

Seeing Xiao Bo appear, Gao Xiaolong's initial excitement slowly cooled.

"Chief Gao, there's just too much data. Yu Hui is a woman, and since I have the ability, I naturally wanted to help," Xiao Bo explained cautiously, bowing his head.

As for Jiang Tian, ​​seeing Xiao Bo's shameful act, he sneered inwardly, but remained silent, pretending not to have seen anything.

As expected.

Seeing this, Gao Xiaolong and Elder Mu, who knew the inside story, both looked at Jiang Tian with even greater admiration.

He remained calm and composed, even in the face of his former enemy's misfortune, without uttering a single sarcastic remark.

Even if Jiang Tian had spoken, many would have considered it expected, but Jiang Tian simply didn't abuse his power.

And this was a huge plus.

It showed that Jiang Tian was emotionally stable, mature in his actions and thinking—a person destined for greatness.

Yu Hui, following behind, turned pale upon hearing Xiao Bo's words.

What did he mean by saying she couldn't carry the documents?

What did this mean?

Wasn't this a direct statement to all the leaders that she was incapable of great things and couldn't shoulder any responsibility?

At that moment, Yu Hui suddenly felt a sense of understanding for Jiang Tian's past experiences.

Indeed, often, a single, casual remark can utterly belittle someone while simultaneously elevating oneself.

"Put the documents down, you can leave." Gao Xiaolong didn't want Xiao Bo to disrupt the overall strategy and subsequent action plans.

But clearly, Xiao Bo wasn't willing. What had he worked so hard to get in for?

That's right.

Xiao Bo realized that what his superior, Gao Xiaolong, valued was ability.

And his abilities were by no means lacking!

Of course, most importantly, Xiao Bo looked at Elder Mu with shining eyes.

Yes, that's right, who was Elder Mu? What was his status?

Actually, there was something Xiao Bo hadn't told anyone.

That was, he had once seen Elder Mu inspecting his hometown in the countryside.

Later, he saw Mu Ya and Elder Mu talking at school, and he instantly realized something, which led to his relentless pursuit of Mu Ya.

He didn't need anything else; Xiao Bo knew that all he needed to do was let people know he knew Elder Mu, and then give him a chance to shine, and he could turn the tables.

"Mr. Mu, I have a different opinion on this case. I hope you can give me a chance," Xiao Bo said to Mr. Mu.

He didn't consult Gao Xiaolong, because he knew that given Gao Xiaolong's hostility towards him, he wouldn't allow him to show his true colors.

But now he wanted to buy time. Xiao Bo cleverly believed that Mr. Mu had a good impression of him and would definitely give him the opportunity.

However, his little scheme was easily laughed at by both Gao Xiaolong and Mr. Mu, who were shrewd and experienced in the political arena for most of their lives. This petty trick would only make them laugh, especially since Mr. Mu knew Xiao Bo's character.

He almost got humiliated.

Even big shots have their pride.

If others found out that he had been deceived by a nobody like Xiao Bo, what would the leaders in Beijing think of him?

He wouldn't recognize him; he would resolutely pretend not to know him.

"I'm just a witness to this matter. It was Chief Gao who initiated it. I don't know much about it and have no right to speak. Young comrade, coming to me won't help." Old Mu didn't even look at Xiao Bo.

Knowing what happened, Old Mu didn't do anything because he was afraid of the dishonorable act of bullying the weak. And you're actually helping him?

Xiao Bo, unaware that Elder Mu already knew the truth, continued speaking, "Elder Mu, believe me, the ironclad rule of investigation is to analyze and view everything with a skeptical eye, only then can we... There are many unknowns in this case, such as how they knew Mr. Yun's identity, that old Chen doesn't seem to have confessed, how they discovered TNT, and..."

"Of course, I'm not questioning Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​but raising questions and solving all the problems is what truly constitutes solving a case."

Xiao Bo spoke eloquently.

However, he didn't notice that the people nearby were increasingly displeased with him.

Similar to the hot-tempered Gao Xiaolong.

Standing up, Gao Xiaolong slapped him across the face: "This is the Municipal Public Security Bureau, not a place for you to settle personal scores. Is this raising questions? I think you're just being disobedient and looking for trouble."

Does it matter?

Do these questions, these processes, matter?

Damn it, the results are already out, and you're telling me the process went wrong?

He was sharpening his knives, brimming with excitement, ready to launch a major operation.

This bastard actually dared to jump out.

Jealousy could drive someone to such madness.

Gao Xiaolong's face was filled with dark, furious anger.

Xiao Bo covered his face; the slap had caused his nose to bleed profusely, clearly showing Gao Xiaolong hadn't held back at all.

However, when Xiao Bo glanced around unintentionally, he suddenly realized that many people were glaring at him angrily.

He had inadvertently angered everyone.

In reality, Xiao Bo had no idea how important this case was to the entire city police department. Once the operation was launched, everyone would at least gain some credit.

Moreover, he was personally involved in a case that could almost be written into textbooks—who wouldn't be excited?

"Where did this shark arm come from? Get out of here!" someone yelled in the conference room.



Chapter 74 Dawn Breaks, Eagle Soars Through the Sky, Clearing the Operation


"Xiao Shen, what's going on?"

Gao Xiaolong frowned, looking at Shen Han sitting not far away.

Hasn't Xiao Bo been dealt with yet? To be honest, given Gao Xiaolong's status and position, there's absolutely no need for him to intervene against someone like Xiao Bo.

Even Jiang Peng didn't need to be involved; Captain Guo alone suppressed him.

Besides, this matter involves the conflict between Jiang Tian and Xiao Bo, so Jiang Peng couldn't act. Regardless of right or wrong, it would only create trouble.

Even if his subordinate Song Liguo had acted, it would have been the same—it would have been easy to create trouble. In the end, Shen Han personally took action, assigning him cleaning work.

"Sir, it's my fault. I've already arranged for him to clean," Shen Han quickly explained.

"Cleaning? I think he should be assigned to clean the toilets. Wait, no, how does he know the details of the case?" Gao Xiaolong suddenly realized something after speaking, turning to look at Xiao Bo.

That's right.

Old Chen didn't interrogate, or rather, Jiang Tian didn't interrogate him at all. Unlike the pampered Mr. Yun, Old Chen was genuinely concerned for his family, and no matter how Jiang Tian threatened him, he wouldn't reveal anything.

Under these circumstances, Jiang Tian didn't interrogate; he went straight to the heart of the matter and arrested the man.

And he did so with such precision.

However, this matter couldn't have been known without thorough investigation.

Even ordinary armed police officers on duty, or even anti-drug police officers on patrol outside, had no idea.

But Xiao Bo, who was currently slated for transfer out of the police force,

knew such detailed information.

Jiang Tian told him? How could that be? The two were sworn enemies. Unless Jiang Tian was a fool, he couldn't possibly have any connection with Xiao Bo.

Because Xiao Bo was already disgraced.

Jiang Tian was already a shrew; how could he possibly associate with Xiao Bo, that worthless clay pot? Even if they were to clash, Xiao Bo, given his current situation, wouldn't have any psychological burden, but it would negatively impact Jiang Tian.

"Who told you?" Gao Xiaolong asked sternly.

Not only did they know this information, but they even knew about the TNT incident.

They knew the whole story so clearly?

No.

Gao Xiaolong frowned deeply, finally realizing something terrifying.

That was, the confidentiality of the narcotics officers' identities and intelligence information hadn't been adequate.

"I... I heard it from everyone at the city bureau," Xiao Bo said, clenching his fists and biting his teeth, but outwardly maintaining a respectful demeanor.

A traitor.

Gao Xiaolong looked at Xiao Bo, his face icy.

"Do you know this information is classified? Even many officers didn't know about it during the operation. From now on, you'll be cleaning the toilets. Don't go near the police station." Gao Xiaolong waved his hand dismissively, issuing the order.

Huh?

Xiao Bo was dumbfounded, utterly stunned.

He never dreamed his meticulously planned comeback would turn out like this.

"What are you still doing here? Get out! Get out of here right now!"

As a leader, dealing with a problem like Xiao Bo's was difficult before because of his relationship and conflict with Jiang Tian, ​​which prevented him from taking direct action.

But now, Jiang Peng slammed his fist on the table, his face contorted with rage.

He had been holding back for a long time. The officialdom isn't just about fighting and killing; it often involves interpersonal relationships, many things to consider, and a multitude of factors.

He had been holding back his anger because things were difficult before.

Now, Jiang Peng had finally found an opportunity to vent.

"Get out! Get out now! Go clean the toilets in the bathroom, don't you hear me?" Jiang Peng demanded angrily.

"Old Mu."

Xiao Bo felt a chill run down his spine. He looked pleadingly at Old Mu, hoping that if Old Mu spoke up, if he spoke up,

everything could be resolved.

Unfortunately, Old Mu, at his level, sat on his bench, keeping a low profile. If he truly had a good relationship with Mu Ya, it wouldn't be so bad. The key issue was his character. Everything else was secondary, but stealing a teammate's girlfriend and even betraying him like this...

Moreover, Jiang Tian has become a legend in Yunhai, even within the police force, through his own abilities.

He's a bit clever, but brainless.

How could Old Master Mu allow Mu Ya to have any contact with Xiao Bo?

If she dares to contact him, he'll break her legs.

Old Master Mu thought of the stubborn Mu Ya and decided he absolutely couldn't let her get away with it this time.

"Alright, the irrelevant people have left. Now, all units must cooperate closely. The upcoming operation is named Operation Dawn. The anti-drug units will lead, the armed police will assist, the criminal police will cooperate, and the laboratory, etc., must also cooperate closely. The provincial department also has an important case, and most of the anti-drug police have already been mobilized. Therefore, this operation will be led by our entire municipal bureau. Does everyone understand?"

"Dawn breaks, eagles soar through the sky, sweeping away darkness, upholding order."



Chapter 75 The entire family wiped out? Good heavens, all of them were completely wiped out.


Xiao Bo walked out of the meeting room, his face full of worry, almost on the verge of tears.

This time, he genuinely had ideas, genuinely wanted to prove himself, but instead, he was met with relentless ridicule.

The roles had reversed.

Jiang Tian had faced a similar situation before, manipulated and secretly framed by Xiao Bo, and everyone was hostile towards him.

But now, he stood on the opposite side of everyone.

Jiang Tian stood among thousands, while he was the target of everyone's vicious mockery.

Especially now, assigned to cleaning toilets.

Xiao Bo could hardly believe it; in fact, he was terrified. What if his family found out? He was practically the hope of his entire family.

Last time he went home, his family had been bragging about him everywhere. Now, everyone in the village knew that Xiao Bo had a bright future, a promising career, and was a glorious cadre.

In the whole village, he was practically a one-of-a-kind individual.

If his family or the villagers found out about his situation, Xiao Bo couldn't even bear to think about it.

His whole family would be ashamed to face the village, and the entire village would have to see them off when he left.

What if the villagers found out he was cleaning toilets?

Xiao Bo trembled slightly, his face turning pale.

"Song Zhi."

Just then, Xiao Bo looked up and suddenly saw Song Liguo running towards him.

Xiao Bo's face broke into a fawning expression.

In the past, relying on his underling Jiang Tian and his superior Sun Haibo, he had managed to maintain a certain level of good relations with Song Liguo.

Although he had made some mistakes, these were just mistakes any man would make. What did they have to do with him?

“Chief Song, I want to talk to you,” Xiao Bo said with a fawning expression.

But unfortunately.

Song Liguo was in a hurry, carrying documents, and paid no attention to Xiao Bo.

Bang!!!

Xiao Bo, who was bowing and scraping, was suddenly struck by the powerful Song Liguo.

Thump!!!

Xiao Bo's nose spurted blood as he was knocked to the ground. Song Liguo, who was running away, turned around, frowned, and said unhappily, “Aren't you blind? Don't you know a good dog doesn't block the way?”

After saying that, Song Liguo ran into the conference room.

Xiao Bo, sitting there, covered his nose, staring blankly at Song Liguo's departing figure. The feeling of being pushed around when someone's down left him stunned and unable to recover.

Things have changed.

Is this what it feels like to be targeted by everyone?

Xiao Bo, reaping the consequences of his actions, felt as if the whole world was against him.

No, it should be said that Xiao Bo had simply received the treatment he was entitled to. Previously, he had merely used Jiang Tian as a stepping stone to gain Sun Haibo's attention and thus become the focus of everyone's attention.

Now, he had simply lost all of Jiang Tian's goodwill and was reaping the consequences of his own mistakes.

But unfortunately, Xiao Bo's miserable state no longer garnered Jiang Tian's attention.

To be precise, Jiang Tian and Xiao Bo were now from two different worlds. If he had the time, he would naturally seek revenge; if not, he could simply ignore him.

As for deliberate targeting, sorry, Xiao Bo truly didn't have that right. He hadn't before, and he certainly wouldn't in the future.

"Here they come, sir, this is the drug dealers' ledger."

Sweating profusely, Song Liguo rushed in, stood at attention, and immediately placed the ledger in front of Gao Xiaolong.

Jiang Tian, ​​standing next to Gao Xiaolong, spotted it immediately.

That's right.

The upcoming operation was targeting this ledger, named "Dawn Break," Operation Dawn of the New Day.

It signified that it would tear apart the dark syndicate shrouded in darkness.

"This is the ledger?"

Gao Xiaolong, his hands trembling, opened the ledger.

At this moment, everyone stared intently at the contents of the ledger.

Because, without a doubt, everyone listed in the ledger, every name, could be directly arrested according to this list. No investigation, no interrogation, no surveillance—just a methodical arrest.

It would completely annihilate an entire criminal syndicate, an entire drug kingpin's criminal organization.

"That's right, it was found in the basement. The contents are basically accurate; this ledger is genuine," Song Liguo said excitedly.

"Okay, okay."

Having received confirmation again, Gao Xiaolong opened the ledger with trembling hands.

The first page.

It was Wei Youdao.

First was Wei Youdao's family information, followed by the time, place, and amount of each transaction with Wei Youdao.

"100 kilograms in January 1990, 100 kilograms in June... 100 kilograms... 150 kilograms in 1991, 200 kilograms in January 1992."

Just looking at the ledger, Gao Xiaolong's eyelids twitched wildly.

"Miao Zifeng, 1993, drug transaction..."

Wait, why does the name Miao Zifeng sound so familiar?

Gao Xiaolong's gaze rose, and he instantly saw it. It was in Wei Youdao's family information; Wei Youdao's wife was Miao Zifeng?

Hiss!!!

Good heavens, the whole family was involved!

"I only thought Wei Youdao and his son were involved, but I didn't expect Miao Zifeng not only knew everything, but even personally participated in drug trafficking."

Gao Xiaolong was astonished.

After Wei Youdao was arrested, Miao Zifeng never came, never even showed her face.

"Without this ledger, we would all have been fooled by Miao Zifeng," Gao Xiaolong said, pointing to the ledger.

At that moment, people from the forensic department immediately entered, checking the information in the ledger and making a backup.

Soon, the results came out.

"Wei Youdao's drug trafficking, starting in January 1990, has lasted eleven years and five months. The total amount recorded in the ledger is three tons." The forensic worker looked up, his face filled with astonishment.

Silence.

Dead silence.

Three tons?

The scale of drug trafficking reached three tons, as much as six thousand kilograms?

"This is only Wei Youdao's."

Gao Xiaolong's voice trembled.

You don't know until you investigate, and what you find is shocking.

If Jiang Tian hadn't uncovered the drug crimes of the Wei family father and son, how could such a terrifying amount of drugs have been discovered?

Is this all?

No.

This is just the tip of the iceberg.

Thinking of this, the man who had just been excited suddenly broke out in a cold sweat.

“Jiang Tian, ​​you lead the team and arrest Miao Zifeng first,” Gao Xiaolong said in a deep voice.

This was a case worthy of being called a major case, but it was only one part of this ledger.

How many more were there?

How many distributors did Wei Youdao have under his command?

“Besides Miao Zifeng, there are three other distributors under Wei Youdao. Some have probably already gotten wind of it and fled. Xiao Guo, you're in charge of setting up the arrests.”

Gao Xiaolong was assigning tasks.

In fact, besides Wei Youdao's family, this ledger also included some major drug lords.

These people all obtained their drugs through Mr. Yun's people.

They were lucky because Mr. Yun had brought over the drug manufacturing plant. All the loose drugs, besides Wei Youdao, involved another major drug lord.

Under the principle of "pulling out a radish and bringing out the mud," basically all the cases recorded in the ledger of obtaining drugs directly from Wei Youdao—no, from the behind-the-scenes boss Wei Youdao—were documented.

A shock.

An unprecedented shock.

Even everyone in the conference room could imagine how terrifying this drug case was.

Gao Xiaolong lost all excitement, filled only with rage.

How could he not be rageful?

Even Elder Mu was speechless, holding the ledger.

The quantities inside were terrifying.

To put it simply, the total amount of drugs traded, recorded in this ledger, reached over five thousand kilograms—that's ten thousand jin!

What does that mean? Fifty grams would land you in the death penalty.

Normally, seizing drugs exceeding a few dozen kilograms is already a major achievement, worthy of a first-class merit.

But this entire ledger contained tens of thousands of jin.



Chapter 76 Returning to My Alma Mater, With a Mission


The records included the names of two drug lords besides Wei Youdao, and their distributors were also documented.

Thanks to Mr. Yun's abnormal control, besides Wei Youdao and another major distributor, there were about ten other distributors who obtained their drugs from them.

The final statistics came out.

"I used to hear people say that most of the country's drugs flowed out of Yunhai, and I didn't quite believe it. I thought, how could that be? Even if Yunhai is incompetent, it shouldn't be a drug paradise. But now, comrades, this is just the tip of the iceberg! This is just the beginning of the battle!" Gao Xiaolong leaned on the table, his tone clearly suppressing his anger.

Good heavens, absolutely good heavens! In ten years, they've pulled off so much?

This case, it can be said,

is the first case in recent years with such a massive quantity and such a terrifying scale.

It's even the kind that will go down in history; from now on, every anti-drug police officer will inevitably see such a terrifying case.

The operation.

"Act immediately!"

"Dawn breaks! Now, immediately, act now!"

Gao Xiaolong slammed his fist on the table and shouted.

It was the same meeting room as before.

The host was the same Gao Xiaolong.

A few days had passed.

For the entire municipal anti-drug squad, it felt like an eternity, a dreamlike experience.

The difference was that for Comrade Zhao Wei, the brother who used to sit next to him, the one Gao Xiaolong had once berated, was now sitting beside him.

Except for Wei Youdao's family, all the individuals on the list involved in the case had been arrested.

Following behind Jiang Tian, ​​Zhao Wei was filled with excitement. Although he hadn't gotten the credit before, it wasn't too late.

The arrested Miao Zifeng was also a big tiger; this operation would likely be a major achievement.

Although compared to Jiang Tian, ​​it was like comparing a sesame seed to a watermelon.

But for Zhao Wei, it was already very satisfying.

The wailing sirens of police cars echoed through the streets. Zhao Wei drove, with Jiang Tian and Li Zijing in the back seat.

Actually, such a large-scale operation couldn't possibly involve just the three of them. What does "dawn breaking" mean? It means sparing no expense, all resources yielding to the plan.

Two armed police vehicles followed behind their car, coordinating the operation to ensure everything went smoothly and without any problems.

"Such a small achievement as a capture is enough?" Jiang Tian looked at the two's satisfied expressions and felt rather bored.

Pfft!!!

Li Zijing burst out laughing.

"Do you know, this drug dealer, while not as big as Wei Youdao, is still a major drug lord who has trafficked over a thousand kilograms of drugs. And if she were involved in Wei Youdao's drug trafficking, Wei Youdao's crimes would also be attributed to her. At the very least, she'd get a collective third-class merit; with luck, even a second-class merit." Zhao Wei's face was flushed with excitement.

“Not only that, after dawn, everyone involved in the operation will probably receive a collective first-class merit,” Li Zijing added.

After saying that, Li Zijing looked at Jiang Tian's bored expression.

The excited expression on Li Zijing's face instantly vanished.

Damn, he's no match for this freak.

Two first-class merits and an individual second-class merit on the way, but this operation is likely to change. That first-class merit deserves Jiang Tian's contribution; everyone involved in the arrest received a collective first-class merit, let alone someone who solved the case alone.

Breaking the drug factory, arresting Wei Youdao, drawing out Mr. Yun, and even arresting Mr. Yun were all done by Jiang Tian. The entire drug trafficking gang, it can be said, was completely brought down by Jiang Tian alone.

"Doesn't that mean that after Operation Dawn, plus what he already had, Brother Jiang will have three first-class merits?" Zhao Wei exclaimed, speechless.

Jiang Tian looked at the blessing of the God of Wealth without speaking.

Because he seemed to see the God of Wealth beckoning to him.

Of course, on the other hand, Jiang Tian frowned as he looked at the scanning radar.

"What's wrong?" Li Zijing asked.

Jiang Tian didn't answer. After getting out of the car, he looked at the villa in front of him.

This was Wei Youdao's house.

However, it was clear that Jiang Tian's observation of the villa, devoid of any radar indication, confirmed one thing: Miao Zifeng wasn't there.

As Jiang Tian, ​​wearing a jacket, got out of the car, more than ten fully armed police officers instantly emerged from the two police vehicles following behind and surrounded the villa.

"Hello, I'm the head of the local police station, responsible for assisting you in the arrest operation. Who is the leader?"

At this moment, a local police officer waiting at the villa entrance jogged up and saluted.

Looking at Jiang Tian and his two companions, he showed a hint of envy.

After all, even a lowly police officer has ambitions and dreams, even a police station chief.

However, after scanning the area, he noticed that only three young men were present, especially Jiang Tian, ​​who looked incredibly young.

This police station chief had heard that the city bureau had an important operation requiring their full cooperation. With so many armed police officers present, the operation was clearly large-scale. Yet, the three young men from the city bureau responsible for the arrest were so ridiculously young—where was the leader?

"My surname is Jiang, I'm a squad leader in the Municipal Narcotics Control Detachment, in charge of this arrest operation. I hope you will fully cooperate." Jiang Tian saluted and showed his identification—just standard procedure.

Without identification, how would anyone know if you're an imposter?

"Hello, hello," the station chief said, his face full of surprise.

Following behind the station chief was a young man who, looking at Jiang Tian in his leather jacket, his serious expression and imposing aura, was filled with envy and a deep-seated inferiority complex.

After all, a low-ranking police officer seemed to be the least important in the entire system. Who wouldn't want to be part of a crucial police unit capable of arresting suspects and solving difficult cases?

Especially when he saw Jiang Tian's rank—good heavens, one bar and three rank insignia?

The junior station chief silently glanced at his own, swallowed hard, and realized he had taken nearly twenty years to reach this position, and Jiang Tian had already caught up to him at such a young age?

"Officer Jiang, we just finished our investigation. Miao Zifeng is a high school teacher at Yunhai No. 1 High School. It's currently class time, so she's not home." The station chief hurriedly told Jiang Tian the information he had gathered.

"A teacher from No. 1 High School?" Jiang Tian exclaimed in surprise.

"Miao Zifeng?"

Jiang Tian's expression changed.

Good heavens, what a coincidence!

When Jiang Tian first heard the name, he hadn't thought about it. After all, he couldn't connect the gentle female teacher he remembered, the object of countless male classmates' fantasies, with Wei Youdao and a major drug lord.

More than ten years had passed, and Jiang Tian had naturally forgotten, failing to recall the name.

Now, reminded, Jiang Tian was instantly shocked.

No. 1 High School was his alma mater, and Miao Zifeng was one of his teachers.

She had even joked with him, leaving Jiang Tian with a good impression.

But who knew it was her?

Could it be such a coincidence?

“Let's go to No. 1 High School.” Jiang Tian's expression was unreadable.

If it really was the Miao Zifeng he remembered, Jiang Tian's worldview would be truly shattered, because everyone knew that drug dealers were inhuman; he couldn't connect the Miao Zifeng of his memory with a major drug lord.

“You all come together, Zijing, you sit in the front.”

Before getting into the car, Jiang Tian extended an invitation to the station chief and the junior officer.

After all, sometimes having the director present makes communication easier, such as with the school.

Jiang Tian kept his eyes closed the whole way, seemingly lost in thought.

But when he arrived at the gate of No. 1 High School, Jiang Tian suddenly opened his eyes, his gaze resolute.

Because a crimson dot appeared on the scanning radar.

He looked up at his familiar alma mater.

He was back.

But Jiang Tian knew that the old principal of No. 1 High School probably wasn't too welcoming.



Chapter 77 Ten years later, you shall see what he will be like?


The campus of No. 1 High School was quiet.

In the height of summer, the lush green leaves and grass stirred something within Jiang Tian, ​​a profound emotion.

Although this was only four years after graduation, nearly twenty years had passed in Jiang Tian's memory.

The gatekeeper.

A sparse flow of people, not many, because Jiang Tian checked the time; it was already four in the afternoon.

At this hour, most students were probably just starting class.

"Uncle Liu."

Jiang Tian, ​​wearing a jacket, got out of the car first, glanced at the school, and then went to the gatekeeper.

Jiang Tian familiarly took out mosquito coils and handed them to the gate.

The gatekeeper, inside the security booth, instinctively picked up his fork upon seeing Jiang Tian; after all, society was a bit chaotic, and being cautious was a good thing.

But upon seeing Jiang Tian's face, he momentarily froze.

"Jiang Tian?" Uncle Liu, flashing his yellow teeth, took the mosquito coils from Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian used this stuff a lot in his previous life, but after being reborn, he no longer had any attachment to it. He'd occasionally take one, and after his rebirth, he might only take two or three every few days.

"Uncle Liu, it's been years." Jiang Tian leaned against the security booth, not in a hurry.

Jiang Tian was very familiar with this security guard because, back in high school, he often sneaked out at night to go online.

Uncle Liu caught him several times and took him to the administration office. Jiang Tian was heavily criticized for this, and even the principal pointed at him and gave him a severe reprimand in front of the whole school. He directly told Jiang Tian that because of his poor grades, he would only be able to do manual labor at the bottom of society.

"Exposed to the wind and sun, while you sit in offices, the chosen ones."

"Jiang Tian, ​​a good-for-nothing who doesn't study hard, just wait until you graduate from university, and you'll see what happens to him."

Back then, Jiang Tian became the school's negative example, even affectionately called the "shame of the school" by the principal.

During that period, the gatekeeper, Old Liu, even patrolled specifically to keep an eye on Jiang Tian, ​​handing him over to the academic affairs office if caught.

Later, Jiang Tian, ​​fueled by a burning desire to prove the principal wrong, worked incredibly hard in the final two months, actually passing the entrance exam and gaining admission to Yunhai Police Academy.

“What are you doing here?” Years later, Old Liu, having let go of some of his prejudice, asked Jiang Tian.

“Let me make it clear beforehand, if you're just here to visit your alma mater, you can't get in. You're considered an outsider now, so forget about it.”

Old Liu quickly added, assuming Jiang Tian was just strolling around his old school.

“What if I insist on getting in?” Jiang Tian asked.

“If you insist on getting in, I'll call the police.” Old Liu scoffed.

Jiang Tian put his hands in his pockets.

“You think you can just walk in with a pack of cigarettes? This is No. 1 High School, not a place you can just walk into whenever you want.” Old Liu was furious, feeling insulted.

Jiang Tian, ​​unperturbed, placed the black leather hardware on the security booth table. "Take another look."

"I told you, don't insult me... I..."

Grandpa Liu was barely containing his anger.

But he stopped abruptly halfway through his sentence.

His gaze swept over the item.

This was a brand-new identification card, just given to him. Jiang Tian's police rank had been promoted, and his card had been updated accordingly.

The card was brand new, with a brand-new national emblem on it.

But the words below almost stumped Old Liu.

“You're a beat cop now?” Old Liu asked with disdain. “What’s a beat cop doing here, showing off?”

Although he was a gatekeeper, his identification was verified. It wasn’t enough for him to just be showing off; he instinctively thought Jiang Tian was here to embarrass him.

“Open it and take a look,” Jiang Tian said.

Old Liu opened the card, and what he saw made his pupils shrink. He stared at Jiang Tian in astonishment: “This…this……”

When Jiang Tian graduated, Old Liu naturally hadn’t paid attention to where he went.

He thought Jiang Tian was just a beat cop, and even wondered if he was showing off now that he’d made it this far.

But what he saw...

Squad Leader of the Municipal Narcotics Control Detachment.

A First-Class Superintendent?

Looking at Jiang Tian's righteous and authoritative demeanor, Old Liu's hand trembled slightly as he put down his badge. If he remembered correctly, this guy hadn't been out of school for long, right?

First-Class Superintendent, is that easy to get promoted to?

"This show-off, barely passable, but enough," Old Liu swallowed hard.

But the next moment.

Boom!!!

Jiang Tian instantly exploded, slamming his hands on the security booth in a fit of rage.

"Screw your show-off! Open the door! I'm here on a mission, arresting drug dealers, understand?"

A whoosh!!!

The instant Jiang Tian spoke, Old Liu was still stunned, his face full of shock.

At that moment, nearly twenty people poured out of three cars, most of them fully armed with police.

"You're guarding the area around the academy to prevent drug dealers from escaping. Leave two of you with me to make the arrest. Try to keep a low profile."



Chapter 78 I'm almost embarrassed to take the credit.


"Open the door."

Jiang Tian's face was grim. He was being taken advantage of.

Why would I be so idle as to come to a school, to No. 1 High School, just to show off to you, Uncle Liu?

I've worked myself to the bone, accumulated so many merits, arrested so many drug dealers, just to show off to you? You think you're so great?

*Gulp!*

Uncle Liu swallowed hard, watching as all the armed police swiftly sprang into action at Jiang Tian's command.

The armed police were cooperating with Jiang Tian.

In other words, there was no limit to the support available to the anti-drug police. To a certain extent, anti-drug police could request the mobilization of all armed forces except for strategic weapons.

Especially the armed police; in fact, the armed police often ended up working right behind the anti-drug police.

These armed police officers were already used to it. What? You think Jiang Tian's rank and position are too low? Let alone Jiang Tian, ​​a first-class police superintendent, even a probationary narcotics officer would obey orders 100% if asked for support or cooperation.

Because they were just cooperating in the operation.

"Yes, sir!!!"

Leaving behind two armed police officers, the station chief, two junior officers, plus Jiang Tian's two junior team members, Zhao Wei and Li Zijing.

"I...I'll call the principal," said Grandpa Liu, sweating profusely.

This was the first time Grandpa Liu had seen such a large-scale operation, and it frightened him.

"No need, I'll go find him directly," Jiang Tian waved his hand.

What? Was he trying to embarrass Grandpa Liu? Wasn't this ridiculous? When did Jiang Tian become so low-class?

Besides, Grandpa Liu wasn't a particularly evil person, and Jiang Tian had no intention of pursuing the matter.

After Jiang Tian led his group into the high school, Grandpa Liu belatedly wiped the cold sweat from his brow.

He kept muttering to himself, "Unbelievable, unbelievable! A first-class police superintendent, commanding so many armed police officers... I never thought the most capable one would be Jiang Tian."

"Wait, I sent him to the administration office so many times before, will he retaliate against me?"

"Wait, what did he just say, drug dealers?"

"There are drug dealers in the school?"

*Buzz!*

By the time Grandpa Liu finally realized what was happening,

Jiang Tian had already passed the teaching building, watching the students studying inside.

However, Jiang Tian didn't go to the principal's office immediately.

Instead, he suddenly stopped as he passed a classroom.

Because at that moment, standing on the podium was Miao Zifeng, the familiar teacher.

Miao Zifeng was Jiang Tian's English teacher for all three years of high school, and among many students, she was even considered a model of teacher ethics.

Of course, they lost contact after graduation. Even after three years of teaching, they were only acquainted, not particularly close. Jiang Tian never imagined that Miao Zifeng would be his teacher, so coincidentally.

Furthermore, no one knew about Teacher Miao's family background; her husband was Wei Youdao, a wealthy tycoon from Yunhai.

Of course, even Jiang Tian, ​​who was searching for the truth, found it hard to believe—she was also a major drug lord.

"Okay, this grammar is..."

Miao Zifeng was writing on the blackboard.

She spoke gently, her demeanor refined.

When she got tired of explaining, Miao Zifeng took a sip of water. But out of the corner of her eye, she saw a look of utter astonishment on her face as she stared at the doorway.

"You are... Jiang Tian?" Miao Zifeng asked in surprise.

All the students in the class also looked towards the doorway with curiosity.

Jiang Tian nodded with a sigh. To be honest, if he hadn't found out the truth, looking at the scarlet dot on the scanning radar, no one would have imagined that this gentle English teacher could be so ruthless.

Yes, ruthless.

Jiang Tian stared at the dot, speechless. Even Wei Rong seemed insignificant compared to this Teacher Miao.

"Jiang Tian, ​​did you come to see me? What can I do for you?" Miao Zifeng asked, puzzled, as she seemed to have no connection with Jiang Tian whatsoever.

If the principal hadn't arrogantly declared to Jiang Tian, ​​"Ten years from now, just you wait and see," she might not even remember Jiang Tian's name. After all, she taught several classes a year.

Jiang Tian smiled slightly.

Miao Zifeng felt a chill run down her spine at Jiang Tian's smile, realizing something was amiss.

Sure enough, the next moment...

A person dressed as an armed police officer appeared from the side of the door.

Seeing the uniform, Miao Zifeng's expression stiffened slightly.

"Jiang, you brought a police officer to see me because of Wei Rong and the others, right? Bringing strangers to see a teacher without permission is wrong!" Miao Zifeng asked with a smile.

Up until now, she hadn't realized the problem, only assuming it was Wei Youdao and Wei Rong's fault, which was why she came to see her.

However, Miao Zifeng was certain she hadn't participated in their crimes and couldn't reveal anything.

But...

Jiang Tian's actions towards the police, however, aroused Miao Zifeng's inward scorn.

"Sure enough, he's the same good-for-nothing from over ten years ago. Now, he's doing all sorts of shady things like showing people the way."

Though she felt a surge of scorn, Miao Zifeng never showed it. Turning to her classmates, she said, "Let me introduce you all. This is my former student. He skipped class to go online and was criticized by the principal, but he reformed. But please, don't follow his example."

Jiang Tian, ​​of course, could see through Miao Zifeng's superb acting skills. She couldn't fool him with her "Genius Criminal Investigation Expert" and "Extraordinary Memory" attributes.

"First, I didn't bring them to you; I came to find you. Second, it's not just because of Wei Rong and the others, but also because of you," Jiang Tian said calmly.

"What do you mean?"

Miao Zifeng's expression changed slightly, before she could say anything.

The next moment.

*Snap!*

Miao Zifeng looked down in shock, only then realizing that she was wearing silver bracelets on both hands.

Looking up, she was startled to see a national emblem and Jiang Tian's cold, stern expression: "Ms. Miao, have you forgotten that I got into the police academy? Let me introduce myself, I'm Jiang Tian, ​​the captain of the anti-drug squad of the Yunhai City Public Security Bureau. Ms. Miao, you've been caught."

*Buzz!*

Miao Zifeng's face turned pale.

Jiang Tian's gaze was icy.

Because the red glow emanating from Miao Zifeng was terrifyingly intense, signifying one thing: her case was more than just drug trafficking.

Perhaps something else is involved.

But something even more heinous than drug trafficking, besides murder, Jiang Tian racked his brains, and there was only one other thing.

Given his connections with Myanmar, the outcome was obvious.

Fraud.

“You’re a police officer, squad leader?” Miao Zifeng’s scalp tingled as she exclaimed, “Wait, why are you arresting me? What do their crimes have to do with me? You must have made a mistake!”

“Let me tell you, Mr. Yun has already confessed to everything. Don’t bother struggling anymore.” Jiang Tian sneered.

Even though he was her former teacher, given the enormity of his crimes, Jiang Tian had no qualms about bringing him to justice.

The moment Jiang Tian finished speaking,

Miao Zifeng collapsed to the ground, her face deathly pale.

The classmates in the class, at this moment, watched this scene in utter silence, their expressions incredibly complex.

*Whoosh!*

The next moment, Li Zijing and Zhao Wei, dressed in plainclothes, excitedly rushed up and subdued Jiang Tian.

Two armed police officers vigilantly surveyed their surroundings.

By the time the principal arrived, Jiang Tian had already completed the arrest. This arrest was unusually simple.

So simple that Jiang Tian, ​​accustomed to handling complex cases, even felt quite uneasy.

"Fall back," Jiang Tian waved his hand.

"Um, Jiang Tian."

The former principal of the school arrived. Jiang Tian had stayed behind, but the police chief and a junior officer had already spoken with the principal.

After explaining the situation to the principal,

the principal rushed over and immediately witnessed Jiang Tian leading his men in the classroom to make the arrest. The sight instantly sent chills down the principal's spine.

Especially after seeing Jiang Tian, ​​the principal's expression became even more complex.

Clearly, Jiang Tian's face reminded the principal of something from the past.

Looking at Jiang Tian, ​​who was leading the arrest with great fanfare, and recalling his own words, the principal felt a burning pain on his face.



Chapter 79: Revealing only the tip of the iceberg was enough to shock the entire nation.


"Ten years from now, you people sitting in offices, even wearing white hats, going to construction sites, will only be supervisors. And the one being supervised, doing the hard labor of carrying bricks, will be him. That good-for-nothing only deserves to carry bricks in the future."

The principal still vividly remembered those words.

"Principal, I'm sorry, four years have passed. I haven't carried bricks. Now I'm a policeman. I don't know if I'll be carrying bricks in six years, but I think I have no interest in it." Jiang Tian's expression was anything but pleasant.

It's right to like good students, but this principal's treatment of students like Jiang Tian with average to low grades was simply appalling.

The principal's expression instantly became incredibly varied, like a rainbow, constantly shifting. Jiang Tian found it extremely interesting.

He was different from Lian Ying, who was a proper high-ranking official. While the principal of No. 1 High School wasn't low-ranking either, the two were completely incomparable.

"You're joking, you're joking, Comrade Jiang Tian. There might have been some misunderstandings before," the principal said, his expression changing.

"No, it's all thanks to the principal. Without your encouragement, how could I have gotten into police academy and gotten this far?" Jiang Tian chuckled meaningfully.

Hiss!!!

A first-level police superintendent who had only recently graduated—there was only one possibility: a promotion beyond the usual ranks, which meant a bright future. The principal's legs trembled slightly.

A moment later, watching Miao Zifeng being led away by Jiang Tian, ​​leaving only her back view, he subconsciously swallowed hard.

This one incident could practically ruin his political future.

The principal couldn't even bear the thought of a university teacher being a drug dealer. And what if further investigation revealed the things Wei Youdao had given him?

The principal felt a chill run down his spine.

Thinking back to how he had mocked Jiang Tian, ​​how the entire school had made a self-criticism, how he had humiliated the student he had stepped on—he thought Jiang Tian would be useless in society, but unexpectedly, the one he had least faith in was the most promising. It was so painful.

"Wow, so cool!"

"Holy crap, are those narcotics officers? With armed police around, they're incredibly cool!"

"Wait, holy crap, Teacher Miao is a criminal! He's been arrested by the anti-narcotics squad, and the person leading the arrest is our senior, Teacher Miao's former student! Holy crap, this is explosive news!"

"That's right, the sky is falling! This is the sky falling!"

The principal's face darkened upon hearing this. For him, it wasn't just the sky falling.

"Quiet down, everyone. Teachers will continue teaching you. Now, keep quiet. And don't talk about what happened today, and absolutely no spreading rumors. Anything that isn't confirmed, just speculation, will be severely punished by the school." The principal finished speaking hurriedly and left.

Afterwards, the principal returned to his office, his face heavy and his brow furrowed.

He never dreamed that the student he once looked down upon would return as a squad leader in the criminal investigation squad.

What did this mean?

Old Liu at the door had no idea.

But how could the principal, being part of the system, not know?

He just glanced at the ID—good heavens, a first-class police superintendent? This can't be real.

Considering the timeline, Jiang Tian has only been out of school for less than four years.

That's right, to be precise, a little over three years.

Because Jiang Tian is a senior this year, and will even have to return to school to register in a while.

And that's when Jiang Tian and Yunda will be taking their entrance exams.

"Could it be fake?" the principal thought boldly, his eyes growing brighter and brighter: "Yes, it's definitely fake."

"Hey, help me investigate someone, yes, that's right, his name is Jiang Tian." The principal nervously made a phone call.

After hanging up, the principal stood at the window. From here, he could even see the main gate of the high school, where three cars were parked.

There were also two armed police vehicles. The principal's heart sank.

Just as Jiang Tian's operation began, the entire Yunhai city exploded.

It was an absolute explosion.

As the Yunhai Municipal Public Security Bureau reported all the case information to the Ministry of Public Security through Gao Xiaolong, it even directly attracted the personal review of the Minister of Public Security.

Even the director of the Narcotics Control Bureau assisted.

Because it was too explosive.

The main reason is that what was uncovered from Mr. Yun this time wasn't the whole story, but just the beginning—and that's the most terrifying part.

Yunhai is a terrifying place.

A mere glimpse of the tip of the iceberg of Yunhai's evil has caused a nationwide earthquake.

After Operation Dawn officially commenced.

The streets of Yunhai were temporarily placed under surveillance. Large numbers of traffic police, armed police, special police, etc., flooded the streets, mainly to prevent any chaos.

There is a fundamental difference between narcotics police and criminal investigation police.

Ninety-nine percent of criminal crimes are crimes of passion or individual acts; no matter how much effort is put into arresting them, there are few worries about the consequences.

But narcotics is different.

Criminal investigation is done by individuals; narcotics is done by teams.

It's organized, premeditated, and involves a large organization; what's even more terrifying is the lack of humanity.

Crimes of passion pose little danger.

Drug enforcement, from the very beginning of the investigation, is accompanied by countless temptations and terrifying dangers. Drug trafficking is an extremely profitable industry, and drug dealers will stop at nothing to make money.

Drug enforcement police face both the temptations of drug dealers and the pressure of retaliation.

The vehicle drove along the road.

Miao Zifeng, detained in the back seat, remained silent, looking at the police officers on guard outside the window.

“You arrested Mr. Yun?” Miao Zifeng asked incredulously.

“That’s right.” Jiang Tian personally oversaw the detention.

“Impossible, the police in Yunhai.” Miao Zifeng didn’t finish her sentence, but Jiang Tian had already guessed.

It was no surprise that Yunhai was a chaotic place, practically a paradise for drug dealers.

Over the years, the drug trafficking power in Yunhai had steadily expanded.

It was also constantly expanding.

According to actual data, more than half of the drugs in the country are shipped out from Yunhai—this statement is indeed true.

"You don't know yet, do you? Mr. Yun has already confessed. Without Mr. Yun's confession and ledgers, how could we have arrested you directly?" Zhao Wei said directly.

As he spoke,

they had already arrived at the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Miao Zifeng, who had remained silent since saying that one sentence on the way, arrived at the police station.

She saw that the police station was full of vehicles.

One by one, ashen-faced men were escorted from police cars.

Having a ledger was practically equivalent to a roll call.

Following the ledger, the arrests would proceed methodically, without any problems.

After all, Mr. Yun had always maintained an air of mystery; even Wei Youdao's arrest went largely unnoticed.

"Captain Jiang."

"Captain Jiang, how did the operation go?"

"You caught him so quickly?"

"This is a big fish!"

Approaching the city police station, officers hurrying by greeted Jiang Tian with broad smiles.

Before joining the police force, they were filled with longing.

They had fantasized countless times about being glorious police officers, admired by all.

But after joining, they discovered it was nothing like that at all.

Unconsciously, they had even become synonymous with incompetence.

The entire security situation in Yunhai was completely out of control, and there was no way to reverse it.

Narcotics police across the country were cursing them.

They felt frustrated.

The pressure from above, the disdain from colleagues elsewhere during meetings and training sessions.

But now.

Everything has changed.

They cracked the first-ever case involving a single suspect exceeding five thousand kilograms of drugs.

What does this mean?

It shocked the entire Ministry of Public Security, shocked the whole country.

Everyone was full of energy, excitedly making arrests.

But among them.

Only one person stood out, wearing a mask, cleaning the toilets, Xiao Bo, clenching his fists, watching the scene before him, watching Jiang Tian being worshipped by everyone.

He saw the entire narcotics squad on the right track, but all of this didn't belong to him.

Ring ring ring!!!

Just as Jiang Tian was casually answering, the phone rang.

Jiang Tian said to Zhao Wei, "You two take them into the interrogation room first for questioning. I'll come in shortly."

After saying that, Jiang Tian answered the phone and walked out simultaneously. "Mu Ya, what? You want to see a movie with me tonight? Great, I definitely have time. I'll go buy the tickets later. What? You bought them? Okay, I definitely won't be late."

A cold wind began to blow.

"Jiang Tian."

Just as Jiang Tian hung up the phone, a voice filled with gritted teeth came from behind.

Jiang Tian turned around and saw Xiao Bo standing in the doorway, holding a toilet mop, staring at him with bloodshot eyes.

However, Jiang Tian waved his hand and covered his nose, saying, "Get away, get away, it stinks! It stinks so much and you're still standing so close to me, have you no morals?"



Chapter 80 Yunlan Villa, Evidence of Sun Haibo's Crimes


"Jiang Tian, ​​don't push your luck. Don't forget who mentored you when you first joined the police force. Now that you've risen through the ranks, you're ungrateful!" Xiao Bo clenched his fists and gritted his teeth.

Jiang Tian, ​​who was on the phone, turned around upon hearing this, his sneer deepening.

Indeed.

In his memory, when Jiang Tian first joined the police force, although he wasn't assigned a mentor, he was just an intern. The plan was to assign a mentor after the internship, so the police force arranged for him to work with Xiao Bo.

After all, normally, even after your internship, you might not be able to stay in the narcotics squad. Perhaps you weren't capable enough, or perhaps you didn't perform well, and then you'd be transferred elsewhere.

It's like joining a new company and finding a senior colleague to mentor you and let you learn.

Back then, Jiang Tian was working with Xiao Bo. To be honest, Jiang Tian truly trusted Xiao Bo completely. In his previous life, Jiang Tian had been incredibly obedient to Xiao Bo.

He even served him tea and water.

But later, Jiang Tian thought about it carefully and realized that besides making him serve tea and water and assigning him odd jobs, Xiao Bo had acted like a king.

He even took on a lackey, and Jiang Tian was the one who used to serve him meals.

After learning Jiang Tian's identity, Xiao Bo's treatment of him became even more excessive.

This was Xiao Bo's supposed kindness.

He even conspired with Sun Haibo to frame Jiang Tian after learning that Gao Xiaolong had arrived.

Damn it.

Thinking of this, Jiang Tian almost forgot about that bastard Sun Haibo.

In his previous life, after Jiang Peng fell from grace, Sun Haibo was the one who kicked him while he was down.

To this day, Jiang Tian still remembers that after Jiang Peng was investigated and dismissed, this guy even had Jiang Tian arrested and severely tortured in the police station.

And Jiang Tian also remembers his wife, Wang Yifei, disheveled and flushed when he returned home.

Thinking of this...

Jiang Tian, ​​who had been prepared to let go of his hatred, instantly had his eyes turn red.

Simply letting these two beasts go was impossible.

He even had to give them a harsher punishment.

“Little Jiang, don’t you understand the importance of forcing growth? Don’t you understand the importance of starting with small things? Without my guidance over the past month or so, would you be where you are today?” Xiao Bo was about to try a softer approach.

Based on his understanding of Jiang Tian, ​​after he had just cleaned the toilets, Xiao Bo realized he was heading down a dead end.

Don’t be fooled by Jiang Tian’s many achievements; in reality, a person’s subjective will is not so easily changed. In Xiao Bo’s heart, Jiang Tian was still the same Jiang Tian.

It’s just that someone might have instigated Jiang Tian; otherwise, how could this fool have realized it? Also, he wasn’t discreet enough; Jiang Tian had discovered his instigation against Jiang’s mother.

“Little Jiang, I really wronged you about those things. Your brother Xiao was wrong. Please give your brother Xiao a chance, okay?” Xiao Bo, suppressing his disgust, decided to soften his stance and find an opportunity to give Jiang Tian a harsher punishment.

But Jiang Tian had already seen through Xiao Bo's beastly nature. Instead of paying him any attention, he spoke into the phone, "Xiao Ya, watching a movie alone is a bit boring. I know you're smart. I'll book a room at a hotel across from Yunnan University later. Tonight, you can help me work on a case. It doesn't involve any secrets, just take a look at it."

*Buzz!*

Xiao Bo's eyes turned bloodshot.

If Wang Yifei's looks were an 80, then Mu Ya's were at least a 95.

If you add in her temperament, family background, and character, Mu Ya would undoubtedly be a perfect score.

Wang Yifei was completely overshadowed.

For Xiao Bo, Wang Yifei was just a fling from the start; that kind of slut was merely a tool to satisfy his desires.

Once he became Wang Yifei's official girlfriend, Jiang Tian's treatment... well, just thinking about it sent shivers down Xiao Bo's spine.

"How could that be? We're just discussing a case. You can give me your opinion. You have to trust me. Love you, muah!" Jiang Tian said into the phone.

Turning around, as expected, Xiao Bo's hair was practically standing on end.

"What's wrong?" Jiang Tian asked curiously.

"Nothing, nothing. You're going to the movies with Xiao Ya tonight?" Xiao Bo still held a sliver of hope.

"Yeah." Jiang Tian nodded matter-of-factly.

"Brother Xiao, can I ask you a favor? Don't take Xiaoya to a hotel tonight." Xiao Bo's fists were trembling.

*Slap!*

The next moment.

Jiang Tian slapped him hard and sneered, "What the hell does it matter to you if I take my girlfriend to a hotel? What, you've already slept with that bitch Wang Yifei, and now you want to keep an eye on my woman?"

After saying that, Jiang Tian left the police station without looking back.

Because that afternoon, he was going to see Sun Haibo.

Jiang Tian knew Xiao Bo and Sun Haibo too well.

Right now, they weren't causing trouble because Jiang Tian was in the spotlight, under the public eye.

If anything went wrong, these two would definitely find an opportunity.

Xiao Bo was one thing, but Sun Haibo was the deputy director, constantly being watched, so Jiang Tian naturally wanted to strike first.

"If I remember correctly, Sun Haibo's villa is in the Yunlan Villa District."

Jiang Tian's gaze was icy. Did he really think he was a saint?

Jiang Tian still vividly remembered how badly these two had taught him a lesson at the police station.



Chapter 81 Sin Value Explosion, Old Deng, Your House is Gone.


The entire city police department, and indeed the entire Yunhai police force, was in an uproar after Wei Rong's case led to the arrest of Mr. Yun.

Under Operation Dawn.

Jiang Tian, ​​sitting in his car, glanced at his phone and was astonished to find his crime points skyrocketing.

In fact, arresting Mr. Yun had brought Jiang Tian a terrifying and enormous reward.

He had contributed several thousand crime points.

And the most terrifying thing was that as the entire Yunhai operation progressed, Jiang Tian's crime points were increasing at an alarming rate.

[Spotless (Gold)]

[Scanning Radar (Gold)]

[Martial Arts Master (Red)]

[Endless Stamina (Red)]

[Criminal Investigation Genius (Blue)]

Collecting Achievement Task 1: Blessing of the God of Wealth (Incomplete)

2: God of Body Management (Incomplete)

Current Sin Value [14344]

[+41]

[+45]

[+6]

It keeps increasing every now and then.

Good, good, good! Jiang Tian looked on with delight, not even glancing at the white entries, as he didn't need them yet.

These sin values ​​were even enough for Jiang Tian to directly upgrade to a red entry.

Criminal Investigation Genius was more than enough for Jiang Tian at the moment. He was primarily a narcotics officer, and his usefulness in criminal investigation wasn't significant.

Currently, both his physical strength and combat ability are sufficient. The main reason Jiang Tian is keeping these "crime points" might come in handy at a crucial moment.

Yunlan Residential Area.

Upon arriving, Jiang Tian directly scaled the wall into the villa area. With his skills, getting in was incredibly easy.

He even avoided all the blind spots of the surveillance cameras.

With his extraordinary memory, he could recall things he had seen in the news in his previous life, even if he had forgotten them.

"Villa number sixteen, that's it."

Jiang Tian glanced around. The environment was elegant and the location was nice. Even though housing prices weren't as outrageous as in later years, and hadn't experienced a period of explosive growth, buying a villa in a first-tier city like Yunhai now would cost at least several million.

Then, Jiang Tian quickly scaled the villa. To avoid making a mistake, he sneaked in, not through doors or windows, but with a leap, instantly climbed to the second floor and slipped in through a second-floor window.

Without even searching, Jiang Tian casually lifted the covers, revealing a layer of cash spread out on them.

"Damn."

Even Jiang Tian had never seen so much cash before. It suddenly reminded him of a TV series he'd watched in his past life—Zhao Dehan, right?

Jiang Tian even rummaged through the drawer and found passports and several fake ID cards, all with different names. But given Sun Haibo's power, Jiang Tian was certain these passports and ID cards could be verified online and were all genuine and valid.

"Good, even prepared an escape route." Jiang Tian sneered.

Click!

But just then, the sound of a key turning in a lock came from behind the curtains.

Jiang Tian darted behind the curtains, peeking out. With a series of footsteps, he saw a figure enter the room.

Jiang Tian initially thought it was Sun Haibo.

But to his utter surprise, when he secretly saw who had entered, Jiang Tian was stunned.

Good heavens.

"I'll just give Mu Ya 20,000 yuan to buy her a Louis Vuitton bag to win her back. Director Sun, you'll definitely support me, right? After all, I have no other choice now."

As the sound rang out, Jiang Tian secretly filmed with his camera.

Xiao Bo.

That's right, the person who came in was none other than Xiao Bo.

Jiang Tian thought to himself, "Wow, that's incredible!"

This Xiao Bo is truly a master thief! He stole away that bitch Wang Yifei he was pursuing, and now he's here to steal Director Sun's secret stash.

As for how Xiao Bo knew about the slush fund, Jiang Tian didn't care.

However, he had already photographed all the evidence at the scene.

Making them a success would be incredibly easy.

But Xiao Bo, having taken the money, didn't leave because Jiang Tian heard a second voice.

"Bo'er, long time no see! Come quickly while that good-for-nothing Sun Haibo isn't around." A woman's voice rang out.

Hiss!!!

Jiang Tian was utterly astonished.

He even felt a chill run down his spine.

This wasn't just a master thief; he'd even robbed Director Sun's entire house.

After Xiao Bo and his companion quickly packed up and left, Jiang Tian jumped out of the window. Of course, he had already taken all the evidence from the house; an open report was out of the question.

If Jiang Tian wanted to stay in the system, he couldn't report others; otherwise, there would be no place for him within the system.

No one, no leader, could use someone who had previously reported their superior.

The round trip took Jiang Tian less than an hour to get back to the Municipal Bureau.

"Jiang Tian, ​​what have you been doing during working hours? Why can't I find you anywhere?"

As soon as he entered the Municipal Bureau, Sun Haibo stood at the door, his face grim, and berated Jiang Tian.

"Out investigating a case. What, Director Sun, is something wrong?" Jiang Tian asked sarcastically.

Let him jump; he won't be in trouble for more than two days anyway.

On the way here, Jiang Tian had already anonymously sent all the evidence he had photographed about Sun Haibo's crimes to the Anti-Corruption Bureau.
