Besides, even without Jiang Tian's intervention, Sun Haibo would have been transferred to another position within a few days. But that would be too easy for him; he'd be out of a job, had embezzled so much, and then just retire comfortably. There's no such thing as a free lunch.

"Why did you leave your post during working hours? What case were you investigating? Did you report it?" Sun Haibo questioned.

"It was Commander Gao's orders. If you have any questions, go find Commander Gao. What, you have a problem with Commander Gao?" Jiang Tian retorted sarcastically.

Sun Haibo's expression remained unchanged as he said, "I know. So far today's Dawn Operation has arrested six drug dealers and collected information on over forty long-term drug users. We'll work overtime tonight to interrogate these people. Also, there's still a lot of information about Miao Zifeng that hasn't been revealed. Don't go home today."

Jiang Tian's expression changed slightly.

This instantly triggered many unpleasant thoughts in Jiang Tian's mind.

Sure enough, at that moment, Jiang Tian caught a glimpse of Xiao Bo peeking at him from outside the door.

Damn it.

That bastard.

Jiang Tian's gaze was dangerous. Sun Haibo's demands seemed reasonable, but Jiang Tian knew perfectly well that these two scoundrels had definitely reached some kind of agreement.

In fact, Jiang Tian could probably guess with a grain of salt that Xiao Bo had revealed Mu Ya's true identity, and after winning her over, they could turn the tables. He just couldn't have imagined that his own home would be stolen.

"Old Deng, your home is gone!"



Chapter 82: To kill someone, you must destroy their spirit; a decisive victory is needed.


"Alright!"

Jiang Tian's gaze shifted, but he ultimately held back, changing the subject to devise a plan.

In fact, Jiang Tian preferred to wait until Sun Haibo was arrested and placed under investigation before showing him the video. By then, Sun Haibo would know the truth, know his wife's true colors—that she was having an affair with Xiao Bo behind his back—and be trapped, unable to escape. How humiliating and desperate would that be?

After all, what kind of revenge is it if you only kill someone without destroying their spirit?

"Shark Arm, indeed, a piece of trash, not worth mentioning."

Xiao Bo, standing at the door, smiled mockingly. He believed such trash posed no threat whatsoever.

However, he didn't see the mocking look in Jiang Tian's eyes.

Of course, Jiang Tian didn't actually have much time. Although Operation Dawn had arrested many drug dealers and users, a large portion had already received advance warning and escaped.

Capturing these people would be a significant effort, and numerous arrest warrants had already been issued.

Jiang Tian didn't care about the escapees.

However, interrogating those arrested was a tedious and lengthy process, but fortunately, they were all apprehended at once, so the interrogations proceeded quickly.

They were interrogated in batches, then moved on to the next.

The information piled up one after another.

Of course, the most excited person was Gao Xiaolong.

He smoked, watching the bustling scene at the police station, the smoke swirling around him as he watched Jiang Tian relentlessly interrogating people in the interrogation room.

"This young Jiang is a talent. He's made such a great contribution, yet he hasn't become arrogant; instead, he's relentlessly interrogating people," Gao Xiaolong said, his eyes seemingly filled with determination.

Shen Han nodded slightly as she watched Jiang Tian's interrogations.

Very busy.

Dealing with so many drug dealers was a massive undertaking.

However, it wasn't actually as complicated as it seemed: interrogation, cross-examination, determining the charges, and then transferring them to the detention center.

As for drug users, those involved in drug trafficking were transferred to the detention center, while those simply using drugs were transferred to a detention center to await trial.

That evening, Jiang Tian sat in the interrogation room. Seeing Mu Ya's call, he felt it was time and stood up to leave.

But as soon as he opened the door, he saw Sun Haibo standing there.

"Where are you going?" Sun Haibo asked.

"Almost everyone's been arrested, and the interrogations are all finished. Those who weren't caught have all escaped. Of course I'm going home," Jiang Tian said matter-of-factly.

"No, you can't go home," Sun Haibo stopped him. "You can't act differently just because you've done something good. Everyone else is working overtime. What does it mean for you to go home? Do you think you can be special just because you did something good?"

As he spoke, Sun Haibo glanced at his watch.

Just then, Mu Ya's call came in. Jiang Tian ignored Sun Haibo and answered Mu Ya's call.

"Jiang Tian, ​​Xiao Bo just came to see me with flowers, insisting on treating me to dinner," Mu Ya said directly on the phone.

"What did you say?" Jiang Tian smiled slightly.

Actually, he hadn't directly confronted Xiao Bo because he was waiting for this moment. On the other hand, he was also testing Mu Ya's attitude to see if it had completely changed. Now, it seemed Jiang Tian was quite satisfied.

If Mu Ya had told him she was busy tonight and wouldn't have time to stand him up, Jiang Tian's attitude towards her would have completely changed.

Fortunately, Mu Ya's personality was just that loyal.

"Of course I can't go. That bastard Xiao Bo, who knows what he'll do?" Mu Ya said.

"No, you have to go," Jiang Tian chuckled.

Mu Ya on the other end of the phone was speechless with disappointment. "What do you mean? I don't want to go, I don't want to see him. Are you testing me?"

"No, no, no, listen to me," Jiang Tian explained to Mu Ya after asking about the location Xiao Bo had agreed to.

"You're so naughty," Mu Ya said, sounding somewhat excited. "But I really like it."

After hanging up, Jiang Tian didn't even look at Sun Haibo, turned around and left the police station. After all, this green-haired turtle wouldn't be able to keep jumping around for long. Now that there was no movement, the anti-corruption bureau had probably already started investigating.

This dismissive attitude instantly made Sun Haibo blush.

Seeing the strange expressions on the faces of those left behind to work overtime, Sun Haibo was furious: "No organization, no discipline! You're all working overtime, but look at him, acting like a lone wolf. Does he think he can be above the organization and discipline just because he's done something meritorious?"

"Director Sun, each of our teams has its assigned duties. Captain Jiang finished his interrogation early, so naturally he should go home."

"Yes, and the more shifts the better. Before Captain Jiang came, who knew the Narcotics Control Division would have overtime?"

"This credit was all earned by Captain Jiang Tian. He even participated in the interrogation for a whole day. Even as a leader, you can't just create trouble for no reason!"

"That's right, he's just creating trouble for no reason. No wonder we couldn't catch drug dealers before; it's because of a deputy director like this. Thankfully, he's finally leaving."

Just then, Sun Haibo, who thought everyone would support him, was instantly dumbfounded. Why was everyone pointing their guns at him?

But what he didn't know was that Jiang Tian, ​​in a short time, had led his team to achieve collective success, a moment of triumph that won everyone's favor.

And all Jiang Tian needed to do was contribute a negligible amount of credit.

He didn't even get a share of the spoils, yet everyone was already deeply grateful.

At this point, how could you dare target Comrade Jiang Tian? Wasn't that ridiculous?

Jiang Tian was willing, but his comrades weren't.

"You guys," Director Sun truly experienced the chill of being forgotten once someone leaves.

Knowing he was being transferred, everyone had lost their respect for him.

"Damn it, you lot who were talking, you just wait. You think I'll be transferred? Just wait. If Xiao Bo succeeds, not only will I not leave, but I'll also get a huge share of the credit. I'll make you all pay." Sun Haibo's face was grim.

Jiang Tian, ​​walking out the door, ignored Sun Haibo.

He took out his phone and started dialing a number.

That's why, even though Wang Chang is a bit of a beast, he has one redeeming quality: he's loyal to his word. Take this phone number, for example; it's still in use more than a decade later, saving Jiang Tian a lot of trouble.

"Hello, who is it?"

Wang Chang's voice was full of anger as he answered the phone.

How could he not be furious?

He, along with Wang Yifei's mother and Huang Mei, had been staking out Xiao Bo's rented apartment for two days, but hadn't seen him at all. They were even starting to wonder if Jiang Tian had lied to them.

But that didn't make sense!

After questioning him, Wang Yifei had also said that Xiao Bo lived there and that she had come several times.

The two of them had even brought bedding and a bed, waiting at the door.

They were prepared for a long-term battle to pay the compensation and medical expenses.

"I am Jiang Tian," Jiang Tian said directly.

"What is it?"

Damn it, Wang Chang was very unhappy to hear this name. He couldn't extort money from him, but he couldn't blame Jiang Tian either. After all, they had such a worthless son.

Giving up Jiang Tian, ​​a second-generation rich kid with a promising future, instead, he foolishly offered himself up to a poor kid from the mountains, and the worst part was, he didn't seem to have any future at all.



Chapter 83 completely ignited the chaos, turning everything into utter pandemonium.


"You haven't found Xiao Bo, have you? I'll only say this once, he's at Alice's Restaurant. By the way, you might not know the location, it's not far from Yun University. Xiao Bo is there right now, you should be able to catch him there." Jiang Tian finished speaking and hung up the phone with a faint smile.

Jiang Tian even drove off in his small Volkswagen Santana, after all, this car had been assigned to their team and they had absolute right to use it.

On the other hand, it wasn't considered misuse of public resources.

After all, Jiang Tian was using the scanning radar, working overtime for free, and maintaining the city's stability and security, so he felt perfectly justified in using it.

Meanwhile.

Alice's Restaurant.

Mu Ya was watching Xiao Bo kneeling on the ground, proposing marriage, with a look of displeasure on her face.

Of course, that wasn't all.

Beside Xiao Bo were two elderly, hunched-over men and women.

They were looking at her with bright eyes.

They kept saying, "Our daughter is so beautiful. Your family is well-off; you won't look down on us just because we're from the mountains, will you?"

"Yes, our daughter is clearly well-mannered. We're truly impressed that Xiao Bo married you. We heard from Xiao Bo that you two had some issues. Daughter, for our sake, please forgive Xiao Bo this time."

The couple's words attracted the attention of everyone in the restaurant, who all looked on with amusement.

That's right, this was Xiao Bo's plan.

When his parents arrived in Yunhai, Xiao Bo didn't even ask them why they came. He immediately told them about his situation. Upon hearing that he was the daughter of a high-ranking official and had a chance to be won over, they immediately forgot the purpose of their trip to Yunhai and completely forgot about their daughter, Xiao Yuan.

"Oh, right, Mu Ya, this is a Louis Vuitton bag I bought for you as a gift," Xiao Bo quickly pressed his advantage.

But little did he know, Mu Ya had already seen through this scheme perfectly.

Not only did it not change Mu Ya's mind, it made her even more disgusted.

Because, many things she understood, though she wouldn't say, didn't mean she didn't know.

"Mu Ya, say yes," Xiao Bo knelt on the ground, revealing what he thought was a handsome smile.

The people in the Western restaurant watched this beautiful scene with delighted expressions.

But the next moment.

Boom!!!

Thump!!!

Thump!!!

Kneeling on the ground, Xiao Bo, who hadn't finished speaking, felt the world spin, his body wracked with pain, and his entire body flew several meters, crashing to the ground and spitting out blood.

And where Xiao Bo was kneeling, Jiang Tian, ​​dressed in a jacket, made a dazzling appearance.

Then he waved to Mu Ya.

As soon as Mu Ya approached, Jiang Tian pulled her into his arms.

"You beast, what's wrong? You got that bitch Wang Yifei pregnant, and now you're targeting my current girlfriend? Do you think I'm easy to bully?" Jiang Tian's face was icy cold.

"You piece of trash, you dare hit me?" Xiao Bo's eyes were bloodshot, but when he saw Mu Ya snuggle into Jiang Tian's arms, his vision blurred and he almost fainted.

He was the one who met Mu Ya first!

That bitch Wang Yifei, whoever wants her can have her.

Jiang Tian swiftly pulled out his baton from behind and mercilessly struck Xiao Bo on the head.

Blood flowed freely.

Xiao Bo, his eyes bloodshot and gone mad, yelled, "I'll fight you to the death!"

Thump!!!

Jiang Tian struck Xiao Bo squarely on the cheek with his baton.

Blood splattered, and Xiao Bo struggled on the ground for a long time, unable to get up. Jiang Tian then raised his foot and stomped on Xiao Bo's face.

"Do you even have the right, you piece of trash?" Jiang Tian sneered.

"You..."

Xiao Bo's eyes were bloodshot.

Seeing Jiang Tian embracing his beloved woman was more unbearable than death for him. What was even more unbearable was that it wouldn't matter if it were someone else, but the very person he once despised—Jiang Tian—was embracing the woman he loved most, the woman he had always pursued but could never have. It almost made him lose his mind.

"You hit my son! I'll fight you to the death!"

"You shameless woman, you actually sent some random man to beat my son!"

Xiao Bo's mother, a middle-aged woman with a dark complexion, rushed forward in a fit of rage, ready to fight Jiang Tian.

Bang!!!

Jiang Tian lashed out with his baton, sending Xiao's mother sprawling to the ground.

"What kind of slut is she? My girlfriend? What does she have to do with your son? Just because your son takes a fancy to someone doesn't mean she's his. You mountain folk, when did you become so audacious?" Jiang Tian sneered.

"I'm telling you, I've already called the police. My son's a cop. I've looked into it; you're even guilty of assault. You'll be going to jail!" Xiao's father, having observed the situation closely, was terrified, sweating profusely as he threatened her with his phone.

Jiang Tian raised an eyebrow. This didn't seem like someone from the mountains; they even had a cell phone.

Seeing Jiang Tian remain silent, Xiao's father immediately puffed out his chest: "Even if you kneel down now, it's too late. Unless she agrees to be my daughter-in-law."

Jiang Tian was practically laughing in anger.

Mu Ya was completely bewildered by this logic.

Wait, what's going on with this family?

Mu Ya stared, speechless.

Jiang Tian chuckled. "You've mistaken me for someone else. Your daughter-in-law is someone else entirely. Oh, right, I forgot to tell you, your son even got her pregnant."

Xiao's parents seemed to forget their pain, their gazes instantly turning to Xiao Bo.

"No..." Xiao Bo was about to frantically shake his head.

He absolutely couldn't admit it, otherwise, his life would truly be over. He knew that bitch Wang Yifei too well; he wasn't even sure if the child was his.

After all, that bitch had more than just him as a friend. He'd brought Wang Yifei into the entertainment circle as revenge.

But before Xiao Bo could shake his head...

"Scumbag, you scumbag, I've finally caught you!"

"Quick, get my daughter to see a doctor!"

"You've been hiding for so long, where can you hide now?"

One problem after another.

At the crucial moment, Wang Chang finally rushed in. Their eyes instantly locked onto Xiao Bo, whose face was covered in blood. They recognized him immediately from the photos.

Yes, it was the same person who took their daughter to a hotel.

Xiao Bo's vision went black, and he pretended to faint on the ground.

But...

*Slap! Slap! Slap!*

Wang Chang rushed up, and without asking any questions, straddled Xiao Bo, slapping him repeatedly across the face with tremendous force.

"Stop! What right do you have to hit my son?"

Xiao's mother, lying on the ground, got up and started scratching at Wang Chang.

"You hit my man, I'll kill you!" Huang Mei rushed in and joined the fray.

Chaos ensued.

Utter chaos.

The people in Alice's Restaurant watched the spectacle in astonishment.

They never expected such a dramatic turn of events.

"Interesting."

"That's more like it. These two families should be a perfect match."

Jiang Tian watched this ethical drama, even occasionally making provocative remarks.

"Without 300,000, I'll take his life. He must compensate me for my daughter's medical expenses and emotional distress."



Chapter 84: Loan Sharking at High Interest Rates, the Painful Torture Officially Begins


Police station.

Criminal investigation team.

Jiang Tian picked his nose and casually flicked it at Xiao Bo, who had a bruised and swollen face across from him. He was very agile and hit the target precisely.

"I'll fight you to the death!" Xiao Bo stood up angrily, his eyes bloodshot.

"Come on!"

Jiang Tian stood up and beckoned defiantly, "You piece of trash, ten of you wouldn't be enough for me to beat up."

"Look at him! Is there no justice left? No law?" Xiao Bo nearly fainted from the heat of the moment, but he remained calm and didn't dare go up to him.

"Useless piece of trash. What good is he besides pulling stunts behind people's backs? Garbage," Jiang Tian said disdainfully.

"Alright, that's enough."

Sitting in the mediation room, Miss Qiu's expression was a picture of utter turmoil.

She was completely overwhelmed, her head throbbing with anxiety.

She even clutched her head, her face contorted with utter despair.

Why, why did she have to handle this?

The process was actually quite simple. Initially, it seemed like a minor dispute, and the local police officer went to the station. However, when they arrived, the scene was rather chaotic; the fight was quite fierce.

Of course, the most important point was that at the police station, Xiao Bo's parents immediately claimed Xiao Bo was a police officer, to which Xiao Bo helplessly replied that he was a narcotics officer.

Jiang Tian slammed his fist on the table and threw out his identification.

Good heavens, the station chief's scalp tingled, and he immediately contacted the city bureau.

When Jiang Peng found out, he knew he couldn't get involved, especially after receiving Jiang Tian's message that Number Three was also involved.

Damn it, Old Mu's granddaughter was also involved! His son had snatched this beauty away.

Jiang Peng was furious.

Good heavens, son, you're defying the heavens!

This made it even more difficult for Jiang Peng to get involved.

So, he simply handed the matter over to the criminal investigation team, leaving it to them to handle as they pleased.

Anyway, Jiang Peng knew that no matter how things were handled, Jiang Tian's current achievements wouldn't be in jeopardy.

However, Xiao Bo might be in a terrible situation within the police force.

After all, Xiao Bo had brought his family to harass Jiang Tian's girlfriend. If things escalated, those powerful figures would probably really want to kill Xiao Bo.

In any case, Jiang Tian was in an invincible position.

The criminal investigation team was shrewd. After learning the details, especially the love-hate relationship between Jiang Tian, ​​Xiao Bo, and Wang Yifei's parents, they were completely dumbfounded.

They then directly handed the matter over to Ms. Qiu, who was somewhat acquainted with Jiang Tian, ​​and asked her to mediate.

"Pay up! He has to pay up! My daughter got pregnant by him, and now she's bedridden and seriously injured. He has to take responsibility! He has to pay 300,000 yuan, no, it has to be 500,000 yuan! Not a penny less! And 200,000 yuan for emotional distress!" Wang Chang shouted, pushing his luck and demanding money from Xiao Bo.

Xiao Bo's face was deathly pale.

Five hundred thousand—he couldn't even come up with that much money, even if he sold himself!

Besides, thirty or twenty thousand didn't matter, but if he took five hundred thousand from Sun Haibo, he would definitely be found out.

If he took it himself, even if his family sold their blood, it wouldn't be enough.

"Impossible! Wang Yifei was hit by a car on her own. What does it have to do with me?" Xiao Bo refused to admit it, no matter what.

"Then just wait until we all die together! Wang Yifei is our lifeline. She has no money for medical treatment. Even if we have to fight to the death, we'll drag you all down with us."

It has to be said, Jiang Tian's plan succeeded. He successfully absolved himself of responsibility.

The two families fought until they were bloodied and bruised, while Jiang Tian, ​​with Mu Ya in his arms, watched the spectacle.

Xiao Bo's heart sank even further.

He knew Wang Yifei's family wasn't to be trifled with. Before anything even started, he'd already been blackmailed.

Especially seeing Jiang Tian with Mu Ya watching him make a scene.

*Slap!*

Xiao Bo slapped himself hard.

Tears streamed down his face as he cried in regret.

Look at Wang Yifei's parents, their eyes bloodshot, crying, making a scene, threatening suicide, beating him half to death, and then throwing a tantrum.

That's right, Huang Mei was rolling around on the ground.

Wang Chang didn't dare to lay a hand on her at the police station, but she continued to hurl vicious curses like a shrew.

Xiao Bo's parents, who came from a mountain village, had never seen anything like this.

They'd just arrived in the city and were already facing a terrifying ordeal.

"Stop making a scene. Since this matter has been entrusted to me for mediation, then stop making a fuss. State your demands clearly. Xiao Bo, you got that girl pregnant, and now that something's happened to her, you're running away. You're the one who's wrong," Miss Qiu said neutrally.

After all, this kind of scumbag—who uses a woman and then runs away, disappearing when the girl needs medical attention—doesn't deserve any sympathy.

Of course, the most important issue was...

Jiang Tian added fuel to the fire, saying, "Xiao Bo, you're a proud police officer! How can you tarnish the image of the police? She has legitimate reasons and demands. You went too far first. If she complains to higher authorities, this is a serious offense. You might not even be able to clean the toilets then."

A fatal blow.

Jiang Tian's words instantly enraged Xiao Bo.

Wang Chang and his wife felt as if a clear path had appeared before them. Huang Mei shouted, "That's right! If you don't give us the money, we'll make a scene, go to your leaders, even file a petition! We refuse to believe there's no place that will stand up for the people!"

Good heavens!

Xiao Bo's face turned pale, and his parents were even more frantic: "You dare!"

"One hundred thousand, one hundred thousand at most!" Xiao's father shouted, almost using all his strength to reach out.

"Three hundred thousand! If you're short even a penny, you can expect your son to be fired!" Wang Chang was overjoyed, having directly exploited their fatal weakness.

"Give me time, give me three days to borrow the money, and I'll lend it to you." Xiao's father dared not haggle any longer.

He thought of how many people in the village were fawning over their family because of Xiao Bo. They had over one hundred thousand of that three hundred thousand; if they could borrow another one hundred thousand, they could get through their current predicament.

Then, when Xiao Bo climbed up the ranks and became a high-ranking official, the reward would be many times greater. Xiao's father was very good at calculating.

“Old man, where would we get that much money? The village can't possibly lend us that much!” Mrs. Xiao thought he was crazy.

“If all else fails, there’s always Old Third in the village, isn’t he a loan shark?” Mr. Xiao said through gritted teeth.

Xiao Bo remained silent. If all else failed, he considered temporarily taking money from Sun Haibo’s secret stash.

But he didn’t know…

Even as the argument raged, officers from the Anti-Corruption Bureau, acting on Jiang Tian's tip, arrived at the Yunlan Villa area.

The entire villa had already been sealed off.

They were wearing gloves and beginning to inventory and sort through the contents.

Meanwhile, Director Sun sat in the deputy director's office.

The sound of dripping water filled the air. Director Sun brewed a cup of pre-rain Longjing tea, savoring its delicate aroma. He stood up and walked to the window, gazing at the expansive and beautiful scenery outside.

From this vantage point, one could even see the grand gates of the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Police cars were gradually returning to their posts.

Because the case was relatively clear, the arrest operation was swift, completing 90% of the operation in almost half a day.

After all, it involved arresting people from a list and then conducting a streamlined interrogation.

Finally, it was time to verify statements.

"Xiao Bo, my future rests on your shoulders. Old Master Mu's granddaughter, you must secure this!" Director Sun thought to himself.

But the next moment...

Sun Haibo suddenly saw two familiar figures.

"Jiang Tian, ​​and who's that?"

Sun Haibo rubbed his eyes. They were on the third floor; he could see them clearly.

The two walked under the lights. Sun Haibo's eyes gradually focused, and looking at the woman again, wasn't that the target Xiao Bo had mentioned?

"Xiao Bo, what the hell are you doing?" Sun Haibo's face darkened.

Knock knock knock!!!

Just then, a knock sounded at the door.

A moment later.

A secretary pushed open the door and said to Sun Haibo, "Director Sun, Chief Gao is preparing a summary report for Operation Dawn. We'll be there in a bit."



Chapter 85: Inviting Sun Haibo for tea, his downfall, his collapse.


“Okay, I understand, I’ll be right there.” Sun Haibo stood up and followed without thinking too much.

When Sun Haibo arrived at the conference room, he found it already quite full.

For example, Gao Xiaolong sat at the head of the table, along with Jiang Peng, the director of the municipal bureau, Shen Han from the provincial department, and Song Liguo, the head of the anti-drug squad.

“Director Sun, please make yourself at home. Today’s meeting is to summarize Operation Dawn and send it to the Ministry of Public Security; they’re pressing us hard,” Shen Han said casually, waving his hand.

Jiang Tian had completely withdrawn from this case.

Even if you reap the rewards, you should leave some for yourself. Otherwise, if you take all the credit, your colleagues will resent it, even if they don’t say anything.

Those who dine and dash won’t last long.

Knowing how to share things you don’t need can earn you everyone’s goodwill; why not?

"So, although you solved the case, you did the right thing. You even arrested one of the most important suspects. If you had arrested the others, your team would have resented you, even if they didn't say anything. Besides, getting this credit for your hard work doesn't affect your original contribution; it only leaves the leadership with the impression that you're greedy for credit."

"You did the right thing now, knowing when to advance and when to retreat. You didn't go overboard after your achievement; you just did what was expected of you."

After leaving the police station, it was completely dark. Jiang Tian didn't hide anything from Mu Ya.

There was no need. Given Mu Ya's status, this information wasn't exactly a secret. Letting Mu Ya consider it, and giving her a sense of involvement, would help advance their relationship.

However, Mu Ya's insights slightly surprised Jiang Tian.

Some women bring good fortune to their husbands and families; some women bring ruin. They're both women, but different women can bring completely different effects.

Jiang Tian thought of Wang Yifei, that wretched woman from his past life, who was constantly causing trouble at home.

Her insane antics made Jiang Tian miserable, even to the point of wishing he were dead. Looking at Mu Ya, Jiang Tian simply thought, "This is unbelievable!"

If Xiao Bo marries Wang Yifei, he'll be so lucky.

"What are you looking at? Are you listening?" Mu Ya waved her hand in front of the lost-in-thought Jiang Tian.

The next moment, Jiang Tian reached out and directly grasped Mu Ya's slender, perfect hand.

Mu Ya symbolically pulled away, then turned her head to look in another direction, tilting her head. Jiang Tian clearly noticed that her fair cheeks were already flushed red.

Hehe.

To be honest, in this respect, Jiang Tian really had to thank Xiao Bo. If it weren't for Xiao Bo's recent brilliant moves, Jiang Tian might not have been able to win Mu Ya over so quickly.

Because of Xiao Bo's appearance, each brilliant move brought Mu Ya one step closer to Jiang Tian.

“Moreover, your case should be closed soon,” Mu Ya said.

Jiang Tian looked even more surprised.

Indeed.

The biggest challenge in any operation is actually identifying the mastermind behind it; capturing that mastermind is the ultimate objective.

However, Mr. Yun was an exception. He was directly arrested, and then Operation Dawn was launched. In a short time, they could simply use a list to arrest people.

Moreover, the biggest drug lord under Mr. Yun was actually Wei Youdao, while Miao Zifeng was a small-time drug lord.

There was another one, but his drug trafficking scale was far smaller.

Even if Jiang Tian were to take action against the smaller drug dealers below him, it wouldn't be of much value.

The entire mission progressed unusually smoothly, with very few escapes.

“However, this case is just the beginning. Mr. Yun doesn't have the ability to supply more than half of the country's drugs,” Jiang Tian shook his head.

“Let’s go, I’ve bought the movie tickets.” Mu Ya took the initiative and pulled Jiang Tian towards their destination.

Jiang Tian, ​​naturally, couldn't resist and obediently followed behind.

Twenty years ago, in the year 2000, Jiang Tian looked at the surrounding night view. Because it was a first-tier city, the infrastructure was already very well-developed.

It was during this time that Jiang Tian and Mu Ya were having dinner and watching a movie.

Xiao Bo was in deep trouble.

Wang Yifei's parents were pressuring him, demanding money from his family within three days.

Xiao Bo gritted his teeth and decided to act immediately that night.

Of course, just in case, Xiao Bo thought of his bleak future; he couldn't bring himself to give up so easily.

Xiao Bo gritted his teeth and simply told his parents to go home and prepare first, while he looked for other ways to get the money.

"Oh, right, there's one more thing, your sister."

"She's in the hospital. Her injuries are very serious. The doctor said her brachial plexus nerve was severed, and her entire arm might be useless for the rest of her life."

*Whoosh!*

Misfortune only strikes at the weakest point; bad luck only finds those with a hard life.

Xiao Bo felt dizzy and almost fainted.

Of course, all of this had nothing to do with Jiang Tian anymore.

Because this was an accidental case involving robbers, even compensation would have to go to Lao Chen. Although Lao Chen pleaded guilty and accepted punishment, taking money was out of the question, absolutely not. What was he doing working for Mr. Yun for? Wasn't it to make money? Taking money from him would be like killing him a second time.

The city police station was brightly lit.

Less than half an hour after Jiang Tian left, an ordinary official car pulled up in front of the city police station.

Six or seven staff members in suits with national emblems on their chests got out of the car.

"Thanks to the joint efforts of the city police, the provincial department, and the special task force, Operation Dawn, which began less than a day ago, is already nearing its conclusion. The case is clear, the facts of the crime are clear, and the details of the attack are detailed. Currently, there are no special points in the case. Except for one or two minor figures, issuing an arrest warrant is sufficient for the perpetrators. The case meets the criteria for closure."

Knock knock knock!!!

Jiang Peng was reporting to Gao Xiaolong when suddenly there was a knock on the door.

"Who is it? What's the matter?" Jiang Peng frowned; this was during meeting time.

Then Yu Hui pushed open the door, her face full of nervousness as she approached Jiang Peng and Gao Xiaolong, whispering to them.

"What? The anti-corruption bureau is here?"

"Did you make a mistake?" Gao Xiaolong asked instinctively.

Jiang Peng shook his head frantically: "No, Chief Gao, believe me, I absolutely didn't do it."

This was true. Although Jiang Peng lacked the ability to break through the impasse in Yunhai, his abilities were rather average, but he never dared to take what he shouldn't.

He never touched what he couldn't touch.

"Please come in," Gao Xiaolong nodded.

A few seconds after Yu Hui left, the conference room door was pushed open.

The people inside the conference room all looked at the figure entering.

"Who are you, and what are you doing here?" Sun Haibo asked, tapping the table slightly and frowning.

But the next moment, as the other party spoke, large beads of sweat instantly appeared on Sun Haibo's face.

"I am Li Yisheng, the head of the Comprehensive Section One of the Yunhai Anti-Corruption Bureau. We would like to invite Director Sun to have a cup of tea with us at the Anti-Corruption Bureau." Li Yisheng produced his identification.

*Buzz!*

Sun Haibo slumped onto the sofa.

Although everyone present was in an uproar, they didn't show it too badly.

Jiang Peng had no wrongdoing whatsoever, and even if he had, it wouldn't be a serious matter, since he hadn't accepted bribes. Shen Han wasn't worried either, because Jiang Peng's rank meant he wasn't someone a mere section chief could arrest.

Song Liguo, influenced by Jiang Peng, had never dared to make any mistakes.

Meanwhile, the deputy head of the special investigation team, an expert with high technical skills, was also present, so there was nothing wrong with him.

Therefore, when Director Sun was pulled up by two anti-corruption bureau officers,

Sun Haibo's legs were completely unable to support him; they were like mushy noodles, impossible to stand.



Chapter 86 I just went on a date, and now I've been offered a huge reward?


Jiang Tian took Mu Ya to the movies.

It has to be said, even in such a short time since his rebirth, Jiang Tian still felt like he was dreaming. Looking at Mu Ya's fair face, sitting demurely beside him, her beautiful eyes glancing at him every now and then.

Good heavens.

No wonder, Mu Ya's demeanor, especially those eyes, was simply irresistible. Those eyes were practically maxed-out phoenix eyes; anyone who looked at them would be mesmerized. Combined with Mu Ya's posture, her smiling eyes were like two crescent moons, enough to completely mesmerize anyone.

"Silly boy, what are you looking at?" Mu Ya asked, her eyes bright.

"I'm looking at you," Jiang Tian said, head held high.

Hehe, although Mu Ya was outstanding, Jiang Tian could confidently say that he himself was just as good.

The old Jiang Tian might not have been good enough for her, but the current Jiang Tian felt more than qualified to be with Mu Ya.

"Go ahead and watch if you want," Mu Ya said casually, turning back to look at the movie.

No wonder, the movie playing was *Infernal Affairs*. Jiang Tian had heard of this film, which had just been released in mainland China, back in his previous life; it was hailed as almost the ultimate pinnacle of Hong Kong police and gangster films.

*Infernal Affairs*, in terms of plot, content, and suspense, was portrayed with remarkable depth.

Even Mu Ya was tense: "Is it that dangerous for you police officers?"

Dangerous?

Jiang Tian shook his head: "That depends on the branch. A low-ranking officer might never face any danger in their entire life. A detective might only encounter a few thugs occasionally. However, narcotics police are the most dangerous branch in peaceful society."

But there was one more thing he didn't mention: the branch where you can earn merit and get promoted the fastest.

Fortune favors the bold.

Just like in peacetime, it's incredibly difficult for soldiers to earn merit and get promoted. Although they're assigned to defend the country, the opportunity to make a name for themselves is gone.

In times of war, while constant danger exists, the opportunities to achieve merit are also countless times greater.

The same applies to anti-drug police. The unfolding of "Infernal Affairs" revolves around a battle between spies within a drug deal.

Some infiltrate the police force to monitor its movements, while others infiltrate the force to monitor criminals.

This is "Infernal Affairs."

At this point, Jiang Tian had to be cautious; the situation in Yunhai was similar.

It was impossible for all police officers to remain uninfiltrated. Drug dealers offered bribes of millions; how many anti-drug officers could withstand that?

However, Jiang Tian was somewhat relieved that, besides Xiao Bo and Sun Haibo, he hadn't found a third culprit within the city bureau.

But this was something he still had to guard against.

While the city bureau hadn't yet infiltrated Yunhai's five districts, what about the branch bureaus in the other five districts?

Thinking this, Jiang Tian felt the pressure mounting.

"Jiang Tian, ​​you must be careful. Although you're very skilled, you must be aware of danger and don't let yourself be caught off guard."

The next moment, Mu Ya suddenly grasped Jiang Tian's hand. This was the first time Mu Ya had taken the initiative to hold Jiang Tian's hand. The soft, smooth hand excited Jiang Tian greatly, because he felt that tonight might bring a breakthrough.

Clearly, the power struggle depicted in "Infernal Affairs" had terrified Mu Ya.

However, this was no joke, because the fight against drug dealers was indeed that brutal, even if portrayed somewhat conservatively in the TV series.

According to Jiang Tian's understanding, far too many anti-drug police officers had sacrificed their lives.

Every year, numerous anti-drug police officers died in battles with drug dealers, while undercover, or in various accidents.

"I have a case I don't understand, can you teach me?" Jiang Tian whispered.

"Okay," Mu Ya nodded innocently, seemingly clueless.

This made Jiang Tian even happier; he never expected the newly released "Infernal Affairs" to provide such a helpful assist.

The Kairun Hotel was just across from Yunhai.

Jiang Tian pulled Mu Ya to the front desk to get the room key; he had already booked a room in advance.

The elevator went up.

Upon arriving at the room, Jiang Tian immediately scanned it with the radar.

This was already a habit for Jiang Tian. Every time he arrived in a new place, he would activate his scanning radar to check for any danger within a kilometer.

After all, Jiang Tian wasn't sure how many drug dealers were watching him.

So, for his own safety, Jiang Tian naturally prioritized safety.

But after just one scan, Jiang Tian froze instantly.

Because, damn it, there seemed to be a billion red dots in the next room.

Had he stumbled upon a criminal's hideout?

Jiang Tian was astonished.

This... I just came here to take a nap, am I going to deliver another major achievement?



Chapter 87 The department was shocked. Such a big case, solved?


"Comrades, never let money corrupt you, and never let bribes from drug dealers lead you down the wrong path. Once you do, there's no turning back. Sun Haibo is a stark lesson!"

In the city bureau's conference room.

Gao Xiaolong tapped the table, speaking earnestly.

Sun Haibo's arrest by the anti-corruption bureau caused a huge uproar in the city bureau. Because it was done in public, everyone on duty was shocked and immediately notified their acquaintances.

However, obviously,

these ordinary people working overtime had no way of knowing what was happening in the conference room.

Even Jiang Tian wasn't involved.

Because even the lowest-ranking person here was at Song Liguo's level, and even Captain Guo and his team weren't qualified to participate.

It was mainly for the final review of the documents, given the Ministry of Public Security's constant monitoring and final case hearing.

What, you ask why so fast?

A joke.

Didn't Gao Xiaolong and Shen Han understand?

It wouldn't matter if the higher-ups hadn't issued a directive, but once they gave the order to solve the case within a specified timeframe, everyone understood the importance of the mission.

That's one way to think about it.

"The Ministry of Public Security issued a deadline for solving the case. The mission was given this morning, and we not only completed it tonight, but we even exceeded it, adding 'Operation Dawn,' and even taking them all down that night. Now, let's do a review report and see if there's anything that needs to be added. No objections, right?"

Gao Xiaolong held a stack of mission documents; this was today's achievement.

The case was even swiftly closed by Gao Xiaolong. After a quick review, he confirmed it wouldn't implicate larger drug lords or have a wider impact. Over 90% of the suspects had already been arrested, and issuing arrest warrants for the remaining fugitives would be harmless.

The case was closed quickly.

"So fast?" Jiang Peng was somewhat surprised.

It was ridiculously fast.

"Mainly because we solve cases quickly. The sooner we do, the sooner we can show the Ministry of Public Security Yunhai's will and determination in the fight against drugs," Gao Xiaolong explained.

After a brief review,

confirming there were no omissions, Gao Xiaolong immediately passed the firsthand information to the Ministry of Public Security.

That's why...

Why is the Ministry of Public Security so easily alarmed by even the slightest disturbance in Yunhai?

The world's drugs originate from Yunnan Province, and Yunnan Province's drugs originate from Yunhai.

Yunhai-certified drugs are guaranteed to be of the highest quality; this reputation has even spread throughout the drug trade.

Countless drug cartels and drug lords are based in Yunhai, like parasites, hiding within the city and difficult to detect.

Yunhai always manages to cunningly evade all pursuit and investigation.

They also possess the most stringent methods of communication.

Drug trafficking is often accidental; even long-term contacts are usually one-way, with no knowledge of the supplier's identity.

And the suppliers, in turn, are unaware of their own limits.

Sometimes, the middlemen even hire people in dire need of money from mountainous areas to disguise their trafficking activities. When the police raid them, they find it's just an ordinary farmer from the mountains who was smuggled goods for a small bribe.

Ultimately, awareness campaigns are inadequate.

These groups are powerful; in fact, major drug trafficking gangs often cover for each other, helping and concealing their activities.

In short, they collaborate to expand their drug trade.

This is the drug trafficking network in Yunhai. These criminal groups have even taken on a clandestine operation; catching them is harder than catching spies.

The transportation route is winding and circuitous, and the natural geographical location of Yunnan Province is characterized by a continuous stretch of mountain roads.

It's practically a paradise for drug dealers.

However, this also presents a huge challenge to the entire anti-drug efforts in Yunhai. Unfortunately, despite actions and various methods being taken to combat drug dealers, no particularly effective amputation method has yet been found.

Even cracking down on them is incredibly difficult.

Every time there's a large-scale operation, the drug traffickers just throw out a few scapegoats, acting as if they know them inside and out, just to get things done for them.

But what use are these few people to the overall situation in Yunhai?

This situation drew continuous attention and follow-up from the entire Ministry of Public Security, and even the capital city, which issued directives and repeatedly criticized Yunhai's drug control problems.

It was, in short, a serious problem that was difficult to eradicate.

But this time, what does it mean to finally stand tall?

This was true triumph.

While Gao Xiaolong was faxing all the information documents, the entire Ministry of Public Security was already in a flurry of activity.

In particular, the Minister of Public Security and the Assistant Director of the 21st Narcotics Control Bureau arrived at the Ministry of Public Security immediately.

“How is the situation in Yunhai?” Minister Tan walked in hurriedly.

“The drug crime problem in Yunhai is getting worse and worse, reaching a point where it’s difficult to manage. Demanding a deadline to solve the case isn’t just forcing Yunhai, it’s forcing Director Gao!” Xu Poye, the Director and Assistant Director of the Narcotics Control Bureau, sighed.

“If we don’t force them, Yunhai will truly be finished.” Minister Tan shook his head.

He then looked at his secretary who was holding the faxed documents.

"What's the situation in Yunhai? What messages are being sent? What difficulties are they facing? Old Gao isn't asking for help, is he?" Minister Tan asked instinctively. After all, he had witnessed the entire case and felt a chill run down his spine; he had no angle to take action.

The secretary looked at the fax and said, "At 8:30 this morning, after the Ministry of Public Security issued the order to solve the case within a specified time, the provincial and municipal bureaus attached great importance to it and immediately established a joint task force. The perpetrator dared to hire someone to use TNT to attempt an explosion on team member Jiang Tian, ​​but Comrade Jiang Tian astutely discovered this and immediately raided the hideout. At 1:00 PM, the major drug dealer, Mr. Yun, and his accomplices were arrested. After interrogation at 2:00 PM, Mr. Yun's ledger was found. At 5:00 PM, the arrest of his accomplices was completed. There are any escapees, but they are not a concern; an application for an arrest warrant has been issued."

"Comrade Jiang Tian performed exceptionally well in this case. From the drug manufacturing plant to Wei Rong and Wei Youdao, it was Comrade Jiang Tian who discovered and identified the key drug lord, Mr. Yun, leading to the complete dismantling of the entire drug trafficking ring. This drug trafficking case, spanning over a decade, involved a total of 6,000 kilograms of drugs, with 500 kilograms actually seized. Under the leadership of the special task force and thanks to Comrade Jiang Tian's heroic capabilities, the operation was completed within twelve hours of receiving the Ministry of Public Security's order to solve the case within a specified timeframe."

"A total of fifteen drug traffickers were arrested, all fifteen of whom had more than 50 grams of drugs. All have been interrogated and their cases concluded. Currently, 300 drug users are being investigated and interrogated; all have confessed and accepted punishment, and communication has been established with the drug rehabilitation centers."

What is speed?

What is shock?

Wow.

Such a big case!

"Solved in just one day! Such a big case, solved in just one day?" Minister Tan raised his voice.

Good heavens.

This was a sustained effort, even prepared for a protracted battle.

To the point that Minister Tan had already discussed with Assistant Xu Poye of the 21st Bureau that if the case wasn't solved within the deadline, Xu Poye would personally go and handle it.

And then this damn thing happened.



Chapter 88: Tan Bu's Attention and Support, Zhao Wei's Sister Appears


"Minister Tan, it's not a day. A day is 24 hours. They calculated it from the time the case was discovered until the deadline for solving it was issued, which was 12 hours. So it's half a day, from the deadline to the case being closed."

What is godlike speed?

This speed astonished even the well-informed Minister Tan.

"By the way, this Jiang Tian, ​​why do I feel so familiar with him?" Minister Tan gasped in shock, then asked, as if remembering something.

"Oh, Minister Tan, you've probably forgotten. A few days ago, Old Gao was in danger and wiped out 150 drug dealers, each with a gun. That was Jiang Tian. You even made an exception and awarded him a first-class merit." Xu Poye explained.

"It's him again this time?" Minister Tan gasped.

"Minister Tan, you're wrong again. Have you forgotten? He's the one who discovered the drug manufacturing plant, wiped out 28 drug dealers, and captured drug lord Wei Youdao alive." Xu Poye reminded him.

"Damn, this guy's a talent!" Minister Tan slammed his fist on the table and stood up.

He was inwardly speechless.

"By the way, Old Gao also urged in the fax that Comrade Jiang Tian saved Old Mu's daughter a few days ago from a very dangerous escaped prisoner, and even killed that vicious criminal. A few days ago, someone attempted to stage a car accident to murder a pregnant woman, but Comrade Jiang Tian bravely intervened and apprehended him. The application for the second-class merit award hasn't been issued yet, so we hope it can be issued as soon as possible." Xu Poye swallowed hard.

He had never seen anything so twisted.

If he didn't know Old Gao, and if these cases weren't so clearly recorded in the case files, he wouldn't have dared to act so rashly.

Xu Poye himself couldn't believe such a twisted person existed.

"So, we owe him two first-class merits and one second-class merit, not even counting Comrade Jiang Tian's arrest of Mr. Yun and the dismantling of a major drug lord involved in tens of thousands of kilograms of drugs." Minister Tan was speechless.

Having lived so long and seen so many talented people...

To be honest, with a population of over a billion, we lack many things, but the one thing we have in abundance is talent.

As Minister Tan, in charge of national security, he has all sorts of talented police officers from across the country.

Many have made great contributions; some are lucky, some have real ability combined with good fortune.

But someone like Jiang Tian...

Calculated...

This guy averages a first-class merit every two days or so.

Buzz!!!

Minister Tan was a little dumbfounded.

“Award it! Immediately and urgently award him a first-class merit. We can't let him be denied credit for anything.”

Minister Tan waved his hand and immediately issued the award slip.

It's just a first-class merit.

It's just two first-class merits.

Three times in just a few days?

“And this merit too,” Xu Poye reminded him.

Huh?

Minister Tan was silent.

If he remembered correctly, he had just awarded him the Anti-Drug Vanguard honor not long ago.

“Recently, didn't we select a group of recipients of the National Public Security System Second-Class Heroic Model Award? Award it to him, add him directly to the list.” Minister Tan immediately said.

"He's only twenty-two years old." Xu Poye was astonished.

A second-class hero—thinking about it carefully, Jiang Tian's contributions were indeed considerable.

However, don't forget, Xu Poye was shocked when he saw Jiang Tian's file; he was only twenty-two years old.

In fact, he had just graduated from university a few days ago.

Moreover, awarding a hero title isn't so simple.

Even Minister Tan doesn't have the authority to add someone to an already approved list. It requires serious discussion and a final unanimous decision and signature from the minister, deputy minister, and leaders at all levels before the award is finally issued.

"Special case, special handling. I'll take the blame if anything goes wrong," Minister Tan said excitedly, slamming his fist on the table.

Putting aside everything else, to be able to break through such a huge barrier in Yunhai, a place where criminal groups have been rampant and where breakthroughs have never been made before, is a huge achievement.

Just for this merit.

He, Jiang Tian, ​​deserves this merit.

Minister Tan sat down, looking at Jiang Tian's file, his eyes full of admiration.

You should know that even he was deeply worried about how to solve the Yunhai problem.

If Elder Gao couldn't break the deadlock, then the matter would have to be transferred to the system.

They directly stationed heavily armed forces on the border.

But the cost would be enormous.

Even so, it wouldn't necessarily eliminate the problem.

Moreover, this would also affect his capabilities. As a minister, failing to resolve the drug trafficking problem in one region, thus disrupting the national system, demonstrates his inadequacy.

Perhaps this will be his final destination.

Of course, Minister Tan's decision wasn't impulsive. With Gao Xiaolong's signature, the matter was already largely accomplished.

Other issues didn't need consideration; this achievement was enough.

Solving a drug trafficking case involving tens of thousands of kilograms of drugs is arguably one of the largest drug trafficking cases in the country's history.

Most importantly, this happened in the terrifying place of Yunhai.

Meanwhile, in the hotel, Jiang Tian was completely unaware of what was happening.

His expression was extremely serious.

"If two groups, about ten people in total, meet in the hotel, seemingly for negotiations, what are their usual objectives?" Jiang Tian asked very seriously.

"Huh?"

Mu Ya looked at Jiang Tian, ​​blinking her beautiful eyes. "If it's two groups, there's definitely some shady deal going on. It could be a triad deal, or a drug trafficking ring deal. But whatever it is, two groups getting together means there's definitely something going on."

"Right, that's right."

Jiang Tian clapped his hands and said directly.

The saying goes, "Catch them red-handed."

To be honest, Jiang Tian had never caught anyone in the act before.

Most of the time, he dealt with things on the spot.

This was Jiang Tian's first time witnessing a crime scene.

Moreover, now it even involved an important issue: the transaction inside the Kairun Hotel.

Those scarlet dots, more than ten of them, none of them were simple; even the worst ones were deep scarlet.

It was obvious they were all death row inmates.

Of course, if they weren't death row inmates, Jiang Tian wouldn't bother.

"Come on, let me show you a show," Jiang Tian said, his eyes darting around.

"What play?" Mu Ya asked with great interest.

"You'll find out when you come with me," Jiang Tian chuckled.

The scanning radar scanned the adjacent room; the red dots from both sides had approached and stopped.

Based on Jiang Tian's experience watching police dramas, this contact of dots most likely meant a formal transaction was about to begin.

However, Jiang Tian still had a slight worry.

It was so late; what if he rashly notified the police station and failed to catch them at the scene, or what if it turned out to be a false alarm and not a real transaction?

Jiang Tian stroked his chin, then took out his phone and called Zhao Wei directly.

"Xiao Wei, are you asleep?" Jiang Tian asked.

"Captain Jiang, my sister and I are looking for a place to eat outside," Zhao Wei explained.

Jiang Tian glanced at the food the hotel had just served on the table.

Jiang Tian had to admit, he gave the Kairun Hotel a thumbs up. No wonder Xiao Bo liked it so much.

A good place with good quality, reasonable prices, and a beautiful environment.

A thousand yuan a night for a top-tier suite, a single room with a double bed, and even several dishes and soup were prepared for them.

Mu Ya drank some soup but ate very little, claiming it was to control her figure.

Of course, most importantly, Jiang Tian suddenly thought of Zhao Wei's sister.

In his previous life, more than ten years later.

Jiang Tian vaguely remembered seeing her on a list of female billionaires.

That's right.

And she was at the top, having invested in many unicorn companies, with assets in the tens of billions.

She was a pure female business tycoon, and a female business tycoon with a terrifying background, living in the innermost part of the first ring road.

Jiang Tian had thought countless times in his previous life about what would have happened if Wang Yifei hadn't existed.

Even if Zhao Wei and Gao Xiaolong had sacrificed themselves, would Zhao Wei's sister's feelings towards him have changed? Perhaps with a little support, she wouldn't be being scolded like a dog by Wang Yifei every day over daily necessities.

Sometimes, Wang Yifei even pressured Jiang Tian to quit his job.

She found him two part-time jobs.

Thinking back on his painful past, Jiang Tian felt a pang of sadness.

Now, with an opportunity, Jiang Tian suddenly had a brilliant idea.

Although Jiang Tian had seen through things after his rebirth and had little interest in money,

he couldn't be truly without it.

Jiang Tian remembered that before his rebirth, he had specifically interviewed Zhao Wei's sister. She had mentioned that she hadn't seized several crucial moments, otherwise her capital could have multiplied tenfold.

“My subordinate’s sister is here. You two can meet her later; you should have something in common.”

“What’s her name?”



Chapter 89 Three Strikes to Sever the Soul: Who Else Is Afraid?


"Zhao Qiuni."

A socialite on the rich list of later generations, a truly exceptional socialite.

In his previous life, she was a woman Jiang Tian could only admire from afar, not even qualified to admire from a distance; he could only see her in newspapers or news reports.

His only meeting with her was when his family suffered a major setback. Perhaps Zhao Qiuni knew about his close relationship with Zhao Wei and came to help him.

But at that time, he was tricked by Wang Yifei, which led to Zhao Qiuni's disappointed departure, and her complete disillusionment with him.

Having suffered such a major setback, yet still fooling around with other women, Jiang Tian's only meeting with Zhao Qiuni in his previous life probably shattered all her hopes.

She probably even considered him a good-for-nothing.

Thinking of this, Jiang Tian smiled bitterly. Yes, he was indeed a good-for-nothing. In his previous life, he was a good-for-nothing. Otherwise, how could he have been so misled by Xiao Bo, a man from a mountain village, to such a miserable state? The destruction of his family and the loss of his home were indescribable.

He didn't even have the right to be abandoned by everyone, because his son wasn't his, his wife wasn't his, and he had no parents or sister.

In the end, he was even beaten to death by that bastard son.

But all of that was in the past. Jiang Tian's eyes flickered, and a cold smile played on his lips.

In his past life, he had never offended Xiao Bo, yet Xiao Bo had schemed against him to such a miserable state. In this life, Jiang Tian would make Xiao Bo experience that feeling firsthand.

Ideally, he would use that bastard son to make Xiao Bo also suffer the taste of betrayal by that scumbag.

Beating Xiao Bo to death by the bastard he had raised, a beast himself, would surely be a sight to behold.

While Jiang Tian was waiting...

On the way to the hotel, Zhao Qiuni followed behind Zhao Wei, driving a Jeep.

Seeing the fawning expression on her brother's face in the rearview mirror, she couldn't help but sneer.

“Brother, if everyone in the compound knew about your fawning manner, they'd all be drooling.” Zhao Qiuni’s beautiful eyes held a hint of curiosity.

To be honest, for her brother to show such a fawning expression, damn it, he was practically a god!

Just who was this person?

“What fawning? I was just having normal conversation,” Zhao Wei muttered.

“Normal conversation, normal phone conversation? Can you please not be so overbearing and bowing?” Zhao Qiuni felt ashamed for her brother as she said this.

A disgrace.

A disgrace to the family!

“I was being respectful,” Zhao Wei said, seemingly oblivious to anything amiss.

A god.

Jiang Tian was a god in his eyes.

“What kind of bewitching potion did Jiang Tian give you to make you like this?”

“Don’t talk about Captain Jiang like that,” Zhao Wei’s tone hardened.

Zhao Qiuni’s mood grew increasingly annoyed.

Before, Zhao Wei was always obsequious to her, but now that Jiang Tian was involved, he even dared to raise his voice at her.

Now that he dared to raise his voice, did that mean he'd dare to hit her in the future? In the future, might he even dare to attack her with a knife because of Jiang Tian?

The thought was terrifying.

Zhao Qiuni's hands were bulging with veins. "Fine, fine, Jiang Tian, ​​is it?"

Although she didn't know what kind of spell Jiang Tian had cast on Zhao Wei,

but…

Zhao Qiuni's gaze shifted slightly.

Her eyes gradually turned cold and sharp.

While her brother Zhao Wei was somewhat useless, he was still her brother, even if he was currently exiled.

Even if he wasn't favored by the family.

But…

Zhao Qiuni absolutely wouldn't allow anyone else to do anything to her useless brother, especially to cast such a spell on him. What could that kind of person do besides empty promises and sweet talk?

Zhao Qiuni decided that she would expose that damned Captain Jiang later.

On the way there, just hearing Zhao Wei constantly mention Captain Jiang had already annoyed her.

Very well.

She was very interested in exposing the true colors of hypocrites.

“Zhao Wei, I never expected this! You were looked down upon in the compound, your family despised you and banished you here, and yet you managed to find some kind of big brother in this backwater. Let me see who he is and what he's capable of.” Zhao Qiuni accelerated the car.

Although Zhao Wei might have been somewhat afraid of his sister, he suddenly perked up, clearly flustered, and said, “Don’t cause trouble.”

Mainly, Zhao Wei was afraid his sister would be beaten to a pulp by Captain Jiang with three batons.

You see, Captain Jiang’s most frequent saying was: when facing any arrogant person, don’t talk back; the first thing to do is three batons.

The first baton, called a leg blow, to prevent escape.

The second baton, called a mouth slap, to prevent screaming.

The third baton, called a decapitation, to prevent struggle.

After the three batons, we can discuss the problem.



Chapter 90 The drug dealers discover it, Zhao Qiuni's fury


Yunhai Kairun Hotel.

Zhao Qiuni led Zhao Wei into the hotel and parked the car.

Hmm?

Zhao Qiuni's beautiful eyes turned cold as she glanced at him, and Zhao Wei shrank back.

"Lead the way," Zhao Qiuni said, carrying her small handbag, gesturing for Zhao Wei to lead the way.

A domineering, very domineering younger sister, but Zhao Wei was already used to it.

Walking towards the hotel, Zhao Qiuni followed behind Zhao Wei, her gaze constantly scanning the surroundings.

At the elevator, five or six men in black, carrying suitcases, suddenly appeared behind them.

Zhao Qiuni didn't think much of it at first and followed Zhao Wei into the elevator, where the five or six men in black followed.

"Seventeenth floor," Zhao Wei thought, recalling Jiang Tian's room number.

The men in black, who were about to press the button, seemed surprised to see the floor number.

"Let me help you, which floor?" Zhao Wei asked.

"Thank you, what a coincidence, we're also on the seventeenth floor." The leader of the men in black glanced at Zhao Wei, his eyes flashing.

After carefully examining Zhao Wei, the man in black smiled slightly and nodded.

Zhao Wei was puzzled; he felt there was something meaningful about the man's smile.

"What's wrong?" Zhao Wei asked, his tone uneasy under the man's gaze.

"Nothing, just a feeling of fate." The leader of the men in black nodded amicably.

Zhao Wei didn't think much of it. After reaching the seventeenth floor, he led Zhao Qiuni and the other woman towards Jiang Tian's room.

However, they didn't see the men in black behind them, their faces grim.

"Damn it, we've been exposed. There are cops." Brother Yu's face was dark.

"Brother Yu, you mean those two?" the henchman asked, somewhat shocked.

Originally, the countless drug dealers, big and small, in Yunhai didn't take the police very seriously. In fact, in the past, the cops posed virtually no threat to them.

Every year, it was like carrying out a mission—finding a few underlings with poor performance, or even those who had made mistakes, and handing them over to boost their quotas, like submitting homework.

But things are different now. In the past few days, rumors have been circulating in Yunhai that Mr. Yun has been arrested. While Mr. Yun wasn't the strongest drug trafficker in Yunhai, he certainly had a reputation, especially with Wei Youdao's downfall.

Mr. Yun's entire network has been completely eradicated.

This has caused considerable shock among the drug trafficking forces in Yunhai.

Now, even the word "cops" sends shivers down their spines.

“Damn it, luckily I got information from a group of police officers a few days ago, and coincidentally, this guy was among them. And coincidentally, the location for our transaction was on the same floor, even in the same direction. Tell me, is that a coincidence?” Brother Yu said shrewdly.

“Brother Yu, what do we do?” the underling asked anxiously.

“What do we do?”

Brother Yu’s face was icy, but when he looked up, his expression instantly transformed into a beaming smile. He said to Zhao Wei and Zhao Qiu Ni, “Friends, wait a minute.”

“What’s wrong?” Zhao Wei turned his head curiously.

Brother Yu led his men closer, his face still beaming. “Nothing, it just feels a bit like a coincidence. You guys live here too?”

“Yes, we’re almost there.” Zhao Wei nodded.

“We’re almost there too.”

Brother Yu glanced at the door out of the corner of his eye. Before Zhao Wei and Zhao Qiu Ni could react, several men had already surrounded them.

Don't blame Zhao Wei and Zhao Qiuni for being careless. The main problem was that several young couples were checking into the hotel across from Yun University when they came up. Such an environment easily makes people lower their guard.

Furthermore, Jiang Tian had already booked a room here, and since he had invited them here, they didn't believe there was any danger.

Besides, the leader of these men, Brother Yu, even gave them a seemingly honest and simple impression when he smiled. By the time they realized what was happening, they were already surrounded.

The crucial point was that Zhao Wei was looking at Jiang Tian's room just two steps away.

Jiang Tian was just two steps away, so what did these people mean?

Zhao Qiuni's beautiful face was now somewhat gloomy. Looking at the people around her, Zhao Qiuni asked dangerously, "Who are you people? What do you want?"

"What do we want? No, what do you want?" Brother Yu turned his head.

*Snap!*

Before the two could react, Brother Yu instantly opened the door with his room key, and the thugs surrounding them shoved them into the room.

Zhao Qiuni, having achieved so much in her later life, was certainly not stupid. She keenly sensed that there might be some misunderstanding.

These people might have misunderstood something.

Before Zhao Qiuni could say anything,

after entering, she suddenly saw clearly.

At this moment, two men who looked like gang leaders were sitting across the table, each with five or six henchmen behind them. There were about ten people in the room.

Of course, most importantly, on the table in front of them were large suitcases.

One suitcase contained money, and the other contained white powder.

Good heavens.

Zhao Qiuni's head was buzzing.

So, she had just arrived in Yunhai, and it seemed she had been misunderstood.

And misunderstood by drug dealers?

"I knew it! That Jiang guy of yours is no good. He deliberately lured you here. He definitely knows what's going on here and is using you as cannon fodder." Zhao Qiuni gritted her teeth, her eyes scanning the surroundings, trying to find a way to escape.

But after looking around, Zhao Qiuni felt a sense of despair.

With a click, the door slammed shut, and all the curtains in the room were drawn. Everyone's eyes were fixed on them. Forget about escaping; the crucial problem now was that they seemed truly doomed.

And the cause of all this, this coincidence, led Zhao Qiuni to only one conclusion: they had all been used by Jiang Tian.

"Perhaps, your Captain Jiang has some connection with these drug dealers." It wasn't that Zhao Qiuni was overthinking things; the reality, in her view, was that this was the only possibility.

Therefore.

This useless brother of hers had not only been completely used, but was even about to be killed.

With Zhao Qiuni's intelligence, she no longer saw any way to break the deadlock.

"Meng Yu, what's going on? Who are these two people?"

The shop owner, sitting at one side of the table, looked up, puzzled.

Two strangers had appeared at the transaction site, and the shop owner's expression turned somewhat strange.



Chapter 91 Arrogant and domineering? Brutality is his true nature.


Meng Yu spoke directly, "Boss, two cops, probably tailing us, they've got their eyes on us. But they're a bit dim-witted; we just let them in without warning. What do we do?"

Blackie's face was full of disdain, not just for the two cops, but also for Old Man Pan. Old Man Pan was just a chicken butcher back in the day.

Later, by chance, he gathered a group of brothers and started out in the market. He was lucky enough to be taken in and sponsored by a foreign boss.

And that's how he made his way up. But because he used to be a chicken butcher, he earned the nickname Old Man Pan.

"Just two cops, and you're scared? A chicken butcher is a chicken butcher, damn it. If your goods weren't so good, I'd be disgusted just looking at you." Black Wolf took the goods and sniffed them.

Good, very pure, but damn it, some stinking chicken butcher has taken control of the supply chain.

Especially fashionable.

Especially her height of over 1.7 meters, and her long, tailored suit.

He vowed to get this woman.

Bang!!!

Pfft!!!

His ambition was to make money; he wasn't particularly obsessed with women.

This hot potato was best left to Black Wolf if he wanted it.

He was also tempted by this beautiful woman.

“Black Wolf runs several underground fighting arenas, and even a deathmatch arena on the border. He has a lot of thugs. He's capable, but we can't. He's insane. We're in it for the money; it's not worth offending Black Wolf and potentially going up against the police for a woman,” Pan Laoji explained.

“Cough cough.” Zhao Wei coughed up blood, lying on the ground.

Bang!!!

Black Wolf kicked Zhao Wei squarely in the face, a vicious blow. Black Wolf spat, a cold smile on his face: “You piece of trash, not even worthy to lick my shoes. Go to the restroom, chop him up, and pack him in a suitcase.”

Hiss!!!

Regardless, Zhao Qiuni was just a young girl who hadn't been out of school for long and had been well-protected. When had she ever witnessed such a horrific scene?

To be precise, Jiang Tian's scanning radar isn't omnipotent; it's limited to scanning individuals with a crime score.

"Zhao Wei should be here by now, right?" Jiang Tian dialed Zhao Wei's number on his phone.

Mu Ya sat on the large bed watching TV. Tom and Jerry was playing, and Mu Ya was watching with great interest, even smiling so much that her eyes narrowed into crescent moons. She seemed so innocent and naive that even Jiang Tian, ​​filled with a filthy heart, felt a pang of guilt.

In other words...

Has something happened to Zhao Wei?

Chapter 92 Blind Shot Shocks the Entire Audience, Are You Human?

He was sharing some with Zhao Wei.

These hotel waiters are amazing!

Especially the ringleader.

Three shots in quick succession, headshots on three people upon entering.

Staring at the thug who had suddenly burst in.

Followed by fury.

Jiang Tian had summoned Zhao Wei to share some of the credit.

With a gunshot.

Boom!!!



Chapter 93 Gladiator Master? One Beating with a Police Baton Forces Him to His Knees and Beg for Mercy


Not only was he a skilled fighter, but he was also a master of underground boxing; such a person was an absolute professional among professionals.

Danger.

Truly dangerous!

Simple, so simple that Black Wolf smiled, and it was a mocking smile.

A burly body, solid muscles, radiating a powerful aura.

No.

This power.

This kind of power?

Crack!!!

*Thud!*

Blood splattered.

*Buzz!*

This scene...

This swift and decisive victory stunned everyone present.

Zhao Qiuni stared, dazed, at Black Wolf, instantly defeated. If it weren't for the stark contrast with Zhao Wei, it wouldn't have been so bad.

Zhao Wei was completely powerless to fight back; in the blink of an eye, all his fighting ability was neutralized, and he was dragged around like a dead dog.

The black-skinned wolf who had done all this was also instantly defeated by the man who had just appeared.

This is why Zhao Qiuni was stunned and dizzy.

There's a saying, "Hero saves the beauty," a cliché, but it's classic precisely because it works.

But it wasn't over yet.

The next moment.

Jiang Tian, ​​seemingly out of nowhere, held a police baton.

It's true, no one saw where Jiang Tian pulled it from, the instant it appeared.

Thump!!!

Crack!!!

One blow, and the baton struck the black-skinned wolf's shin.

One blow, and the leg was broken.

Jiang Tian's face was icy cold. Zhao Wei's miserable state filled Jiang Tian's eyes with a dangerous glint and terrifying killing intent.

"Ah...you madman, you madman, stop hitting me, stop hitting me..."

Black Wolf curled up on the ground, his entire body trembling uncontrollably. Finally, a series of agonizing screams echoed throughout the hotel corridor.

However, the physical pain was far less devastating than the psychological blow.

He was instantly killed.

No, it should be said, his soul was shattered with a single blow.

"Boss."

"Boss was killed instantly?"

"Impossible."

The henchmen in the room were dumbfounded by what they saw.

Especially seeing their once arrogant leader now lying on the ground like a dead dog.

The crucial point is, the most brutal part wasn't over yet. Jiang Tian walked up, stomped on Black Wolf's blood-soaked face, gripped his baton with both hands, and, as if hitting a golf ball, smashed it directly into Black Wolf's mouth.

*Splat!*

Blood spurted out.

"Second strike, slap the mouth."



Chapter 94 caused a sensation throughout the audience. Was he a ghost?


Jiang Tian's lips curled into a sneer as he looked at the black wolf convulsing on the ground. He had to admit, Jiang Tian had exercised restraint; he hadn't killed it.

He could tell at a glance, and the scanning radar could also tell, that this black wolf was practically one of the two with the highest sin count among them.

It even had a sin value of at least three thousand.

That sin value was terrifying; even Mr. Yun wasn't that high.

If he killed it outright, how would he interrogate the criminal's criminal businesses and incriminating evidence?

Of course, Jiang Tian hadn't held back. The black wolf wouldn't be able to get up anytime soon.

"Woo-woo-woo!!!" The black wolf convulsed violently on the ground, only able to whimper.

Its eyes were already bloodshot, filled with blood vessels, making it look ferocious and terrifying.

"Hero."

At this moment, Zhao Qiuni, watching this scene, felt no fear; instead, her eyes shone brightly as she cried out to Jiang Tian.

A hero, this is the kind of hero we should admire.

As for Zhao Wei, that good-for-nothing captain, what kind of person is he?

If it weren't for this hero, Zhao Qiuni didn't even dare to imagine what the consequences would have been.

Even revealing her identity and background wouldn't have mattered, because after beating Zhao Wei so badly, if it came out, they wouldn't survive.

Therefore, even if Zhao Qiuni revealed her identity and powerful background, the other side, for the sake of secrecy, would probably not let her leave alive.

Jiang Tian's heroic rescue deeply moved Zhao Qiuni.

In Zhao Qiuni's view, the other side should have untied her immediately upon seeing her bound, but to her surprise, Jiang Tian glanced at her and then looked at Zhao Wei.

Thump thump thump!!!

One step, two steps...

Jiang Tian approached Zhao Wei with measured steps. At this moment, Zhao Wei's face was no longer clearly visible; to put it bluntly, the other side was aiming straight for his face.

So, Zhao Wei was truly beaten so badly that even his mother wouldn't recognize him.

His face was covered in blood, but thankfully, after struggling a couple of times on the ground, he managed to prop himself up.

"Are you alright?" Jiang Tian squatted down to examine him, his brows furrowing deeper.

The injuries on Zhao Wei's face were quite frightening; his nose was completely flattened, and there were likely fractures throughout his face.

It was fortunate that Jiang Tian arrived quickly. If he had been a few steps later, and the other party had been so ruthless as to drag Zhao Wei into the bathroom, who knows what they would have done.

But based on Jiang Tian's experience, Zhao Wei would likely have been in serious trouble.

These drug dealers were too ruthless.

"I..." Zhao Wei tried to say something, but he only managed a hissing sound when he opened his mouth because of the intense pain.

"Alright, don't talk now. Let me see what's going on with these people." Jiang Tian patted Zhao Wei's shoulder.

Zhao Qiuni, tied up to the side, felt somewhat dizzy.

This is a true hero.

Because only a true hero could see her beauty and disregard it, his first concern being for the injured Zhao Wei.

Therefore, if someone were to comfort themselves first and ignore the wounded, Zhao Qiuni would look down on them.

Full marks.

"This is the kind of hero we should admire!"

"Thank you, hero," Zhao Qiuni said excitedly.

Rip!!!

Jiang Tian, ​​seeing the injuries on Zhao Wei's face, told him to lie down and rest. He then went to Zhao Qiuni and tore off the duct tape binding her.

But before Jiang Tian could speak,

Bang!!!

A gunshot rang out.

As Zhao Qiuni's pupils contracted, she clearly saw a pistol. It was Meng Yu, the one who had captured them. He had been standing behind the door, and when Jiang Tian lowered his guard, he suddenly drew his gun.

"Die, die!" Brother Yu's eyes were bloodshot.

"Are you crazy?" Old Pan's scalp tingled.

Using a gun here was utterly insane. That's why Jiang Tian fired three shots as soon as he entered, completely stunning them.

You have to understand, this is right across from Yun University!

This is a huge incident. They'll likely be pushed out immediately; those people will completely abandon them.

They'll be used as expendable pawns to vent their anger.

Jiang Tian can fire, but Pan Laoji doesn't dare let his men fire, especially since Jiang Tian immediately smashed Black Wolf's frame after entering.

He didn't even notice them. At this moment, Pan Laoji even breathed a sigh of relief.
