He was already 80% certain that this was revenge. This terrifying guy was probably seeking revenge on Black Wolf, after all, he killed Black Wolf as soon as he entered.

Thinking this way, Pan Laoji watched the show with a hint of schadenfreude.

"Boss Pan, if we don't act, we'll be the ones who die. This guy saw our transaction," Brother Yu reminded him.

Boss Pan was startled.

That's right!

He almost forgot about that.

Meng Yu saw Boss Pan waver, and his face lit up with joy.

Without hesitation, he pulled the trigger directly at Jiang Tian.

"I admit you're strong, but your biggest mistake was underestimating us. Carelessness is what cost you your life." After firing, Meng Yu had already sentenced Jiang Tian to death.

Shot in the back, with no time to react—Meng Yu refused to believe he wouldn't die.

You're strong in combat, you're a good shot, but…

At that moment, Jiang Tian was untying Zhao Qiu Ni. By the time Zhao Qiu Ni saw it, it was too late.

"Danger…" Zhao Qiu Ni's pupils contracted rapidly, her heart pounding.

But before she could react, the next moment…

Bang!!!

Jiang Tian, ​​with his back to Meng Yu, fired a shot directly into his back from under his arm.

The movement was swift, accurate, and ruthless.

Clang!!!

The bullets collided.

What does it mean to hit every target without fail?

It means that as long as the bullet is within firing range.

Jiang Tian can shoot however he wants, relying solely on his instincts to hit any target he deems possible.

Even after a bullet has already been fired.

A hexagonal warrior, tell me, how do you deal with that?

"Damn it..." Meng Yu uttered a curse, as if he'd seen a ghost.

The next moment.

Thud!!!

A bullet instantly pierced Meng Yu's forehead where he had fired.

Blood spurted out.

"When?"

This was the blank look in Meng Yu's eyes before he died.

Yes, when? When was this bullet fired?



Chapter 95 Identity Exposed, Stunned, It Was You!


Whoosh!!

Everyone at the scene erupted in shock.

"Holy crap."

"Brother Yu is dead?"

"What just happened?"

"Was he hit by a bullet? How did Brother Yu die?"

"A ghost? Is this a ghost?"

"Holy crap, it wasn't just one bullet! This guy is a top-tier expert! This guy is definitely a world-class assassin! Those were two bullets!"

It exploded.

The scene completely erupted.

At this moment, Jiang Tian calmly unwrapped all the bandages from Zhao Qiuni's body.

Then he stood up, raised his pistol, blew on the still-smoking muzzle, and scanned everyone with his eyes.

This...this...this...my god, even movies wouldn't dare to film something like this! Zhao Qiuni's stunned expression, so innocent and naive, made her seem like someone who had never seen the world before.

She swore that if any movie dared to portray this, she would find the director and slap him a few times. Wasn't that insulting her intelligence? But now, it was happening right before her eyes, and this person had even saved her.

Then, Zhao Qiuni's gaze turned to Zhao Wei. Not daring to delay, she hurried to his side. Her attitude had softened somewhat, but she still asked stiffly, "You're not dead, are you? Can you get up?"

Zhao Wei smiled bitterly, seemingly already used to his sister's attitude.

"Did you see that?" Zhao Qiuni asked.

"See what? Ouch!!!"

As soon as he spoke, Zhao Wei gasped. Several of his teeth had been broken, making his speech slurred, and his mouth was swollen like a balloon, covered in blood blisters.

"If it weren't for this hero who suddenly appeared today, we would be dead," Zhao Qiuni warned, her brows furrowed.

She thought to herself, she'd made it so obvious, yet this useless brother still hadn't grasped it.

"Yes, yes, he really is a hero," Zhao Wei nodded.

Jiang Tian entered with overwhelming power, dominating the area. A dozen drug dealers, too intimidated to even make a move, stood around him, some even backing away.

Their eyes revealed utter shock.

Zhao Qiuni, her face filled with exasperated anger, said, "Haven't you realized what I'm saying?"

"What?" Zhao Wei looked completely bewildered.

"I mean, can't you just say what you mean directly?"

"No wonder he's useless," Zhao Qiuni sighed inwardly, reminding him, "Can't you see? Tonight's coincidences are all part of your Captain Jiang's scheme."

"That's right, wow, how come I didn't think of that? Hahaha, Captain Jiang is truly amazing, he even foresaw this." Zhao Wei laughed with delight, "Little sister, you're so smart! Captain Jiang is practically my god!"

*Pfft!!!*

Zhao Qiuni nearly spat out blood, gritting her teeth as she said, "Zhao Wei, what are you thinking, you pig-brain? Your Captain Jiang clearly framed us, he just wanted to kill you! You praised him, I even suspect that your Captain Jiang is connected to these drug dealers, otherwise how could he have caught us so accurately? He deserves to die! But that bastard probably didn't expect we'd be saved, did he? When we get back, we'll have to repay that so-called Captain Jiang for his treachery."

"What are you talking about?" Zhao Wei was completely dumbfounded.

“Don’t you understand? Look at what kind of person can be admired. Your so-called Captain Jiang is definitely a corrupt cop,” Zhao Qiuni nodded affirmatively.

“Him?” Zhao Wei pointed at Jiang Tian.

“Yes, that’s right.” Zhao Qiuni nodded firmly.

Then, her eyes shone brightly as she looked at Jiang Tian, ​​her face full of tenderness. Zhao Wei could swear he had never seen his sister so gentle in his life.

In the silence, Jiang Tian slowly walked to the coffee table and looked at the goods on it.

A whole suitcase full of white powder, sealed in transparent bags.

Jiang Tian estimated at a glance that it weighed around fifty kilograms.

There were also several other suitcases, all open and filled with cash.

Millions?

Jiang Tian turned his head and saw Meng Yu lying dead on the ground, still holding a large suitcase.

"My friend, every grievance has its source, every debt its debtor. Your feud with Black Wolf has nothing to do with me." Old Pan just wanted to run his business peacefully.

"His name is Black Wolf?"

Jiang Tian pointed to Black Wolf lying on the ground like a dead dog. He had to admit, the nickname was quite fitting. Black Wolf had jet-black skin, a muscular physique, and a wolf-like, aggressive personality.

"Yes, he runs several underground arenas in Yunhai. He's very powerful. It was expected that he'd offend a master like you. Your feud has nothing to do with me. I'm leaving now." Old Pan tried to leave.

"Want to leave?" Jiang Tian almost laughed.

"What, you want me to stay?" Old Pan took a step back.

His henchmen beside him all pulled out pistols from their pockets.

Arsenal was rampant.

Oh no.

It should be said, this was the unique romance of Yunhai.

Because of its connection to the chaotic neighboring country, and because the mountain passes are winding and interconnected by various paths and routes,

crime, especially drugs—the root of all evil—is inseparable from gambling, prostitution, drugs, and the criminal forces involved in illegal firearms possession.

Jiang Tian was somewhat surprised that they could all produce guns, but it was also within his expectations.

“Friend, having more friends means having more options. I just want to run my own business and don’t want to get involved in any disputes or chaos. In fact, if you’re willing, I can even leave you the goods from my deal with Black Wolf,” Pan Laoji said, pointing to the drugs on the table.

“Not enough,” Jiang Tian shook his head.

Pan Laoji’s face darkened. “What else do you want? Money? Fine, I’ll let you have it today, even if it’s just to avoid trouble.”

Pan Laoji had witnessed Jiang Tian’s behavior and found it incredibly strange. His methods were terrifying. Although he had eight or nine henchmen, most of whom were armed, it didn’t give him any sense of security.

To avoid trouble, he would have to pay.

He hadn't expected the other party to be so greedy; Old Pan inwardly cried out that he'd suffered a huge loss.

"Provide information about the person behind the drugs who supplied you," Jiang Tian said with a grin.

"Shoot him, kill him."

Old Pan's hair practically stood on end instantly.

He roared the order at his henchmen.

Old Pan's men seemed to have been holding back for a long time.

As the saying goes, even the best fists can't beat four hands; even the best kung fu master is afraid of a kitchen knife.

No matter how good your kung fu is, can it be faster than a gun?

Fine, even if you're faster than a gun, you can't be faster than so many guns.

The room wasn't big to begin with, and with so many people packed in, no matter how far the distance, it couldn't match the speed of Jiang Tian's single leap.

At this moment, Jiang Tian practically became a blur.

He kicked a drug dealer flying, the next instant grabbing a henchman's throat and snapping his neck with a slight squeeze.

With another powerful kick, the already dead drug dealer flew into the crowd, smashing into pieces.

Thump thump thump!!!

Jiang Tian took two steps forward, his fingers instantly piercing one man's eyes, his other kick landing squarely on another drug dealer's groin.

Before a scream could even escape, Jiang Tian was already in front of a drug dealer about to fire, a slight squeeze, crack, his arm snapped.

The pistol fell to the ground, and Jiang Tian caught it with his other hand.

Bang bang bang!!!

After a few shots, the room fell completely silent.

On the ground lay scattered corpses and people writhing and screaming.

Of all the drug dealers, only Pan Laoji remained standing.

Jiang Tian stepped through the blood and approached Pan Laoji: "Excuse me, I'm a policeman."

Zhao Qiuni: "Cool!!!"

"Zhao Wei, see? He's a policeman too! This is the kind of person you should admire, not those corrupt cops who harm people." Zhao Qiuni was even more excited.

"A policeman?" Pan Laoji's pistol clattered to the ground, his face filled with astonishment.

"Let me formally introduce myself, I'm from the First Squad, First Brigade of the Yunhai Narcotics Control Detachment." Jiang Tian grinned.

"What a coincidence, Zhao Wei, you seem to be from the Narcotics Control Detachment too, right? Yes, I remember you said you were from the First Brigade. This is your First Brigade, what a coincidence." Zhao Qiuni's eyes lit up even more.

Zhao Wei: "???"

"My name is Jiang Tian. This is my identification. I'm officially informing you that you are under arrest."

*Click!*

Jiang Tian took out handcuffs, handcuffed Pan Laoji, and was about to say something when

a *splash* sounded.

The next moment, a shocked, bewildered, incredulous, and endlessly complex voice rang out: "You... Zhao Wei's Captain Jiang, is actually... you?... *splash*!!!"

Jiang Tian turned his head...



Chapter 96 How did the proud queen become a yandere?


"Officer Jiang Tian is so handsome."

Zhao Qiuni's eyes lit up, but the name seemed strangely familiar.

She thought about it carefully.

After pondering for a moment.

"Jiang Tian?"

"Captain Jiang?"

"The First Brigade of the Municipal Narcotics Control Detachment?"

Zhao Qiuni scratched the back of her head. Was she imagining things?

Oh no, wait... Jiang Tian?

Pfft!!!

"Cough cough..."

Realizing herself, Zhao Qiuni coughed twice frantically.

She stared dumbfounded at Jiang Tian, ​​who was handcuffing Pan Laoji. At this moment, Jiang Tian stood in front of Pan Laoji. At Jiang Tian's feet were pools of blood, and corpses or severely injured people lying haphazardly.

Pan Laoji stared blankly at Jiang Tian's identification, lost in endless daze.

In a daze, this name gave Pan Laoji a sense of familiarity.

Zhao Qiuni's head was buzzing.

"How could this be? How is this possible? How could this person be Captain Jiang?" Zhao Qiuni's face gradually stiffened.

From the very first impression, even without meeting him, Zhao Qiuni harbored no goodwill towards Jiang Tian, ​​even subconsciously feeling a sense of repulsion.

From the moment she saw her brother, Zhao Wei, fawning over a mere squad leader like a lapdog, Zhao Qiuni felt a deep disappointment and believed it was all just empty promises from the squad leader.

After arriving at the agreed-upon location,

she encountered ruthless drug dealers. Not only that, she even managed to arrest them. This further solidified Zhao Qiuni's belief that Jiang Tian was not only a smooth talker but also a corrupt cop who had set a trap for her naive and useless brother.

And now, you tell me…

This man before her, standing there, a police baton dripping blood slung over his shoulder, his feet planted in a pool of blood.

Just moments ago, in less than a minute, he had taken down a hero who had dealt with over ten drug dealers.

He was actually Jiang Tian?

The same Jiang, the captain?

This can't be real, right?

The squad leader my good-for-nothing brother followed was terrifyingly powerful?

And, had I misunderstood my brother's squad leader?

No, no, no, it must just be a coincidence of names.

Zhao Qiuni refused to believe the truth. But the next moment, she handcuffed Pan Laoji, dealt with the dozen or so drug dealers, and left them dead or wounded, like a hero strolling through a garden.

Step by step, he trod on the blood-soaked ground.

A few traces of blood remained on Jiang Tian's face, and he exuded a terrifying aura.

But when he stood before Zhao Qiuni, a gentle smile appeared on Jiang Tian's face. Just that one smile, and Zhao Qiuni trembled, her eyes completely losing focus.

Only one thought occupied her mind: how could there be such an outstanding man in the world? Why was his smile so beautiful?

"You're Zhao Wei's sister, right?" Jiang Tian asked knowingly.

"Yes, yes." Zhao Qiuni didn't quite hear what was said, but kept nodding like a chicken pecking at rice, her eyes fixed on Jiang Tian, ​​as if trying to imprint this moment of his image deeply into her mind.

Jiang Tian sighed inwardly, his face full of apology. "I'm so sorry for this accident. Actually, I already found these drug dealers. I could have handled it myself, but I thought Zhao Wei was still a little short of official promotion, so I brought him over. I was planning to let him share some of the credit, but unexpectedly, you were caught ahead of time. This is my fault. Are you alright?"

To be honest, Jiang Tian's eyelids twitched when he saw Zhao Wei's injuries.

Zhao Wei was alright.

Actually, the first thing Jiang Tian was concerned about when he came in was Zhao Qiu Ni. Damn it, if anything happened to Zhao Wei, it would be a huge problem.

If something happened to Zhao Qiu Ni, it would be the end of the world.

In fact, just imagining the scene of Zhao Qiu Ni being hurt made Jiang Tian's scalp tingle and his whole body shudder.

While it wouldn't be all his efforts ruined, Yunhai would definitely be devastated.

Thankfully, thankfully, Zhao Qiu Ni was alright.

"No, no, no, you're not wrong. It was our mistake, Captain Jiang. Please don't say that. Besides, you saved us. We're so grateful, Captain Jiang, thank you so much!"

Zhao Qiuni spoke with a face full of gratitude. Her 1.7-meter height caused her to bend slightly, allowing Jiang Tian to catch a glimpse of a breathtakingly beautiful woman. Jiang Tian gasped.

However, Jiang Tian looked helplessly at his hand. *You can talk, but can you at least let go of my hand?*

To be honest, Jiang Tian was quite surprised. In his previous life, Zhao Qiuni was someone he could only look up to, someone unattainable.

In his one and only meeting with her, her aura instilled in him a deep sense of inferiority, an inferiority so profound he couldn't even lift his head. It was as if she was born to be high above, while he was just a nobody in the mud.

Later, it turned out she not only had a terrifying background, but more importantly, she was known as the Investment Queen of Financial Street.

Back then, Jiang Tian could only admire her from afar through news reports and newspapers, and even felt that he might truly be born a good-for-nothing.

And yet, this very woman...

was now holding his hand with such adoration and affection, her eyes filled with worship. Jiang Tian was undeniably thrilled.

Zhao Wei, lying on the ground, was completely dumbfounded.

He knew his sister well.

He swore he had never seen his sister with such an expression in his entire life; her eyes were practically bulging out of their sockets in shock.

This sister had been arrogant since childhood, disdainful of everything, and looking down on everyone. He, her brother, was a victim of this.

She constantly called people "good-for-nothing" and "trash," her arrogance was outrageous. Even when a rich kid pursued her, Zhao Qiu Ni turned around and yelled, "What kind of trash are you? You think you're worthy of pursuing me?"

And now, what did he see? His proud younger sister was bowing down, her face filled with adoration.

"No, it's only right! Hahaha, Zhao Qiuni, you've finally met your match!" Zhao Wei snapped out of his daze, his mind reeling, and then he felt as if the pain from his injuries had vanished, and he wanted to burst out laughing.

The sister who used to call him a good-for-nothing her whole life, actually got herself into this mess?

"I've already called an ambulance." Jiang Tian smiled. Seeing Zhao Qiuni's condition, he felt both pleased and relieved; her mental state was definitely fine.

Zhao Wei, meanwhile, struggled to lean against the wall, his mental state frightening, but he wasn't in any life-threatening danger, just needed time to recover.

"Why are you so amazing?" Zhao Qiuni's eyes were filled with admiration and curiosity.

"I like to exercise sometimes," Jiang Tian replied casually.

Mainly, it felt great at first, but gradually, Jiang Tian sensed something was wrong. Zhao Qiuni's gaze was becoming increasingly frightening, like she wanted to devour him.

Something's not right, something's not right...

Jiang Tian subtly withdrew his hand. For some reason, he felt Zhao Qiuni's eyes held a lingering reluctance, even a hint of possessiveness.

Jiang Tian was almost dumbfounded. What is she doing? What is she doing?

Where's your aloof investment queen?

Where's your arrogance?

How did you turn into a yandere?

The yandere investment queen, Zhao Qiuni—thinking of this, Jiang Tian's scalp tingled; he dared not even contemplate the terrifying scene.

And just then...

Old Pan suddenly looked up, and in a daze, he remembered—he suddenly remembered that name.

"Jiang Tian, ​​you're the Jiang Tian who arrested Mr. Yun?" Old Pan exclaimed.

"Hmm?"

Jiang Tian's expression changed slightly, his face darkening as he looked at Old Pan: "How did you know?"

Although Jiang Tian arresting Mr. Yun wasn't a huge secret, it was definitely kept under wraps. After all, drug case files have a level of confidentiality; they aren't released to the public, and sometimes, even within their own department, it's not common knowledge.

How could a drug dealer know so much, even his name?

Jiang Tian's face was grim. Mainly, he wasn't afraid, and even his father, as the bureau chief, wasn't afraid, but what about Jiang Tian's mother and sister? He couldn't be on guard all the time.

Even a small-time drug dealer like Pan Laoji knew his name; what about the other big drug lords?



Chapter 97 Everyone stay still, wait, I'm sorry.


"So it was you. I finally understand. Jiang Tian, ​​you've got Mr. Yun arrested. It's not surprising he fell into your hands," Pan Laoji said with a bitter smile.

Bang!!!

But before he could finish speaking, Jiang Tian's baton slammed into his leg.

"Hiss!!!"

Pan Laoji fell to the ground, gasping for breath. The blow had sent shivers down his spine.

"I asked how you knew, what the hell are you dawdling about?" Jiang Tian approached.

He needed to find out. Was it an internal leak, or did these drug dealers investigate secretly? How many people knew, and who spread the word?

"Don't hit me, don't hit me! Not just me, many drug dealers in Yunhai know your name. Someone sent us a text message, but I don't know who it was," Pan Laoji hurriedly replied.

Jiang Tian's expression changed. He slowly lowered the baton.

Someone was targeting him.

That's right.

Jiang Tian's mind raced, but no matter who it was, he wanted them dead.

Especially his younger sister.

Jiang Tian's mother worked at the Finance Bureau not far away; a little caution wouldn't be a problem. The real issue was his sister.

She was far too vulnerable.

Jiang Tian didn't have many relatives. For him, his closest family consisted of his parents and his sister, Jiang Xiaoyu. He had long since seen through the true nature of other relatives and friends in his previous life.

When the Jiang family fell, during Jiang Tian's most difficult time, those people only cared about kicking him while he was down, distancing themselves from his father, and even slandering him behind his back, trying to prove they had no connection to him.

They completely forgot how much his father had helped them over the years.

Jiang Tian had never intended to have any contact with his extended family.

Now, the hardest thing for Jiang Tian was his mother and sister. He never imagined that his name would be known throughout the entire drug cartel.

Who was it?

Jiang Tian's gaze was icy. His extraordinary memory, combined with his genius forensic skills, caused his mind to replay events incessantly.

*Snap! Snap!*

Jiang Tian held a lighter, wanting to light one. Normally, he rarely smoked, but when faced with mental challenges or difficulties, he would light one.

However, the open window and the blowing curtains caused the lighter in Jiang Tian's hand to go out repeatedly, which made him somewhat irritated.

In truth, he never imagined his information would be leaked so widely. Even if it had, Jiang Tian assumed it would be in the hands of certain major drug lords.

Or perhaps a few drug lords connected to the case.

In such a situation, although dangerous, there was still room for maneuver. Having made such a significant impact and achieved such a terrifying feat, even the best secrecy couldn't prevent some information from leaking out.

But Jiang Tian hadn't expected it to happen so suddenly, and to be directly disseminated to so many drug dealers.

This directly exposed Jiang Tian to the eyes of all the drug dealers.

Thinking this, Jiang Tian's eyes burned with rage. Excellent, the person behind him had successfully caught his attention.

However, just as Jiang Tian was pondering...

The next moment, a dark shadow loomed over, and *snap snap snap*...

The sound of a button being torn came from inside. Jiang Tian looked up and suddenly realized that Zhao Qiuni was standing between him and the window, having torn open her coat to shield him from the wind.

The breeze also ruffled Zhao Qiuni's bangs, highlighting her beautiful face, which was full of charm and possessed an understated yet noble air.

Snap!

The lighter finally lit, the flickering flame illuminating Jiang Tian's grim face. At his feet lay a jumble of corpses.

A beautiful woman, corpses, and the one who had started the smoking incident—this scene... Snap... At the doorway, two girls huddled together, trembling. One of them watched in shock.

From this angle, she could capture Jiang Tian's dark profile, the way he had opened his coat to shield her from the wind, and the corpses lying at his feet.

He couldn't help but instinctively take a picture of this scene.

Thump thump thump!

Just then.

At the end of the corridor.

A series of hurried, rapid footsteps sounded. Moments later, several armed police officers with riot shields rushed up to establish a perimeter.

A moment later.

A large group of people surged up the stairs.

Song Liguo quickly peeked out from the stairwell, glanced down the corridor, and immediately saw two young girls slumped on the floor. Song Liguo's expression instantly turned serious.

Then, he silently waved to Captain Guo.

The next moment, Captain Guo, leading more than ten men, stealthily made their way into the room, all carrying firearms and moving cautiously.

Song Liguo's eyes were bloodshot, but he couldn't hide the dazed look and worry within them.

Indeed, his eyes were bloodshot, even his eyelids were swollen—he had been working for far too long.

It's fair to say that, due to the all-night investigation, as the person in charge and the head of Yunhai's anti-drug squad, the final responsibility fell to him.

He had to manage people below, control the details of the case, and oversee the compilation and organization of case summaries above.

In other words, among those above and below him, he, in this middling position, was actually the most exhausted in this case.

But it was also what excited Song Liguo the most.

He and Gao Xiaolong had already transferred all the documents and case files to the Ministry of Public Security at the police station, and after confirming there were no problems, they were preparing to leave work.

But then, he received a distress message on his phone from Zhao Wei.

Although it was just a single "1," among all the anti-drug team members in the city bureau, sending a message with "1" meant a distress call and danger.

This was a one-click SOS.

A long press on the phone button would automatically send the location to the police station. Of course, there had been accidental triggers in the past, but after some modifications, accidental triggers were largely resolved.

At the same time, it also ensures that officers can immediately contact the city police department for help if they encounter danger.

That's why this problem exists—because behind every system lies a painful lesson.

After realizing something was wrong, Zhao Wei secretly sent an emergency distress message to the police department, who were also able to locate his position.

In other words, even if Jiang Tian hadn't arrived in time, reinforcements from the Municipal Public Security Bureau would have arrived shortly.

Song Liguo, who hadn't rested or slept for over thirty hours, was stunned when he suddenly learned that Zhao Wei was in danger and had sent a distress message via his phone.

He immediately mobilized a large number of personnel, who arrived almost instantly.

He brought along Captain Guo and others who were still working overtime at the Municipal Public Security Bureau, and also called for backup from nearby armed police. It wasn't that Song Liguo was overreacting; there was precedent for this.

"Something's definitely up." Song Liguo's heart skipped a beat.

Two young girls sat slumped on the floor by the door, staring at the room in fear. What had happened inside?

Compared to this situation, Song Liguo actually hoped it was an accident.

But now it seemed...

Song Liguo's heart sank. He could only pray that the young man, Zhao Wei, was alright.

"Get ready, follow me in." Captain Guo took a deep breath, then nodded, hoping that Zhao Wei was safe as well.

Because in the past, whenever this function was used, by the time their rescue team arrived, the person calling for help had often already lost all life, tortured to death. Past experiences instilled a deep fear in them every time they received such a distress signal.

“Be careful,” Song Liguo instructed.

Captain Guo, his face grim, raised his pistol and gestured to the armed police and the city's anti-drug officers behind him before charging in.

“Don’t move! Everyone, don’t move! Drop your weapons!”

“Don’t move!”

“Wait, damn it!”

“Damn it, damn it….”



Chapter 98 Song Zhi is here, Song Zhi has gone mad, are you going to defy the heavens?


Elegance met the eye, a bloody elegance.

And, the overwhelming stench of blood assaulted the senses.

Closest to the entrance lay three corpses, the ones Jiang Tian had blown off their heads the moment he entered.

These three corpses were unmistakably still warm, blood still oozing from their wounds.

A handful of people were still standing; the remaining dozen or so lay scattered on the ground, the air thick with the horrific stench of blood. Anyone with a weaker constitution would have their resolve shattered at the sight.

As for those with a stronger mindset...

Like the members of the Narcotics Control Unit. They had already experienced a bloody battle before, the battle in Tucha that had significantly heightened their mental fortitude.

They thought they had seen so much, that their mental fortitude was strong, but seeing this scene still made their stomachs churn, a urge to vomit rising within them.

This is actually an animal instinct. Upon witnessing the death of a fellow human, the instinctive reaction is fear, trembling, and even convulsions, leading to nausea and vomiting.

What's particularly shocking is the scene itself.

Jiang Tian is standing on the head of the black wolf.

In his past life, Jiang Tian had experienced the cruelty of drug dealers. In one operation, only a few members of his entire team survived. His surrender as a survivor was actually a result of his inability to bear the successive blows from drug dealers and his family.

But after being reborn, a mere moment of carelessness nearly cost Zhao Wei his life, and also implicated Zhao Wei's sister, Zhao Qiuni.

Thinking of this, Jiang Tian, ​​the instigator, gritted his teeth in hatred.

"What are you doing?"

Song Liguo, standing behind, saw the group blocking the doorway, refusing to go in, and all cursing furiously. He was instantly enraged.

Don't they know their teammates are in danger? Don't they know how they train?

"What are you doing standing at the door? What if there's a criminal inside? Are you just giving them live targets?"

"No, Chief Song..." Captain Guo stammered.

"No what? Old Guo, you're a veteran narcotics officer, how can you make such a basic mistake?" Song Liguo angrily reprimanded.

He then shoved Captain Guo aside, and in his rage, kicked away the men blocking the door.

Mainly, this kind of action was far too dangerous during a mission.

Then, Song Liguo's vision finally cleared.

He could finally see clearly.

"Hiss...whoosh!!!"

Jiang Tian exhaled a smoke ring, easing his anxiety.

Turning his head, he saw Song Liguo standing at the main entrance, a smile appearing on his face. "Chief Song, you're here."

"You, you guys?" Chief Song's pupils contracted as he stared at the scene before him.

He pointed at Jiang Tian in disbelief.

The first thing that caught his eye was Zhao Qiuni, wearing a yellow coat, open, standing by the window seemingly shielding Jiang Tian from the wind.

Jiang Tian was even blowing smoke rings.

The moment Song Liguo entered, he was completely dumbfounded.

"Okay, okay, so you guys are doing this, huh?

You're even turning this into an idol drama, aren't you?"

"Chief Song, you've come at the right time." Jiang Tian smiled, saving him the trouble of making a phone call.

Of course, Jiang Tian hadn't actually placed much importance on this operation; he'd just stumbled upon a group of criminals and planned to take them out.

Who knew this group of criminals was anything but simple?

This group was utterly ruthless.

Song Liguo shifted his gaze, suddenly remembering his purpose for being there, but upon turning back to see the scene on the ground, he cursed, "Damn it, Jiang Tian, ​​what are you doing?"

The corpses lying haphazardly on the ground were mostly lifeless, meaning most were dead.

Especially one corpse, its face directly facing him, its dead eyes almost bulging out, staring at him, sending chills down his spine.

Song Liguo suddenly realized, damn it, three people already constituted a major criminal case.

A major criminal case that even the provincial department would intervene in.

How many people were there in front of him?

"You did this?" Song Liguo exclaimed.

"Yes, just doing it on the spur of the moment," Jiang Tian said casually, exhaling a puff of smoke and waving his hand.

No, Song Liguo's eyes were bloodshot. So many people had died, and what was with this nonchalant attitude?

Killing so many people wasn't like killing chickens!

"Are you crazy?" Song Liguo roared, his scalp tingling.

But the next moment, Jiang Tian pointed to a box of cocaine on the table.

Song Liguo's roar involuntarily turned into a rooster's crow, and he even forgot to breathe in his shock, his voice hoarse and high-pitched.

"Oh my god!!!"



Chapter 99 Got a big fish! This time it's going to be awesome!


Song Liguo rushed to the coffee table, looked at the drugs on it, took a small amount, smelled it on his fingertip, and then rubbed it between his fingers.

Song Liguo's pupils contracted sharply.

He then turned sharply to Jiang Tian, ​​his expression one of shock, and asked, "What happened?"

"I came to the Kairun Hotel today, and coincidentally, Zhao Wei hadn't eaten, so I called him over. Who knew that this kid, Zhao Wei, had just arrived and been kidnapped by drug dealers? I rushed in and just happened to witness the drug deal," Jiang Tian explained.

On one hand, Jiang Tian thought Zhao Wei had a tough time and desperately needed a small achievement to get him promoted.

Don't underestimate the power of early promotion.

Because Zhao Wei joined the police force after Jiang Tian; in other words, he hadn't even been in the force for a month.

Furthermore, after leaving the force, he didn't even have independent case-handling authority, let alone the right to possess firearms.

Jiang Tian, ​​due to special circumstances and numerous meritorious deeds, had already been promoted to a regular officer position. Furthermore, as a narcotics officer, he was authorized to carry firearms.

However, according to strict firearms regulations, narcotics officers are only authorized to carry firearms under normal circumstances due to danger and special circumstances. Even the bureau chief, Jiang Peng, was not authorized to carry firearms.

Of course, the most important point is that his early promotion, besides allowing him to carry firearms, was a tremendous boost to Zhao Wei's future.

It made his resume extremely impressive.

Because of this, Jiang Tian also shared some credit with Zhao Wei.

Mainly because Jiang Tian had dealt with all the drug dealers, he couldn't really share much.

Jiang Tian's explanation instantly moved Zhao Wei to tears.

Indeed, Zhao Wei knew Jiang Tian all too well. First, the room number was next to the drug dealers' transaction location; in his view, this was definitely Jiang Tian sharing the credit with him.

That's why he invited him to dinner.

After arriving here, not only had he not been of any help, but Jiang Tian not only rushed in to save him, but also shared a portion of the credit for discovering the drug dealers with him.

Such a brother—if loyalty could be measured, Zhao Wei's loyalty would be off the charts.

"What's wrong?" Jiang Tian asked, puzzled.

Because at this moment, Song Liguo's gaze was strange.

He looked him up and down.

His eyes were fixed on Jiang Tian.

After a moment, Song Liguo gave him a thumbs up: "I call you the nemesis of drug dealers. Just casually coming to a hotel, and you manage to stumble upon a drug deal."

Song Liguo's scalp tingled.

Thinking about Jiang Tian's contribution.

Song Liguo really wanted to say that was enough, that it was already enough.

He, Song Liguo, was already stuffed.

What, you say Jiang Tian's contribution has nothing to do with him?

"Nonsense. As the captain of the Yunhai Narcotics Control Detachment, the more drug dealers he arrests in Yunhai, the stronger his abilities are. Besides, Jiang Tian can also be considered his subordinate.

His men have been cracking major drug trafficking cases one after another.

Putting aside everything else, the collective achievement alone is enough to make them rich.

"By the way, this guy's nickname is Black Wolf. I heard he runs a few underground fighting arenas; he's here to buy drugs," Jiang Tian explained.

"Black Wolf?"

Song Liguo stared blankly at the figure twitching and bleeding like a dead dog beneath Jiang Tian's feet.

"You said he's Black Wolf?" Song Liguo pointed at the person Jiang Tian was trampling on.

"That's right, the drug dealer said he was Black Wolf." Jiang Tian nodded.

Pfft!!!

Song Liguo burst out laughing: "Do you know Black Wolf is a wanted criminal, an A-level wanted criminal at that?"

"What a coincidence?" Jiang Tian genuinely didn't know anything about him.

It could only be said that Black Wolf's crime record was very high, and it turned out he was already wanted.

"That's right, Black Wolf was involved in several murder cases before, and the police had been pursuing him for two years. I didn't expect you to kill him here." Song Liguo explained.

Good heavens.

Jiang Tian's eyes lit up. He knew that wanted criminals had bounties.

For example, the guy who escaped from prison was wanted with a 300,000 yuan fine, and the money has already been approved and will be deposited into his bank account soon.

But this wanted criminal, wow.

"Report, on-site testing confirmed that the suitcase contained methamphetamine, a total of 58 kilograms. In addition, a total of 3 million yuan in cash was also found."

Just then, someone from the forensic department reported.

Good, good.

Song Liguo was excited. At least they hadn't made a mistake. If it weren't for drugs, with so many people dead here, Jiang Tian would be in big trouble.

Now, Song Liguo finally breathed a sigh of relief. This wasn't a false alarm.

That also proved that the man in front of him, who looked like a dead dog, really was a wolf in sheep's clothing.

"By the way, who's this handcuffed person?" Song Liguo asked.

Because at this moment, there were three people standing there. A girl who had been shielding Jiang Tian from the wind was now obediently following behind him, not saying a word.

Song Liguo initially thought the girl had been frightened, but then looked at another person, Pan Laoji.

"Chief Song, this is a major drug lord, the big boss of the drug trade, nicknamed Pan Laoji. Do you know him?" Jiang Tian suddenly remembered something.

That's right, Pan Laoji was also involved in this operation. And although Pan Laoji didn't look particularly menacing, and even seemed friendly and unwilling to cause trouble,

in Jiang Tian's radar, Pan Laoji's crime value was far higher than Black Wolf's.

Jiang Tian was genuinely surprised that his crime value was higher than that of the ruthless Black Wolf. Because of Pan Laoji's appearance and attire, no one would dare to imagine, and no one would ever guess, that he was a major drug lord.

In fact, almost everyone would think Pan Laoji was a respectable businessman.

This was mainly because Pan Laoji's appearance was so deceptive.

"Pfft, Old Man Pan?" Song Liguo looked at Old Man Pan in astonishment.

At that moment, Old Man Pan seemed to recognize Song Liguo, a smile spreading across his face: "Chief Song, we meet again."

Jiang Tian was startled. He looked uncertainly at the scanning radar, only breathing a sigh of relief after seeing that there was indeed no red light on Song Liguo.

He was genuinely shocked, because it was clear that Song Liguo and Old Man Pan not only seemed to have met before, but also seemed to be quite familiar with each other.

This made Jiang Tian suspect that the two were hiding something.

But seeing Song Liguo's safety rating on the radar, he finally breathed a sigh of relief.

It wasn't that Jiang Tian distrusted Song Liguo; it was mainly that someone earning only a few hundred yuan a month, facing the temptation of hundreds of millions every day, made Jiang Tian distrust human nature.

Faced with such temptation, anyone could betray their beliefs.

"It really is you, Old Man Pan," Song Liguo finally confirmed.

Because Jiang Tian had just struck Old Man Pan on the head, blood spurting and flowing down his face, making him unrecognizable at first glance.

"You know Song Zhi?" Jiang Tian asked nervously.

Mainly because if Song Zhi really was... well, if he were a... lesbian, Jiang Tian would truly have to betray his own family.

However, based on Jiang Tian's understanding of Song Liguo—he practically grew up with him—he knew Song Liguo's character. But as the saying goes, human hearts are fickle, and he feared his Uncle Song might have truly changed.

"What are you thinking about, kid? You've done a great job, you know that?" Song Liguo rolled his eyes, then said excitedly, his face flushed.

Jiang Tian shook his head blankly. All he knew was that he had taken down a drug lord.



Chapter 100 Another time-limited mission? Sorry, the mission is late.


The main reason was that the seemingly harmless Pan Laoji had such a high level of culpability.

"This person is one of Tucha's men," Song Liguo explained.

Boom!!!

Jiang Tian's eyes widened.

That's right, Tucha.

This was the most sensitive name for Jiang Tian, ​​both before and after his rebirth.

Before his rebirth, he suffered heavy losses in the capture of Tucha. Who could have imagined that Tucha would be so audacious as to try to annihilate the entire anti-drug team?

After his rebirth, he was killed by Jiang Tian.

But back then, Jiang Tian didn't have scanning radar.

It was also pitch black. Although his shots were accurate, he couldn't accurately locate Tucha's whereabouts.

You see, Tucha was already blacked out on the Ministry of Public Security's wanted list.

"And at that time, the intelligence on Tucha came from an undercover agent within his ranks, but the mission ultimately failed, and that undercover agent disappeared without a trace," Song Liguo continued.

Jiang Tian finally understood the whole story.

In other words, the operation back then was orchestrated by Tu Cha, who had already detected the undercover agent and, in collusion with Pan Laoji, passed false information to the Yunhai branch, leading them into a trap.

Hiss!!!

Thinking this, Jiang Tian's scalp tingled.

Oh no, what about Mr. Yun? If Jiang Tian remembered correctly, Mr. Yun was also involved in this.

Darkness.

Jiang Tian suddenly realized that the sky over Yunhai was incredibly dark.

The drug dealers were all more arrogant and audacious than the last.

"Arrest everyone alive! Take them all under guard! Call ambulances for immediate emergency treatment if they're injured. Take those with minor injuries back to the police station for interrogation immediately," Song Liguo quickly issued all the orders.

After issuing the orders, everyone began the frantic preparations.

Meanwhile, Song Liguo immediately called Jiang Peng.

As expected, Jiang Peng was already home. Upon receiving the news, he exclaimed excitedly, "Really? Okay, I understand!"

Jiang Peng then hurriedly put down his chopsticks and dressed.

"You haven't eaten yet," Jiang's mother complained.

"Time is tight and the mission is heavy, I won't eat first, you guys eat first," Jiang Peng said quickly.

"Let's wait for Xiao Tian to come and eat together," Jiang's mother sighed.

But then, Jiang Peng, who had reached the door, turned back and said, "Don't wait, that kid just did another great job."

Yes, a great job, a monumental achievement.

Tucha!

Regarding Tucha, because of the last operation, from the Ministry of Public Security down to the Provincial Public Security Bureau, they were practically going crazy investigating.

Good heavens.

If it weren't for Jiang Tian's last-minute performance, the entire anti-drug squad would have been wiped out, if not completely wiped out.

This incident can be described as a blatant challenge to the bottom line.

It challenged the bottom line of the entire department, the entire Ministry of Public Security, and even the bottom line of the nation.

Because, historically, only when narcotics police planned and arrested drug traffickers has this happened before—for the first time, drug traffickers have retaliated against and annihilated narcotics police.

Although Jiang Tian killed many drug traffickers, Tu Cha never appeared in the end.

And since then, although they have been investigating cases...

However, few from the provincial department intervened. This was mainly because not only the Ministry of Public Security, but also the provincial anti-drug department and others were frantically investigating all information about Tucha.

But the final result was unsatisfactory. Several days passed, and there was absolutely no news about Tucha.

Even Tucha's drug trafficking network within the country remained undetected.

And now…

Jiang Tian simply had a meal at a hotel, and not only did he directly uncover a drug trafficking scene,

he even arrested one of Tucha's top lieutenants. Pan Laoji, while not Tucha's representative in China, was a crucial lieutenant and knew the vast majority of Tucha's information.

And he still had a meal?

Could he even eat?

His son had performed a monumental service; the Ministry of Public Security and the provincial department were already buzzing with excitement.

As a father and a high-ranking official, how could he eat in peace? He had to immediately inform Gao Xiaolong and Shen Han, these high-ranking leaders.

"Let these high-ranking leaders see Jiang Peng's abilities—no, his son Jiang Peng's abilities."

"Wow, my brother did it again?" Jiang Xiaoyu, who was eating, exclaimed excitedly, his eyes lighting up.

You see, after the last operation, Jiang Xiaoyu became quite famous throughout the school.

When he entered or left the school, many students would even run up to him for autographs. Why, you ask?

Everyone at Yun University knew.

Jiang Tian's brother saved the president of Yun University from drug dealers.

There were even rumors that the heroic anti-drug police officer who saved the president would soon be coming to Yun University to pursue a master's or doctoral degree.

In short, Jiang Xiaoyu's identity was pretty much exposed.

Actually, this was unavoidable.

Secrecy is something that remains secret if few people know it, but when your achievements are too great, secrecy becomes impossible.

With so many achievements, including the time Jiang Tian arrested Wei Rong while handling cases—many people in the dormitory had witnessed Jiang Tian's actions.

Jiang Xiaoyu was filled with admiration for her brother, especially after hearing about his latest meritorious service.

Bang!!!

The next moment, Jiang's mother slapped Jiang Xiaoyu and scolded, "What meritorious service? What's so good about it? Don't you know that meritorious service can be life-threatening? There's a saying: a third-class merit is received standing up, a second-class merit is received sitting down, and a first-class merit is received lying down. A major meritorious service must be a very dangerous mission. I told you long ago, we shouldn't let Xiao Jiang join this team; it's far too dangerous."

Originally, Jiang's mother only agreed to let him join the city's anti-drug police force to coast along and gain some minor merits as a temporary measure.

In reality, the anti-drug police had a bad reputation before, and they didn't have many major missions.

Everything was covered up too well by the Yunhai drug trafficking group, with many members even covering for each other. Breaking through their defenses was incredibly difficult.

This was also why Jiang's mother had this idea before. If, as things stand now, the anti-drug police are working overtime every day, Jiang's mother wouldn't let Jiang Tian go in.

"My brother is so capable, he won't be in danger," Jiang Xiaoyu said, with a strange confidence in her brother.

Jiang's mother remained silent, her face filled with worry. She wondered if she could use her connections to get Jiang Tian transferred.

Or perhaps she should talk to Old Gao and reason with him?

Jiang's mother pondered these questions.

Meanwhile, Jiang Peng had already gotten into his ordinary Audi, his official car.

Yes, just an ordinary little Audi, nothing special, a far cry from a Mercedes-Benz or anything like that.

While driving, Jiang Peng held his phone.

He quickly found Gao Xiaolong's number.

Normally, he would need to report to Shen Han, but this situation was different. Even if he told Shen Han, she wouldn't be able to handle it.

Furthermore, Shen Han was still dealing with other cases, so Jiang Peng didn't even consider Shen Han and directly called Gao Xiaolong first.

"Busy?" Jiang Peng stared blankly at the busy line.

While he was making the call,

Gao Xiaolong, whose phone was busy, was talking to Minister Tan.

“Although the credit has been approved for you and will be sent over tomorrow—it was my insistence, a special case—I must make this clear beforehand: we can't let this small achievement make us complacent, Lao Gao. Tucha must show some serious resolve to deter others. The higher-ups are already furious about this matter,” Minister Tan sighed.

Indeed, the Tucha problem is too serious. It's no exaggeration to say it's a challenge to the bottom line. If no serious stance is taken, there's still the risk of retaliation.

If others follow suit, the consequences will be unbearable for everyone. Gao Xiaolong's expression grew increasingly serious as he listened.

"I don't care what methods Yunhai uses, I want Yunhai to find out about Tucha, whatever the information, and about his subordinates. So, this task isn't difficult, but after several days without any news, it's hard to see your efforts and actions. I don't care, if there's still no news about Tucha by tomorrow, it proves the seriousness of Yunhai's overall problems, and the overall problems of the public security system..." Minister Tan continued with a serious expression.

Fortunately, Jiang Tian didn't know about the conversation between Gao Xiaolong and Minister Tan, otherwise, he would have remarked that the task was being carried out much slower than usual.



Chapter 101 Are you crazy? You even brought out a special-class merit award?


Of course, the investigation has actually been handled by higher authorities, without the involvement of the municipal bureau. This is because the municipal bureau has cracked a major case and is dealing with the cases of Wei Youdao and Mr. Yun, among others.

However, the higher-ups are in a rush, and time is of the essence. How can they possibly find any clues in just a few days? They've had almost no leads for years. They finally managed to get an undercover agent in, only to have him fall into a trap.

Gao Xiaolong smiled bitterly upon hearing this: "Old Tan, it's not that simple. Even if you turn Yunhai upside down, you won't find anything in just a few days. That Tu Cha is cunning and shrewd. The Ministry of Public Security has been pursuing Tu Cha for years, and they've still come up empty-handed."

Minister Tan looked helpless. He was well aware of the difficulty of the situation.

However, this matter required a clear stance and a deterrent effect on everyone, especially drug dealers.

"The drug traffickers are too arrogant. This is an absolute bottom line that cannot be tolerated. This issue must be taken seriously. Even if it means turning Yunhai upside down, we must find the crucial clues about Tucha. I don't care who it is; as long as they find clues about Tucha, even just a clue, they will receive a first-class merit. If they can capture Tucha, even a special-class merit, I will apply for it." Minister Tan insisted forcefully.

"Special-class merit?" Gao Xiaolong gasped.

What does a special-class merit mean?

To put it another way, even if Jiang Tian captures Mr. Yun, successfully overturns the dark clouds over Yunhai, and successfully breaks the ice in Yunhai, all this merit would only qualify him for a first-class merit and a second-class hero award.

It's not that a special-class merit is impossible to obtain.

It's that it's already impossible.

This merit is the highest honor in wartime. Those who receive this merit have invariably changed the course of a battle, and even had a certain impact on the overall national war situation.

But then, Gao Xiaolong suddenly remembered what Tu Cha had done.

His egregious influence and the extent of his atrocities were unprecedented in recent years; it was practically a provocation against the entire public security system.

To put it more seriously, it had escalated to a provocation at the national level.

Moreover, the operation to capture Tu Cha had already begun. Besides overseeing the anti-drug operation in Yunhai, another reason Gao Xiaolong came was because news of Tu Cha had surfaced there.

But who could have predicted the outcome?

It turned out to be a trap.

Tu Cha had dared to lay such a massive net for them.

“That’s right, Tu Cha’s capture was successful. I will personally apply for a special-class merit award for him,” Minister Tan affirmed.

“Good, I will do my best to oversee things here,” Gao Xiaolong sighed and hung up the phone.

Then, turning to Old Mu sitting beside him, he said with a wry smile, "Look, Old Tan's talking without any sense of the situation. Do you think Tucha is so easy to catch? If he were, he wouldn't have been so arrogant for so many years."

Indeed, the wanted notice for Tucha had been in the works for ten years.

Old Mu, sitting in the car beside him, gloated, "Weren't you the same way back then? Now that you're down here, do you understand how we feel?"

"It's not the same," Gao Xiaolong pondered, a headache forming in his eyes.

After several days, there wasn't even any useful information about Tucha.

Even with the provincial department's special investigation, there's been no progress. The only good news is that while they haven't found Tucha, they've uncovered another lead related to drug trafficking.

"A special merit award, it seems the Ministry of Public Security is serious. This time, Tucha has really angered the entire Ministry." Even Old Mu was astonished.

If they solve the case and arrest Tucha...

Wow, that's groundbreaking! The sheer amount of political capital that would be, even historic in a way.

The only person to receive a special merit award in peacetime.

What kind of honor is that?

What kind of political capital is that?

But unfortunately, it's something unattainable.

They can only dream about it. If Tucha were so easy to catch, the Ministry of Public Security would have been hunting him for years, only to be outmaneuvered and almost counter-encircled.

Gao Xiaolong was well aware of this.

Just as Gao Xiaolong was about to say something, the phone rang suddenly.

Gao Xiaolong took out his phone.

Seeing the caller ID, Gao Xiaolong looked surprised, seemingly not expecting it to be this person calling.

However, after thinking for a moment, Gao Xiaolong answered the phone.

"Jiang Peng, what's up?"

His tone was a little displeased. Mainly, it was so late, and Gao Xiaolong was preparing to go home to rest; he hadn't had a proper rest in a long time. Besides, Gao Xiaolong's direct superiors were usually the Municipal Bureau and the Provincial Department; calling him directly was going over his head.

Even if it was a major matter, the key was that they had just solved a case. If it was just for showing off, Gao Xiaolong's mood would naturally be even worse.

"Chief Gao..."



Chapter 102 Gao Xiaolong's high-profile support is a bombshell for Yunhai.


“Dajiang, I’ve seen the Municipal Bureau’s achievements, I’ve seen your performance, and the Ministry of Public Security has seen it too. For Xiaojiang’s sake, I’ll pretend I didn’t receive this call today. That’s all,” Gao Xiaolong said slowly.

Reporting to a higher level is taboo.

In other words, within the system, Jiang Peng’s action represents a lack of trust in the higher-level department, the Public Security Bureau. To put it bluntly, are you not taking Director Shen seriously?

Meanwhile, Old Mu looked at Gao Xiaolong with a face full of surprise, as if he were seeing Gao Xiaolong for the first time.

That’s right.

Based on Old Mu’s understanding of Gao Xiaolong, if given a few chances in this situation, he would have already unleashed a torrent of abuse.

He might even have held a public trial, regardless of rank or position, showing absolutely no mercy.

Just like Jiang Tian back then.

But now, Gao Xiaolong is actually showing respect.

"Xiao Jiang's influence is ridiculously high," Old Mu thought to himself.

He was certain that his own influence was useless in front of Gao Xiaolong, which was why Old Mu was shocked by Jiang Tian's display of respect. Of course, on the other hand, thinking of Xiao Jiang, Old Mu understood.

After all, this was a killing god—no, a police god—a crucial talent in Operation Icebreaker.

He was also the nemesis of drug dealers, having carved a path through an almost impenetrable drug cartel.

"Uh, Old Gao, don't hang up yet, I have something important to say," Jiang Peng said quickly after a moment of confusion.

"How important is it? Tell me," Gao Xiaolong frowned. He had already given Jiang Peng face out of respect for Xiao Jiang.

And now he was still ungrateful. Don't be fooled by Gao Xiaolong's incredibly polite attitude in front of Jiang Tian; if you really investigated, Gao Xiaolong was an absolutely terrifying big shot.

He was a big shot in Kyoto.

How could such a big shot be so amiable? His amiability towards Jiang Tian was only because Jiang Tian's contribution was so immense, so immense that even Gao Xiaolong was dumbfounded. Gao Xiaolong felt he hadn't experienced as much shock and excitement in his entire life as he had in the past few days.

Gao Xiaolong sighed, thinking that Jiang Peng was still too young.

"It's like this, at the Kairun Hotel, we caught a drug deal in the act," Jiang Peng hurriedly said.

"Drug dealers, good, good, how much?" Gao Xiaolong's eyes lit up; he perked up at this, temporarily forgiving Jiang Peng.

This efficiency was too fast; not just a clue, but an immediate arrest.

"A full sixty kilograms at the scene, plus about three million in cash," Jiang Peng stated the figures.

Hiss!!!

Don't think that just because Jiang Tian found the drug dealers' ledgers and seized tens of thousands of kilograms, sixty kilograms is a small amount. Keep in mind, anything over one kilogram is a major case.

Ten kilograms is almost enough for a second-class merit award.

Sixty kilograms is almost enough for a first-class merit award.

"Good, well done, Dajiang, I knew it! Hahaha, the Municipal Bureau did a great job, very well done! Who discovered it? Wait, I just left the Municipal Bureau, I didn't hear about any operation by the Municipal Bureau?" After the excitement subsided, Gao Xiaolong asked in bewilderment.

At this moment, Jiang Peng had arrived at the Municipal Public Security Bureau. Hearing the news, a hint of pride flashed across his face, but his tone was full of humility as he said, "Ahem, it's like this, my son..."

"My son?" Gao Xiaolong was astonished.

"That's right. My son went to the Kairun Hotel for a stay and some leisure, and coincidentally, our team member Zhao Wei hadn't eaten, so he asked Zhao Wei to come along. Who knew Zhao Wei would be kidnapped by drug dealers? My son sensed something was wrong and rushed in immediately. Unexpectedly, there were two groups of drug dealers, a total of fourteen people, engaged in a drug deal. My son stepped forward, stopped the crime, and subdued all the drug dealers."

"It's him again." Gao Xiaolong was almost unable to contain himself.

Good heavens! Two first-class merits, one second-class merit, and a second-class hero on his way, working overtime—and before the awards even arrived, this guy had already done another merit?

Wait a minute.

"Fourteen people, all subdued by Xiao Jiang alone?" Gao Xiaolong asked uncertainly.

"Yes, and they were all armed with dangerous firearms." Jiang Peng had already investigated the situation.

Pfft!!!

Damn it!!!

Gao Xiaolong's head was buzzing.

After a moment, Gao Xiaolong finally came to his senses, calming his shaken heart. After all, thinking about how Jiang Tian had single-handedly taken down 150 drug dealers a few days ago, this seemed plausible.

"Have you checked the drug dealers' identities? We must wipe out everyone on both the supply and demand sides," Gao Xiaolong said excitedly.

Leaving aside the crime scene, the key is the network of connections behind them. Interrogating these people's connections will reveal that most of them are criminals.

That will provide a continuous stream of breakthroughs.

Gao Xiaolong's face lit up with delighted praise: "Good, good, good, Xiao Jiang is not bad. Note, from now on you're not allowed to call him 'Little Jiang,' that's ridiculous. You have to call him 'Little Hero,' hahaha, I'll definitely apply for credit for him."

Huh?

Jiang Peng's grin nearly reached his ears, but thankfully he hadn't forgotten the important matter: "This is why I called you, Elder Gao. One of the buyers in the transaction is Black Wolf, a Class A wanted criminal by the Ministry of Public Security."

"Black Wolf, a wanted criminal in Yunhai. I know him. He seems to run a criminal organization. He was pursued by the criminal police for a while, but he escaped." Gao Xiaolong nodded.

All I can say is, good, good, well done.

"And the other group?"

Black Wolf is good enough, qualified enough to give him a report personally. Not bad, not bad, this achievement deserves recognition."

Jiang Peng, suppressing his excitement, said, "Chief Gao, this is the reason I called you. This is Pan Laoji, the senior leader of our undercover agent under Tu Cha. The operation a few days ago was almost a complete annihilation due to false intelligence passed to the undercover agent by Tu Cha through Pan Laoji. Pan Laoji is one of Tu Cha's top lieutenants."

Buzz!!!

"Tucha?"

Jiang Peng's head exploded with a buzzing sound.

He was dizzy.

He was truly dizzy.

Just moments ago, they were saying that after so many years of searching, they had no news of Tucha, and now Yunhai had found intelligence on Tucha in such a short time—wasn't that a death sentence?

Even with a first-class merit reward, this would be difficult!

Then, turning around...

Tucha's top general had been captured.

"Good, good, so you like giving me surprises, huh?"

"Xiao Jiang, I never thought you were this kind of person."

Gao Xiaolong exclaimed excitedly, "Where is he? Where is he? Wait, Xiao Jiang, make your move! Damn, wait, don't rush! How many of those drug dealers are still alive? He wasn't beaten to death, was he?"

Halfway through his sentence, Gao Xiaolong suddenly realized who had made the move—Jiang Tian!

His attacks were always fatal or severely injured.

Thinking of the drug dealers Jiang Tian had arrested, good heavens, they were all in a terrible state; they would need treatment before they could be interrogated.

"Black Wolf's injuries are more serious. Mainly because Black Wolf dared to try and kill Comrade Zhao Wei. My son, oh no, Little Jiang, got a little angry when he arrived and got beaten quite badly. But that Old Man Pan is fine; he only got two lashes with a baton, which won't affect the interrogation at all," Jiang Peng coughed.

He was secretly thrilled.

Damn, I never thought it felt so good to show off.

Jiang Peng could completely understand Gao Xiaolong's feelings at this moment, after all, he had been just as shocked when he first heard the news.

And just as unable to accept it.



Chapter 103: Observing Clues, Finding the Target, and Making History with a Special Merit.


"Good, good, it's good that you're alright. Where are they? Where are they all?" Gao Xiaolong asked impatiently.

"The drug dealers who died or suffered minor injuries have all been transported to the city police station. The seriously injured have been sent to the hospital for emergency treatment. Pan Laoji is already at the police station." Jiang Peng looked at Pan Laoji in the interrogation room.

That's right.

After Jiang Peng received all the information and arrived, Song Liguo, Jiang Tian, ​​and others had already escorted Pan Laoji and the others to the police station ahead of time.

As for the dead bodies, of course, those drug dealers were already dead. Sending them to the hospital would be a waste of medical resources. Of course, a forensic doctor would perform autopsies.

"Damn it, why didn't you say so earlier? So much nonsense."

Gao Xiaolong slammed down the phone quickly and then said to the driver, "Quick, quick, turn around and go back to the city police station."

"Old Gao, you haven't slept all night." Old Mu didn't know why Gao Xiaolong wanted to go back again.

Seeing Gao Xiaolong's bloodshot eyes, Old Mu was somewhat worried about his health.

"Big news! Hahaha, Pan Laoji, one of Tucha's top lieutenants, was arrested just twenty minutes ago!" Gao Xiaolong laughed loudly.

Pfft!!!

He hadn't heard what Gao Xiaolong was saying on the phone earlier, only some talk about drug dealers.

But now...

Minister Tan had just called, speaking earnestly and stating that although the mission was extremely difficult, it had to be done; even if it meant turning Yunhai upside down, they had to find a trace of Tucha.

And now, less than ten seconds after hanging up, he was told that Pan Laoji, one of Tucha's top lieutenants, had been arrested?

Was this surprise really that sudden?

With a sense of skepticism, Old Mu hurriedly followed Gao Xiaolong to the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Upon arriving, Gao Xiaolong eagerly inquired where Pan Laoji was being interrogated. Yu Hui, who was on duty at the entrance, pointed in the direction, and Gao Xiaolong and Elder Mu rushed in.

However, they didn't go into the interrogation room, but instead went to an observation room next to it, equipped with one-way glass.

Jiang Peng and Song Liguo were already waiting there.

Inside the interrogation room, Gao Xiaolong and Elder Mu looked at the clear glass mirror; what they saw was indeed correct.

Pan Laoji's photo wasn't a secret to them, as they had sent it to an undercover agent who had infiltrated Pan Laoji's inner circle, and the photo had already been transmitted.

After all, it was 2000, and the Skynet system hadn't been connected for another ten or twenty years. If Skynet had been connected, it was safe to say that if Pan Laoji had appeared under the surveillance cameras everywhere, he could have been thoroughly investigated within minutes and surrounded within ten minutes.

Unfortunately, in this day and age, security cameras aren't widespread, and they're only installed in relatively important locations.

"It's him, that's right, it's him, Old Man Pan. I'd recognize him even if he were ashes." Upon seeing Old Man Pan, Gao Xiaolong's eyes instantly turned red, and he spoke through gritted teeth.

The person infiltrated by Old Man Pan's side was an outstanding narcotics officer from the 21st Bureau.

She was a young girl personally found and selected by Gao Xiaolong, possessing a touch of roguishness and wildness that suited her perfectly.

Moreover, she was a warrior with unwavering faith.

As for the final outcome, since the other side was able to orchestrate a scheme through an undercover agent, they must have known the agent's identity. And the consequences of the undercover agent being exposed and losing their usefulness are almost self-evident.

But Gao Xiaolong still wanted to know about the undercover agent.

"Tell me about it."

Jiang Tian tapped the table with his baton and spoke to Pan Laoji, who was sitting in the interrogation chair.

Seeing Jiang Tian's baton, as if recalling something terrible, Pan Laoji's eyelids twitched twice, then he took a deep breath and closed his eyes.

But the next moment...

Bang!!!

A sharp pain suddenly shot through Pan Laoji's forehead. The enormous impact caused him to scream in agony. Before he could even open his eyes, he heard the howling of wind.

"Stop! No! I'll talk!" Pan Laoji shouted.

A moment later, Pan Laoji opened his eyes, staring in terror at the baton just two fingers' width from his head.

A single second later, Pan Laoji shuddered violently, even his scalp tingling.

"A wise man submits to circumstances," Jiang Tian shook his head with a hint of regret.

He seemed to lament the fact that he had been caught so quickly, unable to carry out his plan.

This made Old Pan shudder.

Mainly because Jiang Tian's baton was incredibly painful. In fact, even someone as weak-willed as Old Pan, let alone Black Wolf, would writhe in agony under Jiang Tian's baton.

Even for Jiang Tian, ​​a martial arts master, whether they understood the art of controlling the force to inflict excruciating pain without causing serious injury, was rarely able to withstand it.

"My hometown is Panjia Village. Fifteen years ago, I went out to work but almost starved to death. On my way home, I fainted from hunger. Tu Cha rescued me while he was transporting goods, and afterwards, I followed Tu Cha."

Being caught in the act of a transaction meant there was no way to deny it; confessing or not confessing made virtually no difference.

In fact, Pan Laoji even thought that if someone performed many meritorious deeds and behaved well, they might be able to get a suspended death sentence and escape with their life, even if the hope was slim.

Jiang Tian finally learned from Pan Laoji what Tucha had done.

It was utterly heinous and lawless.

Besides a drug manufacturing plant overseas, Tucha also had a parts factory. And what did this factory do?

It could be said that a portion of the people who disappeared in Yunhai each year were turned into parts in that factory.

Some were tricked into going there, while others were forcibly taken there by chance.

"Tucha used two nightclubs in Yunhai as temporary headquarters. They were managed by another of Tucha's men, nicknamed 'Brother Biao,' a former mercenary overseas. He was a very skilled thug. Through these two nightclubs, they could smuggle people to the parts factory and also sell drugs. However, Brother Biao was very careful; he never sold drugs in his own nightclubs, but instead resold them to middlemen."

"Officer Jiang, if I help you capture Tucha, could you give me a major commendation? Don't give me the death penalty, even a suspended death sentence would be fine."

Pan Laoji pleaded hopefully with Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian didn't actually have the authority to do so.

However, Jiang Tian hated nothing more than someone negotiating.

Bang!!!

Jiang Tian raised his baton with terrifying force and slammed it down on Pan Laoji's handcuffed hands.

Crack!!!

The sound of bones breaking could be faintly heard.

Pan Laoji trembled and curled up, his screams of extreme pain escaping intermittently.

"Say yes."

Just as Jiang Tian was about to strike again, Gao Xiaolong's voice came through the earpiece: "If the intelligence is true, we can give him a suspended death sentence. If Tucha is truly captured, we can guarantee him a way out."

Jiang Tian reluctantly withdrew his baton.

Who wants to die if they can live? Old Pan knew perfectly well that if things went according to plan, he was doomed; the death penalty was a certainty.

Now that he had a chance to live, Old Pan seized it frantically: "Actually, your police undercover agent is still alive."

BOOM!!!

Everyone present was stunned.

Especially Gao Xiaolong, whose eyes widened completely. He anxiously spoke into the walkie-talkie, "Quick, ask him how the undercover agent is!"

His urgent tone was completely unlike that of a veteran soldier.

And certainly not with such impatience.

Could the undercover agent have a different identity? Jiang Tian's heart skipped a beat. He subtly thought of Gao Xiaolong; he wasn't this agitated.

Especially as a high-ranking official, his role was to maintain order; his emotions wouldn't fluctuate so easily.

An undercover agent, and he was this agitated?

"The undercover agent is doing well now, but we don't know what will happen after tomorrow. The boss has ordered her to be sent to the parts factory."



Chapter 104 caused a sensation. Very well, if this is how you play, I'll abandon all martial ethics.


Jiang Tian adjusted his headphones, his gaze gradually turning serious.

Actually, regardless of whether it was related to Gao Xiaolong or not, or even if it was completely unrelated, as a proud narcotics officer, Jiang Tian couldn't stand idly by while someone was caught by drug dealers and faced the terrible consequences.

"Where is the parts factory? Will Tu Cha take the undercover agent there tomorrow?" Jiang Tian asked.

Pan Laoji nodded: "The boss originally planned to use the undercover agent and then deal with him, but later, because it's almost time for reconciliation, he plans to send him to the parts factory tomorrow. There will be a meeting and reconciliation there tomorrow. The undercover agent is quite attractive; Boss Tu Cha will reward him based on his performance, giving him to a high-performing subordinate."

He paused, then continued: "I just wanted to push my performance targets one last time, which is why I risked transporting a batch of drugs for a transaction. Who knew that nothing would go wrong on the most dangerous part of the journey, but problems would arise during the transaction itself?"

Too tragic.

As Pan Laoji recounted this, his mood became truly somber.

In the past, although the undercover agent had infiltrated Pan Laoji's inner circle, the police had gone to great lengths to place such an agent with a high-ranking position, and Pan Laoji had genuinely taken a liking to him.

Unfortunately, an undercover agent is just an undercover agent.

Even later, Pan Laoji discussed with Tu Cha's boss whether he could reward the agent, which led Tu Cha to compromise and prioritize performance to maintain order. Whoever generated the most profit would be rewarded.

Because the undercover agent was truly exceptional.

For this reason, Pan Laoji didn't even bother competing for Zhao Qiuni, simply giving her to Black Wolf.

Jiang Tian, ​​listening to Pan Laoji's account, roughly understood what had happened, while through the earpiece came Gao Xiaolong's heavy breathing.

Clearly, Gao Xiaolong's emotions were fluctuating greatly.

Even through the one-way glass, Jiang Tian couldn't see Gao Xiaolong's expression, but he could imagine it was incredibly grim.

"Continue," Jiang Tian nodded knowingly. His impression of Pan Laoji had changed slightly, but only slightly. For these drug dealers, retaining even a modicum of humanity was rare.

However, based on Jiang Tian's understanding, if what he said was true, and he had genuinely performed meritorious service, even pleading for the undercover agent, he might actually be spared after all the factors considered.

Pan Laoji, wanting only to survive, was extremely cooperative.

"The 30th of this month is the accounting day, which is tomorrow. Boss Tucha will be distributing rewards," Pan Laoji said.

"Where is he located? What's the security like?" Jiang Tian asked, slamming his hand on the table.

He knew that Gao Xiaolong, at this moment, definitely wanted to know the location, and even more so, the situation of the undercover agent.

As Gao Xiaolong's right-hand man, solving all of Gao Xiaolong's problems is Jiang Tian's most important task right now.

What? You're saying Jiang Tian is fawning over him?

What a joke! This is serving the people, how can it be called fawning? Rescuing the undercover hero is Jiang Tian's inescapable duty.

This would allow Gao Xiaolong to gain a more comprehensive understanding and appreciation of him, enabling him to reach a higher platform. Of course, this isn't to say that working at the grassroots level is bad, but a better platform would allow him to better demonstrate his service to the people.

“The parts factory is overseas, in Walu Town. That area is controlled by the Tucha regime. They have nearly five hundred armed men there, all fully armed. If you want to operate, it's impossible without support. But I can help you infiltrate and rescue the undercover agent,” Pan Laoji hurriedly introduced himself.

What he said was indeed correct.

Over five hundred men, all fully armed—that's a very large force.

Even special forces would find it extremely difficult to operate in Walu Town, especially without support. Even if they got there, they'd be completely lost, unable to find their way, let alone rescue anyone.

There was another problem: the relationship with Mr. Yun.

Through Jiang Tian's understanding, Pan Laoji had done business with Mr. Yun before. He had received goods from Pan Laoji a long time ago.

Even so, it still meant one thing: Mr. Yun wasn't telling the truth.

Mr. Yun lied.

But putting Mr. Yun's question aside for now, Jiang Tian walked out of the interrogation room with a heavy expression.

When he arrived at the conference room, it was filled with commotion.

Especially towards Jiang Tian.

"Captain Jiang is awesome."

"Damn, another merit!"

"Yeah, Chief Gao just said the Ministry of Public Security issued a reward: a first-class merit for finding clues about Tu Cha, and a special-class merit for capturing Tu Cha. We're applying for an exception."

Hiss!!!

Jiang Tian gasped, his eyes instantly brightening.

Fine, fine, you're playing it this way, huh? Then I won't play by the rules.



Chapter 105 You Write the Slip, I Approve It: Another First-Class Merit


Meanwhile, while Jiang Tian was being interrogated, things were also turbulent at the Ministry of Public Security.

Minister Tan hung up the phone with worry, rubbed his temples, and finally sighed deeply as he looked out at the brightly lit streets.

Drugs have always been a matter of zero tolerance.

Yet drug crimes persist despite repeated crackdowns, and are even becoming increasingly brazen.

“According to statistics from the past few days, since Tu Cha committed that crime, the overall activity of drug trafficking nationwide has increased by about ten percent. Especially in the last few days, some drug dealers have begun to actively retaliate against anti-narcotics police. Tu Cha's actions have had a very negative impact,” Xu Poye said.

“It’s too difficult. Although we’ve given Yunhai a few days, based on Yunhai’s past performance, we can’t hold out too much hope. I think we still need to use people from our headquarters.”

After hesitating for a moment, Xu Poye finally spoke.

Minister Tan looked up, his eyes flashing, and thought carefully.

Suddenly realizing it, it seemed true. Solving Mr. Yun's case had given him a false sense of security. What had made him start to believe Yun Hai's side?

To even let drug dealers betray the counter-encirclement team was utterly disgraceful.

Mr. Yun's case had somehow robbed him of his basic judgment and rationality.

"Although Lao Gao is watching over there, one person's power is limited. You can't expect one person to turn a mountain of waste into jade," Minister Tan said thoughtfully, regaining his composure.

Knock knock knock!!!

Just then, a knock sounded at the door.

"Minister, Zeng Fan from the 21st Bureau is here," the secretary's voice rang out.

"Zeng Fan."

Minister Tan stood up and looked at Xu Poye.

Xu Poye gave a wry smile and said, "Probably here for that girl."

Minister Tan snapped out of his reverie: "Gao Yangyang, sigh... let him in."

The door opened, and a powerfully built man entered, exuding a fierce aura.

"Chief, I'm applying to go to Yunhai," Zeng Fan said, getting straight to the point.

"Just you?" Xu Poye shook his head.

"No, and my squad with me too," Zeng Fan explained.

This was no small matter, and Xu Poye frowned slightly.
