"With those useless guys in Yunhai, how could they possibly save Yangyang? Chief, let me go. I can give you a guarantee," Zeng Fan said confidently, puffing out his chest.

Xu Poye frowned slightly. This Zeng Fan had once been a talent in the local anti-drug team, having received five second-class merits and one first-class merit. Xu Poye had taken a liking to him and brought him directly to headquarters.

After arriving, for some unknown reason, he began a relentless pursuit of Gao Yangyang.

His attitude, however, is becoming increasingly arrogant. While he is talented, such arrogance is not a good thing.

“This matter has already been investigated in Yunhai,” Minister Tan tapped the table.

“Minister, you know how incompetent Yunhai is. If those useless people in Yunhai were to handle this matter, Yangyang would probably be long dead,” Zeng Fan said disdainfully.

“Shut up,” Xu Poye felt that his superiors valued talent too much, which had inflated their egos.

“Alright,” Minister Tan shook his head, stopping him.

Because, deep down, Minister Tan, like Zeng Fan, didn't really trust Yunhai much: "The drug dealers in Yunhai are quite arrogant. We've made some progress in drug enforcement recently, but dealing with Tucha is still very difficult."

However, before Minister Tan could finish speaking, the phone rang.

Minister Tan gave the two men expectant looks and then took out his phone.

When he saw Gao Xiaolong on the caller ID, Minister Tan frowned slightly.

Gao Xiaolong had just expressed his difficulties, and now he was calling—the meaning was obvious to Minister Tan: it was probably because the problem was difficult to solve.

Sigh.

Looks like I overestimated Yunhai!

Thinking this, Minister Tan answered the phone: "Old Gao, are you having some difficulties? I've already prepared some personnel from headquarters to send over for you."

When he mentioned Old Gao, Minister Tan's tone lowered slightly. He knew all too well that, compared to himself, Old Gao probably wanted to capture Tucha even more.

To rescue the undercover agent.

Because that undercover agent was...

He even received a special merit award as a reward, which was, in a way, helping Lao Gao.

But ability is ability. Even with a huge reward, if the ability isn't sufficient, it's still insufficient.

Apart from a recent talent emerging from Yunhai, they're still too inexperienced against Tucha.

Not enough, far from enough.

Just then,

Gao Xiaolong's voice rang out, calm and composed: "Old Tan, I have good news for you. We've found information about Tucha."

"Found it? Wait, you found information about Tucha?"

Minister Tan jumped to his feet, his face full of excitement.

"That's right. It's because we captured one of Tucha's top lieutenants, Pan Laoji, who was also an undercover agent who went to join them." Gao Xiaolong's voice was calm, but his inner excitement was probably about to explode.

Minister Tan understood.

But precisely because of this, he jumped to his feet, his face beaming with surprise: "Old Gao, you're not lying to me?"

"Absolutely, she's confessed," Gao Xiaolong said.

"Good, good, good. In that case, Yangyang, she..." Minister Tan suddenly paused.

Because, even if they found her, what then? Based on past experience, it wasn't uncommon for undercover agents to be discovered.

In China, they'd be sunk to the bottom of the sea, buried alive, or at worst, beaten to death.

But overseas, the fate was countless times worse. Once those people attacked, even if they were rescued, the person would be crippled, truly living a life worse than death, unable to even die, because those people were too cruel.

Minister Tan, speaking of this, still felt some worry for Old Gao.

To everyone's surprise, Old Gao said in a relaxed voice, "The undercover agent is currently very safe, but he was apprehended at the border. We need to go to the parts factory tomorrow to deal with him. We have one night to prepare for the operation."

"Really?" Minister Tan's face lit up with delight. "Who, who did it? That was excellent! You write the note, I'll approve it. First-class merit! He absolutely deserves a first-class merit award! He acted at the perfect time."

Even Minister Tan was distressed by Yangyang's accident.

Let alone Gao Xiaolong.

Now, with such a timely arrest, as long as they have a direction, they can move in.

Whoever does this deserves a huge reward.

This is a tremendous achievement!

"Speaking of which, you know him too, Comrade Jiang Tian," Gao Xiaolong said directly without beating around the bush.

Buzz!!



Chapter 106 Are you going to let the fruits of my labor be stolen? Let's see if you have the strength.


Good heavens.

Minister Tan's eyes widened, his face filled with astonishment.

Astonished.

Truly astonished.

Is it still this guy?

No.

His two first-class merits and one second-class merit were supposedly still on their way, delivered with extra effort.

And now...

Another first-class merit?

Then, Gao Xiaolong recounted the entire process of capturing Tu Cha.

One person.

One gun.

Fourteen drug dealers in total; a small handgun was practically equivalent to being unarmed.

Two fists can't fight four hands, let alone fourteen guns in a room; a small handgun was practically suicide.

But, Jiang Tian actually did it?

Why? It's so surreal. Why? Minister Tan was completely bewildered. Could a human really do this?

"Could it be that Lao Gao lied about military intelligence to get credit for a promising talent under his command?"

"Lao Tan, you don't think I would do something like lying about military intelligence, do you?" Hearing the silence on the phone, Gao Xiaolong, who knew Minister Tan very well, roughly guessed what Minister Tan was thinking and spoke bluntly.

Hiss!!!

Hearing Gao Xiaolong's words, Minister Tan quickly came to his senses. He would rather believe the world was ending than believe this.

He absolutely refused to believe that Gao Xiaolong would lie about military intelligence.

This was simply an insult to Gao Xiaolong.

"No, no, good. It's good to have news from Yangyang. Lao Gao, congratulations. Fax the note over immediately. I'll sign it and have Zeng Fan give it to you directly for signature. Zeng Fan will also take the First-Class Merit Medal directly. When it comes to rescuing you, just arrange for Zeng Fan's squadron." Minister Tan directly instructed.

It has to be said, Zeng Fan, though a bit arrogant, is undeniably skilled; his abilities have been proven.

"Hmm?"

Hearing this news, Gao Xiaolong frowned slightly.

Before he could say anything more, Minister Tan had already hung up.

Gao Xiaolong put down his phone, his brow furrowed in thought.

"I've heard of that young man, Zeng Fan. He's very strong, a well-known talent at headquarters. At such a young age, he's already a company commander. He can change magazines with one hand, and once single-handedly fought off more than ten drug dealers, wiping them all out—it caused a sensation." Old Mu's ears twitched, and he turned to look at Gao Xiaolong.

Sitting together, Old Mu heard the entire conversation clearly.

But...

"What, you're not happy? If that young man Zeng Fan takes action, and with Old Pan's cooperation, I reckon they can rescue Yangyang." Old Mu spoke with genuine admiration.

Because...

Even if Old Master Mu put himself in Gao Xiaolong's shoes, he couldn't have gone to such lengths as sending his own granddaughter as an undercover agent.

That's right.

That undercover agent, Yangyang, is Gao Xiaolong's own granddaughter!

If it were Mu Ya as an undercover agent, Old Master Mu would never have done it, not even if he were beaten to death.

And that's Gao Xiaolong's vision, that's Gao Xiaolong's greatness.

He truly embodied the principle that the people's children are children, and his children are children too. In matters of principle, in facing danger, in matters of duty, if it's the most suitable person, then it's you who should go.

Old Master Mu knew perfectly well the pain Gao Xiaolong had felt a few nights ago when he learned he was surrounded and trapped in Tucha's encirclement.

Because it meant his beloved granddaughter, Gao Yangyang, was likely in grave danger!

But Gao Xiaolong hadn't shown a trace of it.

"Actually, I'd rather send Xiao Jiang," Gao Xiaolong said.

"Comrade Xiao Jiang is still young. He could handle it if he were to act, but those people are cunning and shrewd. One wrong move and he'd be like a moth drawn to a flame, turning a rescue into a suicide mission. Yangyang is your granddaughter, after all," Old Master Mu said directly.

Gao Xiaolong's gaze fell into thought.

......

"The mission is difficult!"

Jiang Tian looked at the location; he had already extracted the exact location from Pan Laoji.

They were almost deep inside enemy territory.

At this moment, Jiang Tian held a map drawn by Li Zijing.

“If we were to attempt a rescue, the nearest border is nearly fifty kilometers away, and along that route are countless criminal gangs, large and small,” Li Zijing pointed to a mark on the map.

Jiang Tian also began to mentally draw the route.

To be honest, although the allure of a special-class merit was immense, even Jiang Tian found the task daunting.

Five hundred fully armed drug traffickers, on someone else's territory, with the advantages of timing, location, and popular support—it was practically a pipe dream for ordinary people to capture Tucha in that environment.

Even headquarters had never considered capturing Tucha directly in that situation, because it was overseas. Large-scale armed operations couldn't cross; they could only send small, disguised groups, and even if they were killed, they couldn't reveal their identities and would die anonymously.

For headquarters, rescuing the undercover agent was a success.

The city police station was brightly lit, and it quickly became late at night.

At this time…

"Comrade Jiang, you've had a long day. Go back and rest." Gao Xiaolong patted Jiang Tian on the shoulder.

This completely stunned Jiang Tian.

To be honest, Jiang Tian hadn't considered this. In the entire city bureau, or rather, in the entire Yunhai, who else besides him could carry out such a difficult mission? Jiang Tian didn't believe anyone could do it.

Especially since he was the one who discovered and arrested the drug dealers.

And then the intelligence came out during interrogation—to put it bluntly, someone else stole the credit?

That's right, that was Jiang Tian's first thought.

Although he was guaranteed a first-class merit this time, even if rescuing the drug dealers wouldn't earn him much of a share, this feeling was still unsettling.

"Jiang Tian, ​​for this operation, a team of experts from headquarters came to conduct an in-depth rescue. They have extensive rescue experience and are colleagues and familiar with the hostages. To avoid any unforeseen circumstances, headquarters ultimately decided to send them to carry out the operation. I'm sorry to trouble you this time," Gao Xiaolong said apologetically.

Experts?

Jiang Tian's brow twitched as he looked at the bright red Pan Laoji icon on the scanning radar. Would this operation go as smoothly as expected? Jiang Tian had some doubts.

However, on the other hand, Jiang Tian had no experience in rescue operations. He understood that the higher-ups, after considering all factors, had assigned the task to professionals.

*Click!*

Just then, the sound of a door opening rang out.

The next moment, a voice came out: "I think this isn't about feeling wronged. One should only do what one is capable of. The useless abilities of those in Yunhai can only handle menial tasks. Even not being able to go was still a way of saving them."



Chapter 107 Old Deng's Rewards Exploded, Keyword Explosion


Hearing this, Jiang Tian turned and frowned at the person who entered.

The figure rushed in, not even glancing at Jiang Tian, ​​and went straight to Gao Xiaolong, saying, "Time is of the essence, Chief Gao, wait for our good news."

That's right, they were leaving the country tonight.

Zeng Fan's brows furrowed even more, his eyes blazing with anger.

You can insult Yun Hai, but insulting Jiang Tian was unacceptable to Gao Xiaolong.

The clues in Tucha and the arrest of Pan Laoji were all Jiang Tian's doing. If you're really that capable, how come you didn't find any information before?

Headquarters' investigation into Tucha has never stopped.

"Who are you calling trash?" Jiang Tian turned around.

Regardless of who it was, Jiang Tian was already unhappy about having his credit stolen.

And now this guy is jumping into the line of fire! I worked so hard to capture people, interrogate them, and gather all the intelligence. And you, without contributing anything, turn around and call me trash?

Jiang Tian was practically laughing in anger.

"Oh, so you're a piece of trash?" Zeng Fan sneered condescendingly. "If it weren't for you, how could Yangyang's undercover work have been discovered? If the undercover work is discovered, you all bear all the responsibility. Are you really that upset about being called trash?"

"Hey!!!"

Jiang Tian's temper flared, his eyes blazing with fury.

He'd suffered enough humiliation in his past life, and he couldn't stand being wronged in this one. Anyone who wronged him would trigger a rage in his hand, and Jiang Tian would instinctively pull out his baton.

Jiang Tian slammed the baton down on his head: "Tucha, what information have you found out this time? Pointing fingers and calling me trash, who do you think you are? I arrested Tucha's men, I found information about Tucha, and now you're out here insulting others while eating their food?"

Zeng Fan's eyes flashed. That's right.

When he learned that the Special Merit Award had been granted through special approval—an exceptional award for the capture of Tucha—he instantly became restless.

He had initially intended to investigate, but he never expected there to actually be information about Tucha.

Considering his experience and Jiang Tian's younger age, the leadership assigned the task to Zeng Fan.

“You dare to lay a hand on me? Do you know what I used to do? Haven’t you heard of the ‘Southern Zeng Eight Extremes Collapse’?” Zeng Fan, his face flushed slightly from being exposed, also attacked in a fit of rage.

He blocked with one hand, while his other shoulder aimed directly at Jiang Tian's heart, a ruthless and deadly move.

Bang!!!

But, the next moment.

In Zeng Fan's mind, blocking the baton with his elbow was a sure thing, but upon contact, the excruciating pain, a terrifying pain that pierced his very soul.

Instantly, Zeng Fan recalled the time he was beaten to tears during his first martial arts training session.

What shocked Zeng Fan even more was that Jiang Tian's shoulder also came at him with a Bajiquan (Eight Extremes Collapse) technique.

It's often said that Tai Chi brings peace to the world, while Bajiquan stabilizes the universe—this refers to Bajiquan, a ferocious move.

But Jiang Tian was a martial arts master; frankly, he could use any martial art he wanted without hesitation.

"I admit you have a slight advantage in strength, but do you think you can compete with someone like me who's trained since childhood in Bajiquan?" Zeng Fan was secretly pleased and decided to teach Jiang Tian a lesson.

Boom!!!

But the moment they made contact, Zeng Fan's expression changed.

Shoulders collided, and it felt like he'd hit a sharp piece of steel.

"Ah!!!"

Zeng Fan cried out in pain, flying two or three meters backward before crashing to the ground.

"Enough, Zeng Fan, what do you think you're doing?"

Just as Zeng Fan sat there, bewildered, Gao Xiaolong was enraged, utterly enraged.

What kind of time is this?

An urgent mission!

His granddaughter, to solve the case, had gone deep behind enemy lines, and now, as an undercover agent, her life was constantly in danger.

And you guys actually started fighting behind their backs?

"Chief Gao, it was him, he attacked first," Zeng Fan said, bewildered, still standing up to continue the fight.

That's right, it was Jiang Tian who attacked first!

*Slap!!!*

But the next moment, Gao Xiaolong slapped him across the face: "Is Xiao Jiang wrong? Without Xiao Jiang, could you have found Tucha's trail? You think you're so great? If you are, why do you still need other people's intelligence? Why didn't I see you arrest Pan Laoji? Comrade Xiao Jiang arrested him! You're just here to provoke me. Who gave you the courage?"

Gao Xiaolong had initially held Zeng Fan in high regard.

However, this simple incident made Gao Xiaolong see him for who he really was.

In fact, Gao Xiaolong was completely wrong.

The main reason was that Zeng Fan wanted to impress Gao Xiaolong. On the way, Zeng Fan thought that the anti-drug efforts in Yunhai were a national joke, and Gao Yangyang's arrest as an undercover agent was inextricably linked to Yunhai.

This proved that Gao Xiaolong definitely didn't like Yunhai, so he came here to flatter Gao Xiaolong and appease him.

But he ended up slapping the horse's leg. Anyone else might have been fine, and Gao Xiaolong might not have said anything, but Jiang Tian had just earned another first-class merit.

Including this first-class merit,

he now had four.

What was there to be arrogant about? Besides, without Jiang Tian, ​​Gao Yangyang wouldn't have had a chance at all.

"Commander Gao, I..." Zeng Fan's face flushed red and then paled as he stubbornly insisted, "He's just a piece of trash. I just made a mistake, I..."

"Enough! Calling someone trash? Have you forgotten why headquarters sent you here?" Gao Xiaolong reminded him.

"Of course, to do this..." Halfway through his sentence, Zeng Fan suddenly realized what he was doing and hurriedly picked up the package he had just dropped.

Seeing the box was dirty, he even carefully blew on it.

Then, his expression excited, he said, "Chief Gao, this is an important item entrusted to me by the Minister. He instructed me to deliver it to you without fail."

"Alright, now I'll tell you about this good-for-nothing you're talking about."

Gao Xiaolong's expression turned serious and meticulous, even reverent, as he stood at attention, facing Jiang Tian.

No, wait, what's going on?

Chief Gao saluted that young man?

Newly arrived, Zeng Fan wasn't stupid. Jiang Tian was too young, still wearing a leather jacket instead of his police uniform, looking only in his early twenties. He was perfect as a stepping stone for Zeng Fan to curry favor.

It was also a way to impress Gao Xiaolong.

"Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​on behalf of headquarters and XXX... with the approval of the minister and the meeting, and to commend your heroic performance in the anti-drug operation and the dismantling of over thirty drug traffickers in the drug manufacturing factory case, it is hereby decided to award you a First-Class Merit."

As he spoke, Gao Xiaolong opened the box, took out the First-Class Merit Medal, and pinned it around Jiang Tian's neck, along with a certificate placed in Jiang Tian's pocket.

[Ding, currently blessed by the God of Wealth, three special achievements of First-Class Merit, 2/3 complete]

A voice sounded in Jiang Tian's mind.

Hiss!!!

It's coming.

Jiang Tian had waited too long for this day.

He had to admit, he was a little short of money and had his eye on bank accounts.

While some were happy, others were worried.

"I came here with a First-Class Merit?"

Zeng Fan, struggling to his feet, stared in shock at this scene.

A First-Class Merit! This is a First-Class Merit! Nationwide, only a few First-Class Merits are awarded each year, especially when received standing up.

Back then, Zeng Fan had received a First-Class Merit Award standing up, which is why he was eventually brought to headquarters.

But it wasn't over yet.

[After review by headquarters, Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​in an operation a few days ago, successfully located Mr. Yun and uncovered all of the drug lord's ledgers, setting a historical precedent. He is also awarded a First-Class Merit Award.]

Buzz!!!

Zeng Fan was completely stunned, his eyes wide with disbelief.

Two First-Class Merits?

Clap clap clap!!!

Just as Zeng Fan was still in shock, enthusiastic applause suddenly erupted behind him.

When he turned around, he found that many police officers had gathered around him, applauding Jiang Tian with excited expressions.

Because Jiang Tian's merit had been awarded, their collective merit would likely be coming soon!

Jiang Tian was also stunned.

Because...

[The special attribute (God of Wealth's Blessing) has been automatically equipped. With the God of Wealth's Blessing equipped, one yuan will be generated every minute.]

[Each additional First-Class Merit earned automatically increases monetary income.]

Jiang Tian calculated: sixty yuan per hour, six hundred yuan in ten hours, and even doing nothing, he could generate an extra 1440 yuan per day?

That's thirty to forty thousand yuan of extra income per month. It doesn't seem like much, but you have to understand, what era is this?

[After discussion and research by headquarters, Comrade Jiang Tian successfully captured a deranged fugitive at Yunshan Prison and rescued innocent hostages in the subsequent operation. He is hereby awarded a Second-Class Merit and the Anti-Drug Vanguard Medal.]

That's right.

That operation was originally a First-Class Merit, but later, Jiang Tian was awarded an honorary title.

This title is incredible.

In reality, Jiang Tian still profited.

Meanwhile, Zeng Fan, standing at the door, was completely dumbfounded and mentally broken.

Because he'd just kicked a hornet's nest. What he'd intended as a stepping stone to establish his authority and curry favor with Chief Gao had turned out to be a terrifying mountain—a mountain of gold, no less.

How could he not collapse?

Who's ever seen two first-class merits awarded at once?

Zeng Fan had never seen anything like it in his life!

But now, not only had he witnessed it, he was being relentlessly humiliated by its owner.



Chapter 108 Earning 100,000 a month while lying down, it's awesome!


[Comrade Jiang Tian made a crucial contribution in last night's arrest of drug dealers, saving his comrades. More importantly, he also captured Tu Cha's henchmen, a key figure, during last night's operation. With the approval of the Ministry of Public Security, Comrade Jiang Tian is awarded the First-Class Merit Medal.]

The third time.

Gulp!!!

Three First-Class Merits?

Zeng Fan was dumbfounded.

Wait, what does this mean?

Are you buying First-Class Merits in bulk?

[Ding, the God of Wealth's blessing has been increased to Lv2, generating two yuan per minute.] Current First-Class Merit Medal (3/4)

Jiang Tian was overjoyed. Two yuan per minute, even if Jiang Tian did nothing all day, he could earn 2880 yuan.

Two thousand eight hundred yuan a day, what did that mean around 2000?

A monthly income close to one hundred thousand!

No shortage of money, absolutely no shortage of money!

[Ding! The "Divine Achievement Quest for Body Management" has been completed. The special attribute "(God of Body Management)" has been automatically equipped.] (After equipping, the most perfect physique will be automatically generated.)

In an instant, Jiang Tian felt a warm current flow through him.

His muscles, height, and bones changed almost instantly.

Before, due to limitations in his skeletal structure and muscles, Jiang Tian's martial arts prowess and boundless stamina were somewhat affected.

Many martial arts moves and killing techniques were unusable.

Now, if Jiang Tian wanted, his elbows, knuckles, arms, even his knees and other joints could become deadly weapons.

Even though Jiang Tian appeared outwardly unchanged—except for his clothes seeming a little smaller—other changes were minimal.

However, with this strength, especially in a duel between experts, Jiang Tian was confident he could even fight two of his former selves bare-handed.

Sure enough, Jiang Tian was excited.

"Damn," Zeng Fan stammered.

His face was filled with disbelief; he even slapped himself, the sting making Zeng Fan realize he wasn't dreaming.

What was happening before his eyes was undeniably real.

Someone had actually received three First-Class Merits at once—it was constantly shattering his worldview.

It was no exaggeration to say that this was a historic moment.

"Comrade Jiang, thank you for your hard work."

He solemnly handed the certificate and medal to Jiang Tian with both hands, as if it were a simple passing on a torch.

It even gave the ever-unyielding Gao Xiaolong the illusion that he had indeed aged.

"Serving the people," Jiang Tian saluted.

Gao Xiaolong gave Jiang Tian a look, then turned to Zeng Fan and said, "Does he think he can be arrogant and look down on everyone just because he earned a first-class merit once? He's just a frog in a well, with a limited perspective. I don't want to pursue this matter any further. Since Old Tan sent you here on this mission, I won't say anything."

Gao Xiaolong hadn't planned to do anything initially.

However, Zeng Fan's actions and words immediately worried Gao Xiaolong.

Damn it, Zeng Fan was a fairly well-known young man at headquarters, but Gao Xiaolong couldn't entrust his granddaughter's life to someone like that.

Even though it was Minister Tan who directly issued the order.

Zeng Fan lowered his head, his heart filled with jealousy. In his view, it wasn't that he was inferior to Jiang Tian.

It was simply because he hadn't had the opportunity to handle so many cases since arriving in Kyoto.

If he were in Yunhai, the crime capital known as "Little Gotham," his achievements would have been phenomenal.

Yes.

Request a transfer to Yunhai.

Zeng Fan's eyes lit up; he suddenly realized his chance to make a name for himself had arrived.

What? You say Jiang Tian is amazing? No, he thought it was just that the locals in Yunhai were too incompetent, allowing Jiang Tian, ​​an intern, to achieve so much.

With him here, wouldn't his achievements skyrocket?

Fine, fine, after this mission, I'll let Yunhai monitor what a true police god looks like. So many people, and an intern stole the spotlight—what a waste!

Thinking this through, Zeng Fan, his composure restored, smiled and said, "Commander, I have a mission to complete, so I'll be leaving now. I don't have time to play games with a recent graduate. After all, while my primary goal is rescuing the hostages, I'll also claim the Special Merit Award."

With that, Zeng Fan arrogantly raised his head, even giving Jiang Tian a disdainful look as he left.

So what if he has three First-Class Merits? The Special Merit Award is waiting for me.

Jiang Tian scratched the back of his head, completely baffled by what this guy had been thinking. How did he suddenly become so stupid?

Especially when Jiang Tian turned around and saw Gao Xiaolong's gloomy face, Jiang Tian narrowed his eyes, increasingly feeling that this guy might be a bit dim-witted.

But before Jiang Tian could even leave, he felt he could go back and rest.

Although the historic Special Merit Award was indeed tempting, and it would also allow him to find Tucha for revenge, if someone else had already done it, Jiang Tian didn't want to compete for it.

Jiang Tian couldn't do anything about Zeng Fan's promising prospects, even though he didn't believe Zeng Fan could succeed.

"Xiao Jiang, I have a task for you," Gao Xiaolong called out to Jiang Tian.

"Is there something else, sir?" Jiang Tian asked.

"Why are you still so emotional?" Gao Xiaolong chuckled.

"Reporting to the sir, I dare not show any emotion when serving the people," Jiang Tian replied firmly, standing at attention.

Gao Xiaolong was exasperated. He was still showing emotion after all. "You little brat, I know you're emotional, but I'm worried about Zeng Fan. You stay behind them, but don't let them spot you."

That's right.

Although Minister Tan and the others strongly recommended Zeng Fan's actions, how could Gao Xiaolong possibly trust him?

He had originally wanted Jiang Tian to go back and rest, but the more Gao Xiaolong thought about it, the more uneasy he became.

He had heard of Zeng Fan before, but this was the first time he had met him, and the more he looked at him, the more suspicious he seemed.

[Ding! Stamp collecting mission triggered. Upon successfully collecting Special Merit, First-Class Merit, Second-Class Merit, and Third-Class Merit, the reward is a special Aura-level affix (Eye of Judgment)] (Using this will have unimaginable effects, distinguishing between good and evil, and automatically displaying crime information.)

"I guarantee I'll complete the mission." Jiang Tian's eyes lit up. Good heavens, this is incredible!

He really wanted to shout, "System, you're a god!"

The Eye of Judgment seemed to overlap with the scanning radar in distinguishing between good and evil, but that wasn't the case. The Eye of Judgment could directly see a criminal's past criminal records.

This meant that no criminal could hide from Jiang Tian; he could skip the tedious work of finding evidence and conducting interrogations.

With the Eye of Judgment, combined with the scanning radar, Jiang Tian felt he was utterly invincible; anyone who saw him would die.

In this crime capital, he could practically walk around doing nothing.



Chapter 109 Drug Traffickers' Lair, They've Made a Fortune


"Of everyone in the entire Yunhai system, you're the youngest, yet you're the most outstanding," Gao Xiaolong remarked.

Yes!!!

Looking at the three First-Class Merits Jiang Tian had just received—no, there was another First-Class Merit—on his chest.

Jiang Tian had kept it at home.

Four First-Class Merits!

One Second-Class Merit.

If I remember correctly, he had also applied for a Third-Class Merit before, apparently for saving a woman in a car accident.

So admirable.

"The undercover agent's name is Yangyang, Gao Yangyang. She's my granddaughter. I hope you can help me bring her back."

Just as Jiang Tian was about to leave, he suddenly heard Gao Xiaolong's words, which shocked him.

The Chief Gao's granddaughter had been sent undercover?

At that moment, images flashed through Jiang Tian's mind—yes.

Scenes from his past life gradually surfaced. After Gao Xiaolong's sacrifice, an old woman came to the city police station. She was Gao Xiaolong's family member, and she tearfully said, "You and Yangyang must look after each other on the road. You couldn't take good care of her when she was alive, so you must take good care of her after she's gone."

Thanks to his extraordinary memory, Jiang Tian recalled even more details.

Jiang Tian turned his head and saw the sadness in Gao Xiaolong's eyes.

At that moment, the aura of mission involuntarily surged into his heart, and the sense of responsibility made him nod heavily.

After Jiang Tian left, Gao Xiaolong, his face filled with anger, directly dialed Minister Tan's number.

"Old Gao, are you satisfied with Xiao Zeng? To be honest, he's very strong. He comes from the Bajiquan family and once took down more than twenty people with his bare hands. With him on the case, there's absolutely no chance of failure," Minister Tan said with a smile.

If it weren't for such talent, and his great contribution in arresting drug dealers, why would Minister Tan value him so much and assign him such an important task?

Both his abilities and his crime-solving skills have a history.

"Old Tan, I hope what you're saying is true, that Zeng Fan is really as good as you say." Gao Xiaolong hung up the phone, his gaze lingering out the window as he muttered, "Yangyang, you must be alright, don't let anything happen to you!"

After this incident,

Gao Xiaolong had no desire to go back and immediately took command in the city police command center.

As for Jiang Tian's operation, because it was at the border, neither Jiang Tian nor Zeng Fan could use helicopters or any other form of escort, as it was overseas and border crossings were prohibited.

They couldn't even carry any identification documents.

Jiang Tian only carried two handguns, seven or eight handgun magazines, and a satellite phone to maintain contact with the city police at any time.

Jiang Tian had just finished packing when his phone suddenly rang.

Looking at the caller ID, Jiang Tian slapped his forehead.

Damn it.

Sure enough, after the call connected, Mu Ya's voice came through: "Where have you been? Weren't you going to discuss the case with me?"

Good grief.

Before Jiang Tian came out, he warned Mu Ya not to go out no matter how loud the noise was or what was happening outside, so Mu Ya had stayed in the room the whole time.

After arresting the drug dealers, Jiang Tian was busy interrogating them at the police station and had completely forgotten about Mu Ya.

"I have an important case to handle right now. Muya, if you're not going home, just rest there," Jiang Tian said and hung up the phone.

He knew Muya; she wouldn't get angry over such a small thing, and besides, it wasn't intentional to break their promise.

It was just a pity.

"Damn it, these drug dealers."

Jiang Tian had originally planned to do something unspeakable with Muya tonight, but now he genuinely didn't have the time.

Besides, there was the reward for Ling Cai's entry.

Jiang Tian knew all too well how terrifyingly difficult it was to obtain a Special Merit. It was practically a great achievement specially created for those who made game-changing contributions during the war.

A role model for the entire system, even, to exaggerate a bit, a role model promoted nationwide.

Moreover, the Special Merit wasn't completely abolished; it's just that since the war, no one has been eligible for it.

And they no longer have the opportunity.

From this, one can imagine the difficulty of obtaining a Special Merit. Even Jiang Tian didn't dare to imagine it, and now, capturing Tucha would earn him a Special Merit.

If he missed the opportunity to obtain the Special Merit Citation, it was uncertain when he would be able to get it again. Even Jiang Tian, ​​with his bad luck, might not have another chance.

Just as he crossed the border, Gao Xiaolong called: "We've already contacted someone to help Zeng Fan and the others cross. Stay put and don't move. I'll arrange for someone to take you there."

While he could choose a mode of transportation, it wasn't Jiang Tian's first choice. He hadn't paid attention to how Zeng Fan got there. For Jiang Tian, ​​the secluded jungle and covert operations were more advantageous. Besides, fifty kilometers—frankly, if Jiang Tian went at full speed, he could probably reach the destination in about three hours.

Taking transportation also carried the risk of exposure for Jiang Tian.

"No need, I'll walk," Jiang Tian said.

"Fifty kilometers? You're running all the way?" Gao Xiaolong asked, astonished.

"That's right. I can take a shortcut; it won't be much slower," Jiang Tian replied directly.

It was the rainy season in summer, and much of the ground was unpaved mud. Taking the main road was risky; there was no guarantee of encountering any unexpected dangers.

Jiang Tian moved through the jungle like a cheetah, thanks to his extraordinary memories.

Following a pre-prepared map, Jiang Tian took shortcuts along the way. According to Pan Laoji, this shortcut was a route they used to transport drugs.

“Almost there.”

Jiang Tian didn't begin his operation until 1 AM. Around 4 AM, he glanced at the time, his eyes brightening slightly.

Jiang Tian crouched on a tree, like a predator ready to hunt, wearing his training clothes, his eyes fixed on the dark town of Walu in the distance.

The hunt was about to begin.

Actually, what surprised Jiang Tian most at this moment was seeing the almost densely packed red crime values ​​on the radar screen regarding the village.

Good heavens, they were truly densely packed.

Is the average crime rate per person high?

Jiang Tian was genuinely astonished.

However, Jiang Tian soon came to understand that, thanks to the chaotic situation and environment here, the warlord-like system, and the virtually unregulated criminal activities, the situation was detrimental.

Just considering the drug manufacturing plants alone, Jiang Tian observed at least several large ones.

If all of them were dealt with...

Jiang Tian couldn't help but click his tongue. If he dealt with all these people, he could at least upgrade one gold-level attribute.

Releasing nearly 100,000 sin points wouldn't be a problem at all.



Chapter 110 Are you crazy? Terrifyingly strong, slaughtering indiscriminately.


"I'm rich." Jiang Tian licked his lips. If it weren't for rescuing the undercover agent, he would have already acted.

Based on Pan Laoji's confession, Jiang Tian quickly located the parts factory Pan Laoji had mentioned.

It was located in the town.

"Damn it, right, there's Zeng Fan too. I'd better wait for him." Jiang Tian suppressed his urge to act.

He planned to wait until Zeng Fan acted, then rescue the undercover agent. If not, he could still act later, taking advantage of the chaos to reap the rewards.

"Chief Gao, where are Zeng Fan and the others?" Jiang Tian asked, holding a satellite phone, hiding outside the parts factory.

Gao Xiaolong, who had been working tirelessly for over two days, was pacing back and forth in the conference room, hands behind his back.

When he suddenly heard Jiang Tian's voice...

Gao Xiaolong's expression instantly turned grim. "Zeng Fan and his men lost all contact an hour ago. We've completely lost touch with him."

"Lost contact?" Jiang Tian was bewildered. Weren't these people picked up by their contact here?

"That's right. Not only have we lost contact with them, but also with their contact. We suspect that contact has betrayed us," Gao Xiaolong said in a low voice.

It's over.

At this moment, Gao Xiaolong's heart sank completely.

Because he knew it was all over.

Actually, Jiang Tian's actions were just a backup plan. Even if the actual operation was unreliable, Gao Xiaolong had pinned his hopes on Zeng Fan.

Minister Tan and Gao Xiaolong knew that Jiang Tian's individual abilities might be stronger than Zeng Fan's, but Zeng Fan had an advantage Jiang Tian lacked: his entire squad of twelve were all experienced veterans, all highly skilled.

They could even be considered a special operations team within the General Headquarters.

There were simply too many adversaries. For one person to cross the border to rescue an undercover agent was an impossible task.

But now, before even taking action, the person was gone?

"This...could there be a misunderstanding?" Jiang Tian asked, bewildered.

"No, our communication with them is satellite communication, just like yours. Even if we lost contact with one of them, it's impossible for Zeng Fan to lose contact with the contact person as well." Gao Xiaolong had to prepare for the worst.

Even worse, Zeng Fan had already been lost inside.

Gao Xiaolong's gaze struggled for a moment before he said in a deep voice, "Xiao Jiang, although I hate to admit it, you should come back first. We need to plan this carefully. I've already requested assistance from the military, sending special forces to see if there's still a chance."

He said that, but Gao Xiaolong knew that even if the special forces arrived, who knew when? By the time they discussed the operational details, it would be too late.

Oh no, Gao Yangyang's body might be cold and untraceable.

But there was no other way; Tucha was too terrifying.

It was terrifying; their team members were all gone before they even got close.

Importing suspects across borders is inherently a dangerous operation, let alone under these circumstances.

"Sir, I'm already at the parts factory. Wait..." Jiang Tian was speaking when suddenly he saw several cars appear on the road, speeding towards the parts factory.

Jiang Tian swiftly appeared outside the factory wall.

"Just as I expected, a group has arrived."

"That good-for-nothing Pan Laoji should have been exposed. The boss is waiting inside. Arrest these people."

"Now only Pan Laoji hasn't returned. He's probably been arrested, and the cops have crossed the border to chase after him."

"Damn it, if I hadn't been keeping an eye on him, I wouldn't have guessed he was a cop too. Thanks for your help with this."

The conversation gradually faded, but based on the information Jiang Tian heard...

Jiang Tian, ​​a criminal investigation genius combined with his extraordinary memory, made his mind work incredibly fast. From just a few sentences, Jiang Tian deduced...

This wasn't a betrayal by the contact, according to what these people said.

They must have been discovered and monitored beforehand.

And this group driving the transport vehicle probably wasn't from Tucha, or rather, this contact belonged to another force.

Jiang Tian's eyes became clearer. Jiang Tian roughly deduced the situation: there might be a force on the border with good relations with Tucha.

The contact was an undercover agent within that organization, but was detected and monitored. When he arranged to transport Zeng Fan and his group, he was discovered by the organization.

Later, for some unknown reason, they learned Zeng Fan's objective was Tucha and sent Zeng Fan and his group directly there.

Another crucial piece of information is that the boss is already at the factory.

That means the captured undercover agent is also here.

A series of loud thuds followed, which Jiang Tian guessed were the sounds of bodies being thrown to the ground. Shortly after, several vehicles left the factory.

While the lookouts were opening the gate, Jiang Tian had already reached the factory wall. He glanced at its height; it wasn't high, only about two meters.

Soon, a series of vehicles drove out of the factory. A voice echoed from inside: "You gatekeepers, be careful! Be very careful. Those people won't let this go. Remember, if you see any suspicious individuals acting suspiciously, shoot them immediately."

"We understand."

The two security guards operating the factory gates immediately agreed. After the vehicles drove away completely, they ran to close the gates immediately.

"Following Boss Tucha was definitely the right choice!"

One of the drug dealers closing the gates exclaimed.

"Yeah, damn it, these guys wanted to be cops to rescue and arrest people, and they got caught as soon as they entered the country, hahaha." Another bodyguard laughed.

His words were full of contempt.

After all, thanks to Yunhai's lax oversight, it had become a haven for criminals, and these people in this lawless, chaotic area had become incredibly wealthy.

Even shootouts had decreased significantly.

Pfft!!!

"Heh heh!!!"

The bodyguard, who had only closed half the door, clutched his neck, letting out a terrifying sound.

Thud!!!

Jiang Tian pulled the dagger from the drug dealer's neck.

Ding...

[+78 Sin Points]

Even a doorman has close to three digits.

Jiang Tian was instantly excited.

"You!!!"

The other person closing the door pointed at Jiang Tian, ​​trying to shout.

But the next moment, a thud.

Jiang Tian's dagger was already plunged into his throat.

Jiang Tian followed suit, quickly covering his mouth.

They then slowly dragged the two bodies into the bushes.

"There are at least a hundred drug dealers in there," Jiang Tian said, looking at the red dots.

Especially since many of them were a deep, purplish-red.

Old Pan hadn't lied to him.

Today really was the day for reconciliation; it seemed like all of Tucha's men who could come were here.

Jiang Tian pushed open the door, glanced around cautiously, and then slowly walked in.

Sure enough, just as the scanning radar had shown, in the yard of the parts factory, a dozen or so fully armed drug dealers, carried in twos and threes, hadn't noticed Jiang Tian's entrance.

Indeed, too much peace makes people complacent.

Five hundred armed men—and that's just Tucha's own armed forces; it doesn't even include Tucha's other forces outside.

For example, the drug manufacturing workers, those collecting money, and those selling drugs on the sidelines.

This could even be called a small-scale warlord operation.

“Ata, you two aren’t out there. It’s not your shift yet.” A drug dealer, half-lying behind the door, feigning sleep, felt a sudden darkness and awoke with a start.

But the next moment, he instantly sensed something was wrong.

He was about to stand up to say something when, in the next instant, Jiang Tian plunged a dagger into his heart with lightning speed.

To be honest,

the number of people on guard duty in the courtyard was truly unbearable to look at; there were about ten people in total.

Although somewhat scattered, some were sitting on the ground, some were still feigning sleep, and some were even actually asleep against the wall.

Four in the morning is the easiest time to feel sleepy.

Three seconds later, without disturbing anyone.

Even a drug dealer next to him, also asleep, had a dagger pierced through his throat by Jiang Tian, ​​who simultaneously covered his mouth.

"Quadruple kill!!!"

Jiang Tian silently noted it down in his mind. Easy, too easy. Ever since his physique was perfected, it was as if all the shackles binding Jiang Tian had been completely removed.

Whether it was strength, speed, or all four dimensions of his fighting style, everything had increased exponentially.

Now, Jiang Tian felt terrifyingly strong.

"I'll use you guys to test the waters first."



Chapter 111 Gao Yangyang's whereabouts, fighting her way out.


Just as Jiang Tian gently set down the fourth person, the drug dealer who had been leaning against a pillar, seemingly asleep a few steps away, yawned groggily.

His vision was blurry; he only vaguely saw several people fall to the ground before him, and he instinctively rubbed his eyes.

At that moment, he saw clearly.

Four people, yes, four people, lying neatly on the ground.

Each person had their hands stiffly clutching their neck, but at this moment, all vital signs had vanished.

"Come..."

He instinctively opened his mouth, wanting to call out.

But just then...

A hand suddenly covered his mouth.

*Pfft!!!*

The slight sound, the sharp pain, made the drug dealer want to scream, but unfortunately, his mouth was already covered.

Even though he desperately tried to break free of the arm and shout—yes, to alert his companion who was only two steps away, leaning against the pillar feigning sleep...

Unfortunately, even with all his strength, he couldn't budge the arm covering his mouth.

"Ugh...ugh!!!"

He struggled to make a sound, but couldn't. His struggles were rapidly weakening, and he could feel his life slipping away.

"Shh...be good, don't make a sound."

In his final moments, a soft voice finally reached the drug dealer's ear.

With the sound faded, he lost all life.

"Pfft!!!" Jiang Tian muttered silently.

"Gao Zi, if you're really tired, find a place to rest. The boss told me to stand guard today, saying someone was coming to make an operation. Those people have been arrested, so there's no danger. I'll come wake you up later."

The drug dealer, feigning sleep just a pillar away, continued speaking calmly.

The entire courtyard was silent.

Ten drug dealers stood in their positions.

Overall, the compound was quite large; the entire parts factory, in terms of area alone, covered at least several thousand square meters.

These dozen or so drug dealers were just on guard duty inside the main gate. Jiang Tian's scanning radar showed others scattered throughout the factory, on guard duty in various corners.

Jiang Tian didn't know the specifics, but he planned to take it slow. It was only four o'clock; summer nights are usually short, and the sun would rise around five.

Jiang Tian had less than an hour to operate. If it got light, it would be very disadvantageous.

However, an hour was enough for Jiang Tian.

"Gao Zi..."

Jiang Tian slowly put down the drug dealer. He knew this dealer was calling for the one he had just killed.

After calling out without response, the dealer tried to turn around, but just then, he felt something wet on his buttocks.

"Damn it, where did this water come from?"

The drug dealer cursed, reaching out to brace himself on the ground, trying to stand up. But the next moment, his whole body stiffened; his palms felt sticky when he braced himself on the ground.

At the same time, a pungent smell of blood assaulted his nostrils, sending chills down his spine.

As a seasoned and experienced drug dealer, he immediately recognized the blood on his hands. The fact that Gao Zi hadn't responded to his calls meant it was definitely Gao Zi's blood.

That meant Gao Zi was dead.

Someone had entered, killed Gao Zi, and might even be right behind him.

When?

The drug dealer's pupils contracted, but based on his experience, he almost instinctively wanted to roll away to ensure his safety.

But the thought had barely formed in his mind, and his body had barely begun to move...

*Thud!*

The drug dealer clutched his neck.

Blood flowed from his neck, and he finally saw clearly that four people lay lifeless in front of him.

They were all neatly arranged.

When?

He hadn't slept at all, and he even clearly remembered that just a few dozen seconds ago, he had been laughing and joking with one of those four people.

A few dozen seconds.

Four people?

Oh no, there's Gao Zi behind him, and him too?

Six people.

In a few dozen seconds, six people were killed silently. A ghost?

What on earth is this?

His neck was stiff with breathlessness, his mind was a blur. Who was it? Who was it?

With his eyes glazed over, he lost all signs of life forever.

Six.

[Sin Value +130]

Jiang Tian acted swiftly, especially seeing his sin value steadily accumulating—it was incredibly satisfying.

Even though Yunhai City was known as the City of Sin, opportunities for Jiang Tian to rack up points were rare.

Yes, that's right. For Jiang Tian, ​​this was like racking up points. It was simple: they were called armed personnel, but in reality, most of them hadn't received much special training.

They were practically freaks. Even if there were mercenaries here, with Jiang Tian's current strength, he wouldn't feel much pressure.

The remaining four were even simpler for Jiang Tian. He had already observed them while he was moving, and the first ones he eliminated were all conscious.

The remaining four men fell into a deep sleep.

If this were in China, Jiang Tian would have considered whether to spare some for later arrest and interrogation.

But here, Jiang Tian acted with almost no restraint.

The remaining four guards were dealt with in their sleep, leaving only one man alive.

He had barely opened his eyes when a knife was placed against his neck.

A hand covered his mouth, preventing him from screaming.

"Mmm...mmm..." he screamed in terror.

Just out of the corner of his eye, he saw his companions, who had been laughing and joking with him not long ago, now lying neatly in the corner, the entire yard stained with bright red blood.

"Mmm...mmm..." the drug dealer stammered, terrified.

"I'll ask you questions, answer them, don't scream, okay?" Jiang Tian asked.

Thankfully, although it was overseas, the language barrier was minimal, except for some dialects, but Jiang Tian could easily understand them if he listened carefully.

“Blink once if you agree,” Jiang Tian continued.

The drug dealer blinked, his eyes filled with terror.

“Where is the female undercover agent your boss arrested?” Jiang Tian asked.

“Waaaaah!!!” The drug dealer seemed to want to speak.

*Pfft!!!*

Jiang Tian pierced the drug dealer's hand with a single stab, leaving the dealer completely bewildered.

"No, I mean, how am I supposed to talk if you don't cover my mouth?"

The drug dealer was both confused and aggrieved.

"Oh, oh, sorry, I forgot." Jiang Tian apologetically pulled out the dagger and released the drug dealer's hand from his mouth.

Huff, huff, huff!!!

The drug dealer gasped for breath, his face filled with terror as he pleaded, "If I tell you, will you let me go?"

"I swear on ten years of my life, tell me now, or I'll kill you right now," Jiang Tian said bluntly.

The drug dealer knew he had no choice, and tremblingly pointed in a direction.



Chapter 112 The Greatest Enemy? No, He's Dead Now.


“We’ve all seen that female undercover agent. She’s incredibly beautiful, and many of the big shots want her, but Boss Tucha said that at the end of the month, whoever performs best gets the reward, so she’s being kept in that villa.”

“You’re relatively honest,” Jiang Tian nodded in satisfaction.

Then, narrowing his eyes, he noticed the villa’s location was vast; there was absolutely no hiding place nearby—it was all flat land.

No, it was less of a villa and more of an estate.

Located in the very center of the parts factory, or rather, the drug-making factory was the interior of the estate.

And to enter the villa…

Jiang Tian realized that the area was too spacious. He needed to clear out the sentries near the parts factory first, otherwise, he would be an easy target for them.

Jiang Tian had just observed that the sentries there weren’t like those here; they weren’t ordinary drug dealers. They all stood menacingly around the villa, their eyes fixed on the outside.

"Can I go now? I swear, I won't tip you off," the drug dealer asked, trembling.

A drug dealer's underling, he was genuinely terrified of this terrifying killer.

He was ruthless, killing without blinking an eye after stabbing him in the arm.

Don't your eyes hurt?

"Go, I keep my promise," Jiang Tian nodded.

The drug dealer seemed incredulous: "Big brother, you really won't kill me?"

"Go, go," Jiang Tian said with a reassuring and gentle expression.

The drug dealer cautiously took two steps back. Seeing that Jiang Tian didn't attack, he repeatedly thanked him: "Thank you, big brother, thank you, big brother! Good deeds are rewarded. I wish you wealth and smooth sailing. Thank you, big brother!"

The drug dealer was overjoyed, then scrambled to his feet and ran towards the villa.

But the next moment...

*Thud!*

The drug dealer clutched his neck, turning to look at Jiang Tian in disbelief. He tried to say something, but only gurgled blood; no sound came out.

“Kid, you think I’m stupid? Running towards the villa? You mean running? You were the one tipping me off, weren’t you?” Jiang Tian walked over and pulled out a dagger.

The drug dealer closed his eyes.

[Sin Value +380]

“He deserved to die,” Jiang Tian cursed.

Three hundred and eighty sin values—how many people had he harmed?

This wasn’t China; otherwise, Jiang Tian would have made him face legal judgment.

After clearing out the last person at the main entrance, Jiang Tian then eliminated the dozen or so guards standing watch around the other parts factory areas.

With the scanning radar and Jiang Tian’s methods, it was safe to say that even if you buried yourself in the ground, Jiang Tian could find you with pinpoint accuracy.

However, because the factory was large, it took Jiang Tian nearly ten minutes to complete the entire circuit.

The gains were substantial, approaching five thousand crime points.

There were over twenty drug dealers in total, some contributing as much as six or seven hundred.

One can imagine the countless heinous crimes these people had committed.

Jiang Tian stood like a ghost in the darkness at the entrance of the parts factory.

He didn't go in, because the scanning radar accurately indicated that two drug dealers were behind that door.

Beyond this, deeper still, were several constantly moving red dots.

Undoubtedly, this was the hideout for the parts handlers, because Jiang Tian had clearly seen Zeng Fan and the other thirteen people being transported in through this door.

However, for Jiang Tian, ​​Zeng Fan and his group weren't the target.

Could he rescue Zeng Fan and the others now?

Undoubtedly, it wouldn't be difficult, because the figures moving back and forth in the scanning radar were likely just people doing the parts assembly work.

There were probably only two drug dealers guarding behind the door, which Jiang Tian could easily handle with his skills. But what if he did? He would definitely be exposed.

Even if he was exposed, Jiang Tian wasn't afraid; these people didn't pose a significant threat to him.

But Jiang Tian's primary target was Gao Yangyang.

Frankly, sending Gao Xiaolong's own granddaughter out as an undercover agent—with that kind of behavior and that kind of heart, how could Jiang Tian really let Gao Yangyang sacrifice herself here?

According to normal operational procedures, rescuing Zeng Fan and the others first would maximize safety.

However, if they were exposed and Gao Yangyang wasn't found, it was entirely possible that the drug dealers would take the risk and kill her.

Thinking of this, Jiang Tian didn't focus his attention on the factory, but rather on a villa next to it.

Just now, Jiang Tian hadn't approached the villa area; he had first cleared out the sentries near the parts factory to ensure no one was behind him.

Those inside the factory wouldn't be able to come out anytime soon, but Jiang Tian was confident he could enter the villa in a few minutes.

Inside the villa, almost every icon was a deep, almost purple red.

They were all major drug lords.

There were at least twenty red dots, and based on Jiang Tian's experience, each dot alone represented a crime worth at least five or six thousand.

In the darkness, Jiang Tian stood in a corner of the factory, looking at the spacious villa complex in the distance.

Directly in front of the villa was an artificial hill. The main entrance had two paths that wound around the hill, eventually converging into a wide avenue, about ten meters wide.

Unlike the grotesque figures from before, two men in camouflage stood on either side of the artificial hill, one on each side.

"It's them."

Even in the darkness, the bright headlights illuminated the area, making it crystal clear.

This allowed Jiang Tian to make out their faces.

Yes.

They were four of the mercenaries who had surrounded the anti-narcotics squad a few days ago when they were ambushed by drug dealers.

That group of mercenaries numbered about ten, and Jiang Tian had killed two of them.

The remaining eight were all quite skilled, and they quickly retreated after Jiang Tian began his attack.

Thump, thump, thump!!

Just then, two more men in camouflage emerged from the villa.

They whispered something to each other.

But one of the mercenaries who had just come out caught Jiang Tian's attention. No doubt about it.

In his past life, Jiang Tian was shot in the back. At that moment, Gao Xiaolong kicked Jiang Tian away, but was himself shot in the spleen and died from excessive blood loss.

That shot was fired at this man.

After his rebirth, Jiang Tian, ​​with his unerring aim, slaughtered his way through the land, and deliberately searched for the right person, but ultimately failed.

But now, he finally saw it.

"It's him." As the saying goes, enemies meeting are always filled with hatred, and Jiang Tian's eyes reddened.



Chapter 113's 1V200 battle record is verifiable.


If Gao Xiaolong hadn't died in his past life, none of this would have happened—the successive deaths of his parents, the tragic death of his sister—none of these tragedies would have occurred.

Because his parents were alive, that bitch Wang Yifei wouldn't have dared to act so recklessly, and Jiang Tian wouldn't have been so passive.

However, Jiang Tian didn't act rashly.

Because the place where Jiang Tian was hiding was about ten meters away from the two mercenaries on duty, it would take him three seconds to rush over.

Although Jiang Tian carried a pistol with a silencer, firing it, even with a silencer, would definitely give away his position in the silent night.

After mentally planning his route, Jiang Tian seized the opportunity during the shift change. While the four men were talking, Jiang Tian sprinted forward, moving so fast he almost became a blur.

In less than two seconds, Jiang Tian instantly covered the ten meters and then jumped directly into a small ditch beside the villa.

"What's wrong?"

"Nothing, probably just a hallucination."

One of the mercenaries rubbed his eyes. He thought he'd seen a dark shadow out of the corner of his eye, but it vanished instantly.

"You're probably too tired. Go back and rest. I'll take over the shift." The substitute mercenary replied.

"Today's the day the big boss gathers all his men to reconcile accounts. A group of people from the north just came by. Be careful."

"I'll handle things perfectly. You go and rest." The substitute mercenary said with a smile.

After the shift was over, only two mercenaries remained, standing on either side of the two roads leading to the villas, their eyes fixed on the parts factory.

"They're elite, after all." Jiang Tian's gaze was deep.

Jiang Tian slowly moved to the mercenary's feet, crawling along a small ditch beside the road.

Looking at that familiar face, the face of his enemy.

He slowly drew his dagger, then took a step forward.

Jiang Tian's movements were lightning fast. He covered the mercenary's mouth with one hand, the other holding a dagger, expecting a direct stab to the heart.

However, the mercenary's wrists were suddenly gripped tightly by both hands.

"You're an elite, after all. You even reacted. But what good does it do? Can you withstand my strength?" Jiang Tian spoke slowly in the mercenary's ear without slowing his movements.

"Who is it?"

The mercenary's eyes narrowed. Surprisingly, he remained remarkably calm, gripping Jiang Tian's wrists holding the dagger.

Almost instinctively, a series of counter-attack techniques flashed through his mind: pry open the mercenary's hand holding the dagger and break it, then strike his ribs with his elbow. As the mercenary bent over, he would use the dagger to kill him—no, perhaps he should spare his life for interrogation.

Thinking this, the mercenary was immediately bewildered.

Terrifying strength.

Even though his opponent was one-handed, the mercenary found himself stumped at the very first step of his fighting routine—a move that involved twisting and breaking the opponent's wrist with one hand before striking. He was gripping the wrist with both hands, yet he couldn't even budge a single finger.

This was embarrassing.

"Ugh! Ugh! Ugh!!" The mercenary watched as the dagger slowly moved, gradually touching the skin near his heart. He was terrified.

He struggled incessantly, even shaking his legs, trying to warn his companions.

Who? Who could it be?

The mercenary thought in terror.

"I'll let you die knowing why. A 1v200 kill record is verifiable." Jiang Tian spoke without stopping his actions.

*Thud!*

The instant the words left his lips, the dagger had already pierced the mercenary's heart.

"Ugh! Ugh! Ugh! Ugh!" The mercenary's pupils contracted, and he gradually stopped struggling, but his eyes seemed to slowly understand.

Once all struggle was gone, Jiang Tian covered the mercenary's mouth, intending to put him to the ground.

But the next moment, the mercenary seemed to unleash all the strength he had before death, kicking heavily onto a small stone by the roadside.

The faint sound of the stone drew the attention of the mercenary standing guard nearby, who quickly looked over.

"Jimmy!"

As he shouted, the mercenary quickly cocked his gun.

But the next moment, a dark shadow attacked.

Before the mercenary could react, a dark figure leaped almost into the air from the artificial hill, clamping his legs around the mercenary's neck.

*Crack!!!*

The mercenary's neck snapped, but it was too late.

*Bang bang bang!!!*

The automatic rifle in the mercenary's hand, its neck broken, spewed fire.

When Jiang Tian stood up, the mercenary was lifeless.

"Damn it." Jiang Tian's face was grim.

It wasn't that he was afraid of being exposed, but rather that Gao Yangyang would be in danger if he were exposed. For Jiang Tian, ​​dealing with these people, whether exposed or not, had little impact on him.

He dared not delay. Now that he was exposed, Jiang Tian drew his silenced pistol and quickly headed towards the villa.

Meanwhile.

Four o'clock in the morning.

Inside the penthouse of the villa in the parts factory, a tall woman with long hair obscuring her face, dressed in a cheongsam, was handcuffed to the bed, one hand on her wrist. She crouched below the bed, head bowed.

Her once beautiful, peach-blossom eyes were now numb, or rather, devoid of any light, somewhat grayish-white.

"Ayawn!!!"

Across the room from the bed, on a long sofa, two drug dealers were also yawning.

One of them, having just finished using the restroom, returned to the sofa, approached the woman, grabbed her hair, and yanked her head up.

Long hair cascaded down her face, revealing a strikingly beautiful face, both strikingly beautiful and fiercely handsome.

The only flaw was that the woman was expressionless, like a walking corpse.

Slap!!!

The drug dealer raised his hand and slapped her across the flawless white face.

The slap was loud, but the woman didn't even flinch. Her eyes remained calm and indifferent, as if she had already accepted life and death.

The drug dealer's expression grew colder. "You wretched woman, look at you like that. Give me a smile."

The woman remained expressionless.

Since her identity was discovered and she was captured, the woman had accepted her fate.

Being captured within the country was already worse than death; being captured abroad was even worse. To be dead was something she longed for.

Because it wasn't unprecedented. The miserable state of her predecessor after being discovered was still unforgettable for her.

“Brother Yu, this woman’s really beautiful. She used to hang out with Old Pan, never had a chance. Now that we two are guarding her, how about we have some fun?” The drug dealer’s eyes gleamed greedily as he spoke.

The other drug dealer, yawning on the sofa, perked up immediately. He was about to reprimand him, but upon seeing the woman’s face, he instinctively swallowed.

Brother Yu’s heart pounded, but he still hesitated: “Old Pan said we can’t touch this woman.”

“Brother Yu, this damn woman, since the undercover agent has been discovered, her fate is sealed. The boss said whoever performs best gets her. Maybe you, Brother Yu, perform better than Old Pan, so she’ll be yours in the end. We’re just having some fun ahead of time.” “Foolish Rabbit,” he said, swallowing hard, his eyes gleaming as he reached out.

He seemed to want to touch her fair, pretty face.

But the next moment…

Pfft!!!

The woman, crouching on the floor with her hands bound to the bed, kicked Sha Tu squarely in the groin.

"Ah, you bitch, I'll kill you!" Sha Tu screamed, clutching his groin.

Then he grabbed a nearby stool and smashed it down on the woman's head.

With a thud, blood streamed down the woman's skull.

"Good, good, good, I love women this fierce. It's satisfying to discipline them, hahaha. Sha Tu, you fucking get out of the way, let me deal with this bitch." Brother Yu took a pill from his pocket, dissolved it in water, then pinched the woman's mouth shut and forced it down her throat.

"Cough cough cough!!!" Gao Yangyang coughed incessantly, completely losing hope.

Looking at the two drug dealers' grinning faces, she gave a bitter laugh. Was even dying so difficult, requiring such humiliation?

But then, Gao Yangyang felt a bulge in the ground. She touched it, then gripped it tightly, letting out a sigh of relief.

"Go do it in your next life."

Gao Yangyang sneered, grabbing a sharp wooden stick from the ground, about to plunge it into her neck.

But the next moment...

Bang bang bang!!!

Intense gunfire erupted.

Accompanying it were shouts from inside the villa.

"Enemy attack! Enemy attack!"

"Quickly, everyone grab your weapons!"

"Damn it, is that a ghost? Fire! He's on the first floor! Fire!"



Chapter 114 News from the informant reaches the country, causing a sensation.


Intense gunfire erupted from the villa, accompanied by the mercenary's sudden fire before his death, alerting the entire villa complex.

Jiang Tian, ​​now without any reservations, grabbed a silenced pistol. Inside the house, a pistol was often far more convenient than a rifle.

Especially for Jiang Tian, ​​whose aim was impeccable, he was practically a killing machine.

But simultaneously, everyone in the villa was alerted.

Jiang Tian's plan was to infiltrate the villa, slowly searching room by room, eliminating the sleeping drug dealers.

However, a slight delay led to Jiang Tian's exposure.

Jiang Tian had no choice but to engage in a fierce firefight with the drug dealers gathered in the villa.

Amidst the deafening gunfire,

the parts factory not far from the villa was also completely alarmed. The drug dealers working overtime inside rushed in frantically, rifles in hand.

Many were wearing white coats.

However, Jiang Tian had already stormed into the villa, which was actually his initial purpose in clearing out the drug dealers around the factory.

While a fierce firefight was taking place at the villa,

the city police station was brightly lit.

Gao Xiaolong, who hadn't rested or slept all night, had bloodshot eyes.

His eyes were fixed intently on the map.

Beside him, Shen Han rushed over immediately. There was no other way; besides Old Gao, Old Mu was also there, especially with a special operations team dispatched from headquarters.

Upon learning this, Shen Han arrived immediately, first giving Jiang Peng a resentful look. This guy had only informed him at the last minute.

"Don't you understand the importance of reporting to higher-ups?"

"According to informants and investigations, since today is the day for the audit, all the drug dealer bosses in the entire parts factory area are here, seemingly observing from the outside. There are about a hundred drug dealers who have entered the parts factory, not counting the armed personnel within the factory itself."

At this moment, Jiang Peng looked at the map on the table in the conference room.

The red dot, in particular, was exceptionally bright.

This location was the parts factory.

“It’s not just that. Don’t forget, in the small town surrounding the parts factory, there are nearly five hundred armed men. In the event of a firefight, these five hundred armed drug traffickers could gather instantly.”

Just then, Old Mu suddenly looked up and reminded him.

The moment these words were spoken, everyone in the conference room fell silent.

All sound vanished.

“Moreover, according to information from our informants, it’s confirmed that thirteen people have been transported to the parts factory and are being prepared for processing.” Jiang Peng’s tone grew increasingly serious.

The situation at the parts factory was becoming clearer.

Gao Xiaolong’s brow twitched occasionally.

The conference room fell even more silent, signifying that one thing was certain.

“Zeng Fan and the others have indeed been captured. Now only Xiao Jiang remains there.” Gao Xiaolong closed his eyes, seemingly aging countless years in that instant.

Was Zeng Fan wrong? No, he wasn't.

Because nobody expected that the person in charge of transportation would betray them.

"The key issue is, we can't contact Xiao Jiang now!" Old Mu said in a deep voice.

"What did the informant say?" Gao Xiaolong opened his eyes, a hint of sadness in them.

Because of the situation, he understood that his granddaughter was likely in grave danger.

Even the rescuers were stuck in the mire, barely able to protect themselves—or rather, their chances of survival were slim.

The crucial point was that this couldn't be said openly, because everyone leaving the country was unidentified; their identities were kept secret.

On one hand, Jiang Tian hadn't traveled by car, but on the other hand, the situation was already a dead end.

The mission hadn't even begun, and it could already be considered a failure.

"The informant is only in the outermost town, but with Xiao Jiang's skills, even if something had happened, there should have been some activity. The lack of news is good news, I suppose," Jiang Peng said, his voice suppressed.

That's right.

After all, Jiang Tian was his son!

Seeing his son in such a dangerous situation, Jiang Peng suddenly understood the immense pressure and pain Gao Xiaolong was enduring.

Gao Xiaolong also looked apologetic, because if it weren't for rescuing the undercover agent and his granddaughter, this operation wouldn't have happened.

Ring ring ring!!!

Just then, Jiang Peng's phone rang.

Everyone held their breath.

This was the phone Jiang Peng used to communicate directly with his informant.

Everyone had seen Jiang Peng obtain information from his informant through this phone.

"It's here." Jiang Peng reached out, his hand trembling involuntarily.

The next moment, Jiang Peng answered the phone but didn't speak, quietly listening to the sounds on the other end.

"Bang bang bang!!!"

Just then.

A suffocating thing happened; a series of chaotic gunshots rang out from the other end of the line.

A moment later, the gunshots became a dense, continuous barrage.

Something had happened.

Everyone realized the problem.

And everyone's faces tense as they waited for the voice on the other end of the phone.

Not long after.

A moment later, accompanied by heavy breathing, a voice came through the receiver: "01, 01, a fierce gunfight has broken out at the parts factory. It's very fierce. Many drug dealers have surrounded the parts factory."

Buzz!!!

Upon hearing this,

everyone's mind exploded.

Because there weren't many outsiders in the meeting room, Jiang Peng had told them about the informant's background, and everyone knew.

The informant's hiding place was in a nearby town, but because of the problems that had arisen tonight, Jiang Peng risked sending this informant from the nearby town to Walu Town to gather information.

No, it should be said, to go to the outskirts of Walu Town, especially near the parts factory, to hide and observe the situation so that they could get updates at any time, especially now that Jiang Tian had lost all contact.

And now.

When the news arrived, everyone's minds went blank.

No news is good news.

But there was news.

"Explain clearly," Jiang Peng said urgently, his eyes red.

Because, after confirming that Zeng Fan and the others had been captured and controlled, their only remaining manpower there, the only person capable of causing chaos in the parts factory, was Jiang Tian!

"01, I'm hiding in a residential building a few hundred meters away from the parts factory. From here, I can see the entire factory. The parts factory is actually a large estate, consisting of villas and factory buildings. The current gunfight and the location of the reinforcements are in the villa area," the informant said.

*Buzz!*

Everyone stood up.



Chapter 115 Death's Roll Call, the More the Merrier


Everyone stood up.

Eyes darted around them.

Gao Xiaolong, in particular, had tears welling in his eyes: "Xiao Jiang, he's...foolish!"

This was practically suicide!

Jiang Tian, ​​who was there, how could he not know the danger?

But knowing the danger, he still rushed into the villa area, which could only prove one thing: he was going to rescue his granddaughter, to rescue Gao Yangyang!

Thinking of this, how could Gao Xiaolong remain calm?

"Xiao Jiang, he..."

"He acted, he acted!"

"How is this possible? Shouldn't he be back at this time?"

"That's the drug dealers' hideout!"

Everyone went crazy.

Because initially, they weren't worried.

In this situation, after a simple analysis, with Zeng Fan and his group exposed and arrested, and the drug dealers already on alert, it was impossible for Jiang Tian alone, let alone to replan the operation.

This mission could already be declared abandoned.

They assumed Jiang Tian would have already begun his retreat under these circumstances.

But now, they were being told that Jiang Tian had entered the factory.

This was insane!

That was the drug traffickers' hideout!

And he was going to try and rescue people there? How could he possibly do that?

"Okay, you must be careful and stay safe. Don't get caught in the crossfire. Report any news immediately," Jiang Peng said urgently.

Meanwhile, Song Liguo was also nearby, repeatedly dialing Jiang Tian's number using a satellite phone.

Unfortunately, Jiang Tian's phone was always switched off.

Now.

They were almost certain.

Silence reigned, a deathly stillness; the entire conference room was so quiet you could hear a pin drop.

Jiang Peng placed his encrypted phone on the conference table, on speakerphone, from which came a constant barrage of intense gunfire.

Very intense.

How intense?

Bang bang bang!!!

The gunfire was almost continuous.

Meanwhile, Jiang Tian stood in the first-floor villa, thoroughly searching the entire ground floor.

He had killed nearly a dozen drug dealers.

This villa was enormous.

While it was called a villa complex, it was essentially just this one massive villa.

It was a mausoleum complex, with only two smaller, outbuilding-like villas on either side of the open ground.

Jiang Tian was standing on the ground floor of the villa, and the ground floor alone had more than thirty rooms.

Jiang Tian searched through the rooms; almost every room was luxuriously furnished.

Luxurious.

But this luxury was built on the murder of others, bought bit by bit with their blood. In Jiang Tian's eyes, it reeked of filth.

"If it's not on the first floor, then it's on the second." Jiang Tian stood at the bottom of the stairs, pressed against the wall, holding a silenced pistol.

Below his feet lay a horrific pool of blood.

The battle had been intense, and it had taken Jiang Tian considerable time to clear out the drug dealers who had recovered, room by room.

This further slowed him down.

However, thanks to the villa's sheer size—more than a villa, it was practically a luxurious palace—

everywhere was opulent and glittering. The villa's vast size provided Jiang Tian with ample room to maneuver.

It could be said that either a strategic weapon would bring peace to this villa, or Jiang Tian would be in serious trouble.

"We can't delay any longer," Jiang Tian glanced at the time.

Instantly realizing that Gao Yangyang was in imminent danger, Jiang Tian's expression turned serious.

The next moment, without any apparent movement, Jiang Tian, ​​with his back to the stairs, pressed against the wall, his hands just enough to grab the iron railing, and with a slight effort, instantly flipped and leaped up the stairs.

As soon as he landed, a drug dealer stared blankly at the radiant Jiang Tian appearing before him.

However, before his mind could even process what had happened,

he didn't have time to think about how an enemy had suddenly leaped up from under the railing so quickly.

Jiang Tian had already made his move.

With a swift movement, Jiang Tian delivered a powerful elbow strike to the center of the armed drug dealer's chest.

*Crack!!!*

*Thud!!!*

The drug dealer coughed up a mouthful of blood, his body flying two or three meters upwards down the stairs. The force was so great that he stopped breathing before even hitting the ground.

"Dead."

The drug dealers, cautiously hiding behind the wall on the stairs, had their pupils contracting rapidly. One of them crouched down, carefully checking his breath.
