A chill ran down their spines.

This wasn't a headshot; he'd been killed by an elbow strike to the head?

This robust adult, weighing at least 160 or 170 pounds, was killed instantly?

As the two were still thinking, a dark shadow flashed before their eyes.

The next instant.

Thud!!!

"The first floor is completely dead. Who was it?"

"That's terrifying! Which bastard provoked this demon?"

"It shouldn't be that bad. How could that demon have killed them all so quickly? Let's go find Boss Tucha and ask him. Holy crap, that's insane."

"More than that. I just went down to take a look. A bullet blew my henchman's head off. He almost got killed the moment he poked his head out."

Looking at the two crimson dots gradually approaching on the scanning radar,

Indeed, these people's sin points were terrifying.

Terrifyingly so. These people had committed so many evil deeds that they deserved to die, even to go to hell.

The two men, still discussing, stopped at a corner a short distance from Jiang Tian.

"Don't you feel something's wrong?"

"No!"

"No, don't you feel something? It's eerily quiet, not a sound. And, can you smell blood? I can."

One of them sniffed, his tone wary.

But the next moment, before they could react...

Suddenly, a sharp pain shot through their throats.

With a snap, they even heard the sound of their own necks being broken.

Accompanying this was a whisper, like that of a demon from hell.

"Sorry, you guessed wrong."

Thump!!!



Chapter 116 Madness, an impossible mission, actually accomplished by you.


Two corpses lay limply on the ground.

Thump thump thump!!

Boom!!

Accompanied by his howl,

chaotic and hurried footsteps echoed throughout the entire second floor of the massive villa.

Bang bang bang!!

Gunfire erupted everywhere.

With his unerring accuracy and utter disregard for consequences, Jiang Tian killed his opponents within three steps with a single blow, and those three steps away were instantly killed the moment they saw him, their heads blown off.

He was on a killing spree.

Jiang Tian had completely gone berserk.

Meanwhile, inside a room in the villa…

Brother Yu and his henchmen stood behind the door, listening to the sounds outside.

Amidst the intense gunfire from the second floor.

Sha Tu looked at Brother Yu in astonishment: "Brother Yu, the gunfire is getting closer."

Yes.

Outside the door, it seemed like there was intense gunfire everywhere.

"Damn it, which bastard caused this trouble, even dragging Boss Tucha into it?" Brother Yu cursed.

Yes, how long has it been since the gunfire started?

*Bang!*

Near this factory, including our henchmen, there are at least a hundred armed men. There's not a sound outside; they must have infiltrated. There can't be many."

At this point, Brother Yu's gaze fell on Gao Yangyang.

Gao Yangyang's face was expressionless, seemingly unchanged.

Even after being forced to drink an unknown drug by Brother Yu, Gao Yangyang showed no reaction, which made Brother Yu approach with a cold smile.

Then he grabbed Gao Yangyang's hair.

Brother Yu slapped Gao Yangyang's pale face, drawing a trace of blood from the corner of his mouth, but Gao Yangyang's lips remained unmoved, a mocking smile playing on his lips.

This is Boss Tucha's headquarters. Hundreds of armed henchmen are probably here to reinforce us now. Even if they had wings, they wouldn't escape." Brother Yu sneered, trying to crush Gao Yangyang's pride.

Although Gao Yangyang's beautiful lips were stained with blood, they still possessed a bewitching beauty. The only flaw was the mocking expression on her lips, which added an extra layer of nobility to her appearance.

This demeanor made Brother Yu feel a shameful sense of inferiority.

This dealt a heavy blow to Brother Yu's fragile self-esteem.

*Slap!*

Brother Yu slapped her again, his face contorted with rage. "You filthy woman, how dare you look at me like that! I'll make you kneel down! Didn't you think someone would come to save you? You filthy woman, I'll let you see with your own eyes which bastard came to cause trouble and how he'll be tortured to death!"

These words caused a change in Gao Yangyang's eyes. She gripped the sharp wooden stick tightly in her hand.

Just now, she had indeed wanted to commit suicide to end it all.

However, Gao Yangyang was afraid. She was afraid that if someone really risked their life to save her, and she died, and when they finally found her, it was just a corpse, how could she face the hero who sacrificed himself for her?

"Besides, you think I didn't see your little tricks just now? I didn't expose you because I wanted to give you hope and then crush your hopes. Want to die? It's not that simple."

As he spoke, Brother Yu suddenly stomped on Gao Yangyang's wrist.

Gao Yangyang's expression finally underwent a dramatic change.

"Hahaha, keep being arrogant! I love arrogant women like you, begging for mercy under my feet, hahaha!" Brother Yu laughed excitedly.

The excruciating pain in her wrist made Gao Yangyang grit her teeth and remain silent, but at this moment, despair gradually returned to her eyes.

Was there really no chance left?

Looking at the arrogantly laughing Brother Yu, Gao Yangyang had never hated anyone so much.

When she went undercover, she had thought about dying, but she never imagined she wouldn't even have the chance to commit suicide.

As Brother Yu spoke, he even rubbed his foot together.

With a sinister grin, watching Gao Yangyang's face gradually contort in pain, he grew even more excited: "Yes, that's it."

"I'll be waiting for you downstairs, it won't be long." Gao Yangyang was utterly desperate.

Just as Gao Yangyang was in despair, a dark figure appeared under the light, standing behind Brother Yu.

*Crack!!!*

But, just as Brother Yu was grinning sinisterly,

Pain!!!

Excruciating pain.

But before he could even utter a sound, the next moment,

Then, a sharp pain shot through his scalp.

His hair was being grabbed.

At the same time,

But it didn't matter.

Because Jiang Tian would make him answer.

Terrifying strength.

Yu Ge coughed up blood again, having been knocked unconscious by the slap, but Jiang Tian slapped him awake with several more slaps.

Upon waking, he couldn’t feel his face. At this moment, with his injuries so severe, he had temporarily lost all sensation, which terrified Yu Ge.

*Slap!!!*

Jiang Tian remained silent, delivering another slap, leaving his hand covered in red blood. Jiang Tian’s face was backlit, his features appearing cold and stern in the shadows: “I’m asking you, what are you laughing at like a dog? Don’t you understand?”

At this moment…

Yu Ge, who had been beaten, and Gao Yangyang, locked to the bed, finally saw Jiang Tian's appearance clearly.

He was covered in blood.

Killing someone meant getting covered in blood; it was inevitable.

However,

Chapter 117 An Unexpected Joy, Important Mission Completed


A silly rabbit?

Buzz!!!

The silly rabbit wasn't just lying on the ground; it had clearly just suffered a horrific beating. It lay sprawled like a dead dog, its eyes filled with fear and terror.

Who was it?

Who could it be?

A young man, covered in blood, his eyes calm, but within that calmness, a murderous intent seemed to be brewing, making him tremble all over.

Oh.

Jiang Tian couldn't see her features.

He primarily killed notorious drug dealers; these people had committed countless heinous crimes.

Gao Yangyang's body stiffened, her face turning even paler.

Her travels across the country forged a strong inner strength. Although born into a privileged family, she had always risked her life for the sake of justice.

She had also made outstanding contributions to the fight against drugs.

She felt a moment of surprise and joy, but this emotion vanished completely in a short time.

Because Jiang Tian's methods were terrifying.

Could a third party kill her?

Chapter 118: Tears well up in my eyes. What a great man he is!

Because Jiang Tian opened the door.

Are you kidding me?

There are drug dealers outside?

The next moment.

Opening the door and encountering love.

Chapter 119 Instant Kill of Tucha: The Fall of a Drug Lord

Corpses.

Dead bodies.

That's right.

And a little dazed.

It was also the location of the Special Merit Award.

Whoosh!!!

Just as Jiang Tian opened the door a crack, a terrifying whistling sound rang in his ears.

This surprised Tucha, who had landed on the ground.

Especially when he saw the corpses lying haphazardly in the corridor, his face suddenly turned grim.

To be honest, Tucha believed he could barely achieve this level of skill, but very few could rival him!

At this moment, Jiang Tian looked at his nearly 70,000 sin points.

He was just 100,000 away from upgrading a gold-level attribute.

And this last one…

Jiang Tian looked at Tucha's bright red mark.

This one was worth over 30,000. Jiang Tian observed the vibrancy of the glowing spot.

He couldn't help but lick his lips. How terrifying were gold-level attributes?

It was practically a concept of perfect accuracy, and the other attribute, Scanning Radar, was even more outrageous.

It was safe to say that of all Jiang Tian's attributes, while Scanning Radar lacked offensive power, it was undoubtedly his most terrifying weapon.

Any criminal would have nowhere to hide before him.

He even adopted a Muay Thai stance, facing Jiang Tian.

A Muay Thai expert?

“Tucha is ruthless. He once brutally killed more than ten people with his bare hands in a fighting arena,” Gao Yuanyuan seemed to know Tucha well.

To be able to brutally kill ten people with his bare hands.

To be honest, that's already top-tier among ordinary people.

Especially if those ten people had some fighting skills and combat ability, it would be even more terrifying. The saying "two fists can't beat four hands" perfectly describes that.

Even a top-tier special forces soldier couldn't do that against ten people with fighting skills, unarmed.

But that's for others.

“Hah!!!” Tucha roared.

Then he charged towards Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian slammed his right foot on the ground, and with a powerful leap, he rushed forward.

At the moment of close proximity...

Tucha leaped up, executing a standard Muay Thai fighting move, launching a knee strike at Jiang Tian to gain the upper hand.

After all, he was an undercover agent.

These people had all undergone some investigation, especially regarding Muay Thai. Jiang Tian was surprised by Gao Yangyang's thorough understanding.

However, what was dangerous for him was far from dangerous for Jiang Tian.

“Your door is open.”

Thump!!!

Tucha landed face-first on the ground.

But this was far from over.

Tucha hadn't even gotten up after rolling over.

Thud!!!

Crack!!!

"Pfft!!!"

Crack!!!

Pfft!!!

A notorious drug lord had fallen.

Chapter 120 Are you fucking fishing?

Similarly, Jiang Tian possessed the qualifications of a martial arts master, mastering all techniques to the point of complete freedom.

When Jiang Tian was serious, the terrifying pressure he exerted was that every move was fatal; there was no way to dodge, no chance of escape.

This young man was terrifyingly powerful; he was no match for him.

The notorious drug lord Tucha died so simply in his own manor.

At the same time, countless of Tucha's henchmen were also wiped out.

The terrifying stench of blood permeated the entire villa.

From heaven to hell, and from hell to heaven, Gao Yangyang had experienced this cycle countless times tonight. If it weren't for her strong mental fortitude, the sheer intensity of the experience would have likely made her faint.

She had poured countless schemes and meticulous planning into this, practically exhausting all her resources, just to finally make contact with one of Tucha's underlings.

Many others were eliminated and sacrificed along with her exposure.

Thirty thousand!

That's a sin value accumulated through countless sins.

This could also be said to be the highest sin value Jiang Tian had ever seen.

In this person, it wasn't just a person; it was a demon, a demon running rampant on earth.

Just in case.

This was a Special Merit Citation. Of course, the Special Merit Citation itself was one aspect; the main thing was the reward it brought—a historic achievement.

No one had ever received a Special Merit Citation in decades.

The political resources that came with creating history.

Bang bang bang!!!

the drug dealer plopped down on the ground with a thud.

Indeed.

Jiang Tian had witnessed a lot of organized crime and criminal gangs, especially in his previous life when he worked in an administrative department.

Those gangsters, always calling each other "good brothers," would give ten-page statements, every page listing their brothers' names.

Bang bang bang!!!

In an instant.

Chapter 122 What? He actually wants to fight back?

Chapter 123 Shedding My Shell, I'm Leaving First

Click!!!

Creak!!!

Two floors in the storage room were sinking.

This storage room was in an absolutely hidden corner. The entire villa had almost dozens of rooms, covering a vast area, and the storage room was a room within a room.

If you hadn't been here before, you would absolutely never have found it.

And the storage room was one thing.

Bewildered.

What Jiang Tian had done today was constantly shattering her worldview and even her physical senses; she was being relentlessly assaulted.

Jiang Tian's lips curled into a smile.

Not a single light.

Bang bang bang!!!

To be honest,

If Jiang Tian hadn't possessed the scanning radar, anyone else, regardless of who they were, would have been in a hopeless situation, unable to break free.

Because the basement and the passageway were no longer occupied by drug dealers.

Jiang Tian didn't know where the passageway led.

However, if these drug dealers hadn't appeared on the radar, Jiang Tian wouldn't have found this escape route at all.

"I don't believe you for a second," Gao Yangyang rolled her eyes frantically.

But Gao Yangyang didn't have time to think about that now, because the escape route was right there.

The hope of survival reappeared before her eyes.

Jiang Tian walked ahead.

Actually, Jiang Tian wasn't lying; he even had to thank the drug dealers.



Chapter 124 The Emergence of a Criminal Gang


"These drug dealers, just in case, hired a few to hide here to prevent us from escaping through the secret passage. But to me, these dealers are obvious targets," Jiang Tian sneered.

Anyone else would have died, but for him, they became evidence of his escape.

Feng Yu and his men were hiding behind cover outside the villa.

An hour passed.

Feng Yu waited patiently for sunrise.

"Boss, let's move!" one of his underlings said impatiently.

Darkness had indeed given Jiang Tian a great opportunity, but with their superior numbers, they were confident they could take him down.

"No rush, we'll just wait here," Feng Yu said calmly.

In his eyes, Jiang Tian was a sitting duck.

He had killed so many of his men, including his older brother.

If he didn't skin this bastard alive, he, Feng Yu, would change his mind.

"Send someone in to check."

As a seasoned drug dealer, Feng Yu ordered his henchmen.

Soon, one of the drug dealers surrounding the villa emerged and cautiously entered.

He was prepared to die, but even facing death, he dared not disobey Feng Yu's orders.

Dozens of rooms.

One drug dealer searched cautiously, hesitantly, taking nearly an hour to check the first floor.

"Boss, no one on the first floor," the henchman shouted.

"Hiding upstairs," Feng Yu thought without much thought.

However, the lack of movement did make Feng Yu anxious.

"Send a few more people to check the second and third floors." Feng Yu needed to confirm Jiang Tian's location.

Soon, several more drug dealers cautiously entered the villa.

From the second floor to the third floor, after a thorough search.

"No one? How could no one be here?" Feng Yu was shocked.

"Find him somewhere carefully! There's no way no one's here; he's definitely hiding!" a drug dealer shouted to his henchmen searching the area.

"Oh no!"

Feng Yu's expression suddenly changed drastically.

He then rushed into the villa like a madman, heading straight for the storage room.

While Jiang Tian was cleaning up on the third floor, Feng Yu had already arrived. At that time, he had immediately arranged for several trusted men to hide in the secret passage inside the storage room.

He hadn't considered that the killer might know that place; it was purely a precaution.

Or rather, it was an instinctive act of vigilance. But now, besides that place, he really didn't know where that bastard could have gone.

Sure enough, after running into the storage room and entering the secret passage, the overwhelming stench of blood and the sight of three corpses filled the air.

This sent a chill down Feng Yu's spine.

"He escaped?" Feng Yu's eyes widened.

That bastard, he escaped?

"Boss, there's writing here!" Just then, a sharp-eyed henchman shouted.

Feng Yu hurriedly looked.

It was writing in the blood of the corpse on the ground.

"I saw you. Keep your head, I'll come back for it next time."

Pfft!!!

Feng Yu, enraged, spat out a mouthful of blood.

That bastard.

He actually did it.

He actually dared.

"Kill him, kill him, I'm going to kill him!" Feng Yu roared.

Feng Yu quickly took deep breaths to calm himself, his voice still low and menacing as he said, "Notify the nearby factions that some cops from next door have infiltrated our territory and killed a lot of people. The target is likely heading towards the border now. Have them help intercept him."

That's right.

The factions here are interconnected.

In fact, many have deep cooperative relationships.

Just like headquarters needed to deal with Tucha to uphold its prestige and regain face, now someone has directly entered their territory and killed a large number of people within their borders.

If he escapes, what if he goes to another faction?

Everyone understands the principle of mutual dependence.

A simple notification is enough, and those people will naturally help, even rushing over.

"Boss, those people are all opportunistic; they might not necessarily act. After all, their interests haven't been touched," his underling asked.

Feng Yu sneered, "They'll definitely make a move. No one can afford to give up their interests next door. They all have a lot of business and agents there. Letting such a dangerous person go back might mean all their agents will be wiped out by this bastard."

Although he hated Jiang Tian to the core,

Feng Yu had to admit that Jiang Tian's strength was terrifying.

His killing methods were unmatched, even by the world's top assassins.

And this person was someone whose business directly conflicted with theirs. No one would stand by and watch him go back.

If he really went back,

their business in Yunhai, built up over so many years, might be completely wiped out.

And they understood this, and everyone in the Municipal Bureau understood it too.

At this moment, dawn broke, and the atmosphere in the Municipal Bureau's conference room was terrifyingly tense.

A gunfight.

A gunfight that had lasted half the night had broken out.

What exactly happened? What did Jiang Tian do at the manor? They had no idea.

But their informants revealed that countless armed drug traffickers had already flooded into the manor.

Was Jiang Tian in danger?

What was the situation inside? They had no idea.

“Tucha couldn’t possibly have that many men. This means that nearby forces have sent reinforcements,” Gao Xiaolong slammed his fist on the table.

At this moment, he desperately wanted to lead his army to wipe out those beasts completely.

However, reality told him that even if he could, he couldn’t.

Because that would mean extremely dangerous and terrible consequences, and it could even become a pretext for many countries to attack their homeland.

At this moment, they could only watch helplessly, and the most agonizing thing was being unable to do anything.

Shen Han sighed and said, "Chief Gao, the saying 'all the drugs in the country originate from Yunhai' has spread because countless criminal gangs in the neighboring criminal haven support a large number of agents who purchase drugs and ultimately transport them into Yunhai. From Yunhai, the drugs further spread throughout the country, making them difficult to eradicate. This is because there are simply too many criminal gangs behind the scenes, and they are all desperate criminals, while the number of anti-drug police has always been too small."

This is part of the reason.

That's because of the inherent danger of being a narcotics officer; very few people are willing to enter this profession.

On the other hand, narcotics officers frequently suffer casualties, and the number of authorized officers is limited.

For example, a peaceful urban area in mainland China might have ten narcotics officers, who could easily complete their tasks.

However, in Yunhai, even having thirty to fifty narcotics officers is a drop in the ocean.

Of course, this only applies to the branch office.

There are also the anti-narcotics squads at the sub-bureau level, and the provincial anti-narcotics brigade, etc., in varying numbers, but compared to the criminal forces involved, they represent a very small proportion.



Chapter 125 Romance in the Mountains, A Wild Run to Conquer Gao Yangyang


“Those people have terrifying and enormous interests in Yunhai; those interests are enough to drive them mad,” Jiang Peng said worriedly.

No one spoke further.

They were all silent.

They didn't know how to react.

Just then, Jiang Peng's phone rang. Instantly, everyone's eyes in the conference room turned to him. At that moment, Jiang Peng was the focus, the very core of the entire meeting.

It was a call from an informant.

Jiang Peng hesitated. He didn't even dare to answer the call, afraid of hearing terrible news.

After a long pause, Jiang Peng's fingers trembled, and he finally answered.

“01, latest situation: the drug dealers at the manor have dispersed,” the informant said.

Buzz!

Everyone stood up.

The drug dealers dispersed—what did that mean?

It was self-evident.

From their informants, they already knew there had been no gunfire for a long time, which in itself had filled them with anxiety.

Now, upon learning this news, they were almost deathly pale.

Firstly, it was certain that Jiang Tian had infiltrated the area, and secondly, it was Jiang Tian who had carried out the attack.

And given the chaos Jiang Tian had caused, how could the drug dealers possibly retreat without capturing or killing him?

This meant that Jiang Tian was almost certainly in grave danger.

Let alone Jiang Peng, even Gao Xiaolong, with his strong mental fortitude, staggered slightly.

For Gao Xiaolong,

even knowing that Gao Yangyang had met with misfortune wouldn't have caused him such a daze, because Jiang Tian's existence

was a strategic weapon in the fight against drug dealers.

Jiang Tian's existence would bring countless criminals to their due punishment.

It would also bring peace and happiness to many more people.

Simply put, Jiang Tian's existence could save countless lives in the future and bring countless criminals to justice.

Gao Yangyang's accident was his personal loss, which he could bear. But, if Jiang Tian were to have an accident, on a larger scale, it would be a loss for the people.

Moreover, this operation was done by Gao Xiaolong for his granddaughter.

This even made Gao Xiaolong unable to forgive himself for knowing how dangerous it was, still letting Xiao Jiang follow behind.

Before the people in the conference room could recover from their shock and grief,

the informant's urgent voice rang out: "No, something's wrong. These drug dealers didn't scatter; they've all gathered together and are running wildly north, as if they're chasing someone."

This is what it means to go from hell to heaven.

Jiang Peng, stunned and bewildered from despair, didn't even have time to be angry.

Because...

If that's the case, Jiang Tian might still be alive!

Thinking this, their eyes lit up.

"Right, these criminals are usually like rats in a burrow, always leaving behind secret passages. It seems Xiao Jiang must have escaped through one of those passages," Shen Han analyzed, stroking his chin.

And this very guess of Shen Han's was precisely the truth.

It was also the most plausible explanation at the moment.

"That's right!"

Jiang Peng slapped his thigh.

Gao Xiaolong's downcast eyes instantly lit up.

"Quickly ask the Sharp Sword Assault team where they went to meet them. Where are they? Have they encountered them?" Gao Xiaolong asked Shen Han impatiently.

That's right.

Right after Zeng Fan and his team's incident, the provincial department immediately sought support from the military region, which also dispatched a special operations team across the border to provide assistance.

Of course, they were all in civilian clothes when they went to carry out the mission.

"This is the provincial department. What's the situation over there? Please report your current location," Shen Han said into the satellite phone.

The satellite phone remained silent.

Amidst this silence...

Knock knock knock!!!

Suddenly, there was a rapid knocking on the conference room door.

A moment later, Yu Hui rushed out and excitedly said, "Boss, the Sharp Sword team has brought Zeng Fan and his team back!"

Boom!!!

The entire room erupted in cheers.

Zeng Fan had been captured!

The Sharp Sword Commando team actually managed to bring Zeng Fan back.

What about Jiang Tian?...

Everyone looked on expectantly at the figure entering the city police station, their gazes sweeping over faces, trying to find the one they hoped to see.

......

Jiang Tian had already left the secret passage with Gao Yangyang and headed deep into the mountains.

It must be said, the secret passage was very, very long, perhaps several thousand meters.

In an instant, Jiang Tian had left the manor with Gao Yangyang.

After exiting the secret passage, a wide river was on the left, and mountains were on the right. Jiang Tian naturally led Gao Yangyang into the mountains.

“Come on, let me carry you.”

Jiang Tian saw Gao Yangyang's pained expression as she leaned against a tree and immediately understood.

After all, this was Gao Yangyang's first time, and some injuries were understandable, hindering her movement.

“Thank you,” Gao Yangyang said gratefully.

That's why, lying on Jiang Tian's back...

that feeling of security, the scent of Jiang Tian filling her nostrils—not unpleasant at all, in fact, Gao Yangyang found it captivating after taking a sniff.

This made Gao Yangyang, who had never been in love before, understand why women seemed to lose their minds after falling in love.

So this is what it's like to be infatuated. She was already this infatuated even before they were officially dating. If they were actually in a relationship, Gao Yangyang couldn't even imagine it.

Thinking this...

A howling wind.

Wind.

A strong wind?

A strong wind?

Gao Yangyang, who was secretly sniffing with her eyes closed, was almost choked by the strong wind.

This made her involuntarily open her beautiful eyes a crack.

In a daze, she only saw the surrounding trees rushing past at the speed of light.

Fast.

Fast as lightning.

She felt as if she were sitting on a mountain bike.

"He...he drove uphill at 40-50 yards?" Gao Yangyang looked at Jiang Tian's profile and subconsciously swallowed.

It wasn't that Jiang Tian didn't want to rest; after all, even with unlimited stamina, he needed to replenish his food. He hadn't eaten all night and was a bit hungry, and the rapid burst of energy only intensified his hunger.

However, Jiang Tian knew clearly that once the drug dealers realized what was happening, they would search the mountain like madmen.

Jiang Tian wasn't afraid.

But continuing to entangle with the drug dealers here was risky; who knew what he might face next? A missile strike wasn't out of the question.

It might not be possible elsewhere, but it was entirely possible in this country.

Jiang Tian didn't waste any time on the journey.

Around 7:30 AM, an hour and a half after Jiang Tian had left the manor,

Jiang Tian's speed gradually decreased.

He noticed that the scanning radar was starting to show scattered red dots representing drug dealers.

Moreover, as time went on, these red dots increased in number.

This proved one thing:

Other forces were definitely involved, and they were absolutely targeting him.

"Damn, I'll kill him sooner or later," Jiang Tian squinted.

If it weren't for rescuing Gao Yangyang, Jiang Tian would have been tempted to stay and fight these drug dealers.

But ultimately, Jiang Tian wasn't afraid in an emergency.

Even if he was discovered, Jiang Tian still had one chance to upgrade his golden attribute.

At that time, whether it's combat skills or physical fitness, Jiang Tian could easily add a gold-level attribute to ensure that any number of drug dealers who came into these mountains would die.

And so it went.

Jiang Tian carried Gao Yangyang and ran at breakneck speed through the mountains, almost without stopping.

"Almost there."

Jiang Tian couldn't help but breathe a sigh of relief.

Even for him, carrying someone and running wildly for a hundred or two miles up the mountain was starting to take its toll.

And during this process...

Gao Yangyang was completely won over by Jiang Tian.

She herself didn't even realize it.

Just then, having crossed the border, a rapid and wary shout rang out: "Who's there?"



Chapter 126 Love at First Sight: The Murderer in a Shocking Case is Arrested


In the silent jungle.

A series of hurried footsteps echoed.

Three men and one woman, four people in total, trudged through the jungle, their backs bent, but they all walked cautiously, glancing around nervously.

“Brother, is this path safe?”

“Yeah, brother, I feel like the atmosphere around here is a bit too quiet.”

The two men behind the lead man asked the leader.

Indeed.

The surrounding atmosphere was too quiet.

This environment, driven by animal instinct, made them feel uneasy.

“Don’t think too much. Fortune favors the bold. Once this cargo is delivered, we can rest for a while,” the leader chuckled.

As he spoke, he gave the backpack on his back a shake.

Each of the four carried several backpacks.

The road through the jungle was winding and twisting.

It's fair to say that the mountain roads stretch endlessly, especially in some areas where dense, primeval forests still exist. Because of this mountainous terrain, Yunhai has only developed about 40% of its area, leaving 60% undeveloped.

In fact, due to the primeval forests, countless tourists get lost in the mountains every year, leading to many areas eventually becoming off-limits.

To combat drug traffickers, Yunhai has also blocked off routes used by them, but these traffickers are like rats, scurrying about throughout the mountains.

Some drug trafficking routes are simply impossible to trace.

Especially given the severe shortage of narcotics police, this was why operations against drug traffickers had been so difficult before.

Once they ventured into the mountains, they were practically lost, because many narcotics officers were far less familiar with the roads than the drug traffickers themselves. A single misstep could lead to disorientation and even death.

“Your brother is right,” the woman said. “We can’t turn back now. Besides, we’ve been on this road for so long without any problems. Ice prices in Yunhai have skyrocketed, almost a third higher. One trip will earn us more than two trips combined.”

“We’ll listen to your sister-in-law,” the leader said, choosing to trust his wife.

It turned out that the two were a husband and wife, with two henchmen.

Zhang Jiasheng’s gaze was nostalgic,

and filled with emotion.

He was accompanied by two henchmen and his wife, a total of four people, who had ventured out into the world.

Years ago, he had committed a serious offense in China, so he handed over all his businesses to his younger brother, Zhang Jiagou.

Not long ago, one of his brother's henchmen called him, saying that Zhang Jiagou was in trouble.

Zhang Jiasheng was far away overseas and couldn't intervene immediately, but who knew the opportunity would come so quickly?

The various factions had gone mad, and chaos reigned everywhere.

Zhang Jiasheng seized the opportunity, taking a few of his men to prepare to return to China to develop his business.

After all, nearly twenty years had passed, and Zhang Jiasheng didn't want to stay abroad any longer. He wanted to take this opportunity to return, to see how to rescue his brother, and to find out what the situation was like back home.

On the other hand, this shipment was also incredibly profitable.

He could even save on a layer of distributor commission.

But just as they emerged from the bushes, they suddenly saw a person standing not far away.

“Who?” Zhang Jiasheng was startled, his voice low and wary.

However, after Zhang Jiasheng looked more closely…

There were two people, but a man was carrying a woman on his back.

Of course, none of that mattered.

Because at that moment, the man carrying the woman was turning around and looking at them.

Wait…

Zhang Jiasheng was observant. Otherwise, why would anyone who wasn't smart or even a little slow-witted be dead long ago? Because a criminal organization is essentially a giant breeding ground for criminals; you're constantly facing criminals, and one wrong move could mean instant death.

Those who survive, and even thrive, are generally not too stupid.

And to Zhang Jiasheng, the man carrying the woman, at first glance, seemed unremarkable, even somewhat dark-skinned.

He looked like an ordinary person, covered in dust and dirt, lost in the mountains.

However, Zhang Jiasheng had a feeling it wasn't that simple.

Looking closely at the man's face, he noticed that the blackish substance on his face didn't seem to be dirt.

It was more like...

"Who are you? What are you doing?"

Just as Zhang Jiasheng was thinking this, one of his henchmen stepped forward and berated Jiang Tian.

"Who am I?"

Jiang Tian looked up at them.

To be honest, Jiang Tian was walking slowly, planning to rest a bit and then find someone to contact headquarters.

As for being overseas, there was no need to contact them, besides, no one overseas could be trusted.

Jiang Tian hadn't even had a chance to breathe a sigh of relief after arriving in the country when he encountered...killing.

He ran into love, oh no.

He encountered drug dealers.

For Jiang Tian, ​​the scanning radar could be kept on continuously if he was in good condition. However, ultimately, it did take a toll on him, especially mentally. He was already quite tired and hadn't kept it on immediately.

After entering the country, he turned it off.

And in that short time, he encountered four people.

That's right.

Where is this?

This is a back alley!

Jiang Tian even remembered when he first came to the city bureau for his internship.

Back then, Jiang Peng emphasized in a meeting that anyone carrying a bag deep in the mountains should be approached without hesitation; 99% of them were criminals, without a doubt.

They were all transporting drugs.

Besides those transporting drugs from the mountains, because the scale was so large, many drug dealers even entered directly through the lower passages, the official border crossings.

These people were small-scale operations, hiding drugs on their bodies, seeking riches through danger.

And things like this...

Jiang Tian glanced at it briefly, then scanned it with the radar.

Good heavens.

“What the hell are you doing here? What the hell are you doing here?” The man with the backpack approached Jiang Tian and berated him.

“What’s it to you if I’m here?” Jiang Tian ignored the man in front of him and looked at the leader not far away.

Having extraordinary memory often proves to be crucial and absolutely useful.

That is, the ability to remember and, at critical moments, recall key information instantly.

Take now, for example.

Zhang Jiasheng.

Jiang Tian's gaze sharpened. This name wasn't one he'd seen in this life, but rather in his previous life when compiling and organizing files.

Or rather, he'd seen the file containing this name.

The countless atrocities committed within it chilled even Jiang Tian to the bone back then.

"Zhang Jiasheng, involved in numerous robberies and six kidnappings, all victims were murdered without exception, involved in twelve murder cases, his methods extremely cruel." Jiang Tian's gaze tightened.

Because he remembered, these cases remained unsolved.

In other words, even before Jiang Tian's transmigration, this case remained unsolved.

It seemed to have been lurking overseas.

Among the case files, the crime scene photos remained vivid in Jiang Tian's memory to this day. Even without extraordinary memories, Jiang Tian felt that those photos alone were unforgettable.

They caused a huge sensation at the time.



Chapter 127 Special Operations Team Appears, Terrifying the Border


However, before his rebirth, this case was a 37-year-old cold case, discovered by Jiang Tian while organizing archives.

Even now, it's been nearly 20 years.

A wanted person for 20 years.

Wow, what a stroke of luck!

“Are you deaf? Can’t you hear me?” the henchman roared, his face contorted with rage.

Because Jiang Tian had actually ignored him?

How dare he.

Absolutely dare.

As he spoke, a large slap came flying.

They outnumbered him; there were four of them, but in his eyes, Jiang Tian was just one person, carrying a defenseless woman on his back.

This drug dealer was a bit arrogant.

But…

In the instant he threw his punch…

Jiang Tian took a slight step back, still carrying Gao Yangyang on his back.

But his leg bent, and the next moment, Jiang Tian kicked out.

*Thud!!!*

The kick landed squarely on the drug dealer's stomach. A mouthful of blood, mixed with some unknown substance, shot out, and the man was propelled seven or eight meters away like a spring.

He fell to the ground without even struggling, his eyes instantly closed forever.

"Xiao Er!"

The woman cried out and rushed over, but when she shook him, she found that Xiao Er was already dead, his eyes wide open in death.

One kick, and he'd killed him?

The three people at the scene were stunned.

At this moment...

Zhang Jiasheng's scalp tingled. He finally understood why the stains on Jiang Tian's body were so familiar—it wasn't dirt, it was the color of congealed blood.

"Brother, one kick and he'd killed him? You went a bit too far!" Zhang Jiasheng gritted his teeth.

These two underlings were his former companions, those who had followed him through thick and thin, even going abroad. They were practically brothers, from the same village, practically related.

“I… I can get down.”

At this moment, Gao Yangyang, on Jiang Tian's back, struggled to get down, but Jiang Tian shook his head.

Then, he turned to look at Zhang Jiasheng.

“If I were you, I wouldn't pull out a pistol,” Jiang Tian reminded him.

“Kid, I'll kill you.”

Bang!!!

Jiang Tian raised his hand and fired.

Because, if Jiang Tian remembered correctly, this case was a nationally shocking crime twenty years ago.

A special task force was even dispatched to apprehend him.

But that bastard Zhang Jiasheng escaped through the winding, mountainous terrain of Yunhai, even crossing the border.

At this moment, Jiang Tian felt even more strongly that the road ahead was long and arduous.

Thump!!!

Zhang Jiasheng's second henchman fell to the ground.

"You killed them?" Gao Yangyang gasped.

She saw it too; in the blink of an eye, both men were dead.

In Gao Yangyang's eyes, this was just a simple verbal altercation.

How could it have escalated into a murder case?

Thump, thump, thump!!!

At this moment, Jiang Tian walked step by step to the dead drug dealer's side.

He opened the drug dealer's body, ignoring the bloody hole in his forehead, pulled off his backpack, and opened it—it was full of white powder.

Gao Yangyang was instantly dumbfounded.

No…

This…this…

And at this moment, Zhang Jiasheng, standing to the side, was even more bewildered.

For twenty years, Zhang Jiasheng had faced countless perilous situations abroad, painstakingly saving money.

After twenty years of saving, he had finally made it to this import deal.

“Who are you?” Zhang Jiasheng asked, his eyes bloodshot.

This heroin was his lifeline.

It was the drug he had saved for so many years, the very thing he needed to make his fortune back home.

Zhang Jiasheng estimated that at normal prices, he could earn hundreds of thousands, even millions.

But now, due to the tense situation in China, the price had increased by a third.

This was his only hope for getting rich quick.

“A drug dealer!” Jiang Tian sneered.

Looking at the nearly five thousand crime points on the radar,

this crime point was already terrifying.

It was even enough for Jiang Tian to upgrade five green attributes.

Jiang Tian wanted to take out his identification, but then realized that he had left these documents at the city police station while abroad.

"Let me introduce myself, I'm from the Municipal Narcotics Control Division."

Jiang Tian's lips curled into a smile.

"A policeman?" Zhang Jiasheng felt dizzy.

"Zhang Jiasheng, you've committed a crime." Jiang Tian sneered.

Boom!!!

Zhang Jiasheng's face changed drastically.

Gao Yangyang's head was buzzing even more.

"Zhang Jiasheng?"

At this moment, Gao Yangyang was also speechless.

That's why.

If it were appearance, twenty years had passed, and much had changed.

But the name Zhang Jiasheng, the perpetrator of a major and heinous crime twenty years ago, was practically etched into police academy textbooks.

It was also a standard case analysis.

And, having learned from past mistakes,

how to blockade and take action against drug dealers in similar situations in the future, learning from this failed operation.

Therefore, the name Zhang Jiasheng was quite famous.

Gao Yangyang, initially caught off guard, was now completely bewildered.

Just as Gao Yangyang was still reeling from the shock, Jiang Tian made his move.

“Run!”

Zhang Jiasheng suddenly shouted to his wife beside him, then fled frantically without looking back.

There were bushes ahead of him.

His two henchmen had been instantly killed.

This left Zhang Jiasheng with almost no will to resist.

His wife, however, only hesitated for a moment before regaining her senses.

She also tried to escape.

They were only three steps away from the bushes.

It was as if they believed they could escape once they entered the bushes.

But the next moment…

Bang!!!

Jiang Tian raised his hand and fired a shot.

Jiang Tian had brought more than ten magazines for this operation, despite the intense firefight in Tucha.

Jiang Tian hadn't actually used many bullets.

But without a doubt, each bullet claimed the life of a drug dealer, and Jiang Tian now had only a few bullets left.

This was enough to prove that the number of drug dealers eliminated had roughly exceeded three digits.

Jiang Tian calculated in his mind, but his hands didn't stop moving.

He fired a shot into the legs of Zhang Jiasheng and his wife.

Zhang Jiasheng lay beside the bushes, his hands trembling as he frantically tried to load the gun.

But the more he tried, the more his hands trembled.

The pistol tried to disengage the safety, but after several attempts, it kept trembling and locking itself back in place.

Thump, thump, thump!!!

Jiang Tian walked up, bent down, and gently took the pistol from Zhang Jiasheng's hand.

Click!!!

Jiang Tian quickly cocked the pistol and disengaged the safety, almost in one smooth motion: "If you don't know how, I'll teach you!"

To be honest, this operation felt like a dream to Jiang Tian; he truly felt no pressure.

In fact, he only felt a sense of surprise.

A chance encounter.

Having just completed a top-tier merit case, before even returning home, and having just entered the country, he unexpectedly encountered the mastermind behind a nationally shocking case, a wanted fugitive on Interpol's Red Notice, Zhang Jiasheng.

Then, Jiang Tian slowly crouched down, just about to check on Zhang Jiasheng's condition.

Crack!!!

The next moment.

A series of rapid, loud cracking sounds erupted.

A steady, powerful gait came frantically from behind them.

Jiang Tian instinctively turned around and glanced back.

About eight men, each holding a Type 95 rifle, stood tall and proud, watching them intently from a short distance away.

And that aura...

instantly made Jiang Tian realize something.

"Don't move! Everyone, don't move!"

"Drop your weapons! Quickly, drop your weapons!"

"Put down your weapons, or we'll shoot!"

With a final, furious shout, the eight men surrounded them from eight directions.

This was professionalism; Jiang Tian could tell at a glance these men had undergone special training.

At least, Jiang Tian felt they were far superior to the anti-drug squad.



Chapter 128 The sword cracked, are you crazy? Two first-class merits in one day?


Eight men, led by a middle-aged man.

Jiang Tian recognized him using his extraordinary memory.

In truth, if Jiang Tian wanted to, he could kill them in an instant.

After all, these eight men had no identifying marks on them, and having a gun pointed at him made Jiang Tian feel very unsafe.

“I don’t like having a gun pointed at me,” Jiang Tian said slowly, his eyes flashing.

Although he recognized them, being pointed at like this still made Jiang Tian’s muscles tense.

For Jiang Tian, ​​his life was not in someone else’s hands.

Even if he couldn’t fire the gun, what if it accidentally went off?

“Behave yourself, who are you?” The middle-aged man leading the group frowned slightly.

It has to be said.

Jiang Tian’s mere presence exerted immense pressure on him.

Because they had just witnessed how Jiang Tian had acted.

Four men, four drug dealers, watched helplessly as Jiang Tian swiftly dispatched them.

If they hadn't received training, it wouldn't be so bad; the key was that they had undergone years of special forces training in the military.

But it must be admitted that even they, in the position of those four drug dealers, had no confidence they could dodge it.

Because his movements were too fast. The most terrifying part was that initial kick, which directly killed one of the drug dealers—an adult! He kicked him seven or eight meters away.

This was an extremely terrifying man.

No.

Perhaps they were thugs.

Because they couldn't even be sure of the four men's identities.

"Who were you before this?" Although there were eight of them, Jiang Tian still held his gun high.

To ensure a fatal blow in case of any unexpected events.

The middle-aged man leading the group hesitated for a moment before speaking, "We are the Sharp Sword Commando Team, currently on a mission. Put down your weapons now. As long as you haven't committed a crime, we won't do anything to you."

Jiang Tian nodded slightly, "Let me introduce myself. I'm the squad leader of the Yunhai City Public Security Bureau's Narcotics Control Detachment. We're on a special classified mission, but we encountered these people along the way. They're all drug dealers, and their backpacks are full of drugs. You can take a look."

Upon hearing this,

the eight men visibly relaxed.

Especially the middle-aged captain, who walked up to a dead drug dealer. Sure enough, the open backpack was filled with drugs.

Seeing this, the captain glanced at Jiang Tian with envy.

Undoubtedly.

This was a significant achievement!

"Brother, congratulations on your merit." The middle-aged captain waved his hand, and everyone lowered their guns.

The captain, codenamed Tianyuan Sword, couldn't suppress his inner disappointment.

Damn it, just a little bit more.

"So close! That kid beat us to it!"

"That's at least a second-class merit, maybe even a first-class merit," the captain asked enviously.

The standards for merit are roughly the same in the police force and the military.

In the military, this kind of merit would at least earn a collective first-class merit, and the individual might even get a second-class merit.

Such a great merit, and he missed it.

What a pity!

“By the way, this drug dealer who led the operation is Zhang Jiasheng, an A-level wanted criminal who committed a major crime that shocked the country twenty years ago and has been on the run for twenty years,” Jiang Tian said.

“What did you say?”

Boom!!!

Tian Yuanjian’s eyes widened, his pupils contracting.

“Boss, you know Zhang Jiasheng?” A younger team member seemed unfamiliar with the name.

“The notorious bandit Zhang Jiasheng, twenty years ago, committed a kidnapping and massacre of an entire family. He was involved in multiple crimes, requiring a total of three thousand military and police personnel to conduct a mountain-sealing manhunt, but he disappeared for twenty years,” Tian Yuanjian explained.

Pfft!!!

Everyone’s eyes widened even more.

No…

A younger team member stared at Jiang Tian in disbelief, wondering how this guy could be so lucky!

Why is this kid so lucky!

"If we caught them, you could say all eight of us get a first-class merit, everyone gets one, and the whole team gets one too," Tian Yuanjian added.

"Damn, we're taking off!"

"Congratulations, brother! Damn, you got this merit, you're going to take off from here!"

"You're so lucky."

"No, luck isn't enough, you also need skill!"

"Brother, aren't you tired? We have a mission, but we can spare two people to help you take these two wounded drug dealers back."

Goodness.

Although the credit goes to Jiang Tian, ​​they should get a share for helping transport the drug dealers.

Don't even mention it, a small third-class merit for helping others would be enough.

"By the way, brother, what's your name? I'm a sniper from Sharp Sword, my surname is Bei, everyone calls me Prince Bei. You fired so fast, I didn't even see it," the young team member Prince Bei said excitedly as he ran up to Jiang Tian.

“Oh, my name is Jiang Tian. The person on my back is the hostage I just rescued.” Jiang Tian nodded.

“Oh, Jiang Tian, ​​nice name.”

“Wait, damn…”

“Your name is Jiang Tian?”

“Pfft!!!”

Captain Tian Yuanjian, who was helping to collect the drugs and drug dealers, paused slightly.

Then, stiffening his neck, he looked at Jiang Tian, ​​completely bewildered.

He was genuinely bewildered.

No…

“Damn!!!”

“You went deep behind enemy lines and rescued the undercover agent?”

“And you even managed to rack up such a huge achievement?”

Tian Yuanjian’s mind went blank. This… this means two first-class merits in one day?

If Jiang Tian knew what they were thinking, he would definitely smile and shake his head, saying no, because the other one was a special-class merit.

Because Tucha had already been killed by him.



Chapter 129 The Municipal Public Security Bureau is in an uproar, is it him?


City Bureau...

In the conference room, even Old Mu, who had stayed up all night, couldn't help but rub his slightly sore eyes.

He hadn't slept a wink.

He couldn't sleep, he simply couldn't.

They'd gone mad.

According to his informant, many overseas forces had practically gone insane.

"These bastards," Gao Xiaolong gritted his teeth, thinking of this.

They were simply too bastards.

Calling them insane wouldn't be an exaggeration.

Under these circumstances, could Xiao Jiang still return safely?

Gao Xiaolong's face was grim.

Just then.

A series of hurried footsteps sounded.

A moment later, Yu Hui knocked and entered.

"Boss, boss, there's news!" Yu Hui said hurriedly.

"What news?" Jiang Peng turned to ask.

To be honest, even now, no matter how hard they tried, they couldn't imagine how Jiang Tian could return safely from so many drug dealers.

The only good news was that the frequent movements of the drug traffickers proved one thing: Jiang Tian was still safe.

"The police station in Mashan Town just called. A narcotics officer named Zeng Fan, leading a large team, is being transported back by the police station."

*Whoosh!*

For them, any news, especially news from overseas, was huge news.

Moreover, this news was definitely a pleasant surprise.

"Zeng Fan is back?" Jiang Peng asked anxiously.

Yes.

Many people looked on expectantly.

Actually, by now,

they had given up all hope of rescuing Gao Yangyang. From the leak of information, from Zeng Fan's arrest, to the increased vigilance in Tucha, they had no hope left.

And as for Zeng Fan...

they had absolutely no hope.

While the drug traffickers were on the move, Jiang Tian, ​​alone, was neither a god nor invincible.

After the initial attempt and the subsequent frenzied attack by the drug dealers, Jiang Tian was already fortunate to have managed to protect himself.

And in this situation...

What did Zeng Fan and his team's return signify? It meant Jiang Tian might also be back!

Whoosh!!!

That's why it's said...

It wasn't that they were unhappy about Zeng Fan's return, but Jiang Tian's importance, in their eyes, far surpassed Zeng Fan's.

Whether considering the bigger picture or personal feelings, Jiang Tian was far, far more important.

Ten minutes later...

At the city police station gate...

At this moment, Zeng Fan, weary from his journey, arrived with his team.

This border crossing had truly terrified them. Or rather, only after crossing the border could they truly understand the ruthlessness of those drug dealers.

They had absolutely no bottom line, especially after the parts factory was nearly dismantled. Every time he thought about it, Zeng Fan felt a wave of fear.

It really was a close call.

If it weren't for his final outburst, their overseas parts factories would have been completely wiped out.

However, Zeng Fan, who was still lingering fear, changed his expression after arriving at the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

It wasn't just his expression that changed.

Zeng Fan's entire demeanor seemed different.

Led by Gao Xiaolong, and behind him were many others, including powerful figures like Director Shen and even Elder Mu.

Almost all members of the city's anti-drug squad stood on both sides of the road.

They were shown the utmost respect.

He shared in the glory.

At that moment, Zeng Fan's vanity was greatly satisfied.

At that moment, he felt that all his efforts were worthwhile and justified.

Especially since, Zeng Fan was already considering:

How much credit would he receive from his superiors?

Although the informant's betrayal had plunged him into crisis, ultimately, it was their own carelessness that had caused the damage.

However, considering that Zeng Fan had saved everyone at a crucial moment, he felt that, in any case, it should at least be a second-class merit award.

Even if it was just self-rescue, there was still effort involved, even if no merit was achieved.

While Zeng Fan was pondering this,

he approached Gao Xiaolong with a smile.

“Commander, I…”

Zeng Fan saluted Gao Xiaolong, who was at the head of the group, gathering his composure and organizing his thoughts to explain his unsuccessful attempt to rescue Gao Yangyang and the situation at hand.

Gao Xiaolong reached out and shoved him aside, then his gaze swept over everyone.

Soon, Gao Xiaolong's expression shifted from surprise to disappointment, and finally, his face turned grim.

No.

No Xiao Jiang?

Gao Xiaolong let out a deep breath.

A forced smile appeared on his face as he said to Zeng Fan, "What situation did you encounter over there? Give a detailed report."

In the operations conference room, Gao Xiaolong led them there without saying a word.

He then questioned Zeng Fan.

Zeng Fan sensed something was wrong, but couldn't pinpoint what it was, so he could only honestly reply, "Sir, after crossing the border, we were betrayed by our informant and drugged."

"Didn't you notice anything?" Gao Xiaolong's face darkened.

Damn it.

If it were Jiang Tian, ​​how could this have happened?

Now look what's happened.

Not only did they not help, but they even exposed their operation.

Isn't this making Jiang Tian's operation more difficult?

"Sir, by the time we arrived, it was too late. Nobody expected the informant to have already betrayed us," Zeng Fan quickly explained.

"And then?" Gao Xiaolong pressed on.

"And then..."

Zeng Fan recounted his harrowing experience of being transported to the parts factory, a near-death experience. His descriptions of what he saw and heard were enough to make everyone in the conference room grit their teeth in anger.

"Those bastards deserve to die," Old Mu said through gritted teeth.

"And then?" Gao Xiaolong's eyes were red.

He was already thinking about his granddaughter.

Gao Yangyang had fallen into the hands of those drug dealers, and how tragic her fate would be.

They could use every inhuman form of torture imaginable.

Every time he thought of this, Gao Xiaolong felt a pang of heartache. How could he face Yangyang's parents in the end?

From the time Gao Yangyang was captured until now, in such a short time, he seemed to have aged decades, his face etched with wrinkles.

Completely gone was the confident and high-spirited demeanor Jiang Tian had displayed when they first met.

“Did you see Jiang Tian? Did he rescue you?” Jiang Peng asked impatiently.

“Jiang Tian? He went too?” Zeng Fan asked, somewhat surprised.

“Yes, he went after you.” Jiang Peng’s heart sank.

Zeng Fan frowned slightly, a polite smile appearing on his face: “Director Jiang, you might be overthinking things. I haven’t met this Jiang Tian you mentioned. He wasn’t involved in this operation; he has nothing to do with it. So, Director Jiang, I hope you can be more rational.”

“Besides…”

“Even if he went, he would just be cannon fodder there, unable to make a ripple.”

Hmm?

Everyone frowned slightly.

Especially Gao Xiaolong, who squinted and asked, “Then how did you escape?”



Chapter 130 The King Returns, At This Moment, All Eyes Are On


"Chief Gao, it was late at night when a large-scale conflict suddenly broke out at the drug cartel's manor. Everyone from the parts factory rushed out to provide support. I saw this opportunity, took the chance to untie the ropes, and brought all the team members back." Zeng Fan couldn't wait to boast about his achievement.

This was no small feat, at least in his own eyes.

Gao Xiaolong narrowed his eyes: "Then let me tell you, what if the chaos at the manor was all caused by Xiao Jiang?"

Hmm?

Him?

"Chief Gao, don't joke around. Do you know how many drug cartel members we saw after we came out? Inside the drug cartel's villa, there were hundreds of fully armed drug cartel members, and none of them dared to attack." Zeng Fan quickly shook his head.

Jiang Tian?

How could that be?

You know, when they were investigating the situation,

they witnessed it firsthand. So many drug cartel members only dared to surround the villa's perimeter, and even after they risked observing for a while...

They discovered that not even a fly could get in.

And yet, this level of armed force dared not even enter the villa. One can imagine how terrifying and horrifying the people inside must be.

In the conference room, Zeng Fan explained.

They gained a deeper understanding of the situation at the villa.

At least for everyone.

The informant was on the outermost perimeter, while Zeng Fan had escaped from the parts factory.

He undoubtedly had the most experience with the scene.

"And..."

Zeng Fan paused slightly before his gaze turned heavy with lingering fear, "And, after risking our lives to investigate, we discovered that at least nearly a hundred corpses were carried out of the villa."

A hundred people killed!

That's incredibly brutal.

To this day, Zeng Fan still doesn't know who entered the villa.

Upon hearing this,

Gao Xiaolong and Director Shen exchanged a glance.

"Are you sure you didn't see who was inside the villa, and how many people were there?" Gao Xiaolong continued to inquire.

“Chief Gao, in that situation, even getting close was extremely difficult. We wanted to come out and rescue Yangyang, but Chief Gao, there were hundreds of drug dealers surrounding the scene. Not even a fly could get in,” Zeng Fan explained, trying his best to illustrate how terrifying the situation was.

“Impossible. What about Xiao Jiang?” Jiang Peng stood up and asked.

Zeng Fan tilted his head and spread his hands, saying, “Who knows? We couldn’t even get in. If Xiao Jiang was alone, he could only be on the outermost edge at most. After all, that kind of terrifying battle, with hundreds of drug dealers dead, couldn’t have been caused by just one person.”

As Zeng Fan finished speaking, the atmosphere visibly relaxed.

Even Gao Xiaolong’s expression softened.

As long as Xiao Jiang was safe, that was all that mattered.

Just then, Gao Xiaolong spoke up: "Regardless, Zeng Fan, you've worked hard on this mission. I'll recommend you for commendation. Since you brought all the team members back safe and sound, I'll apply for an individual second-class merit and a collective third-class merit."

"Yes!!!" Zeng Fan replied, his face beaming with joy.

After all, not everyone is Jiang Tian.

Do you know how difficult it is to earn a merit?

For Zeng Fan, a second-class merit was already a tremendous reward.

"It's just a pity, Yangyang..." After the initial surprise, a strange pain crossed Zeng Fan's face.

Yes.

He had genuine feelings for Gao Yangyang.

If only there had been even the slightest chance or hope this time, he would have acted.

However, Gao Yangyang, having been discovered while undercover, faced that chaotic situation, surrounded by drug dealers.

Ninety-nine percent of the time, she was already dead.

"You did nothing wrong, don't blame yourself." A barely perceptible trace of disappointment and pain flashed across Gao Xiaolong's face.

"In that situation, I wouldn't have given up even the slightest chance, but it was a death sentence..."

Knock knock knock!!

Just as Zeng Fan was speaking, a sudden, urgent knocking sounded on the door.

This drew everyone's attention to the conference room door.

A moment later, the conference room door was forced open.

"Yu Hui, what is it?"

After a brief silence, Jiang Peng stood up and spoke to Yu Hui.

"Coming."

Yu Hui gasped for breath, then shouted.

"They're here?"

Everyone looked confused.

"He's here," Yu Hui said excitedly.

"Be clearer, who's here?" Jiang Peng frowned.

Yu Hui's face was flushed with excitement. She had been working overtime all night and knew what had happened. That's why she was so excited: "Jiang Tian, ​​Jiang Tian's back! He's back!"

*Whoosh!*

*Thump!*

A series of rapid sounds.

It was the sound of a stool falling to the ground.

In their excitement, several people even knocked over the stool as they stood up.

"Xiao Jiang is back?" Gao Xiaolong's eyes widened.

Good, good.

It's good that he's back.

It's good that he's back!

For Gao Xiaolong, his granddaughter's sacrifice was painful.

But what Gao Xiaolong couldn't accept or forgive himself for was that, in saving his granddaughter, the anti-drug system had lost such a crucial talent.

This was the most unacceptable thing for him.

It might even make him feel guilty, regretful, and resentful for the rest of his life.

"Where is he? Where is that brat?" Shen Ting rushed over excitedly.

"At the door." Yu Hui knew them well, so she pointed outside.

At this moment.

Upon hearing the confirmed news of Jiang Tian's return, everyone's attention shifted away from Zeng Fan.

Or rather, Zeng Fan had been forgotten.

Although Zeng Fan had indeed played a small role in the incident, Jiang Tian's safety was paramount to them.

Because only they knew how fierce Jiang Tian had been against the drug dealers, and how immense and terrifying his contribution had been.

However, Zeng Fan, following behind the others after returning from Kyoto, grew increasingly grim.

Thinking back to the indifferent expressions on everyone's faces when he returned, and then comparing it to the eagerness and joy they felt upon Jiang Tian's return,

this made Zeng Fan's expression even more unpleasant.

"Captain, it's just that kid who's back. Who knows what he's been up to? Why is everyone so excited?" A teammate next to Zeng Fan couldn't help but complain.

"Hmph, what could he possibly do alone, even if he went abroad? Did he kill Tucha and then a few hundred drug dealers, turning their world upside down...?" Zeng Fan told a lame joke.

He couldn't help but laugh at the end.

The teammate next to Zeng Fan also laughed.

"Anyone who didn't know better would really think he did that," one teammate said, feeling resentful.

Mainly, this differential treatment naturally fueled their discontent.

It wasn't just that Jiang Tian hadn't shown up; the key issue was that they had risked their lives to return, and while they got the credit, the contrast after Jiang Tian's appearance...

This naturally filled them with resentment.

"Alright, enough of that. The leaders have their reasons for doing this," Zeng Fan said calmly from behind.

Even so, what good is it to be valued and favored by so many leaders?

This is the anti-drug system.

Everything is based on merit and strength.

He only needs to wait a little while. Once Jiang Tian shows up, the drug dealers will probably be scared out of their wits before they even see his face.

That's when his time to step forward will come.

Thinking this,

Zeng Fan followed behind.

The next moment, Zeng Fan, not paying attention, suddenly bumped into someone in front of him.

Zeng Fan frowned, before he could react.

The next moment.

Looking up, he saw that he had bumped into Gao Xiaolong.

It turned out that Gao Xiaolong had stood still sometime during the night.

His face was filled with disbelief as he stared ahead.

Even from behind, one could see Gao Xiaolong trembling visibly.

"Is this kid a ghost? He can scare people like that?" Zeng Fan frowned deeply.

Then his gaze shifted, also turning to the doorway.

What he saw...

was a disheveled face, covered in black lumps and stains.

But that didn't matter. What mattered most was...



Chapter 131 Tell my mother I didn't embarrass her.


"Yangyang?"

That's right.

After a long and arduous journey, especially after being discovered as an undercover agent by drug dealers, Gao Yangyang's appearance was certainly not good, especially considering her encounter with Jiang Tian along the way.

After that, they ate and slept outdoors, speeding through the jungle. Even being carried by Jiang Tian, ​​some bloodstains were inevitable.

Jiang Tian did encounter drug dealers along the way.

However, he dealt with them all with extremely swift methods.

Of course, this was mainly due to Jiang Tian's incredible speed.

For example, drug dealers expected Jiang Tian to reach a certain location in an hour, but when they caught up, they found nothing.

Because Jiang Tian's speed far exceeded their expectations.

There were also drug dealers who tried to conduct a thorough search, but Jiang Tian quietly eliminated two of them and left without alerting anyone.

Along the way, the drug dealers were practically lurking in Jiang Tian's wake. By the time they spotted him, he was already quite far away.

However, their speed was incredible, and the jungle was fraught with thorns. Even a slight scratch from the thorns caused minor cuts, and his clothes were tattered.

Fortunately, Jiang Tian had given Gao Yangyang his black coat.

But even so, he was utterly disheveled.

However, his dishevelment and exhaustion couldn't stop Gao Yangyang's joyful greeting.

When Gao Yangyang saw Gao Xiaolong, tears streamed uncontrollably from her beautiful eyes.

No one knew the psychological torment she endured alone in a foreign land, surrounded by ruthless drug dealers.

But justice and the desire to protect the people gave Gao Yangyang a powerful belief that allowed her to persevere. However, in the subsequent operation, her undercover identity was ultimately exposed.

At that moment, Gao Yangyang felt only despair.

For Gao Yangyang, the only thing she truly longed for was a swift and clean death.

Because she had witnessed what happened to her undercover colleague in the drug den after being discovered.

That colleague hadn't uttered a single word.

Even tortured to the point of mental and physical collapse, she hadn't said a word, nor had she betrayed her.

And back then, Gao Yangyang couldn't even help her end her life.

Because of this experience, Gao Yangyang understood even more clearly that her fate was almost identical to that colleague's.

Tortured, madly tortured, their final value was being sent to a parts factory.

There was no other choice.

And now, she was back, and she had even seen her grandfather.

Tears streamed down Gao Yangyang's face as she took a deep breath.

"Yangyang, is that you?" Gao Xiaolong's voice trembled as he rubbed his eyes, making sure he wasn't mistaken.

Gao Yangyang resisted the urge to pounce on Gao Xiaolong, instead taking two steps in front of him.

"Commander, Wild Rose reporting for duty." Gao Yangyang stood at attention and saluted.

Swish!!!

Even Gao Xiaolong's hands trembled. After confirming several times, his mind finally exploded.

His granddaughter was back?

His granddaughter, whom he never dared to dream of, was back.

The number of people who could return alive after being discovered while undercover was practically zero.

It was almost a certain death sentence.

But at this moment, Gao Yangyang had practically made history.

"Grandpa, I didn't let you down," Gao Yangyang said confidently, her cheeks puffed out.

Meanwhile, Zeng Fan, standing behind Gao Xiaolong, also widened his eyes upon seeing Gao Yangyang, and instantly exploded with excitement.

"Yangyang, back?"

Zeng Fan's head was buzzing. His first thought was, "How did she get back?"

His second thought...

Good, good, good...

It must be because they were captured. After they were taken to the parts factory, a rival force of the drug dealers took advantage of the chaos, and Gao Yangyang escaped.

Thinking this, Zeng Fan suddenly felt that this was the only correct answer.

“Yangyang, thank goodness you’re back! I went to rescue you. Luckily, although I didn’t succeed, I did manage to create some minor chaos, giving you a chance to escape in the confusion…” Zeng Fan ran up excitedly.

He wanted to get closer to Gao Yangyang.

Because only after going abroad could he truly understand the power and terror of drug dealers.

In comparison, the small-time drug dealers in China seemed rather insignificant.

Even after this incident, he felt he had no interest in three or five kilograms of drugs; only transactions exceeding several dozen kilograms could pique his interest.

Zeng Fan sighed inwardly, no wonder everyone liked to study abroad; broadening one's horizons was indeed very important.

Otherwise, one might be complacent about seizing a kilogram or two.

“You escaped on your own?” Gao Yangyang looked at him strangely.

"Yeah, what's wrong?" Zeng Fan asked without suspicion.

"If I had that ability, why would I only be back now?" Gao Yangyang explained with a wry smile.



Chapter 132 Your so-called batch of perverts, sorry Jiang Tian


Zeng Fan's face broke into a knowing smile.

It was definitely because of him that a butterfly effect occurred. He must have played a significant role in this. If they hadn't been captured, Tucha's terrifying enemies probably wouldn't have broken into their hideout.

In such a short time, over a hundred people had been killed, forcing hundreds of fully armed drug dealers to hide outside the door, unable to make any attempt to break in, only able to surround it.

This proved that there must be someone even more terrifying inside the villa.

Could it be that because they were captured, those perverts thought the country was going to take action against Tucha, which is why they continued their attack?

Wasn't this an unintended consequence?

"Yangyang, it's good that you're alright. You don't know how many drug dealers we almost got killed trying to save you. Luckily, a group of perverts wiped out those drug dealers in Tucha," Zeng Fan said with a smile.

Zeng Fan was filled with regret. If he had known Yangyang could escape, why did they rush back like madmen?

If all they had to do was provide backup outside and save Gao Yangyang, wouldn't the credit still go to them?

That would at least be a first-class merit, right?

But now...

They only got a third-class merit.

"A bunch of perverts?" Gao Yangyang's face grew increasingly strange.

Meanwhile, Gao Xiaolong felt like he'd forgotten something.

At this moment...

As his emotions gradually calmed from their initial excitement, as he gradually recovered from his joy and shock,

he looked up.

Sure enough.

Not far away, Jiang Tian walked over from the corner of the courtyard, a towel in his hand, wiping his face.

It turned out Jiang Tian had just washed his face at the pool in the corner of the city bureau's courtyard.

But even after washing his face, Jiang Tian was still covered in too much blood, almost as if he had been soaked in blood.

He greeted him with a smile as he wiped his face.

Jiang Tian…

“Yangyang, you…” Gao Xiaolong’s eyes widened.

Gao Yangyang nodded without hesitation, “Grandpa, it was him, Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​who saved me. If it weren’t for him, I might have been caught by drug dealers.”

As she spoke, Gao Yangyang had already imagined the terrifying consequences of being caught by drug dealers.

Buzz!

As Gao Yangyang finished speaking,

the scene fell silent, all eyes turning to Jiang Tian.

Zeng Fan’s eyes were bloodshot.

In his mind, Jiang Tian must have been following them on the road, and by sheer luck, he encountered the escaped Gao Yangyang and brought her back without any danger.

Thinking of this, Zeng Fan was practically bursting with rage.

If it were him, wouldn’t he have been able to develop a deep bond with Gao Yangyang along the way and even receive a first-class merit award?

Thinking of this, Zeng Fan’s eyes turned even redder.

Luck, this person is incredibly lucky.

But this was only Zeng Fan's own thought, because only those who worked with Jiang Tian at the Municipal Bureau truly understood how much Jiang Tian had accomplished in just a few days.

“Yangyang, he just ran into you on the road and brought you back. Tell me the whole story. I know you're kind and want to celebrate your comrade's success, but for such an important mission, you have to be honest, right?” Zeng Fan suppressed his jealousy and forced a smile.

At this moment, Gao Yangyang's brows furrowed deeply.

She hadn't thought much of it at first, but now, Zeng Fan was clearly targeting her.

Even Gao Yangyang could tell.

Not to mention that she and Jiang Tian had already done *that* in the villa, even if they hadn't, Jiang Tian had gone to great lengths to rescue her from hundreds of drug dealers, carrying her for dozens, even hundreds of kilometers.

And what had Zeng Fan done?

What right did he have to question Jiang Tian?

Initially, Gao Yangyang had been somewhat polite and even smiling at Zeng Fan because he had helped rescue her, even if he hadn't actually contributed.

But now…

Gao Yangyang's face instantly turned cold.

But Zeng Fan, completely oblivious to the change in Gao Yangyang's expression, continued speaking to himself, "Yangyang, we attracted a lot of drug dealers' attention. That's why a group of freaks, seeing our country's attitude, launched a retaliatory attack on Tucha. They fought like dogs, which is how you were able to escape."

Yes, how could things be such a coincidence? Zeng Fan became increasingly certain of his suspicions.

He simply didn't notice that Gao Yangyang's expression had become so cold it was almost dripping with water.

Insulting her for her incompetence and having her identity discovered was one thing.

But insulting Jiang Tian was touching her sore spot.

"Shut up," Gao Yangyang snapped.

"Yangyang, you..." Zeng Fan's face showed disbelief.

Because Gao Yangyang had never spoken to him like this before!

In the past, Zeng Fan had been pursuing Gao Yangyang; even back then, Gao Yangyang was at the Kyoto headquarters, and they were colleagues.

At that time, Gao Yangyang kept rejecting him, but Zeng Fan always believed in himself, that one day he could warm her heart, like water dripping on a stone.

Even though his relentless pursuit annoyed Gao Yangyang, her gentle nature meant she never argued or said anything harsh.

He originally thought that with time, they would eventually get married.

Who knew that later, the General Bureau would target Tucha, and coincidentally, a group that had been trading with Tucha in other areas was arrested.

Among them, a female drug dealer bore a striking resemblance to Gao Yangyang in both appearance and voice.

From then on, Gao Yangyang went undercover in Tucha under the alias Wild Rose.

He thought that with Gao Yangyang back, things could continue.

But who knew that Gao Yangyang would actually tell him to shut up?

“You tell me to shut up?” Zeng Fan said, unable to accept it.

Gao Yangyang coldly rebuked him, “What right do you have to question Comrade Jiang Tian?”

With these words, everyone's eyes fixed on Zeng Fan.

Zeng Fan never expected that Gao Yangyang, who used to be considerate of others' feelings, would show him such disrespect in public.

"Yangyang, I almost got killed by drug dealers to save you! He just met you on the way and brought you back, and you're still defending him like this? That bastard, what kind of spell did he cast on you?" Zeng Fan was furious.

But the next moment...

*Slap!*

A loud slap rang out.

Zeng Fan looked up in disbelief, only then realizing...

It was Gao Yangyang who slapped him?

The gentle, virtuous, and beautiful goddess Gao Yangyang, whom he once admired, had actually slapped him?

And with such force?

Zeng Fan was stunned.

At this moment, the impact of that slap was more psychological than the two slaps Jiang Tian had given him.

"You saved me? What did you save me from? You keep saying he brought me back halfway, but do you know that we were surrounded and pursued by drug dealers for dozens, even hundreds of kilometers, facing danger at every turn? You ran away before us! Do you know what dangers we encountered?" Gao Yangyang roared.

"Yangyang, let me explain," Zeng Fan said, his heart pounding with fear.

But Gao Yangyang didn't give him a chance to explain: "You keep saying that because of you, you attracted the attention of the drug dealers' rival forces. Then tell me, which drug dealer forces? You can't name them. You didn't even see them on the outskirts before running away. Let me tell you, it was Jiang Tian who went in. Inside the villa, he went back and forth, killing over a hundred drug dealers three times. He personally brought me, carrying me on his back from the drug dealers' lair. It was Comrade Jiang Tian who carried me back through the drug dealers' pursuit across the mountains and fields. And what did you do? Not only did you not help, but after returning, you first slandered Comrade Jiang Tian and spread rumors based on your own speculation?"

Boom!!!

As Gao Yangyang finished speaking, everyone present was stunned.

They were all completely dumbfounded by her words.

Their heads were buzzing; they didn't even know what they were thinking.
