Gao Yangyang nodded, trying to appear calm, and answered the phone. Gao Xiaolong's voice immediately followed: "Where are you now?"

"Ah, I...I...I'm at home," Gao Yangyang stammered.

"That's strange. I was just on the phone with Xiao Jiang and I thought I heard your voice. Looks like I must have misheard," Gao Xiaolong muttered. "Alright, we have an operation tonight, I can't go back. You should get some rest."

Jiang Tian wasn't idle either.

He quickly dressed and drove away.

After Jiang Tian got drunk, Gao Yangyang somehow managed to get him transported to a hotel right next to the bar they'd just been at.

As he left the hotel, Jiang Tian saw several couples checking into rooms.

He glanced at the time; it was already past nine o'clock.

He hailed a taxi and arrived at the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

"Captain."

"Captain Jiang."

At the entrance of the Municipal Public Security Bureau, Zhao Wei and Li Zijin were waiting for Jiang Tian. Their eyes lit up with excitement as they rushed over to greet him.

"Your injuries are healed?" Jiang Tian asked Zhao Wei in surprise.

Zhao Wei pointed to the bandage on his head: "It looks scary, but it's just some superficial injuries, nothing serious."

"I've been busy since you were hospitalized. I was just planning to visit you tomorrow, but you've already been discharged," Jiang Tian said with a smile.

"Captain, I've heard everything. What you did was truly impressive, especially rescuing Gao Yangyang. It was incredibly impressive." Zhao Wei knew Gao Yangyang before.

They were both children of high-ranking officials.

"Alright, enough bragging. Tell me, what exactly happened?" Jiang Tian led the way inside.

Upon entering the city bureau's compound, he immediately saw that the yard was completely filled with vehicles.

There were military vehicles, police cars, and even armed police vehicles. This startled Jiang Tian, ​​and he immediately understood that something serious had indeed happened.

Otherwise, given Gao Xiaolong's personality, he wouldn't have been forced to come over overnight after working for a day and a night without rest.

“Captain Jiang, you're late and don't know. Tonight, the military and police mobilized over a thousand people to the transaction location in Gaodian Town. But who knew that before they even arrived, the Yun family's drug dealers received advance warning and laid an ambush, resulting in heavy casualties. Of course, no matter how strong the drug dealers are, it's like an egg hitting a rock. After being scattered, some of them kidnapped a group of hostages and fled into the mountains. The search is still ongoing, and among them is the son of an important figure in our province.” Zhao Wei's intelligence was very detailed.

Jiang Tian was shocked.

He looked at Zhao Wei in surprise. He hadn't expected that Old Master Mu's son had been kidnapped in this way.

Thinking to himself, Jiang Tian walked into the Municipal Public Security Bureau. The bureau was brightly lit.

Jiang Tian glanced around.

Many people were coming and going, all busy at work.

“Little Jiang, you're here. Chief Gao is waiting for you in the conference room,” Yu Hui, sitting in front of the computer, waved and smiled at Jiang Tian.

"Alright, I understand."

Jiang Tian nodded with a smile.

He then opened the conference room door and led Zhao Wei and Li Zijin inside.

Wow.

Even though Jiang Tian was mentally prepared, he was still slightly surprised when he saw what was before him.

As far as the eye could see:

Gao Xiaolong sat at the head of the large round table.

On either side sat Xu Poye and Shen Han. Even Zhou Zhao and another man in an administrative jacket—Jiang Tian recognized them; they seemed to be secretaries from the political and legal system—were present.

Leaders from the armed police were also there.

Good heavens! Jiang Tian only glanced around briefly; those seated at the round table were mostly wearing white shirts, and about 20% were in military uniforms.

Even Song Liguo, the detachment leader, could only sit on the outer benches to listen in. Guo, the captain, and Wei Xun, the captain of the second battalion, were only qualified to stay in the corners.

Even someone of Jiang Tian's rank, the first-rank officer of the third company, didn't have the right to enter.

Besides, unsurprisingly, at the very front of the audience sat Old Mu, his face grim and his expression somber.

Next to Old Mu was a beautiful woman constantly weeping.

Upon closer inspection, Jiang Tian recognized her.

Wasn't this the old hag who had mocked him earlier?



Chapter 160 Now, please welcome Officer Jiang to come forward and speak.


Jiang Tian had no problem with Old Master Mu, but he had a lot of complaints about this old hag. He decided to play dead and keep his eyes down.

After all, if he came out later, this old hag would probably jump out and mock him again.

Jiang Tian had done enough of this in his previous life; he had never suffered such humiliation in this life. Jumping out would be just asking for trouble.

So, after opening the door, Jiang Tian saw an empty seat in the corner, gave Zhao Wei and Li Zijin a look, and quietly went to sit down.

What surprised Jiang Tian was that the person speaking on the stage was someone he knew, someone he had worked on a case with before.

Miss Qiu.

Jiang Tian had worked with Zhu Tianqiu on a case during the search for Mr. Yun.

She was a very slovenly girl with dead fish eyes, but also very elegant and beautiful.

"My God, Brother Jiang, look how smooth Miss Qiu's hair is today! My God, did she put on makeup? How can she be so beautiful?" Zhao Wei patted Jiang Tian excitedly.

Yes, even in his saintly mode, Jiang Tian was immediately struck by Miss Qiu's beauty.

It wasn't that Miss Qiu possessed a breathtaking beauty; it was simply that her appearance—her eyes were lifeless, her mouth was like a goldfish's mouth, and she usually looked rather slovenly.

But when she cleaned up and put on makeup, each of her individually unremarkable features combined to create a stunningly beautiful face, captivating at first glance.

"Miss Qiu has changed her appearance?" Li Zijin asked, equally surprised.

After all, everyone knew about Miss Qiu's slovenly appearance. Even the police said she was the most beautiful flower, and if she weren't so unkempt, she would rival many celebrities.

Now it seemed that their words were true.

Jiang Tian led his men out the back door, causing no disturbance inside the house.

Miss Qiu continued her incessant reasoning: "Logically speaking, judging from the route map, the drug traffickers have three escape routes. If rescuing the hostages is their top priority, there's a 70% chance they'll retreat along the Kela River, and a 30% chance they'll go through Yunding Mountain. But given the speed at which the drug traffickers have taken hostages, even if they killed the hostages, they should have found the bodies along those two routes. But they haven't found anything yet. Now I think there's a third route."

"That means either they're hiding in a cave in the mountains, or they're going in the opposite direction, perhaps heading back into the country. But I'm more inclined to believe it's a cave."

Miss Qiu pointed to a new route.

Meanwhile, Zhao Wei whispered in Jiang Tian's ear, "Captain Jiang, what do you think?"

Jiang Tian shook his head, offering no opinion.

He was determined not to say a word this time.

After all, this operation was led by headquarters, and it wouldn't be good for him to stand out.

With so many leaders present, Jiang Tian had already shown remarkable talent and now wanted to calm down and reflect.

However, Jiang Tian's silent head-shaking gesture was immediately noticed by Miss Qiu, who was analyzing the logic.

If anyone else had shaken their head, Miss Qiu felt it would have been an insult.

After all, the genius detective girl was no joke.

Even in the IQ test, she only scored slightly lower than Einstein.

However, Miss Qiu had witnessed Jiang Tian's logical reasoning firsthand, especially in Mr. Yun's case and when Lao Liu planned to bomb Jiang Tian with TNT—both were discovered beforehand.

"Girl, please continue,"

Mu Ya's mother, who had been sobbing below, looked up anxiously at Zhu Tianqiu.

That afternoon, Liang Ru had firmly rejected her daughter, bluntly stating that a junior police officer had no future.

Not only was her future bleak, but her life was also insecure. Crucially, given Xiao Bo's example, even his character wasn't guaranteed. She had even arranged a blind date with the son of the Laisheng Group.

Mu Ya vehemently refused, infuriating Liang Ru. She even considered using Jiang Tian's father's political career as leverage to force Jiang Tian to willingly give up Mu Ya. After some inquiries, she unexpectedly learned that Jiang Tian's father had been nominated as the deputy secretary of Yunhai, the top official in the political and legal system.

Unexpected as it was, Liang Ru's confidence increased considerably.

Liang Ru tried calling Jiang Tian in the evening but couldn't get through, so she sent him a text message.

While she was busy, a call came from the city police station, interrupting Liang Ru's work.

The news she received nearly devastated Liang Ru: Mu Ya's father had been kidnapped by drug dealers?

Liang Ru felt a surge of anger and almost fainted.

After arriving at the city police station, Liang Ru learned about the situation, especially the family members of the hostages. She also paid close attention due to the special circumstances.

Upon entering, Liang Ru didn't say anything, but she was very impressed with the young woman's analysis.

She had analyzed the other party's characteristics and motives with remarkable clarity.

This gave Liang Ru more confidence in rescuing Mu Ya's father.

But now, she had stopped halfway through her explanation. Didn't she know that the entire city's police and military, and all the leaders in the office, were waiting for the next step of their deductions and plans to apprehend the drug dealers?

Every minute wasted increased the danger to the hostage!

Miss Qiu, without even raising her deadpan eyes, slowly said, "The specific situation requires specific analysis, and more importantly, it requires the expertise of the person conducting the analysis. Regarding the psychological logic analysis of drug dealers, I think we should have Officer Jiang, who is more professional, has extensive experience in drug enforcement, and understands drug dealers best, to offer his opinion. He will certainly have a more accurate and precise analysis."

"Your city bureau has such talent? Why didn't you come sooner?" Liang Ru frowned slightly and said to Jiang Peng beside her.

That's why the two of them were sitting together.

After arriving at the city bureau, Liang Ru met with Jiang Peng. Amidst Jiang Peng's busy schedule, Liang Ru pulled him aside to speak privately about the situation.

She also told Jiang Peng to tell his son not to be so delusional at such a young age, not to strive for advancement but instead to curry favor with the powerful.

"You, as the bureau chief, bear the primary responsibility."

This left Jiang Peng speechless and dumbfounded.

However, Jiang Peng was indeed an honest man, and had always been honest and unassuming.

If his son really did have sex with that girl, and the girl's mother disapproved and looked down on them, there was nothing he could do.

"Just arrived, huh?" Jiang Peng replied reluctantly, his face not looking too good.

After all, this woman had stormed over today, arrogantly pulling him aside while he was still busy with his case, and launching into a tirade of accusations.

Not only did she call him useless, but she also insulted his son, Jiang Tian, ​​calling him useless.

Before, he wouldn't have bothered with being called useless.

But now, Jiang Tian was his greatest pride. If his son Jiang Tian was useless, then who in the world was capable?

Most importantly, his son had risked his life, infiltrating drug dens and fighting drug dealers to the death.

Being insulted like this had been burning a fire in Jiang Peng's heart, but looking at Old Mu, and thinking about what would happen if his son started dating that girl, he really had to keep quiet.

After all, his son had fallen for her.



Chapter 161 Are you crazy? How dare you publicly insult heroes as black sheep?


"You just got here? How did you handle this case? No wonder the whole country is criticizing Yunhai's security. Their police chiefs are always the first to respond to crimes, but you, as the director, aren't proactive at all. No wonder people are criticizing Yunhai." Liang Ru's face instantly darkened.

Why didn't you call in someone with this kind of talent sooner?

Do you realize that the time you're wasting means more time endangering her husband's life?

"Officer Jiang was on an emergency mission last night, working for a whole day and night. He only just got back to rest this afternoon," Jiang Peng explained.

"Even if he was busy, with such a big case, you should have come immediately. I, as a family member, have come, and your professional team has only just arrived. That's more than half an hour late! If something happens to the hostage, it'll all be your responsibility!" Liang Ru accused him urgently.

Jiang Peng's face grew even uglier. Who wouldn't worry about their son?

Jiang Tian had been deep overseas for two days and two nights with almost no sleep. Working such urgent overtime like this, who could withstand that?

Gao Xiaolong had even discussed it with Jiang Peng; they hadn't originally planned to call Jiang Tian for today's operation.

But the implications were too great, and the damage too severe, so they had no choice but to call him. However, they still delayed things a bit, just to let Jiang Tian sleep a little longer.

To rest a little longer.

Even a selfless leader like Gao Xiaolong was so understanding.

And you still dare to criticize him like this?

"Fine, I'll see who this Officer Jiang is," Liang Ru said, her face grim.

Actually, Liang Ru wasn't usually this short-tempered. The key issue was today. First, Jiang Tian—in Liang Ru's eyes, he was just a nobody—she chased him away, and her daughter didn't even understand her.

Then, the pillar of the family had been kidnapped and held hostage by drug dealers.

And the police couldn't even find where the drug dealers had gone.

"Professional talent, for such an emergency, and yet they arrive so late!" Liang Ru was furious, especially since Jiang Peng was the father of Jiang Tian, ​​that blond-haired guy. Seeing him like this only fueled her disgust.

"Officer Jiang, why were you shaking your head just now? Come up here!" Miss Qiu waved at Jiang Tian again.

Just after Miss Qiu's request, Jiang Tian was about to stand up when he heard Liang Ru's sarcastic remark. He really didn't want to be snubbed, and even if he had some thoughts, it wasn't worth revealing them now. So, Jiang Tian shook his head at Miss Qiu.

But Miss Qiu, with her maxed-out IQ and negative EQ, completely missed Jiang Tian's hint.

"Captain Jiang, hurry up, it's your turn to perform!" Zhao Wei excitedly pushed Jiang Tian, ​​but Jiang Tian remained seated, unmoved. Zhao Wei was getting impatient.

Well, another brainless idiot.

Their words instantly drew the attention of all the leaders in the huge conference room, including Elder Mu and Gao Xiaolong, to Jiang Tian.

Among them was Liang Ru.

At first glance, Liang Ru looked somewhat familiar.

The next moment.

Jiang Tian stood up. His height of 1.8 meters and his build, neither too thin nor too fat, made the subject matter appear perfectly natural.

With the enhancement of the keywords, it must be said that Jiang Tian's aura has been getting better and better.

And as Jiang Tian stood up, everyone in the conference room was instantly able to see him clearly.

Those who knew Jiang Tian's achievements were filled with anticipation.

But those who didn't...

Some hadn't seen him in a long time and were completely unaware of his recent exploits, nor had they ever met him. Upon seeing him, they couldn't help but frown.

Among them, the one with the most unpleasant expression was undoubtedly Liang Ru.

At this moment, seeing Jiang Tian stand up, Old Mu, who had remained silent until now, abruptly stood up.

That's right.

Old Mu genuinely admired the young man before him.

He was too outstanding.

So outstanding that Old Mu felt somewhat embarrassed to try and matchmake Jiang Tian with his granddaughter, Mu Ya.

Because he felt that although Mu Ya was also excellent, compared to Jiang Tian, ​​his granddaughter's achievements were utterly insignificant.

Especially since Mu Ya had only studied an ordinary journalism major and was preparing to work at a television station after graduation.

While Jiang Tian... he was already adorned with honors, and his men had killed hundreds of drug dealers.

Old Master Mu hadn't said a word, waiting for Jiang Tian to arrive.

Having seen Jiang Tian and personally learned about his incredible track record, Old Master Mu believed Jiang Tian had the best understanding of drug dealers' habits. This meant his son could be saved.

Old Master Mu stood up, his face beaming with joy.

But the next moment…

A figure stood up even faster than him.

His voice was countless times louder.

“You little brat, where is this place? Who let you in?” Liang Ru stood up and shouted at Jiang Tian.

In her eyes, Jiang Tian was a persistent ghost. Already in a bad mood, seeing Jiang Tian instantly ignited Liang Ru's anger, unleashing all her pent-up resentment.

This shout silenced the entire conference room.

Everyone paused, staring in astonishment at Liang Ru, then at Jiang Tian, ​​and finally turning to look at Old Master Mu.

Some of the more astute individuals, aware of Jiang Tian's contributions, were still speculating whether Old Master Mu harbored any resentment towards the Municipal Bureau or Jiang Tian.

But seeing Old Master Mu's blank expression and slightly agape mouth, they immediately realized they were overthinking it.

Among these.

Gao Xiaolong, at this moment, had a gloomy face.

If it weren't for the fact that Old Master Mu seemed unaware of the situation, he would have cursed Old Master Mu along with him today.

"I am the captain of the municipal anti-drug squad. Why am I not qualified to be here?" Jiang Tian's expression gradually turned cold.

He knew this woman was bound to jump out.

“You good-for-nothing! Everyone here is wearing white shirts. What right do you, a recent graduate and intern, have to come in?” Liang Ru snapped angrily.

Indeed, Liang Ru wasn't stupid. She had already glanced around and saw that almost everyone was wearing white shirts, except for Jiang Tian, ​​who was wearing a leather jacket and looked sloppy.

Her habitual thinking led her to believe that Jiang Tian was just habitually slacking off, daring to cause trouble in this kind of situation. Liang Ru was naturally going to seize this opportunity to teach this toad a lesson. Besides, like father, like son; the father was definitely no good either. His promotion was just a hindrance. She was perfectly positioned to ruin Jiang Peng's promotion path today. Her mind raced with thoughts…

Liang Ru suddenly changed her tone, saying, "I understand. You have a bureau chief father, so you can do whatever you want in the city bureau. But that's one thing on ordinary days, but now, at this critical moment of saving lives, why are you causing trouble? Jiang Peng, you let your son act so lawlessly in the city bureau, daring to waste time like this. With someone like that as a police officer, how can we, the people, feel at ease? This kind of black sheep must be expelled from the anti-drug team."

Silence!!!

The moment Liang Ru finished speaking,

the conference room fell into a deathly silence.

Some leaders who were unaware of the situation and didn't know Jiang Tian looked at him with smug expressions upon seeing Liang Ru's serious words.

Liang Ru's lips curled into a smile, her gaze looking down at Jiang Tian.

Don't say she looked down on Jiang Tian.

Even if Jiang Peng got promoted, she still wouldn't look down on him. What kind of family background did she have? Did she even deserve to be compared to someone from Jiang Tian's humble background?

The toad's lust for swan meat, coupled with Xiao Bo's example, solidified Liang Ru's resolve. She figured even her father-in-law would support her.

Thinking this, Liang Ru looked at Old Master Mu, only to find him standing before her, head bowed.

"Dad, the Municipal Bureau has gone too far..." Liang Ru was about to complain to Old Master Mu.



Chapter 162 Okay, okay, I'm leaving, I quit.


That afternoon, when Jiang Tian was at Mu Ya's house, he explicitly stated that when Liang Ru inquired about his family situation, he said he had just graduated and was currently interning in the anti-drug police force.

Considering...

The second semester has only been going on for a little over a month, and the second semester is generally for university internships, meaning he's just a junior police intern.

In this kind of situation, such an important operation, they actually let a lowly intern in.

What are they doing?

Rebelling?

Is there any hope for their family's breadwinner?

Dad, are you going to save your son? Are you just going to watch them act so recklessly, treating the police station like their own home?

Is this municipal police station public or part of the Jiang family? Does having a bureau chief as a father give them the right to be so arrogant?

Liang Ru's comment, "A junior intern doesn't even deserve to be here," and that everyone here is wearing white shirts, not allowed her to speak.

Not only was Jiang Tian stunned, but Zhao Wei also unconsciously stood up, his face flushed, because he felt that this crazy woman was making veiled insults.

After all, Jiang Tian was already a high-ranking officer, and he was the only junior intern who had followed Jiang Tian into the office.

Li Zijin, on the other hand, had been a full-fledged officer for several years.

Old Mu was also a bit bewildered. Even with his experience, he couldn't understand why his wife had suddenly gone crazy.

Why was she going crazy at anyone?

The key point was, going crazy at Jiang Tian?

Insolence.

But before Old Mu could explode, Jiang Tian turned to Zhao Wei and said bluntly, "Awei, since the hostage's family has a problem with us, we won't get involved. Farewell, everyone."

Without waiting for anyone's reaction, Jiang Tian pulled Zhao Wei and smoothly turned to leave.

This matter had already been taken over by headquarters.

He was the one who found the details and clues of the case.

"Wait," Old Mu raised his leg to stop him.

The crucial point was that he still didn't understand the situation, but one thing was absolutely clear: if Jiang Tian left, who would catch the drug dealers?

Relying on the heavily damaged headquarters?

Or should they send the original anti-drug squad?

Should they send Song Liguo?

If it were effective, how could the Yunhai City Public Security Bureau have achieved nothing in all these years, allowing the drug cartel to run rampant until Jiang Tian suddenly emerged?

Old Mu had just raised his hand when Liang Ru grabbed his wrist: "Dad, what's the point of keeping such a useless person around? The important thing now is to rescue your son, Mu Feng, and catch those drug dealers."

Old Mu was stopped by Liang Ru, his heart sinking completely.

Fine, fine.

You've driven Jiang Tian away, who will rescue him?

Who will lead the rescue team?

Who will make the arrests?

Those drug dealers who took hostages seemed to have vanished suddenly.

They can't be found, they simply can't be found.

Many even suspect they've already crossed the border.

What does crossing the border mean?

So far, in Yunhai, there's only been one successful hostage rescue overseas—the one carried out by Jiang Tian.

If they've already crossed the border, who will go to rescue them?

Click!!!

In that brief moment of delay, Jiang Tian flung open the conference room door.

"Where are you going?"

Just then, Gao Xiaolong stood up, his face grim, and shouted at Jiang Tian.

"Dad, did you see that? Chief Gao has had enough of that good-for-nothing brat. Someone like that shouldn't be in the police force; he's a menace, a cancer. Causing trouble at a time like this, a severe punishment would be too lenient. He should be expelled from the force!" Liang Ru exclaimed excitedly to Old Master Mu, her eyes gleaming.

At this moment, she completely failed to notice that Old Master Mu's expression had also changed.

"Chief Gao, it's not just him, that good-for-nothing; his father, the bureau chief, also bears a great deal of responsibility," Liang Ru continued, intensifying her attack.

This good-for-nothing actually dared to act tough in front of him this afternoon.

Talking about the ownership of a building—it's ridiculous.

A mere trainee police officer dares to come here so boldly.

Bold.

Insolent.

If she doesn't teach him a lesson he won't forget, how can she keep Jiang Tian away from her daughter from now on?

Liang Ru even secretly thought that it would be best to kill him outright.

That way, a little charity would make this good-for-nothing grateful and leave Mu Ya.

A toad dares to dream of eating swan meat? Dream on! He's not worthy. So what if his father is a bureau chief from a poor family?

Now Liang Ru wanted to take this opportunity to teach Jiang Tian a small lesson, to make him realize that in front of their family, he was nothing more than an ant that could be easily manipulated.

However...

Chief Gao ignored her.

Instead, he turned to look at Jiang Tian, ​​and in the next moment, Chief Gao, his face dark, repeated, "Where are you going?"

"I'm leaving," Jiang Tian said irritably.

"How dare you talk to Chief Gao like that, kid, even your father can't protect you." Liang Ru's eyes lit up.

"Leaving?"

Chief Gao raised his voice: "Where are you going?"

"Can't I go home and sleep?" Jiang Tian really didn't want to stay here.

Since being reborn, when had he ever suffered such humiliation?

Slapping that old hag twice would make Jiang Tian look petty, and Mu Ya would lose face.

Not slapping her would make him sick to look at her face.

Since that's how it is.

Can't he just leave?

“You quit?” Chief Gao said in a deep voice.

“Quit. Let whoever wants to do it do it. I quit.” Jiang Tian flung his hand away.

Jiang Tian actually had his own thoughts about this case.

Everyone said they'd take care of him?

But were they ever following his advice?

He discovered the case.

He caught the bloody hand.

Headquarters took over the subsequent investigation, but the mission was botched. Fine, he'd clean up the mess.

He might as well suffer a little.

What is he doing now?

The hostage's family is hurling insults at him. What is he doing here?

Seeing this, Liang Ru's face instantly flushed with excitement. "Good, good, good! This audacious scoundrel dares to speak to Chief Gao like that!"

"Looking for death."

"Bold."

"Defying the heavens."

Liang Ru laughed, her face full of sarcasm. "You good-for-nothing, you have no ability but a big temper. Ha, does the Municipal Bureau not work without you? Does the earth stop turning without you? What can you do besides cause trouble? Chief Gao, expel this kind of trash from the Party immediately!"

*Buzz!*

Old Mu's head exploded.

"Shut up," Old Mu snapped, his face darkening, finally unable to bear it any longer.

"Dad, I was just saying," Liang Ru pouted.

Old Mu's eyes were blazing with fury, his head buzzing. Damn it, if he wasn't careful, his daughter-in-law was going to expel him from the Party?

Chief Gao was almost laughing in anger.

But he didn't have time to deal with Liang Ru now. Instead, he turned to Jiang Tian and said in a low voice, "If you quit, who will?"

Buzz!!!

Liang Ru, who had been watching the spectacle from the sidelines, waiting for Gao Xiaolong to explode and expel Jiang Tian from the Party, even implicating Director Jiang Peng, stared blankly at Chief Gao.

Was she hallucinating just now?

Or had Chief Gao lost his mind?

What...what...what is he saying?



Chapter 163 The old hag was beaten and cried, looking pitiful.


"No, Chief Gao, with so many leaders and so many people, what can a useless trainee policeman do?" Liang Ru asked, her face filled with disbelief.

But to her surprise, no one in the entire conference room paid her any attention, not even uttering a word. Everyone was focused on the unfolding situation.

Especially on Jiang Tian.

Gao Xiaolong walked up to Jiang Tian, ​​his face full of helplessness. "If you quit, are you going to make me do it?"

After saying this, Gao Xiaolong looked at Song Liguo: "Or your branch captain?"

Song Liguo was startled, his scalp tingling, and he hurriedly stood up and shook his head.

What a joke.

Thousands of military and police officers had searched the entire mountain, and they hadn't found a single hair.

The hostages had long since been taken somewhere unknown.

Headquarters had suffered heavy losses.

What use could a mere branch captain like him possibly be?

Gao Xiaolong patted Jiang Tian on the shoulder and said, "Give me some face, stay. You'll lead the team this time."

Buzz!!!

Those familiar with Gao Xiaolong were utterly dumbfounded.

Wait, what did Gao Xiaolong just say?

Give him face?

Especially since Elder Mu was acquainted with Gao Xiaolong, and as a member of the Mu family, Liang Ru was naturally very familiar with Gao Xiaolong's personality.

A typical person who couldn't tolerate the slightest injustice.

The kind who wouldn't give anyone face, utterly upright and unyielding.

And this kind of person actually said to Jiang Tian, ​​"Give me face?"

Liang Ru's head was spinning.

Staring wide-eyed at the scene before her, he was just a trainee policeman, just a good-for-nothing, a good-for-nothing who dreamed of eating swan meat, while you, Chief Gao Xiaolong, were a legendary figure!

Are you crazy?

Is this world too crazy?

"Don't worry, I'll give you an explanation," Gao Xiaolong said to Jiang Tian.

To be honest.

Gao Xiaolong was completely bewildered, utterly clueless as to why Liang Ru harbored such malice towards Jiang Tian.

The crucial point was...

Attacking him from other angles was one thing, but to actually call him a good-for-nothing?

And demanding his expulsion from the Party?

She actually wanted to expel Jiang Tian from the Party!

Gao Xiaolong was utterly dumbfounded.

He vividly remembered that afternoon, Jiang Tian and Mu Ya were inseparable at the door, and Gao Xiaolong had even felt a pang of envy, thinking that his granddaughter Gao Yangyang probably had no chance. If only it were his granddaughter!

From both a public and personal perspective, Jiang Tian was undoubtedly the perfect son-in-law, with limitless future potential.

Thump, thump, thump!!!

Gao Xiaolong approached Liang Ru with a grim expression.

“Chief Gao, why are you still keeping this good-for-nothing?” Liang Ru's eyes widened, desperately wondering if she had just hallucinated.

Why did something feel off?

"Expel him from the Party?" Gao Xiaolong said in a low voice, suppressing his anger.

Liang Ru quickly nodded: "Chief Gao, he's so young, joining the police force, he should be thinking about how to serve the country, he should be proudly doing things that others can't, but this good-for-nothing..."

*Slap!*

This slap wasn't from Gao Xiaolong.

It was from Old Mu.

At this moment, Old Mu's anger completely exploded.

He had just been holding back because he wanted to handle important matters. As for Liang Ru and the conflict with Jiang Tian, ​​he would deal with that after this case was finished.

Now, Liang Ru's incessant chatter had completely enraged Old Master Mu.

Especially when he heard about Jiang Tian's expulsion from the Party, Old Master Mu's mind went blank.

He raised his hand and slapped Liang Ru across the face.

*Whoosh!*

Liang Ru, who was expecting Jiang Tian's expulsion from the Party, was instantly stunned.

Old Master Mu hit her?

Why did he hit her?

Liang Ru's eyes widened in disbelief.

Old Master Mu, suppressing his anger, said, "You keep calling me trash, what are you?"

"Dad, you..." Liang Ru didn't understand why Old Master Mu hit her.

"I'm not your father. Who gave you the right to expel Xiao Jiang from the Party? What do you think this place is? You say someone has a bureau chief father? What, you don't have an official father-in-law to act so recklessly here? Who gave you the right to expel him from the Party?" Old Master Mu was practically driven mad with rage.

Because...

Saying this here is no less impactful than publicly announcing the expulsion of a "living Lei Feng" decades ago.

"Xiao Jiang? Dad, you know him. Don't be fooled by this good-for-nothing," Liang Ru shouted.

Hiss!!!

Old Mu gasped, feeling a chill run down his spine as he looked at Liang Ru.

This daughter-in-law is unacceptable.

Absolutely unacceptable.

Slap!!!

Old Mu slapped her again.

Gao Xiaolong didn't retaliate, because doing so would be an overstepping of his bounds in front of Old Mu.

Even if Old Mu didn't say anything, in the eyes of others, this was tantamount to publicly humiliating him.

Old Mu roared, "He's a good-for-nothing, and what are you? You're even worse than a piece of trash! Xiao Jiang risked his life to protect the people and their property. And what have you done? What have you done? How dare you call him a good-for-nothing?"

"Dad, please don't hit me anymore, sob sob..." Liang Ru covered her face.

A searing pain made Liang Ru recoil in shock.

*Slap!*

Old Mu, without a word, slapped her again.

Liang Ru covered her face and wept.

Only after Gao Xiaolong's gloomy expression gradually softened did Old Mu stop.

Then, Old Mu looked at Jiang Tian and said, "Jiang, this case is indispensable. I hope you can take full charge. I know this isn't a short-term task, but rest assured, whether you succeed or not, no one will blame you. If you succeed, I believe even headquarters will apply for a collective first-class merit for the Yunhai City Bureau."

After speaking, Old Mu looked at Jiang Peng, then at Jiang Tian and said, "Jiang, is that alright now?"

*Buzz!*

The moment Old Mu asked Jiang Tian,

Liang Ru's eyes nearly popped out of their sockets.

No.

What is Old Mu saying?

What are you saying?

"You're a high-ranking leader, yet you still need to ask a junior police intern for his opinion?"

Was she crazy?

The next moment, Jiang Tian's answer filled Liang Ru with even greater horror, as if she had seen a ghost.

"I, Jiang Tian, ​​have never suffered any injustice in my life, but today I'll give you face, Elder Mu." Jiang Tian accepted it very calmly.

Hiss!!!

Liang Ru gasped sharply.

What does this reluctant attitude mean?

What's wrong with this world?

Liang Ru was completely bewildered. She had only made a couple of sarcastic remarks to Jiang Tian, ​​so why did Gao Xiaolong look like he wanted to kill her, and why did Elder Mu slap her so violently?

Because of Jiang Tian?

He's just a useless trainee police officer, why are you all protecting him like this?

Liang Ru felt wronged, and didn't even think she had done anything wrong.

Even...

In truth, if Jiang Tian were truly an ordinary person with no merits, he would be powerless to resist being challenged in this meeting room.

But Jiang Tian now wields absolute and decisive power.

Of course, although Jiang Tian still dislikes this old hag, now that Gao Xiaolong and Elder Mu have made their stance clear, it would be tantamount to being unreasonable if Jiang Tian left.

Therefore, Jiang Tian hesitated for a moment and nodded slightly.



Chapter 164 Alright, so that's how you solve cases!


As Jiang Tian nodded,

it was visibly clear that both Elder Mu and Gao Xiaolong instantly breathed a sigh of relief.

She couldn't understand why everyone was so protective of him, just an ordinary intern police officer.

"Now, let's invite Officer Jiang to come up and share his opinion."

Miss Qiu, with her deadpan expression, seemed to have been watching the spectacle with a hint of lingering satisfaction.

Jiang Tian's face darkened. He seriously suspected that Miss Qiu was doing it on purpose. What was with that regretful, lingering expression?

Thinking to himself...

Jiang Tian walked to the podium. After what had just happened, especially with even Elder Mu and Gao Xiaolong protecting him, even those who didn't know Jiang Tian were now watching intently.

They wanted to see the young man the leadership favored.

Those who knew Jiang Tian were filled with anticipation, eager to hear his insights.

"I believe Officer Jiang's analysis will offer unique insights and help us find this group more quickly."

Miss Qiu, with her deadpan expression, also looked at Jiang Tian with some expectation.

Seeing Miss Qiu's deadpan gaze, Jiang Tian's brow twitched, and he continuously analyzed the situation in his mind.

A moment later.

"I stayed downstairs for a short while and have a general understanding of the situation. The exact location should be around here."

Jiang Tian pointed to the location on the map.

*Pfft!*

Miss Qiu spat out her drink.

Everyone present was instantly stunned.

They stared at Jiang Tian, ​​pointing to the town's location on the map.

[Ding, the "Criminal Investigation Genius" attribute upgrade is complete]

[Criminal Investigation Genius (Blue) - King of Criminal Investigation (Red)] (Your criminal investigation thinking is nearly unparalleled, allowing you to easily find the truth of events from subtle clues.)

Current Sin Value Remaining: [1200]

That's right, Jiang Tian upgraded his attributes when he first came up. The attributes he farmed overseas upgraded his physical attributes, leaving him with seven or eight thousand. After acquiring the Blood Hand, Jiang Tian's sin value increased by nearly four thousand, leaving him with around eleven thousand.

Upgrading to King of Criminal Investigation left him with just over a thousand.

While down there, Jiang Tian had some ideas, but he wasn't certain.

After all, in terms of reasoning and logic, Jiang Tian and Miss Qiu were about equal; he could only glean so much more information.

After upgrading to the "King of Criminal Investigation" red-level skill, Jiang Tian, ​​in that instant, combined with his photographic memory, practically knew the answer at a glance.

Especially since the "King of Criminal Investigation" skill also comes with a strong intuition, a logical intuition about the criminal's thinking.

He could keenly sense the criminal's hiding place.

And his understanding of the criminal's various habits, etc., made Jiang Tian seem to have solved tens of thousands of cases of all kinds in that instant.

"I've made a bold guess, and it can probably only be in Gaodian Town," Jiang Tian said, pointing to the exact location of Gaodian Town with a smirk.

Xu Poye jumped up and said, "Little Jiang, Gaodian Town is a drug trafficking den. It was taken down immediately, and there are still quite a few military and police stationed there to investigate. Are you sure you're not mistaken?"

“Not only am I not mistaken, I even know that those drug dealers are not only hiding in Gaodian Town with hostages, but I even know their exact location.”

Jiang Tian slightly zoomed in on the map, emphasizing a circle drawn near the border of Gaodian Town, and smiled, “It’s in this area.”

“You say it’s in this area, and it’s really in this area?” Liang Ru sneered.

She had genuinely been fooled just now, thinking that with Gao Xiaolong and Elder Mu protecting him, he might have some skills.

But now it seemed…

It was completely unnecessary.

Because this was just a useless fool spouting empty words and talking nonsense.

“Xiao Jiang, you’ve only just arrived. Shouldn’t you take a look at the details of the case?” Jiang Peng stood up, looking worried.

If he were to boast now and his reasoning were completely wrong, it would be a significant blow to Jiang Tian.

Building reputation is difficult, but damaging it often only takes a moment.

Jiang Tian has only been here for a short time; he hasn’t even looked at the case in detail, nor has he investigated the geographical location.

"Are you really that sure where they are?" Jiang Tian asked.

"Since I was able to find Blood Hand, I can naturally find the drug dealers who traded with Blood Hand. I said it's here, so it's definitely here. You can go and search directly," Jiang Tian said.

Actually, up to this point, only the collective achievement had piqued Jiang Tian's interest.

In this case, Jiang Tian was a shoo-in for a first-class merit; just taking down Blood Hand guaranteed him an individual first-class merit.

Especially since they also seized the ledger.

Jiang Tian had received many individual merits.

But he had never received a collective merit before, and knowing the system's usual practices, there was a high probability of a "collecting stamps" reward.

Jiang Tian was extremely envious of the "collecting stamps" reward.

"You said it's here, so it's here. Have you been to Gaodian Town? Do you know what the situation is like? Which direction did they run in?" Liang Ru couldn't help but jump in.

Jiang Tian glanced at the old hag: "Whether I know or not is unimportant. Finding the culprit is the most important thing."

It was quite a coincidence.

Gaodian Town was where Jiang Tian was initially assigned in his previous life.

After Gao Xiaolong was killed by drug dealers in his previous life, Jiang Tian was assigned to Gaodian Town as a police officer in the household registration office for over a month. About a month later, perhaps with the secret help of Zhao Wei's sister, Zhao Qiuni, Jiang Tian returned to the city and became a retired, leisurely archivist.

Jiang Tian had spent a lot of time in Gaodi Town.

After determining the criminal's approximate escape route, Jiang Tian, ​​with his familiarity with the geography of Gaodian Town,

instantly pinpointed the exact location.

"In the southeast of Gaodian Town, there's an abandoned brick factory. This brick factory is underground, the perfect hiding place," Jiang Tian said, pointing to the location he had drawn.

As Jiang Tian finished speaking,

Miss Qiu, also a genius of deduction, looked completely bewildered.

Because she simply couldn't understand Jiang Tian's reasoning.

Jiang Tian knew the general location, that was one thing.

But he even knew the exact location?

He deduced the brick factory from that?

“Impossible! The military and police came from this direction. Even if it's the darkest hour, they wouldn't choose this spot. Besides, they came from the town; they couldn't possibly get past the military and police head-on.” Xu Poye's face was full of disbelief.

“Whether they're there or not, just send the people from headquarters to check.” Jiang Tian said without changing his expression.

Gao Xiaolong also rested his chin on his hand, looking somewhat bewildered. In all his years in the police system, Gao Xiaolong had never seen a case solved like this!

Mainly because…

It was too unbelievable.

Jiang Tian had only been here a few minutes, hadn't even gathered any information on the way, and yet he'd found them right away?

And not only the approximate location, but the exact hiding place?

Xu Poye picked up his walkie-talkie and called the people stationed at headquarters on-site.

He ordered them to go to the brick factory to find the drug dealers.

However, even while giving orders, Xu Poye couldn't help but frown at Jiang Tian.

It wouldn't be a problem if they couldn't find them.

However, if Jiang Tian becomes arrogant, that would be very dangerous.

This is a bad sign.

"How could we possibly find it!" Xu Poye sighed slightly.

The drug dealers really listen to you, pointing wherever you want!



Chapter 165 Headquarters was stunned. Were they sure this wasn't a regular army?


Gaodian Town.

This serves as a temporary command center, housing the headquarters of the operation.

This underscores the crucial nature of this mission.

Yunhai has always been known as a crime capital, a city whose booming economy could easily place it among the top in the country.

But this is all a facade. Beneath this prosperous surface, crime in Yunhai occurs at a terrifying pace every moment.

In unseen corners, countless criminal transactions and killings take place daily.

The more glamorous the exterior, the darker the interior.

Because these criminals dare to lay their hands on even Gao Xiaolong.

Before Jiang Tian's transmigration, all he knew, besides Gao Xiaolong's death, was that his father, Jiang Peng, had been dismissed, and two bureau chiefs sent down from Beijing had all died in various accidents.

The horrific acts are appalling.

Does headquarters not know about Yunhai's crimes? Of course they do. That's why they sent Gao Xiaolong to oversee the operation.

As the investigation progressed, it became shocking to discover the terrifying extent of the criminal organization in Yunhai.

Furthermore,

In the case of Blood Hand and the Yun family, headquarters directly intervened. Xu Poye came to present Jiang Tian with an award and to communicate with the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

And this time...

The person personally leading the investigation was none other than Zeng Fan's mentor, Gong Zhe, a highly important leader in the 21st Bureau.

If Jiang Tian knew, he would understand that this was an opportunity to save face for Zeng Fan.

Of course, even if Jiang Tian knew, it wouldn't matter.

Because at this stage of the case, Jiang Tian had already received all the credit he could. Taking on this mess now was because collective merit belonged to the collective,

and even more so to the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Gong Zhe arrived confidently. Frankly, Gong Zhe had investigated countless major drug cases nationwide.

He had accumulated numerous honors along the way.

Including his legendary past, he had cracked a single drug trafficking case involving over 500 kilograms, which caused a national sensation.

Having solved hundreds of drug-related cases, large and small, throughout his life, Gong Zhe believed that the intelligence for this case was clear.

The mission was clear.

The location was clear.

The crime scene was also clear about the time, so there was no problem at all. Moreover, thousands of military and police officers had been mobilized, and everything was proceeding according to plan. What problems could there be?

That's right.

That's what Gong Zhe thought when he arrived.

Even Jiang Tian from the Municipal Public Security Bureau, who had made so many contributions, was assigned this mission by Gong Zhe. It was purely due to the local government's inaction that the arrogant, incompetent drug dealer was caught.

And his apprentice was too disappointing, failing miserably on his arrival.

He wanted to take this opportunity to make his apprentice proud and save face. His plan seemed to be going smoothly.

The battle plan had been mentally reviewed countless times, ensuring absolute certainty of not underestimating the enemy, going all out, and leaving no room for error. Then came the triumphant moment.

But the result was completely unexpected!

"I've spent half my life arresting drug dealers, and I've never seen anyone carrying a rocket launcher!" Gong Zhe slammed his fist on the table and cursed.

The master had come to bring glory to his apprentice, but instead, he suffered a crushing defeat.

Even the son of a powerful figure had been kidnapped by drug traffickers.

The master himself had suffered heavy losses, completely unprepared for the drug traffickers' overwhelming firepower. Of course, confronting the state apparatus was a death sentence.

Even with such overwhelming firepower, they were ultimately routed and forced to flee.

This mission was completely different from what he had imagined. He thought it would be an easy annihilation, but instead, he suffered heavy losses and many drug traffickers escaped?

"Master, will we still get a first-class merit this time?" Zeng Fan asked expectantly.

Looking at Zeng Fan's innocent expression, Gong Zhe almost slapped him on the head, saying irritably, "Meritorious service? You're lucky if you're not punished. Don't even think about merit unless you arrest those people and atone for your sins."

"Master, where could they possibly run off to?!" Zeng Fan complained helplessly.

"Sigh!!!"

Gong Zhe sighed deeply.

Just then, a voice came through the walkie-talkie. It was Xu Poye. Due to the urgency, Xu Poye directly told Gong Zhe that, according to Comrade Jiang Tian's deduction, the drug traffickers holding hostages were likely at a brick factory in Gaodian Town, near the border, to the southeast.

"Jiang Tian's deduction?"

"He's at the scene?"

"A brute who only knows how to kill can make deductions?"

"And he even deduced the exact location?"

Zeng Fan, who already had a problem with Jiang Tian, ​​jumped up and vehemently refuted him.

Gong Zhe frowned: "The worst thing you can do with this is for an outsider to direct an expert. Send someone over to check it out."

If Jiang Tian had been there analyzing the situation, it would have been understandable. The problem was that he was far away at the city bureau, and they themselves didn't have much information, so how could they trust him?

But Gong Zhe was a highly professional individual. Even if he didn't believe him, he was forced by Xu Poye's orders to take people there.

Just as they were about to leave, a group of armed police officers escorted five or six drug dealers in: "Commander Gong, we just arrested a few drug dealers. They might have some leads."

"Master, what about the brick factory?" Zeng Fan quickly asked.

"Take a few men and check it out first. I don't have time," Gong Zhe said directly.

Because, compared to the illogical reasoning of a newly graduated narcotics officer, Gong Zhe had much more faith in the drug dealers' leads.

If they could deduce it, would they have needed to search for so long?

It's a joke.

Gong Zhe immediately used a memory restoration technique on the drug dealers, and they confessed honestly.

But unfortunately, there were still no leads on the group of criminals who had taken the hostages.

This made Gong Zhe frown deeply.

At that moment, after Jiang Tian finished speaking, he returned to his seat on the edge of the room. After all, everyone there was a leader; even Captain Guo was relegated to a corner, not worthy of sitting at the table. If Jiang Tian were to sit at the table, wouldn't that seem too arrogant?

"Kid, if you can't find it, you'll bear all the responsibility," Liang Ru gritted her teeth, wishing she could bite the little brat to death.

Her cheeks were red and swollen from the slaps; Old Master Mu really hadn't held back!

Jiang Tian didn't even glance at her.

What a joke! Paying any attention to this old hag was a waste of time.

Besides, for Jiang Tian, ​​choosing Mu Ya was already a decision he'd already hesitated over.

It wasn't that Jiang Tian was fickle; in his past life, Wang Yifei had already caused him post-traumatic stress disorder.

Having in-laws like that meant his future life would be absolutely chaotic, and this had already instilled a sense of fear in Jiang Tian.

"I still need some time to calm down; perhaps I acted too impulsively," Jiang Tian sighed.



Chapter 166 You are committing a crime


He sat in his seat, eyes closed, resting.

Quickly.

He even slapped her for it.

If their family's financial situation were worse, if there hadn't been the precedent of Xiao Bo, she might have agreed.

Liang Ru thought to herself.

As for his appearance, even if Jiang Tian was handsome, he was worthless in Liang Ru's eyes.

But before Xu Poyue could even speak,

Whoa!!!

He guessed right?

This time, Xu Poye finally snapped out of his daze and was swept away by extreme excitement.

Meanwhile, Liang Ru, still seated,

was also stunned.

Swish!!!

If you're wrong, admit it; if you're going to be punished, stand up straight.

Jiang Tian might become their benefactor.

But even if he was their benefactor, if he wanted to become their son-in-law, they wouldn't necessarily give him a chance.

Liang Ru's mind raced.

But unfortunately, Jiang Tian didn't listen at all.

Because Jiang Tian had already reached the terrifying red-level "King of Criminal Investigation."

As the King of Criminal Investigation, he encompassed not only the field of crime-solving and investigation.

This includes criminal investigation subfields such as psychology and micro-expression psychology, which have reached a terrifying level.

Now, Jiang Tian keenly sensed something was off about the voice on the walkie-talkie.

He was still thinking about it.

Sure enough, the next moment.

The voice on the walkie-talkie sounded again: "Director Xu, I was just about to report this to you."

Isn't this ridiculous?

At this moment, everyone in the excited office also turned their gaze to the walkie-talkie.

Outsiders see things more clearly; they all sensed something was wrong.

Old foxes like Gao Xiaolong and Mu Lao immediately pricked up their ears, listening intently without saying a word.

When we were about to move out, we had just arrested several drug dealers and were awaiting interrogation, so I didn't go. I sent Zeng Fan's squadron to scout the situation instead. Unexpectedly, the drug dealers noticed and alerted them. Two members of Zeng Fan's squadron were killed in action, and three were shot and wounded."

"As for the drug dealers and hostages, apart from a few killed, they all escaped."

BOOM!!!

It exploded.

The scene completely erupted.

Everyone stood up on the spot.

The expressions on their faces were as ugly as could be.

Bang!!!

Gao Xiaolong slammed his fist on the table and stood up angrily, shouting, "What kind of work have you done?"

That's right.

Such a simple task, and you've made it so complicated.

The key is, when the credit was handed to you, you managed to snatch it away.

Enraged.

Furious.

If Gao Xiaolong was so furious, how much more so would Elder Mu?

To be honest,

before this, no one really took the drug dealers in Yunhai seriously.

Or rather, their misjudgment was quite severe.

Because of Jiang Tian's repeated meritorious deeds—single-handedly slaughtering criminal gangs like pigs—the people from headquarters, while not underestimating these criminal groups, still gave them sufficient attention.

However, they treated it like any other operation they'd ever used against drug dealers.

The first wave of raids resulted in a complete rout.

"We gave you information, we gave you intelligence, and your second wave was even worse—you let all the drug dealers escape?"

"I'm telling you, this is a crime!" Gao Xiaolong slammed his fist on the table, roaring in fury.



Chapter 167: Just left, and he's been found again? Is he a god?


"Director Xu, I was wrong. Don't worry, I've already sent people to search along the route. They definitely won't be able to escape this time," Gong Zhe explained.

The voice on the walkie-talkie went silent.

Because it wasn't just Xu Poye who was furious.

Of course, although they didn't initially believe Jiang Tian's reasoning,

that didn't stop them from realizing, from an outsider's perspective, that the flaws in this matter were too great.

What does sending ten people mean?

Did they mistake your apprentice for Jiang Tian?

Where did you get your confidence?

Actually, Gong Zhe also had his own difficulties.

Who the hell knew that the drug dealers here were so ruthless?

The moment the firefight began, good heavens, he couldn't tell if they were regular troops or a criminal gang.

You're telling him this group of incredibly skilled fighters, armed with rocket launchers, organized, commanded, and armed with firepower, are drug dealers?

Silence.

The scene was deathly silent.

Everyone watched this scene in silence.

How could the drug dealers have been found, only to escape again?

“Song Liguo, you take your men to Gaodian Town immediately. Xiao Jiang, you come with us too,” Gao Xiaolong stood up and raised his hand.

“Yes, sir!!!”

Song Liguo immediately stood up.

After holding back all night, it was finally his turn.

Jiang Tian shrugged, said nothing, and followed Song Liguo away.

Many leaders were present.

However, after the Yunhai Narcotics Control Team left, the outer perimeter became much smaller. The reason the Narcotics Control Team was present was to prepare for any future troubles, to be ready to provide support at any time, and to react quickly should drug dealers or criminal gangs try to cause trouble.

Liang Ru watched as the leaders puffed on their cigarettes, waiting for intelligence from the front lines. She lowered her head and followed them out of the meeting room.

To be honest, this was her first time at the Municipal Public Security Bureau, and she wasn't very familiar with the area.

She didn't know anyone there either.

But when she saw a pretty, voluptuous woman diligently searching for information on a computer in the office area, a smile appeared on Liang Ru's face.

Jiang Tian's confirmation of the drug dealers' location had truly shocked her. On one hand, it told her that such things couldn't be faked, but on the other hand, she still couldn't believe it.

He knew from so far away.

Is he a god?

Isn't Jiang Tian just an ordinary trainee police officer?

Why did Elder Mu and Gao Xiaolong beat and scold her because of him?

Taking advantage of the empty meeting room, Liang Ru came out and spotted the strikingly beautiful and well-proportioned Yu Hui among the many office workers working overtime.

"Big sister, is something the matter?" Yu Hui hadn't witnessed the scene in the meeting room; she had been working tirelessly outside, investigating the arrested drug dealers and entering information.

"Um, I wanted to inquire about Jiang Tian from your police station," Liang Ru said with a smile.

"What do you want?" Yu Hui's face immediately turned wary.

Knowing the precedent set by Blood Hand, drug dealers who learned of Jiang Tian's information almost always resorted to any means of retaliation.

"Don't worry, my husband was taken hostage by drug dealers, but all the previous intelligence missions failed. This time, Officer Jiang went with us, right? The leaders there said he's very capable. Can you tell me about him?" Liang Ru explained.

This put Yu Hui at ease. Actually, it wasn't top-secret information. Jiang Tian's situation had already been largely leaked; anyone with a bit of influence knew the general picture. Besides, could a family member who could enter the meeting room be an ordinary person?

This woman seemed to be Old Master Mu's daughter-in-law.

Yu Hui thought to herself and explained, "Sister, you can rest assured with Officer Jiang. He's never failed in arresting drug dealers."

"He's just a trainee officer, I heard he's only been training for a month. Is he really that capable?" Liang Ru asked with skepticism.

Yu Hui's eyes instantly filled with proud affirmation as she nodded, "Don't underestimate Officer Jiang just because he's only been an intern for a month. He's already won multiple first-class merits, is a legend in the police force, and has already been promoted twice ahead of schedule. He's already a first-class police superintendent, and it won't be long before he's promoted again to police inspector."

Buzz!!!

Liang Ru's mind exploded instantly.

Before this, Liang Ru had specifically researched the police rank promotion system because of Xiao Bo.

After a year of internship, it's not guaranteed you'll be a full-fledged officer. If your performance isn't good, you might be transferred to another department.

And after a year, there's a three-year promotion system.

That is, after becoming a full-fledged officer, it takes three years to reach second-class, and six years to reach first-class.

Jiang Tian accomplished in one month what others couldn't accomplish in seven years—no, it might soon take ten years.

Within the system, one step ahead leads to endless advancement.

"This... how is this possible? He became a first-class police superintendent just one month after graduation! Wait, you just said he has multiple first-class merit awards, are you sure it's a first-class merit award?" Liang Ru asked, her eyes wide.

Even if Liang Ru was ignorant, she knew what a first-class merit award meant, especially in peacetime.

Yu Hui smiled proudly, "Of course, at least five or six first-class merit awards. As for second-class and third-class merit awards, they're not even worth mentioning."

Yu Hui even had something else on her mind.

Who is Captain Jiang? He's a god! He's even applying for a special-class merit award.

Pfft!!!

Liang Ru's eyes widened, and she burst out laughing.

At this moment, Liang Ru was completely dumbfounded.

When Liang Ru came to her senses, she muttered to herself, her face blank, "Why didn't you say so sooner if you were so amazing?!"

Now.

She finally understood why Elder Mu and Gao Xiaolong were so protective of Jiang Tian.

He's only been an intern for a month, yet he already has several first-class merit awards.

How many merits has he accumulated? How many drug dealers must he have arrested?

No wonder that young girl wanted Jiang Tian to go up there.

But why wasn't this said earlier? If it had been said earlier, would she have disagreed?

Family members know their own situation.

Old Master Mu is getting old, and there's no successor in the second generation.

It's fair to say they're facing a generational gap.

And the third generation only has Mu Ya.

It's fair to say there's no one to carry on the family line.

A top-tier son-in-law, and a son-in-law with a bright future.

If she got into their house, it would be a powerful combination.

"What's wrong, sister?" Yu Hui reminded her.

"Nothing, nothing. It's just that I didn't expect Officer Jiang to be so capable. Now I'm relieved." Although Liang Ru was answering, she was already thinking about how to apologize to Jiang Tian.

Her attitude had completely changed.

But just as Liang Ru was thinking about this,

a loud uproar erupted in the conference room.

"Holy crap, they found them again?"

"Little Jiang, this Little Jiang!"

"What kind of god is this?"

Found them?

What found them?

Those escaped drug dealers have been found again?

Liang Ru was a little confused. She turned back to the conference room, but then she heard Gao Xiaolong's excited voice: "Request a commendation! Request a commendation immediately! Call Minister Tan right now! When will Little Jiang's special merit award come through?"

Buzz!!!

What did this guy say?

Is Chief Gao talking about a special merit award?

Liang Ru stared blankly at the uproar in the conference room.

She seriously doubted whether she had misheard. What did a Special Merit Citation even mean?

"I only questioned him a little. With his abilities, he shouldn't take it to heart," Liang Ru reassured herself.



Chapter 168 Even the police dogs couldn't smell it, you're even more awesome than a police dog.


"It's over."

Gong Zhe slumped to the floor, his lips dry.

Before him stood Zeng Fan, his face contorted with resentment, staring blankly at Gong Zhe. "Master, I..."

"You were betrayed by your informant on your mission abroad, and then kidnapped, that's one thing. But why, this group of drug dealers, weren't caught? Why did you let them escape?" Gong Zhe said, exasperated.

Zeng Fan opened his mouth.

Could he say that he didn't trust Jiang Tian at all? Someone who had only ever demonstrated martial prowess, did he even know anything about deduction?

If you gave a general location, he could at least think about it a little more carefully, at least have some logical reasoning, he could at least take it seriously.

But there was absolutely no normal logical reasoning. He just blurted out that it was at the brick factory. If you had at least come to Gaodian Town and searched around, he could at least believe it. How could he believe it in this situation?

This time, he just took his men there, thinking it would be a wasted trip. He didn't take it seriously at all, just took his men for a stroll.

The team ran straight into the drug dealers upon entering.

In an instant, the drug dealers routed the group.

"If I had been more careful, if I had simply followed normal procedures and observed the situation, this wouldn't have happened," Zeng Fan thought, wishing he could slap himself.

"I told you to go and observe, and this is how you observed? You just led your men in! I told you to observe, not to lead men to their deaths and alert the enemy!" Gong Zhe pounded the table, then slumped weakly into a chair.

Rubbing his temples, he knew it was pointless to say anything more.

Because they had already run away.

But...

They had even lost track of them.

Forget about earning merit; after this mission, Gong Zhe even felt he was going to be punished because of Zeng Fan.

Unwittingly, they had been caught off guard.

But then the question arose: if these drug dealers were so ruthless, what about Jiang Tian, ​​who had indiscriminately slaughtered them?

"Hmm?"

Thinking this, Gong Zhe abruptly stood up.

He used to think the drug dealers were just incompetent, letting Jiang Tian get there first; it was just a matter of good opportunity. He figured Zeng Fan would have done the same.

The first time overseas, he thought it was an accident.

The second mission, and such a huge blunder.

How did he manage to take down over a hundred drug dealers all by himself?

Once might be an accident, but twice is no accident.

Gong Zhe suddenly felt a surge of intense curiosity; at this moment, he wanted to see Jiang Tian.

This legendary trainee police officer from Yunhai.

How did he manage to deal with drug dealers of this caliber?

"Director Xu just said that Officer Jiang and the Yunhai branch have arrived. I'll see what's so special about Officer Jiang." Gong Zhe still didn't want to believe it.

Drug dealers of this caliber, one person could kill hundreds.

With that kind of fighting ability...

Are you sure it's not just nonsense?

This fighting strength was almost on par with a regular army; they could hold their own on the battlefield. They must have received formal training.

At this moment, Gong Zhe truly felt a strong curiosity about Jiang Tian.

"Maybe he was just guessing before. What's the use if you can't even find the drug dealers?" Zeng Fan said with a grim expression.

Because Gao Yangyang was now ignoring him completely because of Jiang Tian.

This fueled Zeng Fan's anger. Everything had been progressing well in their relationship, but in his eyes, it was all because Jiang Tian had stolen him away.

What?!

You say there was nothing between them to begin with? But, in Zeng Fan's eyes, Gao Yangyang, whom he had his eye on, belonged to him.

This made Zeng Fan grit his teeth in hatred towards Jiang Tian.

Admitting that he was inferior to Jiang Tian was harder to accept than death.

"Zeng Fan, if this is your attitude, I doubt whether you're fit to continue working on the front lines," Gong Zhe said angrily.

"Master, I... I know I was wrong," Zeng Fan quickly bowed his head and admitted his mistake.

Gong Zhe nodded slightly and said, "But in the end, I was careless. I was also wrong. It's good that we see how capable this trainee police officer really is, whether he's as good as the rumors say."

While Gong Zhe was lamenting in Gaodian Town,

Meanwhile, in the pitch-black night,

Jiang Tian sat in the back seat.

Zhao Wei was naturally driving the beat-up Santana, a small official car, navigating the bumpy mountain roads.

Li Zijin sat with Jiang Tian in the back, glancing at him every now and then.

Her beautiful eyes burned with a strong sense of gossip, but unfortunately, Jiang Tian was in a bad mood, one hand resting on the car window, staring at the darkness outside.

"Gaodian Town is surrounded by mountains, the villages are all nestled among them, it's too hard to find," Zhao Wei said, his face showing exhaustion from driving.

He finally understood why so many people had such a hard time finding drug dealers in Gaodian Town.

It seems that in these damn mountains, if you're not a local, you might easily get lost.

“Nonsense, otherwise the previous anti-drug operations would have been slow, almost nonexistent, simply because the geographical environment is too complex, and the border is unstable. Many smugglers hidden in the mountains are difficult to spot,” Li Zijin, who had been in the police force for two years, couldn't help but roll her eyes.

Zhao Wei smiled awkwardly and followed the convoy ahead.

The entire anti-drug squad was mobilized, and Zhao Wei, running fast, kept up with Song Liguo.

“Stop the car.”

Just then, Jiang Tian suddenly spoke up.

“Captain, what’s up?” Zhao Wei was startled and slammed on the brakes.

Jiang Tian didn't answer, mainly because he was watching the red dot flashing across the radar screen. A thought struck him.

Actually, after the second escape, Jiang Tian also needed to go to the scene to investigate and deduce based on the clues.

But now, Jiang Tian wasn't sure where the drug dealers had gone.

As for whether the red dot now was that group of people or scattered drug dealers, it didn't matter to Jiang Tian; after all, he was killing two birds with one stone.

“Too bad it’s only a kilometer.” Jiang Tian glanced at the fleeting red dot on the radar, then silently leaped into the jungle.

“What’s going on?”

Song Liguo, noticing the vehicles behind them had stopped, got out to investigate.

Zhao Wei pointed in the direction Jiang Tian had disappeared and explained.

“Follow him, quickly.” Song Liguo made a decisive decision.

What a joke.

Song Liguo could praise Jiang Tian’s ability to find drug dealers without reservation; it was like a dog’s nose.

He had definitely spotted the drug dealers.

Sure enough.

After Song Liguo followed, Jiang Tian lay prone in the bushes, watching a small cave in the distance. Two drug dealers stood guard at the cave entrance.

The location was very well concealed.

To be honest, it was only because Jiang Tian was fast that he ran in the direction of the red dot for about four or five hundred meters before the scanning radar showed a dense array of red dots.



Chapter 169 Among the hostages, there was actually her?


"These bastards actually hid here! Even locals might not have found them." Song Liguo, after catching up, cautiously approached Jiang Tian and gasped.

The route was incredibly complicated. They traversed the jungle, reached a pathless area in the mountains, climbed a cliff about four or five meters high, and only found him after walking quite a distance.

After Song Liguo reported to the city police immediately, he looked at Jiang Tian with a shocked expression.

He kept inwardly gasping.

"Your nose is even sharper than a dog's! Even the police dogs couldn't find him, but you spotted him!" Song Liguo thought excitedly.

Because there was a presence of guards, it was clearly not a small, stray group of drug dealers; it might even be the same group that had taken hostages.

Thinking this, Song Liguo's eyes suddenly lit up, and his breathing quickened.

Unlike Jiang Tian, ​​who had received countless first-class merit awards, Song Liguo, in all his years as a police officer, had never even seen one.

This time, he definitely wouldn't get an individual first-class merit award, but a collective first-class merit award is still a first-class merit award, isn't it?

"Uncle Song, are you swearing at me?" Jiang Tian was exasperated.

"I'm praising you, Xiao Jiang, don't take it to heart. Come to my house for dinner tomorrow. Your sister Wan'er is back today and insists on seeing you." Song Liguo extended a warm invitation.

"Sister Wan'er is back?" Jiang Tian's eyes darkened.

"That's right." Song Liguo smiled but didn't continue, instead carefully watching the movements inside.

Jiang Tian frowned deeply. Yes, Sister Wan'er, Song Qiuwan, was about two or three years older than Jiang Tian.

In his previous life, she was driven to her death by a drug-addicted suitor abroad.

This was absolutely a deep pain in Jiang Tian's heart. They had practically grown up together, and because Sister Wan'er was two or three years older than him, she had always cared for him like an older sister.

She even saved almost all her pocket money for him.

In his past life, Jiang Tian felt that Wan'er's strict control hindered his pursuit of Wang Yifei. This led to a major argument with Wan'er, and in a fit of anger, she left the country, severing all contact.

By the time Jiang Tian realized this, Wan'er had already met with an accident abroad. This was a deep-seated regret in Jiang Tian's past life.

Perhaps Song Liguo had told him about Wan'er's arrival in his past life, or perhaps not, but at this time, Jiang Tian was deeply immersed in the grief of losing his loved ones and his obsessive devotion to Wang Yifei, and probably hadn't even noticed.

Thinking of this, Jiang Tian's eyes were filled with melancholy; the regret of his past life...

"I will definitely go," Jiang Tian muttered to himself.

"First squad, occupy advantageous terrain and ambush all key routes. Second squad, guard the cave entrance. The rest of you, prepare to move out," Song Liguo ordered.

"Aren't we waiting for the Municipal Bureau?" Captain Guo asked nervously.

Such a big mission, such an important mission, he felt a little uneasy.

After all, who knew how many drug dealers were inside.

"Wait here. The hostages could be in danger at any moment. Find a few people to figure out how to deal with the ones guarding the entrance." Song Liguo's gaze was steady as he looked at the two drug dealers guarding the entrance.

As soon as Song Liguo finished speaking,

Captain Guo and the people around him instantly turned their attention to Jiang Tian.

After all, while others might not know Jiang Tian's exploits, his fellow members of the same team knew them perfectly well!

Not long ago, Jiang Tian single-handedly took down the entire Blood Hand criminal syndicate, single-handedly dismantled the Tucha overseas criminal syndicate, and single-handedly dismantled the Blood Hand criminal syndicate again.

He also single-handedly dismantled Mr. Yun's criminal syndicate.

Thinking about it now, they shuddered. Jiang Tian seemed almost too abnormal!

But they had never witnessed it with their own eyes.

"Captain Jiang, go up and give it a try," Zhao Wei said excitedly.

We were about to see Brother Jiang personally take action?

"We can't expose ourselves," Song Liguo said, also somewhat expectant.

"I'll give it a try."

Looking at the curious and expectant eyes, Jiang Tian nodded indifferently.

Two drug dealers were really no challenge for Jiang Tian.

Whoosh!!!

The next moment.

In the darkness, Jiang Tian, ​​who had just been speaking, vanished instantly.

In the night, it was as if he had left no trace.

Zhao Wei only felt a slight breeze pass by.

When he looked up,

he saw Jiang Tian standing at the entrance of the cave, waving at them.

The two drug dealers guarding the cave had already collapsed to the ground with soft thuds.

"Hiss!!!"

A collective gasp rang out.

Judging from the merits Jiang Tian has made and the aftermath has been cleaned up, he hasn't felt anything yet.

But it wasn't until they witnessed Jiang Tian make his move that they realized...

How terrifying!

"Holy crap."

Song Liguo rubbed his eyes and approached the two drug dealers, his face completely dumbfounded. "That's it?"

Song Liguo's scalp tingled. These two fully armed drug dealers were dealt with so easily?

"Uncle Song, don't make a fuss," Jiang Tian said calmly.

"You're putting on airs, kid." Song Liguo's face darkened.

But after saying that, Song Liguo cautiously approached the cave entrance.

"Don't look, they're all inside, no one's there." Jiang Tian strolled in leisurely.

"Holy crap, you've got a nose like a dog's." Song Liguo retorted.

Inside, sure enough, the cave entrance wasn't large; you had to bend over to enter.

But once inside, it was a completely different world.

After walking about ten steps, it was already wide enough for several people to walk side by side.

Following Jiang Tian, ​​the thirty-odd anti-drug police officers from Yunhai, who were supporting the effort, entered with tense and cautious expressions.

Facing drug dealers, especially those armed with weapons, one can never be too cautious.

Jiang Tian swaggered around, but they dared not!

“They’re inside.”

Jiang Tian raised his hand, now at a corner of the cave, where a fire had been lit, providing ample light.

Jiang Tian peeked inside.

What he saw:

About ten people, men and women, were bound in the very center of the cave.

Jiang Tian recognized Mu’s father.

He had seen him in his previous life, recognizing him instantly among the kidnapped.

“Let me go! Let me go! What are you doing, you beasts!”

A cry for help rang out.

Jiang Tian looked in the direction of the sound.

Sure enough, deep within the cave, two drug dealers were dragging a stylishly dressed woman into a passageway deeper into the cave.

Although visibility was poor, Jiang Tian's enhanced superhuman abilities meant his eyesight was more than three times that of an average person.

Long hair obscured her face, but during the struggle, her fair complexion and delicate features were revealed.

"It's her."

Jiang Tian was utterly astonished.



Chapter 170 A Seemingly Old Friend Returns: A Completely Different First Encounter


Jiang Tian was familiar with this woman because in his past life, when he was framed by Wang Yifei, Zhao Qiuni came to find him, accompanied by another woman—this was her.

On the other hand, this woman was even more famous than Zhao Qiuni.

She was Zhao Qiuni's mentor in the investment world, and the capital group she founded became, without a doubt, the most famous investment company in later generations.

An absolutely aloof and cold female CEO.

Furthermore, Jiang Tian had read an analysis in his later life suggesting that this woman had a mysterious background.

What exactly that background was, Jiang Tian didn't actually know.

"Was Mu's father discussing business with this woman? But isn't this woman from an investment company? How did she end up here, doing business with Mu's father? No, she came with Zhao Qiuni in his past life, so the two might have been traveling together."

"And in his past life, this case never even existed. The Yun family's deal with Blood Hand wasn't exposed, so there was no hostage situation. Ultimately, it's all due to the butterfly effect."

Thinking this, Jiang Tian suddenly understood.

“I’ve never seen such a beautiful woman. I wonder if we could have her after you’ve had your fill, brother.”

“Yeah, hahaha, she’s obviously a rich woman. This slut has a stubborn streak; she’d rather die than submit.”

Two drug dealers dragged the woman towards the back.

The dozen or so hostages were all silent, not daring to speak or utter a word.

This was only natural.

Jiang Tian showed no emotion, because remaining silent was the right thing to do. Speaking would only increase the risk of death and accomplish nothing.

Mr. Mu’s father also remained silent, which slightly surprised Jiang Tian.
