"You just said you were going to kill me?" Jiang Tian asked, pointing his gun at Boss Deng with a puzzled look.

Drip, drip!!!

In the silent environment, blood dripped from Boss Deng's limp arm onto the ground, making a crisp, weak, yet extremely jarring sound.

"You..." Boss Deng's bloodshot eyes gradually cleared.

*Slap!*

Jiang Tian slapped him hard across the face: "I'm asking you, you just said you were going to kill me?"



Chapter 182 I'm sorry, I'm a police officer


*Whoosh!*

It exploded.

It completely exploded.

Everyone's pupils contracted.

What did this mean?

It was like a group of ordinary classmates' reunion, and then a powerful figure in society showed up, with assets worth tens or hundreds of millions, countless henchmen, and a menacing appearance. At your recent graduation reunion, an ordinary classmate suddenly shot this big boss and then slapped him repeatedly.

What did this mean for Jiang Tian's classmates?

Were they insane?

Especially people like Zhao Kai, who even thought they were going crazy, hallucinating. Otherwise, how could they have seen their boss, Zhao, being shot and slapped repeatedly by Jiang Tian, ​​this good-for-nothing?

Shouldn't it be the other way around?

"Brother, that was too ruthless. Using a gun would attract the cops and kill you," Boss Deng said to Jiang Tian, ​​his emotions calming down.

"Dad," Young Master Deng lay on the ground, his eyes bloodshot.

Jiang Tian pointed his gun at Boss Deng, approached Young Master Deng, and with a sickening stomp, stepped on the mangled face.

Young Master Deng trembled and struggled violently.

He'd gone mad.

Completely mad.

Boss Deng's heart was broken.

"Kill him."

Boss Deng roared and charged forward, grabbing Jiang Tian's arm where he was holding the gun.

Several of his henchmen swarmed forward in an instant.

The seven or eight henchmen were all very burly, looking like seasoned veterans of the underworld.

Qin Shiyu tensed up at the sight, hurriedly pulling out her phone, ready to call the police at any moment.

But just then...

Bang! Bang! Bang!

A series of gunshots rang out.

Accompanied by a series of thuds,

Deng's henchmen fell to the ground one after another, each with a bloody hole between their eyebrows.

In an instant, the five fastest-rushing henchmen lay lifeless on the ground.

The remaining two stood frozen in place.

Deng, who was holding Jiang Tian's arm, was dumbfounded.

Dead.

Seven henchmen came, five instantly killed.

Such accurate marksmanship?

Such terrifying ruthlessness?

Deng was dumbfounded.

Everyone was stunned.

Deathly silence.

Jiang Tian, ​​with just a slight exertion of his arm, pinned Deng to the ground and kicked him squarely in the face, sending him flying two meters.

Thump, thump, thump!!!

Jiang Tian walked step by step, the ground making crisp sounds.

But at this moment, everyone stared at Jiang Tian in terror. As Jiang Tian approached, Li Zhi, sitting on the ground, shrank back, his eyes wide with fear.

Jiang Tian glanced at him, uninterested, and then turned his gaze to Zhao Kai, whose back was pressed tightly against the wall.

“You just said you’ve caused a huge disaster, that you’re about to die, and your family will suffer too. So, if you kill Boss Deng, will you be safe?” Jiang Tian’s lips curled into a smile as he suddenly patted Zhao Kai’s face, asking each word slowly and deliberately.

Gulp!

Zhao Kai swallowed hard.

“Just kidding, hahaha.” Jiang Tian laughed.

“You kill them.” Zhao Kai, now a little bolder, suddenly shouted.

“Yeah, what, surprised? Just a few criminals.” Jiang Tian shrugged, his face nonchalant.

Buzz!

Looking at Jiang Tian’s nonchalant expression, everyone went numb, their scalps tingling with fear.

What allowed him to chat so expressionlessly after killing several people?

Actually, it didn't matter to Jiang Tian. These few people didn't even cause him the slightest psychological fluctuation.

Because he had the lives of hundreds of drug dealers on his hands. He'd killed hundreds of drug dealers, so what was a few more?

"You killed them, you have to pay with your life! You have to pay with your life!" Li Zhi shouted, pointing at Jiang Tian.

But unfortunately.

Jiang Tian had no interest in answering Li Zhi. Instead, he walked unhurriedly to the two henchmen still standing there.

That's right.

He kept these two men around because he was running out of bullets, and also because they were carrying briefcases.

"Give them to me," Jiang Tian said with a slight smile.

Give them to me?

The two hesitated nervously.

Bang!!!

The next moment, Jiang Tian raised his gun and fired.

Accompanied by two soft thuds.

The two henchmen collapsed to the ground, blood slowly trickling from their foreheads.

Two suitcases also fell to the ground.

"If you won't give them to me, I'll take them myself," Jiang Tian said with slight regret, then bent down and slowly opened one of the suitcases.

What he saw was...

It was filled to the brim with snow-white powder.

Twenty kilograms?

More than that.

Don't be fooled by Jiang Tian's usual record of meritorious service and large seizures.

Normally, seizing twenty or thirty kilograms of drugs would be enough to earn a great deal of credit.

But a suitcase weighing twenty kilograms, two of them would be forty or fifty kilograms, nearly a hundred pounds of drugs!

More than fifty grams is enough for a death sentence; this is over a hundred pounds—what does that even mean?

"Jiang Tian, ​​what are you doing? Are you crazy?! That's Boss Deng's stuff! You killed so many people! Are you insane?!"

Just then, Zhao Kai jumped out, shouting at Jiang Tian in alarm.

As he spoke, he tried to run out, frantically attempting to close the suitcase.

"You piece of trash, why did you kill so many people? Are you crazy? You might have survived a moment ago, but now you're just waiting to die!"

Actually, most of the students were just there to watch the show.

After all, subconsciously, people don't want to get involved unless it happens to them.

As for right and wrong, they had no say; they only knew that someone had killed someone, and that was enough.

Jiang Tian looked at Zhao Kai meaningfully.

"You... why are you looking at me like that?" Zhao Kai's forehead was sweating.

The next moment.

Click!!!

Zhao Kai looked at the handcuffs on his wrists.

Buzz!!!

Handcuffs.

These damn handcuffs?

Zhao Kai's mouth opened slightly.

Dead silence.

"Jiang Tian, ​​what are you doing? Where did you buy these handcuffs? They're pretty good quality." Zhao Kai asked, reacting to the situation.

But Jiang Tian didn't answer.

Instead, he glanced around.

He didn't care about these classmates; after all, he probably wouldn't have the chance to see them again.

However,

he was very interested in the Deng family.

These were really good people!

They'd come all this way to deliver credit.

"We didn't know who you were, but we admit defeat. Don't worry, from now on, we'll keep to ourselves." Boss Deng didn't seem to have grasped the reality of the situation.

"Dad." Deng Shao's eyes widened.

So what?

So many people died!

You're just going to let it go like that?

"Boss Zhao?" Zhao Kai was equally bewildered.

He just said it was over, so when did Jiang Tian become a ruthless thug? This fearless man actually scared Boss Deng?

Hiss!!!

But before Zhao Kai could even gasp,

the next moment.

Jiang Tian slowly reached into his pocket.

A moment later, Jiang Tian pulled out a black leather badge with a silver badge that shone brightly and dazzlingly under the light, leaving everyone stunned.

"Let me introduce myself, Jiang Tian, ​​squad leader of the Yunhai Narcotics Control Detachment, First-Class Superintendent. You can choose to remain silent, because every word you utter will become evidence against you drug dealers in court."

To be honest, Jiang Tian was a little stunned; the surprise came so suddenly.

I was just attending a class reunion, and drug dealers dared to brazenly come over with heroin and start a fight to teach him a lesson?

Is this some kind of favor they're doing him?

Jiang Tian tilted his head, a grin spreading across his face. "So, sorry, I'm a police officer!"



Chapter 183 I am a First-Class Superintendent, what are you, a squad leader?


In a deathly silence.

Jiang Tian took out his police ID, lifted the badge to reveal the ID photo, the seal, and a large red character:

Serve the People!

“You’re a policeman?” Boss Deng exclaimed, his eyes wide.

“He’s a policeman?”

Boss Deng suddenly turned to look at Zhao Kai.

Cold sweat beaded on Zhao Kai’s forehead. Only now did he seem to vaguely recall that when Jiang Tian enrolled, someone had mentioned that Jiang Tian had been accepted into the police academy.

But at the class reunion, Zhao Kai hadn’t thought about it at all.

After all, who pays attention to where someone is when they’re not on good terms?

But now…

Thump thump thump!!

Just then.

A series of hurried, chaotic footsteps sounded.

Amidst the rustling sound,

Song Liguo, one hand on his police cap, wearing a white shirt, rushed in first.

"Where are the drug dealers? Where are they?"

Song Liguo shouted impatiently.

Good heavens.

This is absolutely terrible.

Song Liguo's head was about to explode.

Actually, Jiang Tian had already called Song Liguo immediately after finding the small bag of heroin on Deng Shao.

Jiang Tian even had a premonition that Deng Shao was just the beginning, and Boss Deng wasn't the main focus. Digging deeper into this lead could uncover a massive case, no less terrifying than the previous ones.

Having figured this out, Jiang Tian first crippled Deng Shao, and then subdued Boss Deng when he arrived.

Without giving Boss Deng any chance to react, Song Liguo rushed over.

He ran countless traffic lights, arriving at the Emperor KTV from the city police station at top speed.

Upon entering, Song Liguo saw the corpse on the ground, but his expression remained unchanged; he was already quite used to it.

Every time Jiang Tian took action, Song Liguo would only find it strange if he didn't kill several drug dealers.

“Here he is.” Jiang Tian pointed his gun at Boss Deng.

What happened next surprised even Jiang Tian.

“Boss Deng?” Song Liguo frowned.

“You know him?” Jiang Tian looked at Song Liguo with a dangerous glint in his eyes.

Song Liguo trembled under Jiang Tian's gaze and quickly said, “Our police force often comes to karaoke, so we know each other. This Boss Deng was very enthusiastic and offered me a free meal, but I refused. Later, he was in jail for fighting.”

“That’s good, Uncle Song. We can’t make mistakes!” Jiang Tian said quietly.

“That’s right, that’s right.” Song Liguo wiped away cold sweat.

Hiss!!!

This scene stunned everyone in the private room.

Song Liguo, putting everything else aside, was wearing a white shirt!

This guy, clearly a high-ranking leader, looked like a greenhorn in front of Jiang Tian.

How could this not be shocking?

Of course, the most shocking thing was that Jiang Tian was their classmate, and should have only just started his internship!

Some of them were even still living at school, not yet having found internships.

And yet Jiang Tian actually…

Right…

“You, a First-Class Superintendent?” Wang Cuifang stared wide-eyed at Jiang Tian.

“Yes.” Jiang Tian nodded matter-of-factly.

Wang Cuifang almost choked.

No wonder.

No wonder they dared to do that!

"What does 'First-Class Superintendent' mean?" Li Zhi asked, his face tingling with fear as he stood before Wang Cuifang.

Such a large-scale arrest scene was something these greenhorns had never seen before.

Wang Cuifang didn't hide anything, saying directly, "My cousin works at the police station. He often tells me that he's been working for five years and is still only a Third-Class Superintendent. He says there's a promotion every three years. If you missed the last promotion, you have to wait another three years. A First-Class Superintendent requires at least ten years of service, unless you make a significant contribution."

Yes.

Jiang Tian had become a First-Class Superintendent in such a short time. What did that mean?

What did it represent?

Wang Cuifang's mind was buzzing. Even within the official system, this rank was easy to understand.

The three-year promotion system didn't mean you could always get promoted smoothly. If you missed it this time, you'd have to wait another three years for the next batch. And even then, there was no guarantee you'd be promoted in the next three years!

Li Zhi's eyes widened.

His eyes were bloodshot.

Back in school, Li Zhi was the discipline monitor, a class leader. Because Jiang Tian initially had poor discipline, Li Zhi often used his authority to target Jiang Tian.

Even during his internship, Li Zhi was preparing for the civil service exam; he loved the feeling of being an official.

But now...

The student who was at the bottom of the class back then, whom he looked down upon and even targeted, was now ahead of him—no, he was ten years ahead!

What did it mean to be ten years ahead in physical education?

Wouldn't that mean that if his achievements were significant, he'd be at least a provincial-level official in ten years?

A provincial-level official from the junior high school—that was explosive!

How could he be so lucky?

How could he be so lucky?

Li Zhi's face turned green.

But unfortunately, Jiang Tian didn't notice Li Zhi's expression at all.

Because at that moment, Song Liguo had already arrived in front of the two suitcases, squatted down, and looked at the suitcases on the ground.

Looking at the two boxes overflowing with heroin,

"What brings you here today?" Song Liguo asked Jiang Tian.

"A class reunion, and some thug showed up. After a fight, this thug was wearing heroin, and then he called his family—even more shockingly, they were probably halfway through a deal and rushed over, right?" Jiang Tian stroked his chin.

"Just that simple?" Song Liguo found it hard to believe.

Investigating drug dealers used to be incredibly difficult, fraught with obstacles, even finding clues was extremely challenging.

They often had no leads at all, relying on small favors from drug cartels to occasionally pull off a couple of minor cases to appease them.

And now…

A class reunion, and you say you've already accomplished something?

After Song Liguo took over, Jiang Tian didn't ask any further questions.

Leaving the KTV, Jiang Tian turned to look at the brightly lit entrance, a smile playing on his lips.

It was a smile that seemed to say, "It's like something that could vanish in the blink of an eye."

"So cool!"

At least that's what Fang Jie thought.

"Don't I have to go to the police station?" Fang Jie asked somewhat nervously, following behind Jiang Tian.

Jiang Tian also looked helpless.

After the fight just now, Fang Jie seemed incredibly timid and hesitant.

He was completely unlike his natural self from before.

Once the difference in their social status becomes apparent, the relationship between them can break down imperceptibly.

"No need, those people are just going over to give a statement," Jiang Tian replied.

Meanwhile, Song Liguo arrived with his men. Several ambulances were parked outside, and stretchers were being carried out one by one, but it was clear that these people were beyond saving.

A large crowd had gathered outside the KTV.



Chapter 184 Qin Shiyu's Test? What are you going to do?


"Alright, don't overthink it. Our relationship is still the same, it won't affect anything," Jiang Tian said with a smile, patting Fang Jie on the shoulder.

Before his transmigration in his previous life, Fang Jie was Jiang Tian's only brother and friend.

"How did you get here? Want me to give you a ride?" Fang Jie asked, a slight sigh of relief on his face.

"You bought a car?" Jiang Tian asked, surprised.

At this, Fang Jie's face instantly lit up with excitement. "That's right, a Mazda, what do you think?"

As he spoke, Fang Jie touched his car keys, and two lights instantly illuminated the parking lot.

A bright red Mazda appeared in everyone's sight.

"Damn, you've struck it rich, kid?" Jiang Tian exclaimed in astonishment.

Because in his previous life, he hadn't heard that this guy had bought a car.

"Just picked it up today, what do you think?" Fang Jie patted the hood of the Mazda.

"Damn, I almost forgot."

Jiang Tian slapped his forehead. Back in his previous life, Wang Yifei's family had asked for a dowry of 150,000 yuan. Jiang Tian, ​​lacking the funds, borrowed 100,000 yuan from Fang Jie.

At that time, Fang Jie gave Jiang Tian 100,000 yuan without hesitation.

Although Jiang Tian returned the money to Fang Jie shortly afterward, he could never forget that gesture.

Given the opportunity, and if he had the means, he wouldn't mind helping him out.

"Awesome!" Jiang Tian gave a thumbs up.

"Let's go, I'll take you home," Fang Jie grinned at Jiang Tian.

"No need, we drove here."

Just then.

Qin Shiyu, standing next to Jiang Tian, ​​suddenly spoke up.

"You drove too?" Fang Jie was taken aback.

You see, it wasn't long before the year 2000. Car ownership was very low. Ordinary families had little savings and struggled to make ends meet, let alone buy a car.

According to Jiang Tian, ​​Fang Jie's father had previously been a businessman who had made a fortune.

However, a sudden misfortune struck, shattering his family's fortunes.

Fang Jie, who had grown from a relatively well-off family, returned to poverty. Lili ran away, leaving him to raise another man's child while he worked to pay off debts.

In short, life was incredibly tough; in their past life, the two were truly brothers in misfortune.

In this life, since Jiang Tian had changed his own destiny, he could certainly change his brother's fate too!

"You bought a car?" Fang Jie continued to ask.

"No, it's your sister-in-law's," Jiang Tian smiled slightly.

Ding-dong!!!

A ding-dong sound rang out.

Jiang Tian held a gleaming car key in his hand and pressed a button in the distance.

Then, a pink Rolls-Royce was displayed before everyone.

Pfft!!!

Fang Jie's eyes widened.

The greatest pain in life is when your best friend succeeds.

The most painful thing in life is when your best friend finds a rich and beautiful woman and reaches the pinnacle of life.

And what is the most painful thing of all?

When your best friend's career reaches its peak, and in love, he marries a rich and beautiful woman.

"A Rolls-Royce?" Fang Jie looked at Jiang Tian, ​​bewildered.

Jiang Tian casually got into the driver's seat, and Qin Shiyu obediently followed him into the car.

At that moment, a sentence inexplicably popped into Fang Jie's mind.

Everyone else drives BMWs, Mercedes, and Rolls-Royces, but you drive a Mazda. No wonder you get stuck in traffic.

*Gulp!*

Lili watched the departing figure with envy, swallowing hard.

"Driving a Rolls-Royce, with a girlfriend as beautiful as an angel, this life..." Fang Jie was bewildered.

For a moment, he had a distorted view of life.

Meanwhile, Jiang Tian drove straight home.

In fact, Jiang Tian hadn't been home all day.

While in the car, Qin Shiyu added Jiang Tian's contact information.

"I'm home," Jiang Tian said as he got out of the car.

"Aren't you going to invite me up?" Qin Shiyu asked.

*Hiss!*

Jiang Tian looked at Qin Shiyu's aloof demeanor and felt a chill run down his spine at her question.

Unlike Gao Yangyang, who seemed quirky and mischievous, Jiang Tian handled her with ease, though not effortlessly.

But Qin Shiyu...

gave Jiang Tian the feeling that she was mysterious, completely out of his control, and he had no idea what she would do next.

She was utterly uncontrollable.

Most importantly, Jiang Tian knew his place; Qin Shiyu was a woman of even higher status than Zhao Qiuni.

Such a woman was simply beyond his current control.

“Never mind, I have family at home,” Jiang Tian refused.

“That was your own choice, you know. My flight is at 11 PM tonight, back to Donghai,” Qin Shiyu winked.

“Have a safe trip,” Jiang Tian waved his hand, his expression unchanged.

Qin Shiyu's expression instantly returned to its cool, aloof state. She glanced at him, and just before the car window closed, Jiang Tian vaguely saw the corners of her beautiful lips curve into a smile.

Jiang Tian swore that no man could resist the allure of that one glance.

"I'm a loyal man, what are you thinking?" Jiang Tian said, hands behind his back, swaying as he walked home.

To be honest.

At this moment.

Looking at the tranquil, quiet environment of the neighborhood.

The restless heart that had been pounding since Jiang Tian's transmigration gradually calmed down.

To be honest.

Since his rebirth, Jiang Tian's tragic memories of his past life were excruciating.

His past life was worse than death for him; to put it bluntly, he had a perfect start, but ended up living a hellish life.

After his rebirth, Jiang Tian was eager to make changes.

Now.

Jiang Tian looked at his phone; he saw Gao Xiaolong's, Xu Poye's, and even Shen Han's contact information.

Oh, right, and President Bai's too.

"Boss Bai's case should be nearing completion," Jiang Tian thought to himself.

These people were all firmly established connections for Jiang Tian.

He didn't need them now.

But when Jiang Tian needed them to further his efforts, they would be invaluable.

As for Boss Bai...

Jiang Tian was always filled with regret.

An outstanding, exceptionally handsome, and heroic figure in the fight against drugs.

Jiang Tian might have had some personal motives, but Boss Bai was absolutely dedicated to the cause of drug enforcement.

What other company's captain would personally be on the front lines?

And in the end, after being ambushed by drug dealers, not even a complete body was found.

However, just as Jiang Tian arrived at the entrance of the apartment building...

Two figures vaguely appeared at the entrance.

They seemed to be arguing about something.

The woman was also talking incessantly.

The sound carried far, even reaching Jiang Tian's ears.

"Sister Wan'er?" Jiang Tian walked to the entrance of the apartment building.

When he could see clearly in the dim light of the apartment building, Jiang Tian's brows furrowed deeply.

The man seemed to be saying something agitated, and Song Qiuwan seemed to be burning with anger, but she seemed to be suppressing it, forcing a smile to appease him.

A familiar face.

How long had it been since they last met?

A long time, it must have been.

The moment Jiang Tian saw Wan'er, he was completely stunned.

Her bright eyes, even with a hint of anger, were so gentle, like those of an older sister.

In his previous life, Jiang Tian and Sister Wan'er's relationship, or rather, before Wang Yifei appeared, could be described as inseparable.

But after Wang Yifei appeared, Jiang Tian gradually distanced himself from Sister Wan'er.

Thinking of this, Jiang Tian felt a slight pang of pain in his heart.

In his previous life, Sister Wan'er must have returned abroad this time.

She was ultimately killed by a crazed suitor abroad who took drugs.

So, was this the suitor?

Jiang Tian glanced at the scanning radar.

"Hmm?"



Chapter 185 Are you crazy, Sister Wan'er? You are actually...?


Jiang Tian was slightly startled.

Red.

It was him.

But what shocked Jiang Tian was...

He had almost never seen such a blood-red spot of light. Even Blood Hand and Tucha appeared so dim in comparison.

If a comparison had to be made, if Tucha had three thousand, this person had almost fifty thousand.

A tenfold difference, or even more than tenfold.

Jiang Tian had dealt with so many drug dealers, but he had never seen such an outrageous criminal.

"Can I still gain Sister Wan'er's forgiveness now? It's probably very difficult now. I've completely broken Sister Wan'er's heart." Jiang Tian felt a slight pang of pain in his heart as he thought of this.

In his previous life, because he was with Wang Yifei, Uncle Song probably secretly prevented the two from meeting, fearing problems might arise.

But in this life, Uncle Song even took the initiative to invite Jiang Tian to meet Song Qiuwan.

It was by chance that they met.

“Wan’er, I’ve followed you all the way from Meili to the rest of the country. Isn’t that enough to prove my feelings?” the man said to Song Qiuwan.

Song Qiuwan’s expression remained unchanged: “I’m sorry, we really don’t have a chance.”

“Wan’er, don’t worry, I won’t give up.” The man’s face was full of a warm, sunny smile.

He wore a white suit, exuding an air of impeccable manners.

If Jiang Tian didn’t possess the talent of a master detective and the ability to scan like a radar, standing before him, he would never have imagined how terrifying this man was.

And…

When the man smiled and waved goodbye,

Jiang Tian’s eyes narrowed slightly.

He vaguely sensed something unusual about the man’s gait.

For example, the way he walked, and the range of his arm swings. While one arm swung, the other’s movement was very subtle. This ensured one thing: the ability to draw and fire a gun at lightning speed.

His seemingly casual gait concealed a constant readiness to strike.

This was definitely a seasoned fighter, someone who had survived the flames of war.

Jiang Tian watched the man leave intently, not in a hurry to act. Besides, there was no opportunity now, because there was no evidence.

Even if Jiang Tian arrested him, he believed that with his current abilities, finding evidence against this man would be an impossible task.

Song Qiuwan, her face full of helplessness, sighed softly. She didn't see Jiang Tian standing not far away, turned around, and walked towards the apartment building.

But coincidentally...

Thump thump!!!

From the apartment building stairs came a series of hurried footsteps.

The next moment, a young girl rushed out of the building, not even seeing Song Qiuwan, but immediately spotting Jiang Tian standing ten or twenty meters away across the street.

"Brother, you're back?"

Jiang Xiaoyu exclaimed excitedly, her eyes wide.

That's right.

Since the incident with the bloody hand early this morning, Jiang Tian hadn't been heard from at all. Heaven knows how worried Jiang Xiaoyu was.

Jiang Tian's gaze shifted from Song Qiuwan's back to Jiang Xiaoyu.

His face was full of gentle smiles.

"It's not safe outside so late at night. Why did you come down?" Jiang Tian asked.

After what happened this morning, although nothing seemed amiss on the surface, Jiang Tian sensed something was amiss when he arrived. Someone was secretly watching him; it wasn't the drug dealers, but, according to the "King of Criminal Investigations," bodyguards sent from above.

Besides, although the Blood Hand gang had been eliminated, the drug trafficking network in Yunhai was vast and deeply entrenched, like a spider web. Even without the Blood Hand, there were bound to be other drug dealers.

Now was not the time to be careless.

"Mom asked me to go buy vinegar, but we're out. Brother, will you come with me?" Jiang Xiaoyu said, holding the vinegar bottle.

"Okay, I'll take you," Jiang Tian said indulgently.

After all, who wouldn't love their sister?

As for Song Qiuwan, Jiang Tian knew she was absolutely safe in the country; any danger she encountered in his previous life only occurred after she went abroad.

Since that was the case, Jiang Tian wanted to play it safe and planned to investigate this man's detailed identity information tomorrow.

After Jiang Tian left with Jiang Xiaoyu...

But she didn't notice.

At the entrance of the building, Song Qiuwan stood quietly, her eyes blankly staring at Jiang Tian's retreating figure, lost in thought.

A moment later.

Song Qiuwan bit her lip, then went upstairs and left.

Come to think of it, they were neighbors growing up together.

Uncle Song lived in building one, while Jiang Tian's family lived in building two; they weren't in the same building, which was why Jiang Xiaoyu hadn't seen Song Qiuwan.

"Why didn't you say hello?"

Just as Song Qiuwan was about to enter the stairwell, a woman with her arms crossed asked with a mixture of amusement and interest.

Her back was almost invisible in the dim stairwell light.

Song Qiuwan shuddered at the question, her expression a mixture of pain and struggle. "If I join you and associate with him, it will only harm him."

"Don't say that. After all, we're fighting for justice, fighting for the national interest. Don't make us sound so dangerous," the mysterious woman said, stepping out of the shadows.

She was a young girl who looked about eighteen or nineteen, not very tall, but her voice was surprisingly mature.

Song Qiuwan smiled but didn't answer.

"Aren't you going to say hello to him? Perhaps he still likes you," the woman said, increasingly curious.

"He's probably too busy enjoying himself to think about anything else. He probably hasn't even forgotten what his sister Wan'er looks like. He's doing well. I don't want to disturb him, and I certainly don't want him to get into danger," Song Qiuwan said expressionlessly as she went upstairs.

"That's such a pity. We missed out on a show," the mysterious woman sighed slightly.

Song Qiuwan went upstairs with her head down.

No one saw the expression on Song Qiuwan's face.

Actually, if Jiang Tian had overheard their conversation, he would have been extremely shocked.

Because Song Qiuwan's death didn't seem to be as simple as it had been described in his previous life.

Meanwhile, Jiang Tian had finished buying soy sauce.

He was strolling back to the neighborhood with his younger sister, Jiang Xiaoyu.

As I entered the residential complex, I saw the man in the white suit from before, smiling broadly.

He was talking to the person in front of him.

It was difficult for others to hear clearly.

Because the corner they were in was very secluded.

Even though Jiang Tian was walking several dozen steps away from them, it wasn't difficult for him given his physical abilities.

"This woman thought I'd really fallen for her trick, huh? Little does she know, I'm also using her to find their mole embedded in our ranks. Damn it, she's caused us huge losses. Don't let me find out who that mole is, or I'll tear him to pieces! I risked my life, taking all this trouble to find that mole, and this woman won't give me a single chance! Damn it!!!"

Hiss!!!

Jiang Tian's eyes widened in astonishment.

No way.

Sister Wan'er, who is she?



Chapter 186 I almost forgot, I'm still a student!


Jiang Tian watched the two leave. The man in the white suit also saw Jiang Tian, ​​smiled slightly at him—a bright and sunny smile that made it impossible for anyone to guess he was a criminal.

The moment they brushed past each other,

the man in the white suit stopped.

"Brother, what's wrong?" someone asked.

"Nothing, maybe I'm overthinking it."

The man in the white suit looked at Jiang Tian's back, confirming once again that his intuition might have been wrong.

That's right.

Just now, in that fleeting moment of passing by, the man in the white suit felt a chill run down his spine.

A deadly sense of threat washed over him.

In that instant, almost every muscle in his body hardened, ready to spring into action at any moment.

This was honed from countless life-or-death crises on the battlefield.

Could an ordinary person, just an ordinary person he encountered, really pose such a danger to him?

Overthinking it.

Definitely overthinking it.

The man in the white suit shook his head and chuckled. Perhaps he'd been too stressed lately, leading to such an absurd illusion.

After all, he came from that organization!

How could anyone pose such a threat to him?

But he didn't know.

In the instant they brushed past each other, Jiang Tian's raised hand ultimately fell back down.

Just now, Jiang Tian had a fleeting impulse to finish him off.

But in the end, he decided against it.

He decided to investigate this man's identity first, to see if he could find out.

Back home, Zheng Fang had already prepared dinner.

That's right, Jiang Tian's mother was named Zheng Fang. She worked at the Finance Bureau, holding a minor leadership position.

Jiang Tian inherited his mother's genes completely; he was very handsome.

Zheng Fang liked him more and more the more she looked at him.

"Mom, why are you looking at me like that?" Jiang Tian asked curiously while eating.

“Look at my son.” Zheng Fang’s lips curled into a doting smile. “I shouldn’t have let you go to the police station in the first place. Your dad was often gone for days at a time. Now look what’s happened, you’re even busier than your dad.”

“Mom, my brother prioritizes his career.” Jiang Xiaoyu quickly served Jiang Tian some food. “Right, brother?”

The nagging continued, but Jiang Tian really enjoyed this harmonious family atmosphere.

“It’s a pity about your sister Wan’er. If your sister Wan’er were here,” Zheng Fang said regretfully.

Jiang Tian ate with his head down.

“Alright, alright, what’s the point of saying all this? Let’s eat.” Zheng Fang realized she’d said too much and quickly changed the subject.

“Mom, I’m full.”

Jiang Tian returned to his room, his mind filled with thoughts of Sister Wan’er.

It wasn’t that Jiang Tian was fickle or promiscuous; rather, he harbored a deep sense of guilt towards Sister Wan’er.

Wan'er was truly sincere to him. He still remembered the tears that welled up in her eyes the moment she turned away after their falling out because of Wang Yifei.

But back then, Jiang Tian's heart was completely devoted to Wang Yifei, and he only felt disgust towards Wan'er.

Now that he was older, he wanted to make amends, but Wan'er had already given him no chance.

"Wait..."

Jiang Tian sat bolt upright in bed.

Because he had always believed that the person who helped him in his past life was Zhao Qiuni.

Because he didn't know anyone else besides her; Wan'er was abroad at that time.

What if Sister Wan'er knew about his ordeal and was paying attention to him?

If Sister Wan'er really had a mysterious identity...

"I'm probably overthinking it."

Jiang Tian shook his head, recalling the countless horrible things he had done because of Wang Yifei in his previous life.

Even if Sister Wan'er had a mysterious identity, how could she possibly help him?

If she were to inquire about someone dying in the neighborhood, would she be the one to help him?

Ring ring ring!!!

Just then, Jiang Tian's phone rang.

Glancing at it, Jiang Tian was surprised to find that it was Lin Jinxuan, the contact name.

This was his advisor's ex-girlfriend.

Strictly speaking, Jiang Tian hadn't graduated yet; he was currently in his senior year internship.

In his previous life...

Jiang Tian remembered clearly that his advisor's ex-girlfriend, Lin Jinxuan, had never called him.

Speaking of which, it was all because of his advisor's ex-girlfriend. She was incredibly beautiful, even Jiang Tian remembered her vividly. She was the kind of girl whose parents were both highly educated professors, and who was proficient in all the arts, from music and chess to calligraphy and painting.

She was very cultured, even conservative in her thinking, and worked as a counselor in the forensic medicine department.

Later, Lin Jinxuan broke up with her advisor because they felt their values ​​clashed, and she wanted to find a job off-campus. Jiang Tian happened to run into her.

At the time, Jiang Tian, ​​wanting to help, planned to introduce her to Uncle Song at the municipal police bureau.

However, the advisor found out about this and assumed Jiang Tian was trying to steal her away, leading to some arguments and conflict. The matter was eventually dropped.

Afterwards, the advisor harbored a deep resentment towards Jiang Tian and targeted him relentlessly.

In his previous life, when Jiang Tian was down on his luck, he returned to the school after graduation to collect his diploma. By then, the advisor had been promoted and stayed on as a faculty member, but was still relentlessly pursuing Lin Jinxuan.

However, Jiang Tian felt that this counselor was truly unworthy of Lin Jinxuan. Before his transmigration, the counselor had already become an associate professor, but was imprisoned after being exposed for misconduct.

As for Lin Jinxuan, they were merely nodding acquaintances; Jiang Tian had never considered poaching her.

"Counselor Lin's forensic expertise is absolutely top-notch. If we brought her to the Municipal Bureau, it would be much more convenient to handle cases with acquaintances around!" Jiang Tian stroked his chin.

In his previous life, Counselor Lin had been transferred to the General Bureau and later even became a leading authority in the field of forensic medicine.

Jiang Tian pondered for a moment, but his hands moved quickly, answering the phone.

"Jiang Tian, ​​I remember you did your anti-drug internship at the Yunhai branch, right?" Lin Jinxuan asked.

"That's right, Sister Lin," Jiang Tian quickly replied.

Jiang Tian had a very good impression of Lin Jinxuan.

"The teachers just sent a group message saying all drug enforcement interns need to return to school tomorrow. Headquarters and drug enforcement personnel will be there to guide you on missions. This is a huge opportunity for you; I heard good performance could lead to early promotion," Lin Jinxuan said directly.

"Okay, thank you, Sister Lin," Jiang Tian quickly replied.

"I was worried your advisor, Sun Shuo, might have a problem with you and wouldn't have notified you, so I'm letting you know in advance," Lin Jinxuan continued.

Jiang Tian's lips twitched.

He knew what had happened without even thinking; it was definitely because of the Yun family's gang in Gaodian Town. Gao Xiaolong had mentioned it to him that evening.

They even wanted him to lead the team; he hadn't expected things to move so quickly.

Lin Jinxuan's guess was indeed correct; Sun Shuo really didn't call him.

Generally speaking, this was a headquarters-led initiative. If Jiang Tian was just an ordinary intern with no connections and missed this opportunity, he would truly be missing out.

But now...

Although it wouldn't be of much use to him, Jiang Tian didn't mind going back.

Thinking to himself, Jiang Tian opened his phone and entered a chat group.

To be honest, if it weren't for Fang Jie and his high school classmates tonight, Jiang Tian would have almost forgotten about it.

He found the group chat for Class 3 of the 2000 Detective Studies program.



Chapter 187 Liang Ru, abandoned by everyone, was filled with deep regret.


Sure enough, the group chat was already discussing this issue.

Jiang Tian majored in criminal investigation in university. Out of fifty students in his class, Jiang Tian remembered that only about seven, including himself, had gone on to do narcotics control internships.

The rest had mostly gone on to internships in criminal investigation and other branches of the police force across the country.

After all, those who know about narcotics control know it's the most dangerous of all police branches.

The class monitor exclaimed, "Holy crap, this is crazy! It seems to be an operation by the General Bureau, but only narcotics control interns were selected."

"Do those of us who went on to criminal investigation still have a chance?"

"Probably not."

"I think only seven of us from our class went on to become interns, right?"

"@Jiang Tian, ​​I remember, out of the seven of you, you were the only one who got a permanent position in the Yunhai City Narcotics Control Bureau, right? What happened?"

The class group chat was already buzzing.

Some even tagged Jiang Tian.

Luckily, he had a photographic memory; otherwise, Jiang Tian would probably have forgotten most of his classmates long ago.

But now, seeing the nicknames, Jiang Tian recognized them all. The one tagging him was the vice-class monitor, a burly-looking girl with glasses who had joined the Yunhai Xicheng Branch Narcotics Control Team.

The class monitor said, "What's the point of calling him? Liu Zhong, come out and explain what's going on. Even if Jiang Tian, ​​that good-for-nothing, is lucky to stay in the city bureau, it's already an extraordinary achievement that he hasn't been expelled."

Jiang Tian also remembered Liu Zhong. After graduation, he went directly to the headquarters as a trainee police officer, one of the seven from his class who chose narcotics control.

As for the bottom-of-the-class good-for-nothing...

Actually, Jiang Tian's grades weren't that bad, but this class monitor, Wang Hao, was a lackey of their advisor, Sun Shuo. He was very good at flattering people. Because Jiang Tian had a conflict with the advisor, this guy also targeted him, often calling him a good-for-nothing and a drag on the team whenever he had a bad day.

Wang Hao, the squad leader, had gone with Zhou Yun. Jiang Tian stayed in the Yunhai branch, but despite being the best-performing squad leader, he was relegated to the Xicheng branch. Wang Hao was deeply dissatisfied and harbored resentment towards Jiang Tian. "What a waste! Why does he deserve to be in the Yunhai branch?" he thought.

Liu Zhong: "No, I'm just a menial worker in the headquarters. But I heard that the Ministry of Public Security deployed nearly a thousand police officers and soldiers to Yunhai to raid a group of terrifying drug dealers. The search is still ongoing, and they're preparing to send some narcotics trainees in. Those who perform well can be promoted to full-fledged officers on the spot."

"Damn."

"I regret it. I should have just chosen to be a narcotics officer."

"My god, promotion directly! That's so brutal! That shortens the process by a year!"

"So what? I heard the squad leader got a second-class merit and is already on the promotion process. He definitely won't care."

"Damn, that's fierce!"

On the other end of the phone, Wang Hao's lips curled into a smile.

In the world of physique, the saying goes, "One step ahead leads to many more."

He started with a second-class merit award, albeit by luck—he ran into a wanted criminal and got caught.

But this gave him an incredible start.

“@Wang Hao, squad leader, get up! Don't forget to mentor us.”

“I definitely will if I get the chance. By the way, where’s that good-for-nothing Jiang Tian? Still doing his internship, right? Oh, and I’m about to be promoted to the Yunhai branch. Want me to look after you then?”

“Why isn’t Jiang Tian saying anything? Is he embarrassed because he’s doing so badly?”

Jiang Tian stared at his phone silently, unmoved, even a little amused as he typed, "Thanks, no need to trouble yourself, I'm fine now."

For Jiang Tian, ​​going back to school tomorrow was pointless.

He could just wait at the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

Besides, there was a lot to do tomorrow—so many cases, so many leads, all needing review. The Narcotics Control Unit was practically overwhelmed; even the Criminal Investigation Unit had to be seconded.

Deputy Class Monitor Zhou Yun: "Jiang Tian, ​​are you going tomorrow?"

Wang Hao: "This good-for-nothing got lucky and got into the Municipal Public Security Bureau, but a good-for-nothing is a good-for-nothing no matter where he is. Even if he gets to the Municipal Public Security Bureau, what use is he? Tomorrow's operation will test his abilities. With this good-for-nothing's abilities, even if he goes, he'll probably just embarrass himself. I doubt he'll go."

Counselor Sun Shuo: "@All members, I'm organizing everyone to report to the Municipal Public Security Bureau tomorrow. All narcotics interns."

"Roger!!!"

Jiang Tian glanced at it but didn't reply.

Six people in total.

As for what the others in the group were saying, Jiang Tian didn't care.

Because this was just their class.

Tomorrow, it won't just be them.

It'll be the entire Yunhai, all the anti-drug trainee police officers.

The total number adds up to at least three digits.

And just as Jiang Tian was resting peacefully.

Lakeview Villa District.

Mu Feng's face flushed red and then turned pale as he looked at the woman in front of him.

*Slap!*

A moment later, Mu Feng couldn't hold back any longer and slapped her.

"Look at what you've done!" Mu Feng roared angrily.

"I don't know, that little bastard Jiang Tian, ​​how could someone as high-class as Miss Qin be interested in that little bastard Jiang Tian?" Liang Ru cried, covering her face.

"Do you know what five or six First-Class Merits, even a history-breaking Special-Class Merit, means?" Mu Feng roared angrily.

It represents an infinitely bright future.

Mu Feng truly didn't know that Jiang Tian and Mu Ya had this kind of relationship.

But this vile woman had even kicked Jiang Tian out.

"I know, I admit I misjudged him, but I just wanted to scare him. Who knew? But was I wrong? That little bastard knows Miss Qin, he must have been cheating on her long ago, he's no good." Liang Ru was still trying to defend herself.

*Slap!*

Mu Feng couldn't hold back any longer and slapped her again.

"Mu Feng, you dare hit me again! Waaah, I don't want to live anymore!" Liang Ru covered her face and sat on the ground, crying bitterly.

Mu Feng, panting heavily with bloodshot eyes, said, "You idiot, do you know that Jiang Tian and Qin Shiyu just met tonight? Jiang Tian is Qin Shiyu's savior. Seeing you insult Jiang Tian like that, he couldn't stand it and stood up for her. You idiot, can't you even understand that? You not only offended Jiang Tian, ​​you've also offended Miss Qin to death! Do you want me dead?"

Mu Feng roared, almost enunciating each word.

*Buzz!*

Liang Ru stood frozen, as if struck by lightning.

Her expression was one of utter confusion.

She looked at Mu Feng with a look of utter hatred, as if he wanted to kill her.

Her daughter covered her face and wept uncontrollably, occasionally glancing up at her with hateful eyes.

Even...

Even Old Master Mu...

looked at her with eyes filled with disappointment and sighs.

"Dad, what did I do wrong? I did it all for this family. Can't I just kneel down and apologize to Jiang Tian?" Liang Ru was utterly heartbroken.

"Divorce! We have to get a divorce! You stupid woman, you've ruined me!"

Mu Feng was furious. If only he hadn't offended Jiang Tian so badly today.

Jiang Tian would not only be marrying Mu Ya, but he would also gain Qin Shiyu's favor because he saved her. Their investments would be even more successful, perhaps even better than expected.

What kind of person was Qin Shiyu?

Even a tiny bit of her money was enough to propel Mu Feng's entire company to new heights.

The Queen of Financial Investment.

This wasn't a joke. Anyone who invested in his company could get unlimited loans from banks simply because Qin Shiyu had invested, without even needing to use any connections.

No illegal operations required.

A future son-in-law, a promising successor to Old Master Mu, a financial queen, someone who could save his company.

She'd actually been completely offended by this stupid woman.

I told you to apologize tonight, and you went and offended her instead?

"Dad, Mu Feng wants a divorce." Liang Ru was stunned.

She didn't think she'd done anything wrong.

"Sigh, you can go now." Old Master Mu seemed to age considerably in an instant, his back hunched as he walked out.

Buzz!!!

Liang Ru was dumbfounded.

She was abandoned by everyone.

Why?

Liang Ru's face turned pale. In that instant, Liang Ru was filled with deep regret.

She wanted to slap herself a few times. Why, why did she have to be so foolish!

Clearly, if she hadn't said anything, a special meritorious service recipient would have become their son-in-law, and Miss Qin treated her son-in-law as her savior. Naturally, she would have received Miss Qin Shiyu's full support.

They could even have directly left the Cloud Sea and broken through to the peak.

And now everything is gone.

He's now utterly filled with regret, but unfortunately, there's no going back.



Chapter 188 You few interns dare to target me, a first-class police superintendent?


“Mu Ya…”

Liang Ru looked at her beloved daughter, the gentlest and most considerate girl she knew.

“You’re not my mother,” Mu Ya said, her eyes red.

Liang Ru broke down: “Mu Ya, I was doing this for your own good. I even found you a blind date—he’s the son of the Laisheng Group! Why, why did you do this?”

Upon hearing this,

Old Mu, who had just reached the door, trembled, his face darkening with rage. “Do you know that the Laisheng Group is involved in illegal activities? Do you know that just now, Xiao Jiang arrested a drug lord, and who was the person who made the deal with him? It was the Laisheng Group!”

*Buzz!*

“The Laisheng Group is finished,” Old Mu added.

Liang Ru staggered.

“Give me back Jiang Tian! Waaah! Why did you do this to me? I don’t want your kindness. Everything you did was just your own wishful thinking. You’re the most foolish person,” Mu Ya cried, her eyes blazing.

These words were like the final straw that broke the camel's back.

Liang Ru's eyes rolled back, and she fainted completely after her blood rushed to her head.

Of course, Jiang Tian was unaware of all this.

He hadn't slept soundly in a long time; he'd even turned off his phone.

When Jiang Tian woke up and turned on his phone, it was almost eight in the morning.

His phone was overflowing with missed calls.

There were five missed calls from Lin Jinxuan.

Jiang Tian guessed that everyone else had arrived before eight in the morning, but he hadn't.

Jiang Tian didn't reply, because he hadn't planned to go in the first place.

There were also calls from Gao Xiaolong; Jiang Tian had to admit, the old man's energy was truly boundless.

Besides that,

there were also calls from Jiang Peng. Good heavens, Jiang Peng had been working overtime all night.

He hadn't come home all night.

Later, there were other people, like Fang Jie who sent a text message asking if he had arrived home safely.

There were so many calls and messages that his phone froze for several minutes after he turned it on.

Before Jiang Tian could even look at them properly, the phone rang again. He took it out and saw it was Zhao Wei.

"Brother Jiang, I'm waiting for you downstairs," Zhao Wei's voice came through.

Jiang Tian drew back the curtains. In the bright sunlight, Jiang Tian couldn't help but squint as he looked down. Sure enough, a police car was parked there, and Zhao Wei was waving at him.

"Come up and have some food with me," Jiang Tian said.

"No, no, I've already eaten. I even bought you breakfast. I heard that the interrogation of Boss Deng last night, which involved working overtime, has yielded some information. The city bureau is waiting for us," Zhao Wei said eagerly.

"You got anything out?" Jiang Tian asked, his eyes lighting up as he quickly washed up.

"Brother Jiang, not only did we get the information, but you'd never guess who's involved!" Zhao Wei said excitedly.

"Okay, wait for me, I'm coming down right away." Jiang Tian hung up the phone, quickly washed up, grabbed his leather jacket, and prepared to leave.

"You silly child, why aren't you even eating?" Zheng Fang came out with breakfast.

"Mom, there's a case at the police station, it's urgent."

“Don’t rush off. Your sister’s school called. What’s going to happen? She can’t just stop going to school, can she?” Zheng Fang called out to Jiang Tian.

This surprised Jiang Tian slightly.

Indeed.

Skipping school wasn’t an option.

It wasn’t realistic to expect his sister to stay home forever just because he was a narcotics officer.

“It’s alright now. The case is basically closed,” Jiang Tian said.

“That’s good,” Zheng Fang breathed a sigh of relief.

“Don’t worry about going to school. There’s no danger. Just be careful.”

After all, the higher-ups had arranged for someone to protect her secretly, so going to school shouldn’t be a problem.

“Brother Jiang, over here!”

Just as they came down from the apartment building, Zhao Wei got out of the police car and waved excitedly.

“Why did you come?” Jiang Tian opened the passenger door.

"Chief Gao called you but couldn't get through, so he asked me to come pick you up."

Zhao Wei lit a cigarette for Jiang Tian. Jiang Tian nodded, exhaled a smoke ring with practiced ease, and was about to get into the car when

he looked up and saw...

The woman emerging from the first building, wearing a white T-shirt, sweatpants, a high ponytail, and sports headphones, was none other than Sister Wan'er.

An unexpected encounter.

Jiang Tian was filled with complex emotions.

Standing frozen in place alongside Jiang Tian was Song Qiuwan, her gaze terribly complicated upon seeing him.

The sun was shining brightly.

But quickly, as Jiang Tian's lips moved, unable to utter a word,

Song Qiuwan shifted her gaze elsewhere, simultaneously tucked the sports headphones around her ears, and seemingly indifferently ran off into the distance.

Jiang Tian's lips were dry.

"Brother Jiang, Brother Jiang!"

Zhao Wei called out loudly from the side.

Jiang Tian raised his hand and slapped her: "You're not dead yet, are you trying to summon your soul?"

Zhao Wei was speechless: "They're gone, what are you looking at? Are you leaving or not? Chief Gao's calling again soon."

Jiang Tian silently got into the passenger seat. Sure enough, Sister Wan'er still hadn't forgiven him?

But, based on the experience and observation skills he gained from being a criminal investigator, it didn't seem like that.

Or perhaps, it was because she hadn't forgiven him?

Sure enough.

"Perhaps, I was such a useless beast back then, I didn't deserve Sister Wan'er's forgiveness at all." Jiang Tian sighed inwardly.

But Jiang Tian didn't know.

After the police car left, not far away around the corner, Song Qiuwan stared blankly at the departing car.

Meanwhile.

The Municipal Public Security Bureau compound.

That's how it is.

In 2003, the economy had begun to develop.

The Municipal Public Security Bureau building had been renovated in 2000, becoming a six-story building.

In the courtyard directly in front of the building, a red flag fluttered in the wind in the center.

Under the red flag,

a considerable number of people had already arrived.

These people, all dressed in proper police uniforms and wearing the rank insignia of probationary officers, stood under the red flag with expressions that were a mixture of excitement and trepidation.

The municipal police bureau, and the municipal police bureau of the provincial capital at that, was impressive enough for them.

Especially the officers coming and going, all with busy, hurried expressions, completely different from what they had imagined.

Various discussions filled the surrounding area.

Amidst this...

A woman in a snow-white dress was frantically dialing her phone.

“Answer it! Answer it!”

She muttered anxiously to herself.

Unfortunately, the line was constantly busy.

“Stop calling. That good-for-nothing. Even if he comes, what can he do? He'll just make a fool of himself.” A man of similar age to the woman said sneer.

“Sun Shuo, even if Jiang Tian’s grades are mediocre, he’s not a burden. He didn’t even fail any courses. Why do you call him good-for-nothing? Why didn’t you submit Jiang Tian’s name?” the woman said angrily.

“The time’s up. What does it have to do with me if he doesn’t come?” Sun Shuo looked at the woman with a cold, mocking smile.

In his eyes, the woman was looking down on him, which was why she broke up with him.

Yes, this woman was Lin Jinxuan.

Wang Hao couldn't wait to jump out excitedly and said, "The counselor is right. If he can't handle it, who can he blame? Once he signs up, he can't change it. Even if he comes, it's too late. I heard that this operation was actually because the anti-drug police were short of manpower. This review was just a formality for recruitment. Basically, most of those who participated could be promoted to full-time positions, just to expand the manpower. Tsk tsk, what a pity. Jiang Tian, ​​that good-for-nothing, couldn't even seize the opportunity when it was right in front of him. He deserves to fail!"



Chapter 189 If you don't give them face, what kind of counselor are they?


Wang Hao chuckled, echoing Sun Shuo's words and relentlessly belittling Jiang Tian.

"Congratulations, squad leader! But weren't you already about to be promoted to a full-fledged member, and even received a second-class merit award? Aren't you at a disadvantage this time?" someone fawned.

Sun Shuo stepped forward and shook his head, saying, "You're wrong. A second-class merit represents Wang Hao's illustrious resume. Compared to that good-for-nothing Jiang Tian, ​​in ten years, Wang Hao could become a squad leader with this second-class merit, and with good luck, even a battalion commander isn't out of the question. That good-for-nothing will probably be discharged in ten years, and even if he isn't, he'll at most be a newly promoted third-level police superintendent. A second-class merit is not only a resume, but also your capital for promotion. Congratulations, Wang Hao!"

"My god, class monitor, don't forget to promote us old classmates in the future!"

"Yeah, yeah, the class group chat is exploding. Among our 2000 interns, you're probably the only one who received a second-class merit, class monitor."

"Definitely. This is only a month of internship. A good-for-nothing like Jiang Tian is probably still serving tea and water."

At this point, a very handsome man, about 1.8 meters tall, wearing glasses, suddenly stood up and said, "I remember now, I met Jiang Tian when I came to the Yunhai branch with the leaders from the General Bureau to hand over a case half a month ago."

"What's the situation with Liu Zhong? Tell me the details." Zhou Yun, the girl with glasses, was quite burly and eagerly looked at Liu Zhong.

Actually, back in school, Zhou Yun felt that Jiang Tian's background wasn't so simple, but he acted so ordinary, leaving her somewhat puzzled.

Meanwhile, Sun Shuo and Lin Jinxuan couldn't help but prick up their ears.

All eyes were on them.

Liu Zhong scratched his head and smiled, "I saw Jiang Tian back then. He was very hardworking, serving tea and water to a senior who graduated last year and had already been promoted, wiping tables and asking for advice, so everyone doesn't need to say that."

"I knew it, that good-for-nothing, that's all he can do." Sun Shuo's face was full of sneers.

Lin Jinxuan's brows furrowed deeply, her eyes flashing with fire.

Even with her upbringing, she couldn't help but want to curse them. How could they do this?

Especially the instigator.

People tend to follow the crowd.

With Sun Shuo leading the charge against bullying, "good-for-nothing" seemed to become the label attached to Jiang Tian.

But what did Jiang Tian do wrong?

He was just trying to help her find a job, wasn't he?

Although this helped her see the man's disgusting true colors, Lin Jinxuan still felt guilty towards Jiang Tian.

However, Lin Jinxuan wasn't in the mood to argue with them right now. Holding her phone, she kept dialing Jiang Tian's number.

Was Jiang Tian not answering?

Sorry, cell phones in this era don't have call hold. Jiang Tian was currently on the phone with Xu Poye.

As the director of the General Bureau.

You could say that.

The number one in drug enforcement.

This activity, spearheaded by Xu Poye and Minister Tan, was aimed at addressing the main problem of a shortage of drug enforcement police personnel in Yunhai.

And it was a way to reward Yunhai for its recent outstanding achievements.

Help Yunhai recruit more outstanding anti-drug police officers and provide them with more positions.

"Director Xu, stop rushing me. I'm already here, in the parking lot." Jiang Tian's head was about to explode from all the nagging.

Even when fighting with Tucha overseas, he wasn't in this hurry. The phone kept ringing non-stop the whole way.

It was a call from Chief Gao, and even from Elder Mu. Jiang Tian didn't know what they wanted, but he simply didn't have time to answer!

Then there was Jiang Peng's call, and later Xu Poye's calls were practically non-stop. Jiang Tian seriously suspected they had planned it all.

"Remember to change into formal attire. We don't care when you're on duty, but as a role model for the new officers, you're mentoring them, so you absolutely must wear formal attire," Xu Poye said sternly.

Click!

Jiang Tian opened the car door, phone in hand. Zhao Wei, like a lackey, followed behind carrying Jiang Tian's pink backpack.

Jiang Tian was quite comfortable with that pink backpack; he used it as a briefcase, sometimes storing things like batons and handcuffs inside.

Sometimes, when on missions, Jiang Tian wouldn't carry them, but when there were no missions, carrying so many iron blocks and sticks was very uncomfortable.

"So many people have come?"

Jiang Tian glanced around; wow, a whole bunch of people gathered under the red flag.

The noise continued to emanate.

On the flag platform stood Jiang Peng, glancing at his watch every now and then.

His brow was initially furrowed, but in the distance, Jiang Peng spotted his son, and a smile spread across his face.

Others might not know, but Jiang Peng knew that today wasn't just an opportunity for these trainee police officers.

It was also a reward for Jiang Tian.

This also implied that the higher-ups seemed to be preparing to cultivate Jiang Tian's reputation.

"I understand, Director Xu. Don't worry, I will strictly follow your orders. I will definitely wear formal attire. I'll go change now and come out looking presentable." Jiang Tian was issued a police uniform, including the rank of First Superintendent, but he had hardly ever worn it and left it in the locker room.

After all, any narcotics officer knows what they're talking about. How many narcotics officers have you seen wandering around with narcotics insignia?

That's a joke.

"Jiang Tian?"

"That piece of trash Jiang Tian is here?"

Suddenly, Wang Hao, standing on the outskirts of the crowd, saw Jiang Tian striding briskly through the parking lot, a sneer and mockery on his face.

"What use is he? The headquarters' list is submitted through schools. Even if this kid is in the city bureau, he can't get in through connections. I heard that people from headquarters are doing the statistics here." Sun Shuo sneered.

Soon.

Jiang Tian hurried past them.

He was hurrying along, phone in hand, looking very busy.

Sun Shuo sneered, ready to take advantage of Jiang Tian, ​​this toad trying to eat swan meat—a pathetic waste.

He stepped forward, blocking Jiang Tian's path.

"You're too late. I've already submitted the list. Even if you arrived now, it's too late. Begging me won't help... huh?"

Actually, it wasn't that late.

Jiang Tian arrived just in time, at 8:30. The university lists were confirmed to be submitted before 8:30 as well, so anything before 8:30 wasn't too late.

But Sun Shuo was deliberately trying to humiliate Jiang Tian, ​​so after the school finished compiling the statistics, he intentionally submitted the list very early, before eight o'clock.

Just as he was about to properly mock Jiang Tian...

The next moment...

Bang!!!

Jiang Tian instantly shoved Sun Shuo aside.

"A good dog doesn't block the way, why are you blocking the road?" Jiang Tian cursed irritably and walked away quickly, not even bothering to acknowledge him.

As for who he bumped into, did it matter to Jiang Tian?

Even if it was the counselor, frankly, it made no difference to Jiang Tian. Suddenly blocking someone's way for no reason—wasn't that just plain stupid?

"You..."



Chapter 190 The Examiner Who Holds the Power of Life and Death, Here We Come!


Sun Shuo's face flushed red, his head spinning.

How dare this good-for-nothing?

Back in school, this good-for-nothing was completely under his thumb, not daring to utter a sound.

A slight misstep would result in him reporting him to the academic affairs office and imposing disciplinary action.

He was thoroughly punished.

"This good-for-nothing thinks he can treat me like this just because he's doing an internship?" Sun Shuo's head was spinning in disbelief.

"Was that Jiang Tian just now?" Zhou Yun exclaimed in astonishment.

"Has this good-for-nothing gone mad?" Wang Hao was equally incredulous.

You see, if Sun Shuo were to go and add names to the list, it would still be in his hands. The right to participate in this operation was also in Sun Shuo's hands!

Thinking of this, Wang Hao sneered and mocked, "Advisor, this good-for-nothing doesn't even respect you anymore. Does he think he's all grown up now that he's graduated?"

That only made things worse.

After those words, Sun Shuo's head exploded instantly.

Humiliation, disgrace, and a host of other negative emotions surged through him.

"Fine, fine, even if he kneels and begs me, I won't fill in his application. Without the school's stamp, even if he's from the Municipal Public Security Bureau, he can't get in. That good-for-nothing just waits!" Sun Shuo roared angrily.

Zhou Yun watched their discussion without speaking, but her eyes were fixed on the person carrying the bag following Jiang Tian, ​​and on Jiang Tian's hurried pace.

This was completely unlike the person Liu Zhong had just described as serving tea and water!

Meanwhile, Lin Jinxuan couldn't hear their conversation at all.

Because when she saw Jiang Tian, ​​her face lit up with joy, but Jiang Tian didn't even see her, bumping past Sun Shuo and rushing towards the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

She assumed Jiang Tian was rushing to register, and hurriedly ran up to remind him, "Jiang Tian, ​​registration requires the school's stamp; you can't register on your own."

Hearing Lin Jinxuan's voice, Jiang Tian hung up the phone and turned back: "Teacher Lin, who said I went to register?"

"If you don't register, don't you know how important this operation is to you? This is led by the General Bureau, a supplementary operation for all police officers, a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity. Becoming a full-fledged officer a year early—don't you know what that means for you, how much it will benefit your future?" Lin Jinxuan instantly became anxious.

Jiang Tian looked helpless: "Who said you can only participate by registering?"

"Jiang Tian, ​​I'm talking to you seriously, I'm not joking. If you register, you're participating. This is the General Bureau. What, are you going to be a team leader?" Lin Jinxuan said seriously, even a little angry.

She sounded quite disappointed.

"Yes, I'll be a team leader," Jiang Tian nodded.

“Jiang Tian, ​​what's wrong with you? Your abilities are mediocre, but you work very hard, which I've seen. I believed you had a bright future, but if you continue like this, your teacher will be very disappointed.” Lin Jinxuan was getting increasingly angry.

Jiang Tian was about to speak when he turned around and saw Director Xu, wearing a white shirt, waving at him from inside the large glass doors of the Municipal Public Security Bureau.

From a distance, Jiang Tian could see that Director Xu's expression was unpleasant; he was probably getting impatient. Jiang Tian hurriedly said, “Teacher Lin, I really have something to do. We can talk later.”

Jiang Tian didn't care about others.

However, Jiang Tian still had a good impression of Teacher Lin. Back in school, Sun Shuo often made things difficult for him, and Teacher Lin always came to his rescue.

Otherwise, he might have accumulated countless disciplinary actions because of Sun Shuo.

Jiang Tian hurriedly finished explaining and ran off quickly. Zhao Wei followed behind, carrying his bag, his face full of gossip and amusement.

“A good-for-nothing, Jin Xuan, why do you value this good-for-nothing so much? Look, everyone, even you, is dressed formally, while he's dressed like a child. If he's not good-for-nothing, who is?” Sun Shuo appeared unnoticed, a sneer on his face.

“Mind your own business,” Lin Jin Xuan sulked.

She hadn't cried when Sun Shuo had angered her earlier, but now she really wanted to, because she genuinely thought highly of Jiang Tian, ​​who had mediocre grades but worked incredibly hard at school.

But Jiang Tian's current state disappointed her greatly.

“Half an hour late. If it were anyone else, I would have slapped them twice already,” Xu Poye said, his face gloomy.

"Director Xu, that's me."

Jiang Tian quickly ran to the changing room.

After all, everyone present was in formal attire, and the trainee officers were all impeccably dressed.

Soon.

At precisely 8:30.

A group of people in white police shirts walked onto the stage.

And the nearly one hundred trainee narcotics officers present all consciously fell silent.

Even their breathing stopped.

After all, those standing there were figures of immense stature to them; every single one of them was dressed in a white shirt.

Sun Shuo introduced the trainee officers he had brought: "The first one on the stage, that's Gao Xiaolong, is a deputy leader from headquarters; the second is Director Xu Poye of the 21st Bureau; the third is Director Yunhai, Jiang Peng. These three are all high-ranking leaders."

"Instructor, how did you know?" Wang Hao asked, his breathing rapid and his eyes filled with excitement.

He was confident that his performance would definitely attract the attention of these high-ranking leaders. Once they noticed him, wouldn't his future be bright?

Wang Hao was already excited and eager to prove himself.

"When I went in to register, the staff told me that we should seize this opportunity and not end up like that good-for-nothing Jiang Tian," Sun Shuo explained.

"Teacher Sun, putting us alongside that good-for-nothing Jiang Tian is an insult," Wang Hao said dismissively.

While they were discussing...

Gao Xiaolong stood up and said directly, "Yesterday, a large number of drug traffickers from abroad entered the country and caused trouble. Through joint law enforcement efforts by the military and police, 70% of the traffickers have been arrested, but 30% are still on the run. Of course, we have laid a tight net; they cannot escape. Today, we are giving you an opportunity to prove yourselves."

"Furthermore, to allow you to further develop your skills in real combat, today you will be issued live ammunition and sent into the mountains to capture and kill those drug traffickers. This operation will be evaluated by a special team leader. The person with the highest score will be awarded a second-class merit and immediately promoted to the municipal bureau. Those whose scores are below the passing grade will return to their original units and have their names recorded in their files."

Gao Xiaolong spoke calmly, hands behind his back.

Boom!!!

Even with speculation...

But the scene instantly erupted in chaos.

Rumors were just rumors, but now that they were confirmed, they completely blew everyone away.

Almost everyone present was a senior intern, and getting a full-time position during their internship was practically unheard of!

This even meant they could get a full-time position a year early.

This reward was terrifying.

The key was the importance of the examiners.

Wang Hao was already thinking about how to gain the examiners' approval.

If he could get two second-class merits after just one month of internship, his future... Wang Hao's breathing quickened with excitement, and his whole body trembled.

"Jiang Tian, ​​that good-for-nothing, couldn't even seize such a good opportunity. He deserves to be a good-for-nothing for life," Sun Shuo said with a mocking look of pity.



Chapter 191: Awarded Special Merit on the Spot—I Made History


"If you ask me, someone this disorganized and undisciplined should have been fired long ago. What's the point of keeping him around? He's probably only good for harming his teammates. I'm coming to the Municipal Bureau soon, and I'll definitely expose this good-for-nothing's true colors then," Wang Hao added sarcastically.

The reason Wang Hao harbored such malice towards Jiang Tian was because Jiang Tian had already entered the Municipal Bureau, while this good-for-nothing had gotten in directly.

He, on the other hand, had been assigned to a branch office.

But...

Good-for-nothing is good-for-nothing.

So what if he got into the Municipal Bureau? He still got in, didn't he?

Just then, Gao Xiaolong, who had been speaking, paused slightly and continued, "Now, please welcome your examiner."

"The examiner is here?"

Everyone's eyes widened.

Undoubtedly, the examiner's opinion was the most important factor in this operation.

Furthermore, this was their commander for this operation in the mountains; his evaluation could directly affect their future prospects.

Thinking of this, everyone's breathing became even more rapid.

*Clap clap clap!!!*

Soon, enthusiastic applause erupted as everyone clapped in welcome.

Just then,

a figure appeared behind the stage.

and gradually, walked towards the stage.

Was it an examiner?

with the sound of footsteps, Jiang Tian's face finally came into view.

"My god, so young!"

"Yeah, is this an examiner?"

"Damn, he looks about our age!"

"Is there a mistake?"

Sun Shuo suddenly jumped out, pointing at Jiang Tian and shouting angrily, "Jiang Tian, ​​how dare you cause trouble in a situation like this? Get down here right now! Who told you to go up there?"

Because things had escalated, he, as the team leader, could potentially face serious consequences.

Sun Shuo was furious.

Sure enough, as Sun Shuo's pupils contracted, Chief Gao Xiaolong stood up from the platform, his face grim and uncertain, and walked towards Jiang Tian.

This is terrible.

Jiang Tian, ​​that good-for-nothing, was incredibly audacious. He not only ruined himself, but he also ruined him terribly.

That good-for-nothing deserves to die!

Because, Gao Xiaolong's rank—a single word from him could cause a major upheaval throughout the entire system.

A single word from him could cause an uproar throughout the entire police academy.

His rank is even higher than their principal!

Silence.

Dead silence.

But the silence lasted only a moment. Gao Xiaolong's gaze shifted from Jiang Tian to Sun Shuo, scrutinizing him for a long time.

Even Sun Shuo's legs trembled; those eyes were incredibly intimidating.

Finally, Gao Xiaolong spoke, his tone calm yet utterly unquestionable: "I let him come up, so what?"

Buzz!

Sun Shuo's eyes widened.

Wang Hao, who had followed Sun Shuo out to accuse Jiang Tian, ​​also stood frozen, mouth agape.

Staring blankly at the platform.
