"Good-for-nothing?"

Gao Xiaolong frowned deeply.

As Jiang Tian's alma mater...

Because of Jiang Tian's contributions...

The entire headquarters was already drafting and discussing what level of reward to apply for on Jiang Tian's alma mater. After all, they had cultivated an outstanding talent for the entire anti-drug team and the entire system.

Especially the teachers who nurtured Jiang Tian should be rewarded.

But now...

Gao Xiaolong didn't answer Sun Shuo, but pointed at Jiang Tian and said, "This is your classmate, who entered the police training program with you. You might be wondering why this person became your team's assessment officer."

He paused slightly at this point.

Everyone present widened their eyes.

Because in the next moment...

Xu Poye stood up, and as he did, a team of headquarters police officers marched out from the glass doors of the municipal police station building behind them.

Gong Zhe carried a tray with only a box visible on it, but a closer look revealed his hands trembling, as if the small wooden box weighed an immense weight.

Upon reaching the platform, he shakily presented the tray, laden with the small box and other items, to Xu Poyue.

Xu Poyue glanced at the cold sweat on Gong Zhe's forehead and understood perfectly.

Even he, holding the box, trembled slightly.

As the director of the 21st Bureau, he knew all too well what this box represented.

It was something only heard of in legends.

Something he had heard of, discussed, dreamed of but dared not even imagine, a precious item he had never seen with his own eyes.

Xu Poyue solemnly accepted it, holding it out to his chest, then marched forward to Jiang Tian and Gao Xiaolong.

"Didn't you want to know why, and by what right, this person became your examiner?"

Gao Xiaolong glanced at the tray, then coldly looked at Sun Shuo and Wang Hao, before scanning the hundreds of young people standing there, their faces filled with disbelief and resentment.

Gao Xiaolong sneered, "Then I'll tell you why."

At this point, Gao Xiaolong directly shouted at Jiang Tian, ​​"Jiang Tian!"

"Present!" Jiang Tian immediately stood at attention.

"Now I announce a very important commendation. Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​in the short time he has been on the job, has demonstrated courage and fearlessness, repeatedly completing seemingly impossible and arduous tasks, saving the country and the people from enormous losses. His merits are outstanding. Furthermore, to save his teammates, he dared to single-handedly storm a drug cartel's hideout, wiping out a large number of drug dealers, establishing a world-shaking feat, and setting an excellent example for the national anti-drug campaign."

"Now, with the approval of headquarters, and after careful deliberation and discussion by XXX, it is hereby decided to award Comrade Jiang Tian the Special Class Medal of Merit."

The words fell.

Gao Xiaolong carefully opened the box.

To be honest, even Gao Xiaolong was envious and excited by what was inside.

Because it was a belief, and even more so, a spirit.

This meant that when this medal was awarded, it was destined to be etched in history.

He should have gone to Kyoto to receive his award, but due to special circumstances and the need for emergency handling, the award was simply presented to Gao Xiaolong.

A first-class merit was more than enough, but a second-class merit?

As for the people present...

they were completely dumbfounded.

They seemed to be hallucinating; otherwise, why would they hear...?



Chapter 192 Promotion, are you crazy? A twenty-year-old two-bar squadron leader?


Gao Xiaolong pinned the Special Merit Medal to Jiang Tian's chest.

Even Gao Xiaolong couldn't help but linger, reluctant to part with the medal.

This was a Special Merit!

*Whoosh!*

Gong Zhe, standing to the side, was practically red-eyed with excitement.

A Special Merit.

This was a Special Merit?

In all his years as a police officer, he had never seen what a Special Merit Medal looked like. In fact, for many, this was the first time they had ever seen one.

"As expected..."

Zhou Yun, below, had a glint of light in her eyes behind her glasses. She had sensed something was amiss earlier.

Jiang Tian was clearly a trainee police officer, so why did he have a henchman carrying his bag? Now it seemed she was right.

But while she had guessed it wasn't simple, she never expected this.

*Pfft!*

A Special Merit?

Sun Shuo's eyes widened in horror.

Wang Hao was also dumbfounded.

What happened?

"How could that good-for-nothing like him possibly get it?" Sun Shuo exclaimed in horror.

Wang Hao's eyes were also bloodshot.

He thought back to how he had been so smug about his second-class merit just moments before, while Jiang Tian had directly received a special-class merit.

Is there any comparison? Absolutely not.

Especially since, at this moment, Jiang Tian stood on the platform, turned sideways to them, his body ramrod straight, but the special-class merit medal on his chest gleamed brightly.

Thinking of this, Wang Hao felt a sting in his face.

No one had slapped him, but he still felt a burning pain in his face.

"How can that good-for-nothing have such good luck? How can an intern get a special-class merit?" Wang Hao clenched his fists.

But before he could finish his thought,

Gao Xiaolong on the platform placed the empty box containing the special-class merit medal on a tray, then picked up a certificate of honor and handed it to Jiang Tian.

After shaking hands with Jiang Tian, ​​Gao Xiaolong carefully ripped off the two police rank insignia on Jiang Tian's shoulders with a sharp tear.

Then he took out two new rank insignia from the tray.

"Now, listen to the new appointment!"

Gao Xiaolong looked at Jiang Tian with a serious expression.

Looking at the young face, Gao Xiaolong was slightly taken aback.

Young, unbelievably young.

"Comrade Jiang Tian, ​​with excellent conduct and outstanding performance, after a thorough review by XXX and discussion at the meeting, meets the special conditions for promotion. Now, with the approval of headquarters, Comrade Jiang Tian is permitted to be promoted to Third-Level Police Inspector."

After speaking,

Gao Xiaolong held the two-bar-one rank insignia and placed it on Jiang Tian's shoulder.

How dazzling was it?

You could say that.

Look at Captain Guo and Wei Xun of the Second Brigade; these two captains only have the rank of Second-Level Police Inspector on their shoulders.

But without exception, they have both served in the police force for over ten or twenty years, and many have even made some contributions.

And Jiang Tian? In such a short time, after only a short internship, not only has he been promoted to a full-fledged position, but he's even going to be working alongside them?

The Yunhai Municipal Public Security Bureau is different from other places.

Being located in the provincial capital, it's generally staffed with high-ranking personnel holding lower-level positions.

For example, Jiang Tian, ​​a third-level police inspector, could easily become a bureau chief in a county-level city.

"With headquarters approval, it has been decided that Comrade Jiang Tian will be appointed as the captain of the first brigade of the Yunhai Narcotics Control Detachment."

Is this a complete upgrade?

Jiang Tian blinked. He truly hadn't expected the surprise to come so suddenly, and the worst part was, it wasn't over yet.

[The "Eye of Judgment" achievement quest has been completed. The special attribute "Eye of Judgment" has been successfully equipped by the host.]

[The "Stamp Collecting" quest, awarded First-Class Merit, Second-Class Merit, and Third-Class Merit, has been completed by the host. The reward, the "Rebirth" attribute, has been issued and automatically equipped.]

Hiss!!!

Almost instantly, Jiang Tian's eyes widened. A single glance through his eyes revealed the specific amount of sin value automatically appearing above everyone's head.

Most terrifyingly,

it even allowed him to directly unlock case clues by consuming a certain amount of sin value to see more details.

This sent a jolt through Jiang Tian's heart, and he suddenly thought of the dangerous man chasing after Sister Wan'er.

If he had these eyes, consuming a certain amount of sin value... Jiang Tian's eyes grew brighter and brighter as he thought about it.

And among these rewards, there was another important one: Rebirth. Jiang Tian read the description and discovered it allowed for rapid wound healing. If it were upgraded to gold, wouldn't wounds heal instantly?

A severed finger regenerated?

Hiss!!!

Invincible!

Jiang Tian gasped, his heart pounding with barely suppressed excitement.

This haul was ridiculously huge.

But just as Jiang Tian was excitedly tallying his gains, the scene fell into a deathly silence.

Everyone froze in place.

Special Merit, Third-Level Police Inspector?

These individual achievements were one thing, but when combined, they left them utterly dumbfounded.

You mean, the person who interned with them is now a Police Inspector?

Who could withstand this?

A police inspector!

They're still struggling to get promoted, while he's already jumped three levels.

Lin Jinxuan, amidst the crowd, suddenly snapped out of her daze, her mind returning to normal.

A huge smile involuntarily curved her lips, her eyes gleaming as she looked at Jiang Tian. After a moment of excitement, her expression turned slightly reproachful: "This kid, he's so awesome, why didn't he say so?"

And in the midst of this complete silence.

The next moment, Gao Xiaolong's voice rang out: "Now you know why he became your examiner, right? Because he's the only recipient of the Special Merit Award in recent decades. Isn't that honor enough?"

Boom!!!

The scene erupted.

Lin Jinxuan, watching the crowd's outburst, thought of Jiang Tian's timid and hesitant demeanor at school, almost to the point of being bullied.

Back then, Jiang Tian always kept his head down, lacking any confidence.

And now...

How spirited and radiant they were!

And how dazzling!

"Do you know why I made you the chief examiner?" Gao Xiaolong whispered.

Jiang Tian shook his head, stunned.

"Now you're a squad leader too. According to standard protocol, you'll be assigned six people. Besides Zhao Wei and Li Zijin under your command, the remaining four will be chosen from among them," Gao Xiaolong chuckled.

"Chief Gao, these are all new recruits!" Jiang Tian was dumbfounded.

This is a bunch of people who haven't even finished their probationary period, haven't even experienced the harsh realities of society, and you're making them fight drug dealers?

Is this definitely not targeting him?

"Others certainly wouldn't do this, but you, both headquarters and the city bureau have great faith in your abilities. You'll definitely train more anti-drug talent." Gao Xiaolong's lips curled into a smug smile.

Finally, they'd see this kid get his comeuppance.

He'd been tricked.

Jiang Tian wasn't in a good mood, his face grim as he looked at the trainees standing below. What if they were all eliminated?

"Don't think about them all failing. Even if they all fail, your team members will still be selected from here," Gao Xiaolong quickly said.



Chapter 193 Not convinced? A slap brought everyone to their senses.


Jiang Tian's anger flared even more. His face darkened as he looked up. Since he couldn't vent his anger on Gao Xiaolong, he'd take it out on these students.

He was pressed for time. Sister Wan'er was in danger, and the police station was swamped with cases. Asking him to joke around with a bunch of students? Was he kidding?

"Although we're classmates, you're just a bunch of rabble. In my eyes, you're no different from trash," Jiang Tian said calmly.

Boom!!!

These words instantly enraged the hundred-plus people below.

They all glared at Jiang Tian, ​​their faces flushed, wanting nothing more than to rush up and beat him up.

"Report! I disagree!"

Just then, Wang Hao, standing below, stepped forward, a sneer on his face as he looked at Jiang Tian.

He admitted that Jiang Tian was luckier than him, but how many times had he beaten Jiang Tian back in school?

Now that Jiang Tian was putting on an act, seeing an opportunity, Wang Hao excitedly jumped out, eager to prove himself.

"You're not convinced?" Jiang Tian raised his voice.

On the platform, Gao Xiaolong, Xu Poye, and Jiang Peng, who were about to leave, suddenly stopped in their tracks upon hearing this.

Their gazes turned to the person who had stepped forward, their eyes filled with confusion.

What did this mean? Someone was still unconvinced by Jiang Tian?

"Yes, I'm not convinced." Wang Hao, seeing the three big shots looking at him, became even more excited, eager to prove himself.

"What do you mean you're not convinced?" Jiang Tian asked.

"You called us unlucky, we admit it, but what right do you, a piece of trash from the police academy, have to call us trash? I want to fight you, a level two cripple, one-on-one!" Wang Hao's anger intensified. This piece of trash dared to call him trash.

Why? Does good luck guarantee a promotion to police inspector?

He had to admit, his eyes were red with envy. He didn't care about others, but why was it Jiang Tian?

"You don't dare?" Wang Hao's lips curled into a more sarcastic smile.

At school, he called Jiang Tian a level-two cripple. He'd only graduated a month ago. Even if Jiang Tian were a god, could he possibly beat him?

Today, he was determined to take down Jiang Tian.

"Holy crap, this young man!" Gao Xiaolong's eyes widened.

A one-on-one fight with Jiang Tian? He'd never heard of anything so outrageous in his life.

Xu Poyue and Jiang Peng exchanged bewildered glances.

A one-on-one fight with Jiang Tian? Was he insane? They'd watched every single frame of Jiang Tian's fight against the drug dealers.

The next moment, Wang Hao charged towards the platform, bracing himself with his hands. With a leap, he instantly jumped onto the meter-high platform, a cold smile on his face as he waved at Jiang Tian, ​​saying, "I'll give you a handicap with one hand..."

*Slap!*

Jiang Tian delivered a powerful slap.

Wang Hao was stunned: "You piece of trash..."

*Boom...*

The next moment...

He hadn't finished speaking.

His entire body was sent flying backwards at several times the speed he'd come up, covering more than ten meters.

Thump!!!

Wang Hao landed on the ground, clutching his stomach and rolling around.

His eyes were almost bulging out of their sockets as he stared at the platform, where Jiang Tian was still standing on one leg, one leg raised high.

When?

What just happened?

Wang Hao's clear eyes gradually filled with confusion.

Meanwhile, the crowd, who had just been filled with righteous indignation, fell silent in an instant.

They all stared blankly at this scene.

"You're all dissatisfied? Very well. It's 8:40 now. You'll now walk into Gaodian Town. My assessment criteria are simple. I originally wanted to keep half of them and make them permanent, but I've changed my mind. Gather before 5 PM, and only the ten best performers will remain." Jiang Tian slowly stopped walking before speaking.

Boom!!!

Jiang Tian's words immediately stunned everyone.

Eyes widened.

"Report: We have no objections."

"We respect our leader."

"Team Leader Jiang, we all respect you."

Everyone groaned, because what was originally a 50% chance had now become hellish difficulty.

Who knew what the assessment criteria were?

"Start walking to Gaodian Town now. Those who haven't arrived in two hours will be eliminated and kicked out. Your performance will even be recorded in your files." Jiang Tian glanced at his watch.

Wang Hao, who had struggled to his feet, looked up and suddenly saw that a group of people had gathered around him.

They had surrounded him and his mentor, Wang Shuo.

“I saw they were in cahoots just now.”

“This CS guy ruined us.”

“We all had a chance to advance, but because of him.”

“Kill him!”

Pfft!!!

Wang Hao felt a surge of rage and spat out a mouthful of blood.

Of course, the most important thing was that he couldn't accept reality. He didn't understand why Jiang Tian, ​​that good-for-nothing, had done that.

Seeing Wang Hao faint, the others exchanged glances, then looked at the leaders on the platform. After much hesitation, they finally rushed off to Gaodian Town.

Once everyone had left, Wang Hao got up and limped after them like a madman.

After all, failing meant it would be recorded in his file, a fact that was simply unacceptable to Wang Hao.

“Will it work? Will it be dangerous?”

Jiang Peng was still a little worried. After all, only the father knows his son. Although Jiang Tian had changed, he hadn't even graduated yet. Leading newcomers into such a dangerous assessment—how could Jiang Peng not be worried?

Xu Poyue chuckled, "Don't worry, kid. Eighty or ninety percent of the drug dealers in Gaodian Town have already been arrested. The rest are mostly local small-time drug dealers working on the periphery. They probably don't even have any dangerous weapons; they're just running around and hiding. There's no danger."

"That's good." Jiang Peng breathed a sigh of relief.

"Comrade Jiang Peng, don't worry. According to our estimates, there are only about twenty drug dealers left, scattered in residential areas and mountains. They're basically local small-time drug lords connected to the Yun family. There's no chance of anything unexpected happening," Gong Zhe said with a stiff smile.

This time, things are really bad.

He's about to be disciplined.

Gong Zhe was in a terrible mood.

He thought he came here to gain merit and make a name for himself, but instead of merit, he received a severe punishment.

Because of their mistake, it almost caused serious consequences—no, it already caused serious consequences.

This was a huge blow to him; his superiors no longer trusted his abilities.

The crucial point is that Jiang Tian swiftly and decisively dealt with the drug dealers, leaving him no chance to salvage the situation.

This time, however, he was confident.

After interrogating the drug dealers and making a comprehensive assessment, there was no real danger.

But they didn't know that.

While they were discussing this,

Jiang Tian sat in the police car, watching the crowd slowly approach Gaodian Town. He held a megaphone in his hand: "Are you all useless? How can I possibly pass your tests and promote you to full-fledged positions with this kind of attitude? My conscience wouldn't allow it. If you continue like this, even passing one of your tests would make me feel like I'm betraying the country, the people, and the nation's trust."

From the city police station to Gaodian Town, it was at least twenty kilometers.

Two hours, even with stops and starts, would be enough.

Jiang Tian had even given them ample time.

But even so, a large portion of them couldn't walk any further.

Jiang Tian knew them all too well, having just graduated from school. While schools had physical education classes, the main focus was on academic subjects; physical education was a small part of the curriculum.

What surprised Jiang Tian was that Liu Zhong was actually at the front of the group, running with apparent ease.

These people were even boldly equipped with rifles and bulletproof vests, carrying at least 20 to 30 kilograms of weight.



Chapter 194 The sky collapsed, a tremendous change occurred in the mountains, and murderous intent emerged.


"Captain Jiang, isn't this going to provoke too much hatred?" Zhao Wei, the driver, swallowed hard; the gazes of those below seemed to want to devour them.

"If you're not satisfied, come out and fight one-on-one, or all at once, otherwise just keep it to yourself," Jiang Tian shouted, glaring out the window.

Since he was put in charge, Jiang Tian would see it through. Prematurely promoting someone without sufficient ability, whether in terms of technical skills or experience, would not only harm himself and others but also be irresponsible to the people.

At this moment...

The vehicles slowed down. Running alongside Jiang Tian were Sun Shuo, gritting his teeth, and Wang Hao, his face swollen.

Besides these two, Zhou Yun and Sun Zhong were also in the second group.

This activity, besides ensuring talent selection, also prioritized their safety.

In addition to Jiang Tian, ​​Wei Xun led a squadron of six men from the Second Brigade, with two vehicles following at the rear of the group.

As for the trainee officers, it's even simpler. Letting them act on their own, they probably wouldn't even know where to go. Their supervising instructors will guide them, which is also a way for headquarters to test the police academy.

It's dangerous, but the growth is rapid.

"Jiang Tian, ​​don't think you're all that just because you got lucky with a Special... Special Merit, you can act like you're all that. I..." Sun Shuo, panting and sweating profusely, glared at Jiang Tian with fury.

What makes this good-for-nothing so special? And Wang Hao is even worse; he was easily defeated by Jiang Tian.

After a month of internship, has Jiang Tian lost his mind? Why is he so incredibly skilled? Sun Shuo is completely baffled because he wasn't like this at school!

If he had shown promise and potential, why would Sun Shuo dare to target him?

Right now, Sun Shuo feels worse than if he'd eaten shit.

Jiang Tian glanced at Sun Shuo, who was holding a megaphone, and shouted, "I was wrong just now; I shouldn't have called you all trash."

"Don't think that just because of this, I'll forgive you, you piece of trash," Sun Shuo sneered inwardly. In his view, Jiang Tian was trying to curry favor with him.

But the next moment...

Jiang Tian's voice continued, and Sun Shuo's face stiffened and turned red.

"You're just a bunch of fresh-out-of-school interns. What do you know? Your incompetence isn't your fault; it's because of your counselor. Because your counselor is incompetent, that's why he produces trash."

Pfft!!!

Sun Shuo, pampered and untrained to begin with, was suddenly subjected to intensive training. Jiang Tian's words enraged him, and he bent over and began vomiting violently.

"Look at this! This is trash! I'm going to write a report and submit it to the school in the name of headquarters. How could someone like this become an instructor? He probably couldn't even handle a gatekeeper's job," Jiang Tian continued his tirade.

"You're using your position for personal revenge." Sun Shuo was dumbfounded, even feeling a chill run down his spine.

Having just witnessed what happened, he understood all too well what a Special Merit Award recipient represented, even the honor of the police academy.

Moreover, this mission was dispatched by headquarters; Jiang Tian was actually capable of doing this.

This was what it meant to destroy someone's spirit.

Everyone present witnessed it with chills down their spines.

Jiang Tian coldly glanced down at Sun Shuo with disdain, saying, "Using public office for personal revenge? You dare to challenge me? You're insulting me. Sister Li, give this counselor his name. Yes, Sun Shuo, counselor of Class 3, 2000, Yunhai Police Academy. Record this: Disorganized and undisciplined during an important operation. A severe reprimand."

Li Zijin, in the passenger seat, quickly pulled out the file.

Sun Shuo, outside, was dumbfounded.

His eyes widened. He was serious! How dare he?

He was the counselor!

Jiang Tian hadn't graduated yet, there were still six months left. As long as he hadn't graduated, Sun Shuo was Jiang Tian's counselor. How dare he treat his counselor like this?

Sun Shuo's head exploded.

The sky had fallen.

Sun Shuo trembled, pointing at Jiang Tian, ​​seemingly about to unleash a torrent of curses, but seeing the form in Li Zijin's hand, he swallowed the words back.

Before he could calm down, his gaze swept across the scene and he suddenly spotted a long-haired figure in the back seat of the police car.

“Wait, who’s in your car? Who’s sitting next to you? Who’s sitting there? Who…” Sun Shuo chased after the car, his eyes red with rage, because he had just seen Lin Jinxuan sitting next to Jiang Tian.

This good-for-nothing, no, this beast, she really was trying to steal him away! Sun Shuo hadn’t wronged this good-for-nothing!

Although they weren’t actually dating, in his heart, Lin Jinxuan was his.

“Is it inappropriate for me to sit in the car?” Lin Jinxuan asked shyly, noticing the many girls and female teachers with their interns below.

Jiang Tian shook his head without speaking. It wasn’t that he had any ulterior motives; it was simply that Lin Jinxuan was a good friend of his at school and had helped him a lot, so Jiang Tian bringing her into the car was just a convenient arrangement.

Half an hour later.

At noon, the team finally entered Gaodian Town. Adhering to the core principle of assessment, training, and combat readiness, Jiang Tian sat in the police car at the mountain pass, watching the team members form their own groups and enter the mountains under their instructors.

He didn't pay much attention; to Jiang Tian, ​​they were just a rabble. With his skills, he could easily wipe them all out by himself.

"Teacher Lin, why don't you come to the Municipal Bureau? See if that Sun Shuo still has designs on you," Jiang Tian invited.

After all, the more acquaintances you have, the easier it is to solve cases. More importantly, Jiang Tian absolutely respected Teacher Lin's skills.

Being directly invited to headquarters within a few years speaks volumes about Teacher Lin's professional level.

"I'll think about it," Lin Jinxuan said hesitantly.

"Whatever," Jiang Tian shrugged indifferently.

Just then,

a sudden gasp and panic erupted from the group entering the mountains.

"What's going on?" Jiang Tian got out of the car and ran quickly towards the direction from which the sounds came.

Soon.

Liu Zhong, still shaken and panting, saw Jiang Tian and said with a complicated expression, "Someone found a comrade's body up ahead."

"A body?" Lin Jinxuan ran up after him.

Jiang Tian had already arrived at the scene, where more than ten people had gathered, all pale-faced. For them, this was the first time they had ever seen a body.

"He's been dead for less than an hour; the body's still warm," Lin Jinxuan confirmed after a quick glance.

"There's more here."

Just then, the voice rang out again.

Jiang Tian walked over with a grim and cold expression. Sure enough, another fully armed comrade had fallen.

Jiang Tian had a distinct premonition.

Something's wrong.

Because according to the intelligence,

the drug dealers had virtually no weapons, and moreover, Jiang Tian glanced at the guns—all were fatal shots.

Most importantly, these men had all been shot through the back of their heads. This meant one thing: a dangerous person had infiltrated?

Jiang Tian frowned, but soon, his scanning radar suddenly showed a large number of red dots.

Jiang Tian looked up at the tranquil mountain.

But inside…

Hiss!!!

"This is a total disaster! No, the numbers in Gaodian Town have been tallied, there definitely aren't that many. So, they came from outside?"

Jiang Tian grew increasingly alarmed.

Because there wasn't a single piece of information!

If it weren't for the scanning radar, and this was a mass influx of trainees, they probably would have been wiped out.



Chapter 195 Going Crazy? You say Comrade Xiao Jiang needs to calm down?


*Bang!*

City Bureau.

Jiang Peng's gaze was grave.

Then he looked at Gong Zhe.

Gong Zhe responded with a bewildered expression.

"You just said Gaodian Town is safe?" Jiang Peng confirmed.

"I'm very sure." Gong Zhe nodded.

"Why? The undercover agent just reported that the Yun family is planning an operation to rescue a big shot." Jiang Peng inquired.

Gong Zhe's eyes were blank.

A big shot?

The Yun family is planning an operation?

A big shot?

"What's going on?"

At this moment, Gao Xiaolong took a cup of water from the tea room, blew on it to cool it, looked at the two people standing facing each other in the conference room, instantly sensed something was wrong, and then placed the teacup on the table.

Gong Zhe smiled: "Director Jiang was just joking with me."

"What joke?" Gao Xiaolong pulled out a chair and sat down.

“Director Jiang just said an undercover agent claimed a big shot from the Yun family is trapped and needs a rescue. Isn’t that a joke? Do you think I don’t know if there are drug dealers in Gaodian Town, what kind of dealers, how many, and whether they’re dangerous?” Gong Zhe sat down beside him.

“Is what the undercover agent said credible?” Gao Xiaolong looked at Jiang Peng.

Actually, after the previous undercover agent’s betrayal, Yunhai’s problem was exposed again: its control and management of drug trafficking.

Gao Xiaolong had one more thing he didn’t say when he came to Yunhai.

That is, if Yunhai’s drug dealers still can’t be controlled, then the only option is to send a large army to completely seal off the border.

However, this involves international issues, and the debate is complex and sensitive. But when it comes to drug trafficking, it’s an absolute red line that cannot be crossed. If it really comes to the last resort, this is the only option.

“We were betrayed last time. We’ve investigated Gaodian Town for so long and arrested so many criminals. How could there possibly be a big shot there?” Gong Zhe shook his head.

If there really was a powerful figure involved, wouldn't so many drug dealers know?

What if there's a trap, or some kind of conspiracy?

After being ambushed by the drug dealers last time, they were no longer willing to easily trust Yunhai's so-called undercover agent.

"This undercover agent can't possibly be a problem..." Jiang Peng's lips twitched.

But if he were caught red-handed, or subjected to interrogation, Jiang Peng really couldn't be sure.

After all, few people could withstand the methods of those drug dealers.

Just then.

Knock knock knock!!!

A series of urgent knocks sounded at the door.

"Come in." Gao Xiaolong stood up, holding a teacup, preparing to check on the drug dealers being interrogated.

Yu Hui opened the door and walked in, her expression somewhat hurried.

"Relax, what is it?" Gao Xiaolong asked, holding his teacup.

"Commander Gao, something terrible has happened! Jiang Tian just sent word that a group of drug dealers encountered them in Gaodian Town," Yu Hui said.

"So lucky! They found them so quickly?" Gao Xiaolong exclaimed in surprise.

After all, based on their reconnaissance and detailed surveillance, the area had become a training ground.

"No, Commander, they're not ordinary drug dealers. They're all wearing bulletproof vests, carrying grenades, some even rocket launchers, and equipped with sniper rifles and automatic rifles—more than thirty of them," Yu Hui said hurriedly.

*Clatter!*

Gao Xiaolong's cup fell to the ground.

Gao Xiaolong, however, remained completely oblivious.

Staring blankly at Yu Hui, he asked, "What did you say?"

"More than thirty elite soldiers suddenly appeared in Gaodian Town. According to Officer Jiang's observation, it seems they're here to rescue someone," Yu Hui explained.

Buzz!!!

Gao Xiaolong's mind went completely blank.

Jiang Peng was also dumbfounded.

But at this moment, the most bewildered was Gong Zhe.

"Impossible! Thousands of military police have sealed off Gaodian Town. How could they get in?" Gong Zhe roared.

He had to be furious.

If he didn't, it wouldn't be just a simple punishment; he could even be expelled from the Party on the spot for serious negligence.

"Make sure Xiao Jiang understands clearly: falsely reporting military intelligence is a serious crime," Gong Zhe said anxiously.

Could he believe it? No, he couldn't.

"Did you see it with your own eyes?" Gao Xiaolong asked seriously.

This was no small matter.

“Well, the team members didn’t see it, but Officer Jiang said so,” Yu Hui hesitated for a moment before saying.

Because it seems they haven’t encountered it yet.

“How can we be sure? What if it’s fake news? What if it’s a drug dealer’s conspiracy to mobilize so many people?” Gong Zhe said matter-of-factly.

“Probably not,” Yu Hui said, full of confidence in Jiang Tian.

Just then.

Zeng Fan walked in from outside. He had just visited his comrades in the hospital. Several were seriously injured, but thankfully, surgery saved their lives, though they would likely suffer significant aftereffects, making it difficult for them to return to the front lines.

Zeng Fan was already in a heavy mood, and after listening outside for a while, he became completely enraged.

What does this mean?

Is this a denial of their efforts?

They’ve already tried their best.

What more do you want?

"What do you mean, 'no'? I admit Jiang Tian did get credit before; his methods for arresting drug dealers are indeed exceptional. However, Gaodian Town was the result of several thorough investigations, with thousands of police officers working day and night. There's absolutely no way a large-scale armed drug cartel could be operating there," Zeng Fan said angrily.

Because this was a denial of their efforts.

Furthermore, it meant that this problem represented a major oversight, a serious error, perhaps even more serious than before.

Because of this erroneous intelligence, it could very well lead to the annihilation of hundreds of trainee police officers.

This was practically playing with their careers.

“I admit Jiang Tian has unique insights, but I absolutely disagree with this assessment. Dozens of fully armed drug dealers, especially those like this—I think Officer Jiang is definitely mistaken.”

Gong Zhe, being an expert in psychology, paused, then frowned, pondering based on his experience: “Of course, it's also possible that Comrade Jiang has experienced too much bloodshed in the past and hasn't received psychological counseling, so he might have misjudged.”

“My suggestion is that Comrade Jiang's current problems are very complex. After he returns, I recommend a period of psychological counseling; his current mental state is quite problematic.”

Gong Zhe was confident that his judgment was absolutely flawless.

Having ruled out all possibilities, only Jiang Tian was at fault.

That makes sense.

But why does it feel off?

Gao Xiaolong carefully took a sip of water, unable to help but frown.

After all, it seemed reasonable at first glance, but upon closer examination, something just felt off. Could Jiang Tian, ​​with his keen sense of smell, really be wrong?

Gong Zhe continued, “Psychological counseling should have been started long ago. He's too young, and experiencing so much in such a short time must have serious psychological issues. I suggest that Jiang Tian is too young to be an examiner. His recent promotions aren't helping him; they're harming him. This examiner role is crucial for future promotions in drug enforcement. I think it's much safer to have someone experienced and qualified like Zeng Fan lead him.”

“Perhaps he could directly confront drug dealers, but Jiang Tian is too young right now.”

Gong Zhe went on and on.

Zeng Fan couldn't help but take a step forward, puffing out his chest to show off.

This made Gao Xiaolong frown even more.

He had absolutely no liking for Zeng Fan.



Chapter 196 The sky has fallen! Picking the peach that got fired?


“Youth is ultimately not up to the task. Forcing growth has its price. I rose from a trainee to a third-level police inspector, accumulating so much merit in ten years. Such rapid advancement is detrimental. He needs time to mature.” Gong Zhe pressed his advantage.

Just then,

Jiang Peng’s phone rang.

The conference room was unusually quiet.

Song Liguo and his team were investigating the Blood Hand case and interrogating the previously arrested drug dealers. They also had some leads requiring further arrests.

The police force at the station was almost fully mobilized.

Even the branch office had mobilized almost half of its officers.

Jiang Peng took out his phone in the empty conference room, took a drag of his cigarette, and exhaled a smoke ring.

The smoke drifted.

In this high-pressure workplace, even the honest Jiang Peng couldn't avoid developing a smoking habit.

“Whose is it?” Gao Xiaolong asked.

“Old Chen from the Armed Police,” Gao Xiaolong said in surprise.

“Armed Police?”

Gong Zhe turned his head to look.

Jiang Peng answered the phone without hesitation: “Old Chen, what’s up?”

Jiang Peng knew the leaders of the Armed Police very well, or rather, both sides knew each other very well.

During missions, they almost always cooperated closely.

This wasn’t the first time Jiang Peng and Old Chen had worked together; they had collaborated countless times before.

After the call connected, Old Chen’s urgent voice rang out: “Old Jiang, something’s happened. A group of mysterious armed men appeared in Gaodian Town. This group is swift, heavily armed, and experienced in combat. Just now, more than twenty of my men stationed near the brick factory in Gaodian Town were all killed, killed silently. If someone hadn’t gone to relieve them, we wouldn’t even know until now.”

*Buzz!*

Jiang Peng’s eyes widened.

Gao Xiaolong stood up abruptly.

At that very moment, Gong Zhe, who had just picked up his teacup to take a sip, nearly popped his eyes out when he heard this.

Gao Xiaolong snatched the phone, furious. "This is Gao Xiaolong! Why hasn't any news reached us about these ten people?"

"Chief Gao, some of them were probably shot with silenced guns, and others had their throats slit, leaving them no chance to react," Old Chen replied.

*Whoosh!*

Gong Zhe's cup fell to the ground.

His eyes were glazed over.

Damn it.

The sky has fallen!

What? You're saying Old Chen, the armed police officer, isn't responsible? That's ridiculous.

You see, he was in charge of all the arrangements and planning for this mission and the arrest operation.

He confidently guaranteed everything would be foolproof.

And yet, this terrifying blunder occurred in an instant.

"That group is currently at Dongguo Mountain. I order you to immediately lead everyone, notify everyone, and take your men to Dongguo Mountain," Gao Xiaolong said urgently.

"Yes, Commander Gao," Old Chen quickly replied.

Gao Xiaolong explained anxiously, "Just now, over a hundred rookie police officers entered Dongguo Mountain and discovered this group. You must go there immediately."

Hiss!!!

Old Chen gasped.

Rookie police officers, and a large number of rookie officers at that?

More than twenty experienced armed police officers had been killed.

No news even got through.

How they did it is unknown, but regardless, there's no doubt.

This is an absolutely terrifying group.

They're even experts.

Old Chen couldn't even imagine what would happen if these newbies encountered them.

Isn't this ridiculous?

Gao Xiaolong hung up the phone.

Gong Zhe stood up, wanting to say something, but Gao Xiaolong put down his phone and slapped him across the face without hesitation.

*Slap!*

The sound of the slap rang out, and Gong Zhe's face was filled with disbelief.

Gao Xiaolong's face was dark with rage: "Is this what you meant by 'no problem'? Is this your guarantee?"

"You said Comrade Xiao Jiang is hallucinating, even having serious psychological problems, even needing psychological counseling?"

Gao Xiaolong continued, raising his voice in fury.

Heaven knows what Gong Zhe was feeling at this moment.

He was wrong.

Completely wrong.

But why, when things had never gone wrong before, did these drug dealers in Yunhai become so terrifying?

Who has ever seen a criminal dare to attack the police?

Isn't this absurd?

But after arriving in Yunhai, he realized how naive he had been. The drug dealers here are absolutely ruthless!

These drug dealers are not as simple as he imagined; no, they are not as simple as drug dealers anywhere else in the country.

Using normal investigative thinking, facing these drug dealers...

He understood.

At this moment, Gong Zhe's eyes widened. He finally understood where he had gone wrong.

"That's right! You saw how many merits Comrade Xiao Jiang had made. Did you think all the drug dealers in Yunhai were useless? Did you think you could manipulate them at will, that you could joke around with the lives of your comrades? Did you think you had everything under control? But this problem, which could have been avoided with a little more carelessness, has cost the lives of so many comrades!" Gao Xiaolong slammed his fist on the table, furious.

Gong Zhe froze. "Chief Gao, there must be some misunderstanding."

"Prepare the car. To the scene, immediately!" Gao Xiaolong ignored him, throwing on his coat and rushing out.

Gong Zhe followed behind Gao Xiaolong, walking unsteadily, Gao Xiaolong's words echoing in his mind.

"Master," Zeng Fan jogged to catch up.

"Shut up! From now on, I'm not your master!" Gong Zhe waved his hand angrily.

What made him underestimate the Yunhai drug dealers from the start? It was this apprentice.

The relentless brainwashing around him—while he himself had some issues, Zeng Fan's subtle influence had subconsciously made him underestimate the drug dealers.

This was a fatal mistake!

So here, his undefeated myth had transformed into a series of defeats.

He even made a series of serious mistakes, all because of this apprentice.

Thinking of this, Gong Zhe looked at Zeng Fan with a hatred that made him want to kill his apprentice.

The car sped along the road.

Jiang Peng even acted as the driver himself.

"Director Xu just left, and I've already made such a huge blunder." Gong Zhe felt increasingly devastated as he thought about it in the car.

His bright and promising future, his perfect career!

It's all been shattered.

Now, if things were to get serious, it would be questionable whether he could even stay in the police force. A severe investigation could even lead to his expulsion from the Party.

As for Xu Poye, he was originally sent from headquarters to personally deliver the medal to Jiang Tian and to conduct an inspection.

Having stayed long enough and seen that the situation was largely resolved, Xu Poye boarded a plane to Kyoto immediately after Jiang Tian left.

However, Xu Poye left with a clear conscience. If Gong Zhe knew the situation here, he would be even more frustrated and want to vomit blood.

“Hurry up, speed up,” Gao Xiaolong urged continuously.

Jiang Peng was driving at over 60 km/h on the mountain road; the car was practically flying, and the shock absorbers were shaking violently.

In 2003 and 2004, the domestic economy hadn't yet experienced rapid growth, especially infrastructure, which was incomparable to later generations. The mountain roads were rugged and riddled with potholes.

Jiang Peng was practically driving a police car to the point of breaking it apart.

Soon.

The vehicle was getting closer and closer to Dongguo Mountain.

In the car, Gao Xiaolong carefully studied the map, meticulously gesturing towards Dongguo Mountain.

"Dongguo Mountain is located northwest of Gaodian Town. They've broken in from the southeast, taking a huge risk."

Gao Xiaolong's heart skipped a beat.

Such a huge risk, such terrifyingly skilled experts.

Considering what Jiang Peng had just said about the information from the undercover agent.

This time, he completely understood.

"I see. But what kind of powerful figure could command so many experts from the Yun family?" Gao Xiaolong's eyes widened.

"Hurry, hurry, faster!" Gao Xiaolong urged impatiently after figuring it out.

He had a terrifying premonition.

This powerful figure.

Is probably incredibly powerful.



Chapter 197 An earthquake of magnitude 18 or higher; Jiang Tian is shot.


The Yun family, operating overseas, is a massive criminal syndicate.

It's a major headache for the entire headquarters.

From human trafficking to drug manufacturing, the amount of drugs smuggled into the country through the Yun family each year is astronomical.

They use every means imaginable, from air and sea freight to kidnapping and human trafficking.

Mr. Yun isn't a particularly important figure in the Yun family, which is why he was allowed to enter the domestic market.

And Mr. Yun's arrest has no impact on the Yun family.

But...

If...

There really is a big shot involved in this operation, judging from the current situation...

This big shot is probably a core member of the Yun family, perhaps even a tiny fraction of them.

Hiss!!!

Gao Xiaolong's breathing became increasingly rapid.

By this time, the number of vehicles on the road had increased significantly.

Large numbers of armed police and police cars from all directions were converging on Dongguo Mountain.

The journey slowed considerably afterward.

While Gao Xiaolong was in a panic.

At this very moment, on Jiang Tian's side.

Although Jiang Tian ordered them to retreat immediately, it was too late.

Within a kilometer, the drug dealers scattered in several directions along side paths.

The drug dealers had already been spotted by lookouts the moment they saw the bodies, so the drug dealers had immediately retreated and spread out to surround them with guns.

Elite troops are indeed elite; after the initial engagement, Jiang Tian felt a slight pressure.

Even Jiang Tian was held back by several guns, facing countless muzzles. Crucially, the students held him back, preventing him from moving freely.

And these students also suffered heavy losses against the elite drug dealers.

Upon initial contact,

eight were killed on the spot, and three were even crippled, all in an attempt to lure them in.

This was, to put it bluntly, the first time Jiang Tian had faced such elite drug dealers in his countless encounters since becoming a police officer.

The last time he'd been caught out by gunfire, back in Tucha, it was a group of elite mercenaries.

These people were no less formidable, and in fact, far more numerous.

Upon contact, Jiang Tian dared not delay and ordered a swift retreat.

Otherwise, with several men holding guns while protecting them, he couldn't unleash his full power. They wouldn't even peek out; even with Jiang Tian's incredible skill, bullets couldn't bend!

Lin Jinxuan was pale, her gun barely moving, completely outmatched by her opponents.

Many of the other trainees were in the same state.

After all, colleagues who had just been chatting and laughing were now corpses; the psychological impact on them was devastating.

"Everyone, retreat immediately! Seek backup!" Jiang Tian fired two shots behind him, headshotting the two drug dealers who had just peeked out.

However, Jiang Tian's situation was also precarious.

As for Wang Hao, he initially rushed forward to impress the drug dealers, but was nearly shot in the head, though he was also shot in the thigh.

In the end, Liu Zhong and the others frantically carried him to safety.

They were not even in the same league; they were completely crushed, utterly outmatched in every aspect. These students weren't even rookies compared to them.

They were cunning, so cunning that even Jiang Tian found it troublesome.

Without these trainees, Jiang Tian could have done whatever he wanted, but their delays made him hesitant to change direction, otherwise all the trainees' backs would have been exposed to the drug dealers hiding behind the rocks and secretly observing.

"Hurry up."

Jiang Tian said urgently to Lin Jinxuan.

“Let’s go together.” Lin Jinxuan grabbed Jiang Tian’s wrist.

The attack had been sudden; the drug dealers practically charged down. At a distance of a kilometer, Jiang Tian shot and killed seven, but the remaining dealers quickly dispersed, taking cover and firing wildly at them.

There were even snipers and close-range gunners, making it impossible for Jiang Tian to maneuver effectively.

He could only fire headshots the moment the enemy couldn’t resist peeking out to fire.

In short, more than ten students were killed or wounded.

“You’re just holding me back.” Jiang Tian pushed Lin Jinxuan away from cover.

Bang!!!

But the instant Lin Jinxuan left the rock, a sniper bullet flew from afar.

Jiang Tian pulled Lin Jinxuan away, a snowflake flying off his arm.

“Die.” The sniper, about two hundred paces away, had a blade of grass dangling from his mouth, checking the wind direction, a relieved smile on his face.

Jiang Tian had really scared them.

But before he could rejoice.

The next moment.

Bang!!!

Before the sniper lost consciousness, as if his body was in a coma, he heard a gunshot.

Then, with a soft thud, a bloody hole appeared on the sniper's forehead.

"Let's go," Jiang Tian said urgently.

"Jiang Tian..."

Lin Jinxuan hurriedly wiped away her tears and ran quickly towards the corner of the road.

Once everyone had left,

Jiang Tian finally breathed a sigh of relief.

He was injured.

But Jiang Tian wanted to curse inwardly. For him, the danger wasn't the drug dealers, but these trainees.

He couldn't command them at all.

Their coordination was nonexistent.

They only half-understood the instructions; some ran around aimlessly, while others found cover and insisted on engaging the drug dealers in gunfights, only to get headshot by snipers.

If Jiang Tian hadn't intervened, they would all have been killed within five minutes. Jiang Tian himself wouldn't have been able to explain himself.

However, if Jiang Tian hadn't noticed and pushed Lin Jinxuan away beforehand, the bullet would have hit Lin Jinxuan in the back of the chest, ultimately striking Jiang Tian in the forearm. This was Jiang Tian's first time being shot.

But now, with everyone gone, Jiang Tian finally breathed a sigh of relief.

The figure vanished silently from the spot.

So...

When Gao Xiaolong hurriedly met Old Chen and they arrived together at the key point at the foot of Dongguo Mountain,

they bumped into a group of trainees, all wounded and missing many heads.

When Gao Xiaolong saw this, his heart sank: "Where is your examiner?"

At that moment, Lin Jinxuan rushed out from the crowd, her eyes red, and shouted, "Commander Gao, Comrade Jiang Tian was shot while saving me! He's covering our retreat inside and hasn't come out yet! Go save him quickly!"

Buzz!!!

This.............



Chapter 198 Gun Jamming? Do you even know what a God's-eye view is?


"What did you say?"

Gao Xiaolong's face turned deathly pale.

Jiang Peng, who had just gotten out of the car, felt his legs go weak.

As for Xu Poye,

his head was also spinning as he looked at Lin Jinxuan, who was crying her eyes out.

"What happened?"

At that moment,

Old Chen, the leader of the armed police, rushed over from a distance and shouted to the tearful Lin Jinxuan.

He had vaguely heard that Jiang Tian had been shot.

Old Chen and Jiang Peng were old buddies, often working together.

As for Jiang Tian, ​​Old Chen's first encounter with him was during the Tucha counter-encirclement, when Jiang Tian fought 1v200, leaving Old Chen completely dumbfounded.

Since then, Jiang Tian had risen against the tide, almost creating a legend.

At twenty-one or twenty-two, he was already a police superintendent, with a limitless future.

At the very least, he would be a provincial-level officer.

So he's been shot?

Of course, the most important thing is, such a drug-fighting genius has been shot?

Lin Jinxuan wiped away her tears, her eyes red, and said, "Leader, after the trainees went into Dongguo Mountain, they found several bodies. I went with Officer Jiang. It's all my fault. I didn't expect those drug dealers to be watched. They were already lying in ambush when they saw us coming. Officer Jiang covered the trainees' retreat, and in the end, to save me, he even pushed me aside and was shot himself."

*Buzz!*

Gao Xiaolong's eyes turned red.

What does this mean?

To save someone.

Gao Xiaolong's whole body trembled.

Before this, Jiang Tian had only displayed invincible strength, killing drug dealers with every shot, every move, and a nose even more sensitive than a police dog, able to smell drug dealers from afar.

But his loyalty and political integrity hadn't been very apparent.

But now...

Gao Xiaolong understood.

Thinking this, he took a deep breath to calm himself and hurriedly asked, "Where was the shot?"

"I didn't see it clearly, not at all. He was behind me, I just saw blood spurting from his body," Lin Jinxuan said hastily.

She knew that when she ran out,

Jiang Tian had already been shot. When she turned around, Jiang Tian, ​​covered in blood, was firing his gun at her.

Because the instant she ran out, bullets from several directions were aimed at her.

"Okay, don't panic."

After quickly comforting Lin Jinxuan, Gao Xiaolong hurried towards the entrance of Dongguo Mountain.

"Old Chen, immediately take the armed police and riot police and rush in. Jiang Peng, you coordinate with the anti-drug police on site, be ready to provide support from the rear." Gao Xiaolong quickly gave orders.

"Full speed ahead! Everyone, full speed ahead!"

At this moment, they had reached the road leading into the mountain, a bumpy, overgrown path.

That explains why the person hiding here hasn't been found.

The main reason is that the route is too difficult to find. There's almost no path up the mountain, just thickets of bushes and trees—it's practically undeveloped land.

You even have to cross a river to get to the top.

The path is extremely rugged, and the group could only cross by running.

"Gunshots!"

Just then.

Xu Poye shouted, pointing into the distance.

"It's gunshots!" Gao Xiaolong shouted after confirming.

Actually, during their rescue, Jiang Tian's situation wasn't as optimistic as they had imagined.

This wasn't ancient times; in this era, teamwork is paramount.

Jiang Tian had mostly killed drug dealers before, the only ones he encountered were the mercenaries from Tucha, whose gunfire eventually revealed their location.

Fortunately, Jiang Tian had upgraded beforehand.

Ultimately, Jiang Tian was only human; he could run fast, but he couldn't dodge machine gun fire. Being held at gunpoint was a challenge for him.

Of course, that's under normal circumstances.

Jiang Tian had another trump card: the scanning radar, which he used at this moment.

With the scanning radar, he could accurately pinpoint the location of all drug dealers within a kilometer range.

This was equivalent to having a god's-eye view on the battlefield. Even if Jiang Tian didn't personally take action, or even just commanded others, he would be invincible.

And personally taking action would be like a fish in water.

Within Jiang Tian's scanning radar's field of view, there were approximately twenty-five drug dealers.

Jiang Tian didn't know if any important members of the Yun family were among them.

However, the drug dealers around him were shifting positions from both sides. Having studied at the police academy, Jiang Tian knew this basic tactic: encircling from three or more directions.

This leaves the enemy vulnerable from both sides, exposing those behind cover to his line of sight.

In a one-on-one fight, a normal person facing multiple enemy positions would be helpless.

Jiang Tian hid behind a tree, with bushes just five steps away. Using his radar, he saw the enemy shifting their position to circle around behind his cover.

Jiang Tian seized his opportunity and, with terrifying speed, instantly plunged into the bushes.

Bang! Bang! Bang!!!

After entering the bushes, only those directly in front fired; those on the flanks continued to shift, giving Jiang Tian the chance to counterattack from the left.

Jiang Tian was in such a difficult position because, being below the mountain, the enemy had a commanding view and their firing positions put him at a significant disadvantage in the fight. Their shrewd teamwork further compounded the pressure.

Now, without the students and no longer needing to protect them, it was Jiang Tian's time to turn the tables.

Using the scanning radar to accurately track the enemy's movements, Jiang Tian instantly used the bushes to his left as a jump point, leaping almost instantly onto the two-to-three-meter cliff face on the left.

Bang! Bang! Bang!!!

The bullets struck the cliff face, but Jiang Tian had already reached the platform.

The sniper, holding his gun and hiding behind cover, looked on in disbelief and rubbed his eyes.

After checking his scope and confirming he hadn't hit the target, and seeing the enemy had already reached the top,

"Old Ghost, the enemy has come up the cliff below you. They're dangerous. Be careful," the sniper hurriedly said into his walkie-talkie.

"Shut up..."

Bang!!!

Thud!!!

The walkie-talkie's voice trailed off abruptly halfway through.

"Old ghost, old ghost." The sniper's scalp tingled.

Fast.

Incredibly fast.

What the hell was that?

The sniper's eyes widened.

Bang bang bang!!!

But the next moment...

A series of gunshots rang out from Old Ghost's direction.

Jiang Tian was at the mountain pass, flanked by cliffs on both sides, and was being held back by people from several directions.

The people on the mountain could even quickly scale the cliffs on either side.

In short,

If he were held back from three directions,

even a god like Jiang Tian would be helpless. He was facing dozens of guns from the front, two or three meters high cliffs.

These two cliff platforms were directly opposite Jiang Tian's sides; if anyone climbed up there, Jiang Tian would have no choice but to flee.

It was simply a matter of whoever came would die.



Chapter 199 Total Annihilation: Drug Dealers Kill Each Other?


But at the same time...

The enemy's relocation.

Even though Jiang Tian could see through the radar scan that the enemy's command and coordination were highly trained and swift, this was still an opportunity for him to seize.

Upon jumping onto the platform, he bumped into three drug dealers jogging with their legs bent. Both sides were stunned when they met eyes; one of them was even holding a walkie-talkie.

Unfortunately, Jiang Tian didn't even look. He went up and shot them in the head three times.

Then, with a leap, before the drug dealers from other directions could fire, Jiang Tian had already easily eliminated the three and rushed from the platform into the woods on the mountainside.

He headed straight for the area directly in front of where he had just been standing, where a large group of drug dealers had gathered.

Because there...

Five or six drug dealers were surrounding a red dot in the center. Compared to the others, this red dot was almost too bright.

It was like a sun among the stars.

"Surrounded by so many military and police personnel, the sudden appearance of so many drug dealers...this must be someone important!" Jiang Tian raised an eyebrow.

According to his "King of Criminal Investigation" skill, analyzing this was incredibly easy. After all, they couldn't have broken in just to retaliate.

Even if it were revenge, the probability of failure was high. Even if they killed Jiang Tian, ​​Jiang Tian sensed that reinforcements would arrive quickly, and these people would likely all be dead.

These people were clearly elite; how could they be ordinary people?

Clearly, the other side had a purpose in bringing in so many elites, and considering the red protective light in the middle, Jiang Tian was convinced he had no idea.

"Where is he?"

"Are you all useless? So many of you couldn't take down one person?"

"Quickly, kill him."

At this moment, in a dilapidated farmhouse nestled in the mountains, it looked like it had been abandoned for a very long time.

It was probably inhabited some time ago but abandoned long ago; most of the courtyard had collapsed.

At this moment, a young man in a white suit stood with a grim expression.

He vented his anger and anxiety on five burly men in full camouflage uniforms, all armed with weapons.

“Young Master Yun, this is a master.”

*Slap!*

Before the man could finish speaking, Young Master Yun slapped him across the face. “What’s the point of you coming in? I might have been fine hiding here. You’ve only exposed me, you bunch of useless trash!”

Young Master Yun was practically roaring by the end.

The burly armed men exchanged glances, their faces full of helplessness. If that were the case, they wouldn’t have sent anyone.

The crucial point was that the military and police search was still ongoing, and they had somehow managed to find this mountain.

Young Master Yun’s identity was special.

“Young Master Yun, don’t worry. There’s only one person here. He won’t cause much trouble. He just got careless at first and didn’t expect to run into a master,” one of the armed men explained.

*Bang! Bang! Bang!*

But as they discussed it, intense gunfire erupted outside.

There were even sounds of grenades exploding.

Intense.

The fighting grew increasingly fierce, and the gunfire became more and more frequent.

"Boss, the enemy is about to break in."

"Has the enemy sent reinforcements? How many?" the armed man beside Yun Shao asked urgently into the walkie-talkie.

"Only one person."

"What did you say? Are you all useless? Dozens of people, and one person managed to break in?" the armed man roared in fury.

"His marksmanship is too good. We'd be instantly killed the moment we peeked out. He's elusive, we can't find him at all. He's here, he's here! Don't come any closer!"

The voice on the walkie-talkie gradually grew more terrified and screaming.

This stunned the people in the farmhouse courtyard.

Has he gone mad?

What's happening?

Bang bang bang!!!

The people in the farmhouse courtyard heard intense gunfire, even the frantic sound of bullets firing in rapid succession.

But then, after a short while, all the gunfire ceased.

"Third Brother," the armed leader shouted into the walkie-talkie.

But unfortunately, there was no sound from the walkie-talkie.

Death is not frightening.

But the most terrifying part is the wait for death and the horror of the unknown.

They were all professionally trained; no one knew their subordinates' capabilities better than them.

But it was precisely this knowledge that fueled their shock and fear.

“It’s him, he’s here.” Young Master Yun seemed to realize something.

“That person?”

The four armed men’s expressions changed drastically.

Why were they so terrified?

During the Tucha operation, almost many overseas criminal groups were involved, yet Jiang Tian still managed to escape. However, stories of Jiang Tian’s exploits have circulated ever since.

“Young Master Yun, I’m sorry.”

Just then, the armed leader suddenly drew a gun from his waist and pointed it directly at Young Master Yun.

“What are you doing?” Young Master Yun asked in horror.

“When Miss gave us the order, she told us that if the mission failed, she would kill you.” The armed leader finished speaking and quickly cocked his pistol.

“No, I can still be saved! I can still save him! He’s just one person!” Young Master Yun frantically retreated.

“You can’t escape now. We’ve delayed for so long; the police have most likely arrived by now. There’s no way we can escape with you. Even for us, the chances are slim. So don’t worry, if you die, we’ll all go down to join you,” the armed leader explained.

“Bullshit! That bitch just wants to fight for the family fortune! I can’t die! I’m the heir of the Yun family, the only heir! The entire Yun family is mine! You can’t listen to that bitch!” Young Master Yun roared.

“Rest in peace, Young Master Yun. Miss said that if you’re caught, your survival will only be a burden to the Yun family,” the armed leader said without further ado.

Bang!!!

A gunshot rang out.

Accompanied by a thud.

Young Master Yun’s legs trembled, and he wet his pants in fear.

He stared at the armed leader lying dead before him, his face deathly pale.

In front of him…

A young man in a police uniform, with two stripes and one badge on his shoulder, a youthful face but an aura of menace, stood where the armed leader had just been.

On the young man's arm.

There was still a patch of bright red blood seeping out.

Jiang Tian had just been shot, but for his physical condition, it wouldn't have much impact on his fighting ability.

"I'm not dead?" Young Master Yun's face was pale.

"Yes, you're not dead. I saved you," Jiang Tian said calmly.

His thoughts were the same as Gao Xiaolong's.

Given Jiang Tian's attitude towards drug dealers, his willingness to save someone was purely because...

This guy was far from simple.

A person the Yun family would risk so much to rescue couldn't be an ordinary individual.

The other three armed drug dealers hurriedly reached for their guns.

But they had only just placed their hands on their waists...

"Big Brother, save me, save me!" Young Master Yun shouted.



Chapter 200 Shocking! You've done such a great service, are you out of your mind?


Before Yun Shao could even scream, Jiang Tian had already made his move. In Jiang Tian's eyes, their actions were slowed down dozens of times.

The instant their hands touched their waists,

bullets had already pierced their foreheads.

It all happened almost instantly.

Before Yun Shao could react, with a click, Jiang Tian's handcuffs were already on his wrists.

"Dead?"

Yun Shao stared blankly at the four corpses before him.

Just moments ago, he had closed his eyes and screamed in terror.

He had only heard a gunshot.

And now, before him,

the three armed men who had been standing were lying on the ground.

"Thank you, thank you for saving me." Yun Shao's body went limp.

Jiang Tian grabbed the handcuff chains and pulled him away.

Although Yun Shao had been captured,

now that his life was spared, Yun Shao's heart felt like it had gone from hell to heaven.

"Tell me honestly, what's your relationship to the Yun family?" Jiang Tian demanded.

He knew this person's identity was important, but he didn't know exactly who he was.

"I..." Yun Shao stammered.

*Slap!*

Jiang Tian slapped him across the face.

The slap made Yun Shao dizzy and nauseous, his teeth spitting out. Jiang Tian asked again, "What's your relationship to the Yun family?"

*Bang!*

Jiang Tian pulled out his baton from behind him and brought it down on Yun Shao's head.

Yun Shao had acted recklessly when there was danger, but now that he was safe, seeing Jiang Tian in his police uniform, his mind started racing again, and he couldn't help but think about how to escape.

*Thump!*

But before Yun Shao could even formulate a plan,

Jiang Tian's baton sent Yun Shao sprawling to the ground, blood gushing from his head.

Almost, Yun Shao felt it; he almost went to hell with the four bastards who wanted him dead.

He even felt like the top of his head had been nearly shattered by that police baton.

"Aren't you a policeman?" Yun Shao asked, bewildered.

Jiang Tian grabbed Yun Shao's hair and sneered, "I'll ask you one more time: what's your identity? What's your name? Why do they want to kill you?"

Jiang Tian already had a vague feeling.

He'd run into a big fish.

This big fish was even more unexpected than he'd anticipated.

Yun Shao was still hesitating.

But the next moment, without giving Yun Shao a chance to speak,

*Crack!!!*

Jiang Tian's second blow struck Yun Shao's shin instantly.

A crisp sound of bone breaking rang out, and amidst Yun Shao's scream, his eyes cleared completely.

Therefore, Jiang Tian established the rule of three batons.

This tactic, in particular, is incredibly difficult to master when interrogating criminals. Each blow inflicts the fear and pain of death, yet it's not fatal.

Most criminals succumb to the first blow; very few survive the second.

"My name is Yun Bao, I'm the heir of the Yun family. Those people were sent by my sister to kill me! I'll talk, I'll talk, please stop!" Yun Bao cried out rapidly, clutching his head.

"If you'd said so earlier, it would have been over already," Jiang Tian said, standing up, his face filled with delight.

Wasn't it said that capturing Young Master Yun would immediately deal a huge blow to the Yun family?

They didn't even understand why Sister Yunbao had ordered that if they couldn't be rescued at the last minute, they should be killed.

It was because Young Master Yun was alive.

That meant they could directly punish the Yun family from the top down, through higher-level connections.

They could even legitimately send in and wipe them out.

Because your Yun family's heir has been captured and committed crimes domestically, what reason do you have to stop them?

And the strategic significance of this is terrifying.

After capturing Young Master Yun, Jiang Tian dragged him out of the farmhouse courtyard.

Upon stepping outside, they were met with a dark, shadowy group of figures.

Especially.

When they saw Jiang Tian,

there was an instant uproar.

"Found him."

"Found him, here he is."

"He's here!"

The riot police, shield in hand, shouted with delight upon seeing Jiang Tian.

They had already seen the body on the ground when they arrived.

Almost every shot was fatal.

Especially seeing some drug dealers carrying grenades, and some even armed with rocket launchers.

This sight sent chills down their spines.

This terrifying criminal gang was definitely professional, and had undergone rigorous training.

And yet, they died here so silently.

And Jiang Tian, ​​the mastermind behind it all, was now dragging a man in a suit, covered in filth, with one hand, and holding a gun in the other.

Especially.

At this moment.

Standing before them, Jiang Tian's police uniform was covered in blood on one hand.

"Xiao Jiang, how are you? Where were you shot?"

Jiang Peng rushed over in a single stride.

He reached Jiang Tian.

He immediately noticed the blood on Jiang Tian's hand and looked at it.

The bullet had actually hit Jiang Tian's forearm, penetrating the bone, but was stopped by the bone itself.

"It's nothing, just a minor injury, nothing serious." Jiang Tian casually holstered his pistol at his waist.

This caused everyone around to widen their eyes in surprise.

No.

We mean...

You were shot in the hand!

How did you manage to do it so smoothly, and with such a calm expression?

An ordinary person shot like that would probably have their entire arm ruined on the spot.

"Don't move," Jiang Peng said, his face tense.

At that moment...

Following behind, Gao Xiaolong, seeing that Jiang Tian was not in mortal danger, finally let out a visibly relieved sigh.

Gao Xiaolong then waved his hand, and immediately two people rushed over carrying a stretcher.

"No need, I'm really fine," Jiang Tian said with a wry smile.

"You were shot, and you say you're fine? You absolutely can't be so dangerous next time. It would be safer with our reinforcements, why did you run in here by yourself?" Xu Poye said with a hint of reproach.

Jiang Tian didn't hide anything and explained what he had just seen. He acted so quickly because he was afraid of encountering problems.

By the time these people arrived, Young Master Yun would probably already be a corpse.

"Young Master Yun?" Xu Poye suddenly raised his voice to ask.

"Yes, Yun Bao, the heir of the Yun family." Jiang Tian nodded with absolute certainty.

"Hiss!!!"

Gao Xiaolong looked up sharply.

Sure enough, there was Yun Shao, dragged along the ground by his hair like a dead dog.

"Holy shit."

"You brat..."

Xu Poye pushed through the crowd.

He quickly ran to Yun Shao, even using tissues to wipe the blood-stained face.

When he finally saw the face clearly, Xu Poye was shocked.

Then, his gaze turned to Jiang Tian in astonishment, "Holy shit, do you know what you've done?"

"Isn't he the heir of the Yun family? I know." Jiang Tian nodded firmly.

"This kid is indeed the heir of the Yun family, but he's also a top-level wanted criminal by headquarters. According to the information headquarters currently has, this kid's involvement in drug trafficking and sales records reaches tens of thousands of kilograms, with a bounty of one million." Xu Poye exclaimed in complete disbelief.

"You've done such a great service, are you out of your mind?"

On his resume, cracking down on dozens of kilograms of heroin is a huge achievement and honor.

So Jiang Tian's resume shows he's solved thousands of kilograms of drugs?

Xu Poye has seen the cases Jiang Tian has cracked.
